### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] FlexRayFilterTypes


# FlexRay Filter Types

FlexRay Filter » Filter Types

## Frame Filter

There are two types of frame filters:

- Frame Filter uses frames from a database or frames defined via Absolutely Scheduled Timing

- Slot Range Filter uses frames that are defined via a specified slot range, base cycle and cycle repetition

You can insert a frame filter:

- via the context menu (right mouse-click)

- via the menu bar (Edit menu)

- Add From Database… opens a symbol selection dialog that you can use to insert frames from a database

- Add Frame Filter… opens a parameter selection dialog that you can use to configure a Frame Filter using the timing, FlexRay channel and application channel

- Add Slot Range Filter… opens a parameter selection dialog that you can use to configure a Slot Range Filter using the slot range, timing parameters, FlexRay channel and application channel

The frame filter parameters view is divided into two areas.

- the left side (main view) lists all of the filters.

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Active | Activates/deactivates a filter |
| Name | Shows the frame name from the database (SHORT NAME) for frames that are present in a network description or database; otherwise only the timing is shown |
| NF | Null frame flag(if * is selected, the Null Frame flag is ignored) |
| Direction | Direction of transmission(if * is selected, the transmission direction is ignored) |
| Channel Mask | FlexRay channel number (A, B or AB)(if AB is selected, frames on ch

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] help_channelfilter_0


# Channel Filter

Measurement Setup » Filter » Channel Filter

|  | Note Filters in the Measurement Setup Filters in the Measurement Setup are tailored to specific (superordinate) objects such as frames, PDUs, messages. Note that subsequent CAPL blocks usually address subobjects such as signals directly via the signal server and are therefore not subject to filtering. See Filter and CAPL Blocks. |

With a Channel Filter it is possible to completely block or open the channels of all registered bus systems.

|  | Note The channel filter availability depends on the installed CANoe option. |

The Channel Filter appears in the Measurement Setup as a small block with a number of red and green lines. These lines symbolize the individual channels of a bus system and their momentary states. A green line means an open channel, while a broken red line indicates a blocked channel.

With this feature it is possible to read off the settings for the first 6 channels without having to open the configuration dialog. When more than 6 channels are being used only the first 5 channels are shown, and a dotted line indicates that additional channels are configured.

In CANoe you can insert Channel Filters in the Simulation Setup between ECUs and the bus.

|  | Note If you insert an unconfigured Channel Filter at the beginning of the Measurement Setup you automatically get a display of how many CAN channels are being used in your system. |

## Context Menu

Configuration... Opens the Channel Filte

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] HELP_CHANNELFILTER_1


# Channel Filter: Configuration

Channel Filter » Configuration

Context Menu: Configuration

All configured CAN channels are shown in the configuration dialog for the Channel Filter, whereby active and inactive channels are differentiated.

- Inactive channels are CAN channels that were already configured once, but are not being used in the current configuration.

- Active channels are shown with their status (block, pass), and they can be reconfigured.

The status of a channel can be inverted by double clicking a channel with the mouse or by pressing <Space>.

Either all channels or the selected channels can be influenced after activating buttons on the right side of the dialog or by calling the context menu:

Pass The selected channels are passed

Block The selected channels are blocked

Pass all All channels are passed

Block all All channels are blocked

Invert all The states of all channels are inverted

Delete inactive channels All inactive channels are deleted

Channel name With this option you can activate/deactivate the labeling of the channel filter symbol in the Measurement Setup.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] help_filter


# Filter (Pass Filters and Blocking Filters)

Simulation Setup/Measurement Setup » Filter (Pass Filters and Blocking Filters)

The Filter is used to intentionally reduce the volume of data. Furthermore, individual messages and network nodes can also be configured in the Filter.

|  | Note In the Measurement Setup only already inserted filters can be configured. You cannot insert a new filter. Please use other filters. |

When Pass Filters are configured only the specified messages, message ranges and/or messages of a particular network node are passed. In contrast, blocking filters can be used to intentionally mask out specific ranges from the data stream.

Moreover, the type of messages to be filtered can be configured, whereby it is also possible to observe Error Frames.

Pass filters appear in the data flow plan as small blocks with the label dF. Blocking filters appear with the label sF.

When these blocks are clicked with the right mouse button the filter can be configured, deleted or copied in the context menu that appears.

In CANoe you can insert Message Filters in the Simulation Setup between ECUs and the bus.

|  | Caution! A Pass Filter which is unconfigured (empty) does not let any messages pass through and thereby blocks the entire message traffic. The MOST specific application filter, available since version 6.0, can also be used as a pass and blocking filter, but it allows much more flexible configuration of MOST messages. |

|  | Note Configurations of previo

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] HELP_FILTER_ENVAR


# Pass and Blocking Filters for Variables

Measurement Setup» Filter » Pass and Blocking Filters » Pass and Blocking Filters for Variables

A targeted reduction of data quantities can be undertaken in the evaluation branch using pass and blocking filters for variables. Only the specified variables are passed or blocked.

Pass filters or blocking filters for variables appear in the data flow plan as small blocks with the label PE or SE. A context menu appears when these blocks are clicked on. The filter can be configured or deleted from this context menu.

A dialog is used for configuration to select variables from the configuration.

|  | Note In keeping with its meaning, a Pass Filter which is not configured, i.e. is empty, does not pass any variables. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] IDD_MOST_FILTER


# MOST Filter

Measurement Setup » Filter » MOST Filter

Context Menu of a Hot Spot: Insert Event Filter|MOST Filter

|  | Note If configured in the same way, the MOST application filter, which has been available since version 6.0, can deliver the same results as the MOST Filter described here. The application filter has the advantage that it is much more flexible and does not require the use of different Filters. |

The MOST Filter can be used to filter a wide variety of MOST messages and events. The filter always allows all non-MOST messages and events (e.g. CAN messages) to pass.

A MOST Filter can be added at any point in the Measurement Setup.

## Options

Control Channel messages are subdivided into

- AMS atoms AMS atoms are the MOST Control Frames into which a long AMS message is disassembled, and which are then sent onto the bus. (TelID 1, 2 or 3)Even messages with TelID=0 are AMS atoms for these non-fragmented transmissions can be displayed as an AMS message e.g. in Trace Window.

- AMS messages AMS messages are the messages composed by the AMS service which may contain up to 64KB of useful data.

- Other messages All MOST Control Frames of a different type (RemRead, Alloc, etc.) or which have another TelID (>3) are included under Other messages.

|  | Note Only AMS atoms can be written into logging file. In case you want to write MOST messages into a logging file, make sure that in each MOST Filter before a Logging Block the several AMS atoms are let through. To u

[...truncated for brevity...]

# Filter block: Node Selection dialog

In this dialog you can choose one or several nodes for the filter dialog.

In the left window the nodes are listed separately according to databases. Furthermore, the selection options on the right side allow the user to further specify which messages of the selected nodes should be filtered. As a check, the naming convention for the particular selection is shown at the bottom.

Additionally you have to select whether you want to see

- transmitted messages of the node,

- received messages of the node or

- both.

this means that the node itself should act as

- a sender,

- a receiver or

- both.

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] IPFilterAddressFormat


# IP Filter: Address Formats

IP Filter » Address Formats

|  | Note Currently the IP Filter does not support GeoNetworking addresses. |

The following address formats are supported in the address fields of the IP Filter:

## IPv4

An IPv4 address is a 32-bit value. An address is input that consists of four numbers delimited by dots.

|  | Example 92.168.1.2 255.255.255.255 |

Optionally, a forward slash may be used to indicate how many of the address bits should be considered in a comparison.

|  | Example 192.168.0.0/16 considers all addresses that begin with 192.168. |

## IPv6

An IPv6 address is a 128-bit value. The address is input in eight 16-bit blocks. Each block is input as a hexadecimal value, and the blocks are each delimited by a colon.

Two colons may be used :: to combine blocks that are 0. This is only possible once per address.

|  | Example 0001:0002:0003:0004:0005:0006:0007:0008 0001::0008 is equivalent to 0001:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0008 :: refers to all blocks 0 |

Optionally, for an IPv6 address a forward slash may be used to indicate how many bits should be considered in a comparison, e.g. FE80::/10.

|  | Cross Reference Overview of valid and invalid IPv6 addresses |

## Ethernet MAC Sddress

An Ethernet MAC address is a 48-bit value. This value is input with six hexadecimal numbers, each delimited by a colon.

|  | Example 02:00:00:00:00:00 FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF |

Optionally, a forward slash may be used to indicate how many bits should be conside

[...truncated for brevity...]

| Edit menu | You can add new filter conditions or deactivate and delete existing filter conditions here. Note Filter conditions that are added during a running measurement remain unconfigured and pass all packets. ### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] IPFilterPagePacketFilter


# IP Filter: Packet Filter

IP Filter » Packet Filter

| Protocol Filter | Port Filter | Packet Filter |  |

On the Packet Filter page you can configure filter conditions for the protocol fields of Ethernet packets. The protocol fields in which address and numeric values may be input are configurable here.

You can enter the values for the protocol fields in the Value column on the right side of the table. The Ethernet packets are then either passed (with a Pass Filter) or blocked (with a blocking filter), if the values of the Ethernet packet's protocol fields match the values of the configured protocol fields. If you use value * for a protocol field this protocol field is mandatory and has to be in the Ethernet packet. The value can be arbitrary then.

You can specify a range for protocol fields that expect a numeric value. Write two points between the beginning and end of the range, e.g. 34900..35100.

In addition, you can configure the following settings:

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Enable | You can use this check box to activate or deactivate the filter condition. Filter conditions that are added during a running measurement are initially in a deactivated state. |
| Channel | Here you select the channel on which the filter condition should apply. |
| Ports | This column is available for network-based access only. Here you select the measurement ports and simulation ports on which filtering should be performed. |
| Direction | Here you select the direction 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] IPFilterPagePortFilter


# IP Filter: Port Filter

IP Filter » Port Filter

|  | Note Currently the port filter does not support BTP packets. |

| Protocol Filter | Port Filter | Packet Filter |  |

On the Port Filter page, you can configure filter conditions for UDP and TCP packets. The channel, direction, ports and addresses of the packets are configurable here.

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Enable | You can use this check box to activate or deactivate the filter condition. Filter conditions that are added during a running measurement are initially in a deactivated state. |
| Channel | Here you select the channel to which the filter condition should apply. |
| Ports | This column is available for network-based access only. Here you select the measurement ports and simulation ports on which filtering should be performed. |
| Direction | Here you select the direction in which the packets should be filtered. Tx - only sent packets are filtered Rx - only received packets are filtered Rx/Tx - both sent and received packets are filtered; default value |
| Source Address | Address from which a packet is sent You can use an IPv4 address, IPv6 address or MAC address (see address formats). The default value is *, i.e. all source addresses are accepted. |
| Source Port | The Tx Port is input as a 16-bit numeric value, which must lie in the range 0 to 65535 (or 0x0000 to 0xFFFF). The default value is *, i.e. all values are accepted for the Tx port. When entering the value, you can indicate a hexa

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] IPFilterPageProtocolFilter


# IP Filter: Protocol Filter

IP Filter » Protocol Filter

| Protocol Filter | Port Filter | Packet Filter |  |

On the Protocol Filter page, you can configure simple filter conditions for certain protocols. The following settings are configurable here:

| Setting | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Enable | You can use this check box to activate or deactivate the filter condition. Filter conditions that are added during a running measurement are initially in the deactivated state. |
| Channel | Here you select the channel on which the filter condition should apply. |
| Ports | This column is available for network-based access only. Here you select the measurement ports and simulation ports on which filtering should be performed. |
| Direction | Here you select the direction in which the packets should be filtered. Tx - only sent packets are filtered Rx - only received packets are filtered Rx/Tx - both sent and received packets are filtered; default value |
| Protocol | Here you select the protocol on which filtering should be performed. The list contains all protocols current supported by CANoe. The default value is *, i.e. all protocols are accepted. |
| Address | Here you input the address from which a packet is sent or the address to which a packet is sent. You can use an IPv4 address, IPv6 address or MAC address (see address formats). The default value is *, i.e. all addresses are accepted. Note Currently the IP Filter does not support GeoNetworking addresses. |  | Note Cur

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] J1587FilterColumns


# J1587 Filter: Column Configuration

J1587 Filter » Columns

Context Menu Path:View|Column...

You open the dialog Column Configuration via the command Column... in the View menu.

In this dialog you select the settings that are displayed in the main view of the filter. With that you can display the selected settings for all configured filter. The properties view still display all settings of the current selected filter.

The available columns depend on the filter type: Parameter Filter or Node Filter. Which columns are available is described on the help pages of the Parameter Filter and Node Filter.

The column configuration is set individually for each filter and is stored in the configuration file of your CANoe configuration.

### [FunctionBlocks/Filter] J1587NodeFilter


# J1587 Filter: Node Filter

J1587 Filter » Node Filter

On this page you can define filters for the communication of specified nodes as senders. To identify the node the message identification number (MID) is evaluated. This must match with the MID of the node that is configured by the node's address field in the database. Assignments of messages to nodes (Tx/Rx messages) in the database are not considered.

You can select the nodes from the database or define them freely. You can set further restriction for the filters by using additional settings.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [FunctionBlocks/FlexRayPDUPanel] FlexRayPDUPanelConfiguration


# FlexRay PDU Panel: Configuration

FlexRay PDU Panel » Configuration

Context Menu: Configuration

Before the start of a measurement, PDUs are inserted into the transmit list in the FlexRay PDU panel's configuration dialog. The data components (signals and undefined payload areas) of configured FlexRay PDUs can be modified during the measurement.

The dialog consists of a PDU list and a signal list. In the PDU list, you can select a PDU and configure it. A signal list is allocated to each PDU, corresponding to the definitions in the database.

## PDU List

One PDU must be listed for each data update that is required.

The following functions are available to you for configuring your FlexRay PDUs:

- Click the [Add PDU...] button (or select this option from the function menu) and select the PDU to be transmitted from the Symbol Selection dialog that opens. Multiple selections are also possible.

- Use the [Remove PDU] button or the [Del] key to remove selected items from the list.

- The [Remove All] button removes all PDUs from the list.

## List Structure

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Update | Use this button to pass the signal value settings to the hardware interface. |
| PDU Name | Shows the symbolic name of the PDU. |
| Cluster | The name of the cluster on which the PDU is transmitted. |
| Tx Node | Name of the PDU's transmission node. |
| Initial Tx | The PDU is sent automatically when the measurement starts (in accordance with the send flags). |

### [FunctionBlocks/Generator] dlg_gentrig


# Generator: Trigger Conditions

Generator » Trigger Conditions

|  | Changes — Further program versions will no longer support the Generator! Please use the improved functionality of Visual Sequences, the Interactive Generators and the Signal Generators instead; see Migrating the Generator functionality. You can easily convert existing generators to Visual Sequences with the Convert to Visual Sequence… command of the context menu of the generator (symbol) in the Test Setup/Simulation Setup. Existing send lists of the generator can be exported to a .GEN file (context menu of the generator: Write configuration to file).This file can then be loaded as a Visual Sequence in the Automation sequence configuration dialog or imported to an existing visual sequence. |

The trigger conditions for the Generator are entered in this dialog. The user can form combinations of three possible conditions:

- On key When the specified key is pressed, the next message is transmitted.

- With period Beginning at the start of measurement the next message is transmitted from the Transmit list according to the specified period.

- On message If a message with the specified ID arrives, the next message is transmitted after the delay time specified below has elapsed.

There is a check box in front of each condition. You should check the corresponding box in order to activate a condition. When using a symbolic database, a message can be selected from the list of all symbols with the button to the righ

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Generator] DLG_SENDLIST


# Generator: Transmit List

Generator » Transmit List

|  | Changes — Further program versions will no longer support the Generator! Please use the improved functionality of Visual Sequences, the Interactive Generators and the Signal Generators instead; see Migrating the Generator functionality. You can easily convert existing generators to Visual Sequences with the Convert to Visual Sequence… command of the context menu of the generator (symbol) in the Test Setup/Simulation Setup. Existing send lists of the generator can be exported to a .GEN file (context menu of the generator: Write configuration to file).This file can then be loaded as a Visual Sequence in the Automation sequence configuration dialog or imported to an existing visual sequence. |

In this dialog the user enters the list of messages to be transmitted by the Generator. When one of the trigger conditions occurs (compare Generator triggering) the next element in this list is transmitted. When the end of the list is reached in the cyclic mode, transmission starts again with the first element. The list may also consist of only one element.

If you want to transmit several messages with different cycle times, you can use several Generators with different triggering or one of the new Interactive Generators.

The active line, to which the dialog buttons relate, is identified by the "»" symbol at the beginning of the line. The active line moves automatically to other dialog input boxes by activating the <TAB> key o

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Generator] GeneratorBlock


# Generator

Simulation Setup » Function Blocks » Generator

|  | Changes — Further program versions will no longer support the Generator! Please use the improved functionality of Visual Sequences, the Interactive Generators and the Signal Generators instead; see Migrating the Generator functionality. You can easily convert existing generators to Visual Sequences with the Convert to Visual Sequence… command of the context menu of the generator (symbol) in the Test Setup/Simulation Setup. Existing send lists of the generator can be exported to a .GEN file (context menu of the generator: Write configuration to file).This file can then be loaded as a Visual Sequence in the Automation sequence configuration dialog or imported to an existing visual sequence. |

The Generator serves to create messages for transmitting. The activating or triggering condition can be entered using a dialog. A list of messages to be transmitted is entered In a second dialog.

Triggering conditions can be incoming messages with a certain identifier, or key and timer ticks with a prescribed period that is entered.

When one of the triggering conditions occurs, the next message on the transmit list is transmitted. When the end of the list is reached, processing begins with the first message again.

|  | Note In the CANoe 64-bit variant, generator blocks contained in a configuration are automatically converted to visual sequences. |

Context Menu | Generator: Signal Form | Generator: Signal Input

| Versi

[...truncated for brevity...]

Generator » Migration

Existing send lists of the generator can be exported to a .GEN file (context menu of the generator: Write configuration to file).This file can then be loaded as a Visual Sequence in the Automation sequence configuration dialog or imported to an existing visual sequence. |

If you want to define a frame using symbols from the database and then send it, use the Set and Send CAN message commands. You can specify the interval between two send processes in the Wait column or by using the Wait command. |
| Send a CAN message cyclically | CAN Interactive Generator (CAN IG) Define the frame and activate the option under cycle time. |
| Send a message sequence cyclically | Visual Sequencer You can arrange to execute visual sequences repeatedly. This replaces the single/r

### [FunctionBlocks/Generator] help_genblock_0


# Generator: Context Menu

Generator » Context Menu

Configuration of Triggering... Opens dialog for the configuration of the triggering.

Configuration of Transmit List... Opens the dialog to create transmit list of the Generator.

Read Configuration from File... Opens file selection, in which the Generator configuration can be read in from a file.

Write Configuration to File... Opens file selection, in which the active Generator configuration can be saved in a file.

Convert to Visual Sequence... Migrates the functions of the Generator in a Visual Sequence.

Import DOS Configuration... Opens file selection, in which a DOS CANalyzer configuration can be read in.

Comment... Opens the Comment: Function block dialog. Here you can insert a comment for the function block and choose the font settings of this comment.

Rename... Opens a dialog field to enable the renaming of the chosen block.

Block active If this option is active the function block is connected with the bus.

Copy Copies the function block to the clipboard. Choose a new bus via the tabs on the above margin of the Simulation Setup and paste the copied block with the right mouse button as shortcut. Now you can use the copied block as gateway.

Remove... Deletes the block out of the configuration.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Generator] idd_preview


# Generator: Signal Form

Generator » Generator: Transmit List » Signal Form

This dialog provides assistance in creating a function or signal form. Parameters are developed for a trapezoidal function. Afterwards, the appropriate lines are generated in the Generator transmit list.

A trapezoid and the meaning of its parameters are displayed in the upper part of the dialog.By setting certain parameters to zero the following forms can also be created:

- Square ta = tf = 0

- Triangle th = tv = 0

- Sawtooth th = tf = tv = 0 or ta = th = tv = 0

- Constant n1 = n2

The levels n1 and n2 can be entered as either decimal raw data or as physical values. In the latter case the applicable limitations apply.

Specification of a transmission spacing identifies the spacing between every two messages and corresponds to the entry in the Generator Trigger Activation Period dialog. Since CAN is message based and not signal based, all signals of a message get the same transmit spacing!

Since this signal response generator is only an aid for the Generator and is not an independent block, the following must be observed when using period lengths that vary for multiple signals within a message:

The generator creates a list of messages-with-period and transmit-spacing-messages, i.e. exactly one period. If the transmit list contains more messages, they are rejected. Using the toggle for residual data the user can define how the remaining signals are to be handled when additional messages are cr

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Generator] idd_signalinput


# Generator: Signal Input

Generator » Generator: Transmit List » Signal Input

You can use this dialog to enter single signals in a message.

The window title informs you about the message name.

All possible signals of a message are displayed in the list box.

The value of a signal can be modified by selecting the corresponding line and row "Value". If intermediate signal values can’t be displayed they will be rounded up or down to the nearest value.In case of the database containing symbolic names for special values (enumerations), the "enum" listbox shows these names.

## Signal input with mode dependent signals:

It this case two list boxes appears.

The upper box contains all standard signals, the lower box shows the mode dependent signals.

The lower box shows only signals which belong to the current mode. For messages with "mode dependent" signals the field "mode signal" displays the name of the signal and the field "mode" shows the current mode.

You can also change the mode. That way, the mode dependent signal list will be refreshed.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] FlexRayFramePanelConfiguration


# Configuration

FlexRay Frame Panel » Configuration

Context Menu: Configuration

The frames are added to the transmit list in the configuration dialog box of the FlexRay Frame Panel before measurement. During measurement, the data content (payload) of the configured FlexRay frames can be modified. The frames are transmitted to the bus according to the transmission type (time-triggered, event-triggered).

## Vector VN Interfaces

As well as changing the payload, Vector FlexRay interfaces can also be used to add and edit frames during the measurement period, as well as to end or restart transmission.

## View

The dialog is divided into a transmit list for static and dynamic frames and in a payload list with raw data and signal interpretation.

You can select and configure a frame in the transmit list. Each frame is assigned a payload list.

|  | Note Use the image to navigate to the individual topics. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] FlexRayFramePanelPayloadList


# Payload List

FlexRay Frame Panel » Configuration » Payload List

The payload list is divided into the Raw Data View and Signal View tabs:

## Raw Data View

In this view, you can change the individual databytes of the selected frames.

## Signal View

- If there is no symbolic interpretation for a frame, no transmit list is available.

- If there is a symbolic interpretation for the selected frame, you can change the signals directly.

The following fields are available in the signal list:

- Signal Name Name of the signal, is taken from the database.If a signal belongs to a signal group, it is specified in the signal name: Signal group::Signal name

- Raw Value Raw value of the signal, configurable

- Physical Value Physical value of the signal, configurableConverting the raw value into the physical value and vice versa is completed using the conversion formula:[physical value] = ( [raw value] * [factor] ) + [offset]You need only enter the raw value or physical value because the corresponding value is always automatically calculated using the signal's conversion formula.

- Unit Unit of the signal, is taken from the database.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

Fields for Static and Dynamic Frames

- Tx Flag: Time Triggered Frame whose transmission is time-triggered. The FlexRay controller controls the actual transmission.

- Tx Flag: Event Triggered Frame whose transmission is event-triggered. Transmission is initiated in addition to the simple data update.

- Dynamic Frames(Event Triggered)

- Static Frames

- Dynamic Frames

- Static Frames

- Dynamic Frames(Time Triggered)

- Name Displays the symbolic name of the frame. If not present, the column remains empty.

- ID Displays the symbolic name of the frame. If not present, the column remains empty.

- Offset This field describes the basic cycl

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] FlexRayFramePanelUseCases


# Use Cases

FlexRay Frame Panel » Use Cases

The FlexRay Frame Panel simplifies the transmission of frames. This can be useful with non-complex models:

|  | Example Support During FlexRay Network Start-Up The FlexRay Frame Panel can be used without CAPL code to configure a sync and start-up frame. The FlexRay network passes into the normal mode in connection with a second node. Simulation of Signal Values A frame can be transmitted interactively with different signal values (e.g. switch on, switch off). Enter the frame multiple times in the transmit list. Enter the respective signal value in the transmit list. The switch state can be easily adjusted during the measurement by transmitting the values of the respective line to the hardware interface using the [Update] button. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IDD_VG_CFG_DLG


# IG: Signal Configurator Configuration

IG » Configuration » Signal List » Signal Configurator Configuration

You open this dialog via the [Define...] button in the signal list.

You can define the signal chart and test the response of an ECU with the help of the Signal Generator.

Depending on the generator type, you can determine whether the signal chart should be sent

- Only once or repeatedly

- Time bound or not time bound

A list of the calculated and output values as well as a graphic representation makes it easier for you to evaluate the defined signal chart.

Because not all signal chart values are output in the case of a time-dependent transmission, but instead only the calculated value at the cyclical transmission time, the time [ms] is displayed in the value list in the case of an activated time-dependent transmission.

When defining the signal chart, you are free to use the default generators or enter/import your own values.

With [Copy All] you can copy the values within CANoe and e.g. reuse and modify them in the User-defined generator type in the Values section by inserting them with the context menu command Paste.

## The following generator types are available:

| None | Toggle switch | Range of values |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Value generator remains inactive | For bit value tests | For simple range of value tests |
| Ramps and pulses | Sine | Random |
| To generate mathematical curves | To generate mathematical curves | For system stress tests |
| User-de

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IDD_VG_CFG_ENVIRONMENT


# Value Generator Type: Variable

IG » Configuration » Signal List » Signal Configurator » Type: Variable

This generator type receives its value(s) from the selected environment variable or system variable. The selected variable values can be entered, for example, via a panel.

Because a permanently defined signal chart sequence is not determined for this generator type:

- This signal chart is only available under the Timing settings in not time bound sending mode

- The signal chart is only automatically sent once (see Common settings)

- A list of calculated values, the output values and the graphic representation can not be created as a preview

## Target settings

Here you can find information about signal values and cycle time.

- Sample time Shown here is the time period at which the generator transmits.You can set this value under cycle time in the transmit list of the IG configuration dialog.

- Default Value The default value that is predefined for the selected variable is shown here.You can set this value in Raw value/Phys value in the signal list of the IG configuration dialog.

- DB min This field displays the minimum variable value as defined in the Database.

- DB max This field displays the maximum variable value as defined in the Database.

- Type min Minimum variable type value.

- Type max Maximum variable type value.

## Common settings

The basic generator type settings are displayed and defined here.

- Generator Active If this option is activated the 

[...truncated for brevity...]

# Value Generator Type: Random

IG » Configuration » Signal List » Signal Configurator » Type: RandomCAN Interactive Generator » Signal Generator » Type: Random

This generator type sends random numbers between two values. Generating a random curve can be used, for example, to test system stress or responses to undefined events.

Because the signal chart is based on new random values for each transmission in this generator type:

- This signal chart is only available under the Timing settings in not time bound sending mode

- The signal chart is only automatically sent once (see Common settings)

A list of the calculated and output values as well as a graphic representation makes it easier for you to evaluate the defined signal chart.

- CANdb max This field displays the maximum signal value as defined in the Database

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IDD_VG_PRECREATE_ERROR_DLG


# Handling Invalid "Generator-Signal" Assignments

IG » Configuration » Signal List » Signal Configurator » Assignment of Signal and Generator

This dialog opens automatically if message information is changed in the database and the system could not automatically accept the changes.

Using the [Assign...] button you can reassign signals to a certain generator type if this assignment was lost due to changes in the message information.

This typically happens after the layout of a message is changed (e.g. signal length changed).

Each line in the list contains the following information:

- name of the signal

- bitcount of the signal

- start bit of the signal

- type of the assigned generator

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IDD_VG_timebound.htm


# Value Generator: Time Bound/Not Time Bound Sending Mode mode

IG » Configuration » Signal List » Signal Configurator » Time Bound/Not Time Bound Sending Mode

## Time bound sending

In the case of time-dependent transmission, the calculated signal values are only output at the time of the cyclical transmission. The cycle time determines the frequency of the sampling and therefore the accuracy of the signal chart.

## Not time bound sending

In the case of time-independent transmission, all the signal values calculated at the time the generator was defined are output at the cycle time intervals.

|  | Example of a sine value generator type Note the shape of the sine curve and the time it takes to run a sine. Message cycle time: 200 ms Specific settings: Amplitude 500Periode 1000 [Samples - in case of not time bound sending][ms - in case of time bound sending]Phase 0 [deg]Offset 500 Time bound sending Every 200 ms the corresponding sine curve output value is sent -> red tags in the graphic display.The period [ms] indicates the time within which the sine will run. The cyclical transmission time does not affect the running time. Graphics Window display Not time bound sending Every 200 ms always the next sine curve output value is sent -> red tags in the graphic display)The number of samples (calculated points) and the cyclical transmission time determine the time within which the sine will run. Graphics Window display |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] ID_LEX_HELP_IMPORT


# Signal import

Signal Generators and Signal Replay » Signal Generators » Type: User-defined » Signal ImportIG » Configurator » Signal List » Configuration » Value Generator Type: User-defined » Signal Import

In the Signal Import dialog, you can import value lists (e.g. of log files) into the Signal Generator.

For Source file, choose the file that contains your value list.

With [Extract] the signal information of the source file will be listed in a table.

With [Import] the signal values of the selected signal will be imported into the Signal Generator.

|  | Note If the messages you save in a MDF Frame Logging File (format MF4) have a time distance of more than 240 seconds they cannot be replayed with their correct time stamps (e.g. in offline mode and in the Replay Block). Here you find further notes for logging file formats. |

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGCANConfig


# Configuration

CAN Interactive Generator » Configuration

The CAN IG configuration dialog is divided in the following areas:

## Toolbar

The toolbar contains the functions:

- add frame to send list

- removing frames from send list

- undo/redo user steps

- column configuration of Frames view and Signals view

## Frames View

Displays the currently available frames and their properties. The properties can also be configured within this view.

## Frame Property View

Displays the properties of the selected frame from the Frames view. The properties are editable and can be configured within this view.

## Signals View

The Signals view displays the signals of the selected frame from the Frames view. If the selected frame is no CAN frame from an assigned database, the Signals view is deactivated. (See also Signal Generator).

## Raw Data View

To configure the data of the frame by bytes the Raw Data view can be used. The Raw Data view is available for both database and raw frames.

Examples (CAN IG)

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGCANConfigMsgProptertyView


# Frame Properties

CAN Interactive Generator » Configuration » Frame Properties

The Frame Property view displays all properties of the currently selected frame in the Frames view.

| Group | Column | Options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Basic Properties | Channel | — | The CAN channel the frame should be send on. |
| DLC | — | The data length code of the frame. |
| ID | — | The identifier (ID) of the frame. If the ID has the suffix x, the ID is an extended ID (29-bit). |
| IDE | — | If the frame is an extended CAN frame, the check box is checked. |
| IDE | — | If the frame is an extended CAN frame, the check box is checked. |
| Name | — | The name of the frame. If a CAN frame from an assigned database is used, this column is not editable for this frame. |
| Trigger | Manual / OnKey | The frame is send on bus if the button is pressed within the Frames view. A trigger key can be defined for the frame. If the key is pressed the frame is send on bus. Manual and OnKey trigger condition can be used together. |
| Periodic | The frame is send on bus, with the defined period after the button is pressed within the Frames view. Manual and Periodic can be used together. |
| Trigger frame | When the specified trigger frame is received on the specified channel, the frame is transmitted on the bus. |
| System Variable | When updating or changing the value (selectable) of the selected system variable, the frame is sent on the bus. The system variable must not be marked as U

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGCANConfigMsgView


# Frames

CAN Interactive Generator » Configuration » Frames

Via the Frames view you can configure frames that you want to send over the CAN bus. You can add CAN frames from an assigned database via the symbolic selection dialog and adjust their properties before sending. Alternatively you add CAN or CAN FD frames and modify their identifier or further properties.

To customize the columns use the toolbar button . A description of each column shows the table below.

The Frame Property view always displays all properties.

| Column | Options | Displayed by default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Row | — | Yes | Displays the row number of the frame. |
| Send |  | Yes | If the trigger condition type is manual, this button sends the frame. If the trigger condition type is periodic, this starts the periodic transmission of the frame. |
|  | — | If the trigger condition is periodic and currently active, this button appears. Pressing it stops the periodic transmission of the frame. |
| Send Now |  | No | This button sends the frame exactly once, even if a periodic transmission is defined in the Trigger column. |
| Trigger | Manual / On Key | Yes | The frame is sent on bus when the button is pressed. Additionally a trigger key can be defined for the frame. If the key is pressed the frame is sent on bus. Manual and OnKey trigger conditions can be used together. |
| Periodic | No | The frame is sent on bus, with the defined period, after the button is pressed. |
| Trigger

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGCANConfigRawDataView


# Raw Data

CAN Interactive Generator » Configuration » Raw Data

The Raw Data view shows the payload of the selected frame of the Frames view is transmitted on the bus. It is also possible to toggle between Raw Data and Signals view.

The view is a byte represented view of the frame. A row contains 8 payload bytes. The byte position within the frame can be calculated by the row number + 1 * column number + 1.

It is possible to insert values and navigate between bytes by keyboard. The editing mode of a byte must be enabled before the value can be changed. For entering the editing mode double click the byte value or use the <F2> key.

If the input mode is decimal the digits 0-9 are allowed for byte values. If the input mode is hexadecimal, additionally the hexadecimal digits A-F are allowed.

If a digit is entered, the editor moves to the next nibble of the byte. If the nibble is the last nibble within the byte, the next byte is selected for inserting values. It is also possible to use the arrow keys to navigate to the previous/next nibble or byte.

To show the context menu set a right click on a byte. The context menu shows following commands:

Copy (hexadecimal) Copies the selected data to the clipboard.The data is copied in the following format:0x01 0x0B 0x7C

Paste (hexadecimal) Inserts the copied data from the clipboard.The formatting must correspond to one of the following variants:- Variant 1: 0x01 0x02 0x03- Variant 2: 01 02 03- Variant 3: 010203If the format in the 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGCANConfigSignalGenerator


# Signal Generator

CAN Interactive Generator » Signal Generator

The generator types can be defined by the Generator Type column of a signal of the Signals view. Possible Signal Generator types for the CAN IG are:

| Sine | Ramps and Pulses | Value Range |
| --- | --- | --- |
| To generate mathematical curves. | To generate mathematical curves. | For simple range of value tests. |
| Toggle Switch | Random | User-Defined |
| For bit value tests. | For system stress tests. | For individual configurable tests. |
| Variable |  |
| To map values from the GUI (panel etc.) on a message without programming effort in CAPL |

The properties of the generator types can be defined by the Generator Settings column of the Signals view.

Each generator type has the states:

- running

- paused

- stopped

Examples (CAN IG)

| Phys. value | Physical value of the signal, user-configurable Converting the raw value into the physical value and vice versa is completed using the conversion formula: [physical value] = ( [raw value] * [factor] ) + [offset] |
| Phys. step | The physical value step size. Default is 1/20 of the physical value range. |
| Unit | Physical unit of the signal value, taken from the database. |
| StartBit | Start bit of the signal inside the frame payload, taken from the database. |
| Length | The length of the signal. |

### Situation

A CAN Interactive Generator should be added to the Simulation Setup.

### Solution

- Select the bus line in the Simulation Setup and open the context menu.

- Select the Insert CAN Interactive Generator command to insert the block.

### Alternative Solution

- In the Simulation Setup open the System View (via context menu).

- In the System View set a right click on Interactive Generators.

- Select the Insert CAN Interactive Generator command to insert the block.

Example 2: Add Frames to Frames View

To add frames to the Frames view two options are available:

- Add raw CAN/CAN FD frame

- Add CAN frame from an assigned database

The following frames should be sent with the trigger conditions shown:

- CAN frame with Id 0x7ff on CAN2 periodic (cycle time 33 ms)

### Solution

- Add a CAN Interactive Generator in the Simulation Setup of the PowerTrain network.

- Disable all other active blocks.

- Add the frames to the Frames view by using the button for the CAN frame and the button for the CAN FD frame of the toolbar.

- Change the IDs of the CAN and CAN FD frame to 0x7ff and 0x900x within the ID column.

- Configure the fr

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGCANSignalConfigVariable


# Signal Generator Type: Variable

CAN Interactive Generator » Signal Generator » Type: Variable

This generator type receives its value(s) from the selected environment variable or system variable. The selected variable values can be entered, for example, via a panel.

Because a permanently defined signal chart sequence is not determined for this generator type:

- This signal chart is only available under the Timing settings in not time bound sending mode

- The signal chart is only automatically sent once (see Common settings)

- A list of calculated values, the output values and the graphic representation can not be created as a preview

Generator Settings

- Start the generator at measurement start If this check box is activated, the generator starts automatically at measurement start.

- Variable name Displays the name of the selected system or environment variable.

- Database name If it is an environment variable, the database from which the variable was selected is displayed here.

- Initial value The initial value of the variable is displayed here.

- [Select] This button opens a selection dialog in which you can select a system or environment variable.

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGEthernetPageMsgList


# Ethernet IG: Messages

Ethernet IG » Configuration » Messages

| Messages | Configuration | Raw Data |  |

The message list manages the messages to be sent. You can add new messages to the message list, configure available messages and remove messages from the list.

The following table shows the information belonging to each message.

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Message name | Name of the message, changeable |
| Protocol | Protocol the message is sent with |
| Payload length | Payload length |
| Send | Send button to send a message instantly and uniquely |
| Cycle time (ms) | Message cycle time in milliseconds If you activate this check box the cyclic sending of the message is started. |

With [New] you can add new messages to the message list. By default an UDP packet is added. If you want to add another packet, open the list with the arrow of the button and select the protocol. New messages are initialized with parameter values defined in the settings.

With [Delete] you remove the current selected message from the message list.

## Configure Messages

If you select a message in the message list you can configure this message in the properties area. The parameters that are available for the message are displayed in a table. With a double click on the parameter you can edit the parameter value.

You have to configure the following parameters for the different protocols.

### Raw Ethernet

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Source MAC address | Sourc

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGEthernetPageRawData


# Ethernet IG: Raw Data

Ethernet IG » Configuration » Raw Data

| Messages | Configuration | Raw Data |  |

On this page you can enter the payload of the message by using the hex editor in hexadecimal and ASCII values.

## Manual Input

To change the data bytes click on the byte position you want to change, then insert the hexadecimal value you want to insert. If you want to insert the values in ASCII format click in the ASCII column and enter your values. With <Tab> you can switch between the hexadecimal field and the ASCII field. The byte position of the cursor persists.

Changed values are highlighted in color. To commit the changed value press <Enter> or click in another window. To discard your input press <ESC>.

## Copy and Paste Input

If you don't want to insert the values manually you can use the copy/paste mechanism. Copy the data in the clipboard, set the cursor to the position you want to insert the data and paste them with <CTRL>+<V>.

|  | Note If the cursor position is in the hex column, only hexadecimal numbers can be added. If there are other characters within the stream, the complete data will be discarded. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGEthernetSendMsg


# Ethernet IG: Send Messages

Ethernet IG » Send Messages

You can send messages of the message list by pressing [Now].

Additionally you can send messages cyclically. For this activate the check box of the appropriate message. Then the message is sent with the specified cycle time in milliseconds. With a double click on the cycle time you can edit the value.

For sending messages the Ethernet IG uses the source addresses that are defined in the category Ethernet IG in the settings.

## Send UDP/TCP Message

To send UDP and TCP messages the IP address must be resolved. The Ethernet IG uses the ARP protocol to get the MAC address. Then the Ethernet IG sends a broadcast message (ARP Request) and requests for a node with the appropriate MAC address.

|  | Caution! If no node answers the ARP request the IP address could not be resolved and the message is not sent! |

Before sending a message with TCP a connection with the remote station must be established. The addressed node of the remote station must listen to the destination port to receive the message.

After the message is sent the connection is kept for a waiting time of ten seconds. If no further message is sent within this time the connection is closed. If a message is sent the waiting time is reset to ten seconds.

|  | Caution! If no connection could be established the message cannot be sent! |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

# J1587 Parameters in the Interactive Generator

IG » Configuration » Transmit List » J1587

The Interactive Generator can send J1587 messages. A message can be inserted from the database with the button [New]. A click on the arrow of the button [New] opens a menu. With the menu command J1587 Message a new message can be defined. The user must enter a PID in the column ID.

|  | Note The Interactive Generator can send only one parameter per message. |

The Interactive Generator contains a page J1587 with these J1587 specific columns:

| Column | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| MID | The message is sent with this MID |
| Receiver | MID of the receiver. This field is enabled for proprietary messages only |
| Priority | The message is sent with this priority |

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGpduConfig


# Configuration

PDU Interactive Generator » Configuration

The PDU Interactive Generator window is divided in the following areas:

## Toolbar

The toolbar contains the functions:

- activate/deactiavate PDU IG

- add AUTOSAR PDUs to the PDUs view

- remove AUTOSAR PDUs from the PDUs view

- undo/redo user steps

- column configuration of PDUs view and Signals view

- suspend payload changes

- rename window of the PDU IG

## PDU View

Displays the currently available AUTOSAR PDUs and their properties. The properties can also be configured within this view.

## PDU Property View

Displays the properties of the AUTOSAR PDUs selected in the PDUs view. The properties are editable and can be configured within this view.

## Signals View

The Signals view displays the Signals of the AUTOSAR PDU selected in the PDUs view.

## Raw Data View

To configure the data of the AUTOSAR PDU by bytes the Raw Data view can be used.

CANoe supports the creation of multiple windows for the PDU IG. Each of the windows can contain its own set of PDUs. This allows you to manage PDUs separately by network, for example.

|  | Note When saving the configuration in an older version that supports only one window of the PDU IG, CANoe copies all PDUs to a single window of the PDU IG before saving the configuration. Thus, no data is lost. |

Tip: You can move PDUs from one PDU-IG window to another by drag and drop or copy and paste. However, each PDU may only be contained in one window at a time, so that

[...truncated for brevity...]

| Group | Column | Options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Basic Properties | DB comment | — | Comment of the PDU from the database. |
| Comment | — | User-defined comment. |
| Length | — | Length of the PDU in bytes. |
| Name | — | Name of the PDU from the assigned database. |
| Timing | external | The timing of the PDU is not affected. An external timing e.g. by the interaction layer controls the transmission of the PDU. |  |
| event based | The PDU is sent immediately in the event of a payload change, taking into account the set debounce delay. The debounce delay specifies the minimum time in milliseconds that must elapse before the PDU can be sent a second time. |
| cyclic | The PDU is sent cyclically with the set period (in milliseconds). The first transmission takes place after the time period specified with Offset (in milliseconds). |
| Trigger | manual/ on key | A click on the button updates the payload of the PDU and triggers the PDU. Additionally a trigger key can be defined for the PDU. Pressing the key then has the same effect as clicking the button. |
| periodic | A click in the button updates the payload of the PDU with the set period duration and triggers the PDU. |
| PDU | When the specified PDU is received on any channel, the PDU is triggered. |
| system variable |

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGpduExamples


# Examples

PDU Interactive Generator » Examples

Example 1: Add a PDU to the PDUs View

|  | Note The following example uses the System Configuration (CAN) AUTOSAR sample configuration. |

### Situation

The following PDUs from the assigned database PowerTrain should be triggered with the trigger conditions shown:

- ABSdata triggers cyclically (50 ms cycle time). Update payload periodically every 50 ms.

- EngineData triggers interactive (key press or Triger button) and update.

### Solution

- In the Simulation Ribbon Tab, click the PDU Interactive Generator button. In the list under New, select the PDU Interactive Generator command. A new window of the PDU IG will appear.

- Deactivate all other active function blocks in the Simulation Setup.

- In the toolbar of the PDU IG, click the button . The Symbol Selection dialog appears. In PowerTrain under PDUs, select the ABSdata and EngineData PDUs.

- Configure the ABSdata PDU via the Timing column so that it is transmitted cyclically with a cycle time of 50 ms.

- Activate periodic update by clicking the button in the Trigger column. In the Trigger Settings column, configure the PDU to update periodically every 50 ms.

- Now configure the EngineData PDU so that it is triggered interactively by pressing a key. Leave the setting in the Timing column at the Event based (10 ms) setting.

- Start the measurement.

- Using the Analysis Ribbon Tab and the Trace list, open the Trace PowerTrain Trace Window.

- The ABSdata PDU is tr

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] IGSendListLIN


# IG: LIN Transmit List

IG » Configuration » Transmit List » LIN

You can use the Interactive Generator with LIN support to send response messages and header messages on the LIN bus.

## LIN characteristics of the block

- Adding messages to the transmit list Not available during measurement. This condition is set by the LIN hardware configuration. Send trigger and message data can be changed during the measurement.

- Send column Pressing the Update button while configuring a response message results in an update of the message data an not in immediate sending of the message on the bus. The message will not be sent until receiving the corresponding header message.

## LIN frame types

- LIN header The LIN hardware must be in master-mode to be able to send header messages.

- LIN response Updates the answer data of the slave answer on the LIN hardware.

## LIN specific columns

- Active This column is only available for response messages. In case that a message is not configured at start of measurement but the entered data must be kept, you can set the message inactive by clearing the control box.

Option .LIN

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] ig_value-generator_shortcut-menu


# Value Generator: Context Menu

IG » Configuration » Signal List » Signal Configurator » Context Menu

The following context menu is available in the Value Generator Configuration dialog in the Values area.

The individual menu commands are available depending on the type.

Cut (only with user-defined Signal Generator type)(Cuts selected signal values from the value list.)

Copy (Copies selected signal values from the value list into the clipboard.)

Paste (only with user-defined Signal Generator type)(Inserts copied signal values into the values list.)

Delete (only with user-defined Signal Generator type)(Deletes selected signal values from the value list.)

Select all (Selects all signal values in the value list.)

Import... (only with user-defined Signal Generator type)

Export... (never available)

Mark limited values (By activating this option you determine whether signals that are outside the permitted value range (see Target settings CANdb min and CANdb max) should be marked in color.)

Marker color (Under this menu command and by selecting Set..., you can set which signal values will be displayed, which are outside the permitted value range.)

You can reset the default values by selecting Reset.

|  | Note This context menu command is only available if the Mark limited values option is active. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] ohid_canwin_ig_addto_configuration.htm


# IG: Integrating in the Configuration

MOST IG » IG: Integrating in the Configuration

## You can integrate an Interactive Generator in the configuration as follows:

- In the Simulation SetupTo send messages to the bus, you insert the IG via the context menu of the simulated bus.

- In the Measurement SetupTo control the analysis without sending messages to the bus, you must insert the IG in the Measurement Setup before the desired evaluation block via the context menu of the hot spot.

MOST IG: Application Examples

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_Configuration


# MOST IG: Configuration

MOST IG» Configuration

Context Menu: Configuration

In the configuration dialog of the MOST IG, the messages are collected in a send list and their contents and the transmission channel to be used are defined. In this dialog, the messages can be sent by pressing buttons.

The configuration dialog for the MOST IG is divided into a transmit list, a hexadecimal representation of the selected message, and a parameter tree.

|  | Note You can navigate with help of the picture to the single topics. |

MOST IG: Application Examples

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_configuration_buttons


# MOST IG: Buttons in the Configuration Dialog

MOST IG » Configuration » Buttons

The following buttons are provided to assist you in compiling the transmit list:

## [New]

Inserts a new message into the send list.

The MOST Symbol Explorer opens, which enables messages to be selected directly from an integrated XML function catalog.

It is also possible to insert a new raw-/system message or a raw packet into the send list via the context menu of this button. The type of the raw-/system message (normal, remote read, remote write, ...) can be chosen in the send list. The data values of the system message can be entered in the hexadecimal representation of the MOST messages. There is no parameter tree view for raw-/system messages or raw packets.

## [Clone]

Duplicates the marked message. The duplicate is added at the end of the transmission log.

This function is very useful if a message of the same type is to be sent with different data bytes.

## [Delete]

Deletes the marked message from the send list.

## [Up]

Moves the message marked in the send list one row up.

The first message of a sequence is removed from the sequence.

## [Down]

Moves the message marked in the send list one row down.

At first the last message of a sequence leaves the sequence, further clicks on the button will move the message down.

## [-> Panel]

Resizes the MOST IG Window so that only the "Send" buttons are visible.

## [Layout]

Arranges the column widths in the send list automatically to

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_hexdump


# MOST IG: Hexadecimal Representation of the MOST Messages

MOST IG » Configuration » Hexadecimal Representation of the MOST Messages

The hexadecimal representation of the MOST messages (hex dump) is located in the configuration dialog of the MOST IG between the transmit list and the parameter tree.

If a message is marked in the transmit list, the hexadecimal representation of this message is shown in the hex dump. Most values in the hex dump fields can be modified directly.

Most fields match the fields described in the transmit list.

## Setup of the hex dump:

Application Messages

- Send …via this button the message can be sent. Corresponds with 'Send' in the transmit list.

- Dest. …physical address of the recipient. Corresponds with 'Destination' in the transmit list.

- Rtype …this field represents the transmission type of the message.Normal messages have the transmission type 0x00, system messages a transmission type between 0x01 and 0x05 according to the type of the system message.

- FBlock …Function block. Corresponds with 'FBlock' in the transmit list

- Instance …corresponds with 'Instance' in the transmit list

- Function …Function within a given function block. Corresponds with 'Function' in the transmit list

- OpType …Operation within a given function. Corresponds with 'OpType' in the transmit list

- TelID …this field is only editable with messages that were not sent via the AMS. The TelID designates the respective protocol and the message type within the

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_Load_and_Save_Sendlist


# MOST IG: Load and Save the Transmit List

MOST IG » Configuration » Transmit List » Load and Save the Transmit List

You can store the transmit list in a file (.igm), in order to load in another MOST IG. Note, that only the content of the transmit list is stored, general IG settings or column widths are not considered.

Loading and saving of the transmit list is done via the context menu of the block in the Simulation Setup or via the context menu of the transmit list. In case there are already messages in the message list, you can decide whether

- to discard the existing messages, or

- to append the new messages to the message list

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_parameter_tree


# MOST IG: Parameter Tree

MOST IG» Configuration » Parameter Tree

Each message is assigned a parameter tree, in which the parameter values can be set. The parameter tree for the message marked in the send list is displayed.

The parameter tree is located in the bottom area of the configuration dialog of the MOST IG.

## Setup of the parameter tree

Parameter

This column displays the parameters of the message that is currently active in the send list as a parameter tree. As is usual in Windows, the sub-branches can be shown by clicking on the '+' sign and hidden again by clicking on the '-' sign.

If a configured messaged is not described in an XML function catalog, a parameter tree is not shown for this message.

## Special parameters

- Remaining Bytes

If the Hex dump of a message cannot be fully disassembled with the help of the XLM Catalog specified in the configuration, then the superfluous data bytes are shown as Remaining Bytes in the message’s parameter tree. For example, this is the case if the Message-ID (FBlock, Function, OpType) does not appear in the catalog. In this special case all data bytes are shown as Remaining Bytes.

## Limitations regarding parameter types

MOST IG supports most parameter types that might be used in the function catalogs. However, the following limitations apply:

- For strings, only coding types ASCII and ISO 8859-15 are available (and No string!, see next item). However, entries are then not recoded, so the strings that are transmi

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_sendlist


# MOST IG: Transmit List

MOST IG » Configuration » Transmit List

The transmit list is located in the top area of the configuration dialog of the MOST IG.

The various buttons can be used to add, delete and rearrange messages in the transmit list.

Every message that is to be sent via the MOST IG must be included in the transmit list. Each message is described in one line.

You can send the individual messages with the press of a button or a key from the send list during the measurement operation.

## Setup of the transmit list

Send

With this button, located at the beginning of each row, you can send the corresponding message during the measurement operation.The button can be renamed via the context menu.

If sending is disabled for a message the Send button is shown in gray.This may be the case if:

- The AMS service should be used although this service is inactive for the channel.

- FBlock, FunctionId and OpType were not input completely

Normal messages can be sent out over the Application Message Service (AMS) or Control Message Service (CMS). This can be configured from the transmit list’s context menu for the selected messages. For this purpose, AMS must be activated for the channel being used (Network Hardware Configuration). If more parameter bytes have been configured than fit into a Ctrl frame, AMS automatically performs fragmentation for the transmission.

|  | Note In the Parameters area there is a check box for each individual message indicating whether AMS 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_use_cases


# MOST IG: Application Examples

MOST IG » Application Examples

In the following, three typical scenarios for using the MOST IG will be presented.

|  | Example Addressing Real or Simulated MOST Devices Testing is to be carried out to determine whether a given real or simulated MOST device responds correctly to control commands (MOST messages). In this example a MOST CD player is to be tested. On principle, a panel may be created and the control buttons connected with CAPL programs; these can then compile and transmit the corresponding MOST messages. However, the procedure is simpler (and faster) with the MOST IG. An MOST IG must first be inserted in a MOST bus in the Simulation Setup. Then all relevant MOST messages can be entered in the transmit list via the configuration dialog. In the case of a MOST CD-Player the following messages are used: CDPlayer.Track.Set with data value 'Track: 7' to set the CD-Player to the seventh title of the inserted audio CD.Rename the send button to "Track 7". CDPlayer.Deckstatus.Set with data value 'Mode: Play' to play back the selected title.Rename the send button to "Play". CDPlayer.Track.Get to obtain information from the CD-Player on which title is currently being played.Rename the send button to "Get Info". The reaction to the Set messages can be observed on the real CD-Player and the CD-Player's reaction to the Get request can be seen in the Trace Window. Note In a context menu the Send buttons can be named according to their function

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_working_without_database


# MOST IG: Working without a Database

MOST IG » Configuration » Transmit List » Working without a Database

Even if you have not assigned a function catalog to the CANalyzer/CANoe configuration, you can still send messages. However, since in this case there is no parameter structure, such messages can only be sent without parameters

## Generating a new message

- Generating an empty message by clicking on the bottom (highlighted in dark gray) row in the transmit list.

- Manual entry of FBlock, Function, OpType and Instance, and Destination in the send list or the hexadecimal representation of the message.

- Selection of the transmission channel; MOST channel 1 is set by default.

All values must be entered in the fields numerically. If an invalid value is entered in a field, a corresponding message appears in the status line. The FBlock, Function and OpType fields in the send list are highlighted in color, because no correspondence is found in a function catalog.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_CANwin_IG_MOST_XML_function_catalog


# MOST IG: Add XML Function Catalog

MOST IG » Add XML Function Catalog

The MOST IG receives all its information about the setup of messages from the integrated XML function catalogs. Information from DBC databases is ignored.

An XML function catalog for MOST can be added by inserting the XML file in the system view of the Simulation Setup.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_IG_buttons


# IG: Buttons in the Configuration Dialog

IG » Configuration » Buttons

The following buttons are provided to assist you in putting together the transmit list:

## [New |▼]

Adds a new message to the transmit list. This button contains two parts and offers the following functions:

- Insert a new message

If a database was associated, the Symbol Selection dialog is opened, in which you can select messages directly.

If no database was associated, a new message is opened in the transmit list, in which you must manually enter the following:

- Identifier (ID)

- Transmit channel

- Number of data bytes (DLC)

- Open the button's menu

In this menu you can open a message that is included in the send list. There are entries available for every allowed message type.

Additionally you can define the default settings for entries. This predefines the message type whenever you enter a message with no database associated.

## [Duplicate]

Duplicates the message selected by ">" and its trigger conditions.

The duplicate is added at the end of the message list.

This function is very helpful if you want to send multiple messages with the same message identifier but with different data bytes.

## [Special Frame |▼]

- CAN Error Frame

Enters an error message (Error Frame) in the transmit list.

You can use this function to intentionally send Error Frames onto the bus.

## [Delete]

Deletes the message selected by ">" from the transmit list.

## [Cut]

Transfers the selected message to t

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_IG_configuration


# IG: Configuration

IG » Configuration

Context Menu: Configuration

In the Interactive Generator's configuration dialog messages are placed in a transmit list and their contents and trigger conditions are defined.

The configuration dialog of the IG is subdivided into a transmit list and a signal list.

In the transmit list you can select individual messages and configure them. Assigned to each message is a signal list in which signal values can be configured.

The currently selected input boxes are explained in the status bar of the configuration window.

Special key combinations (Shortcuts) can be used for working without a mouse.

Triggering on key press: Activate the check box for key triggering. Enter the key that should initiate the measurement when pressed. B. Triggering by the Send column: Activate the now box. The following applies to both triggering methods: Immediate transmission of the message during a measurement run. Stimulation of a real device by various signal values A message should be sent interactively with signal values that change (signal form).
