### [FunctionBlocks/IG] ohid_ig_functions


# IG: Functional Overview

IG » Functional Overview

Using the Interactive Generator, you can configure messages and their signals before and during measurement.The configuration dialog of the Interactive Generator can be used like a panel.

| Insert messages |
| --- |
| In the configuration dialog of the Interactive Generator, you can insert messages into the transmit list on the following way: Enter manually Insert directly from the existing database using the buttons provided to you The signals of a selected message are displayed in the signal list. If the message is entered manually, the signal list remains empty. |
| Configuring messages and signals |
| You can configure messages within the transmit list and signals within the signal list. |
| Sending messages |
| Various options are available in the transmit list to send messages: Cyclical sending Send via the defined button [now] Send via any key |
| The IG as gateway |
| You can use the Interactive Generator as a gateway to transmit both individual messages as well as entire bus communication from one bus to another. To do this, activate the option Gateway in the transmit list in the configuration dialog of the Interactive Generator. |
| Use of Signal Generators |
| You can define the signal chart and test the response of an ECU in the signal list in the configuration dialog with the help of the Signal Generator. |
| Generating a high load situation |
| To generate a high load situation, the next send command for the

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_IG_gateway


# The IG as a Gateway

IG » Configuration » Transmit List » Triggering: Gateway

|  | Note The gateway function is not supported for ARINC-429 messages. The gateway function is no longer available for new configurations. If you load an existing configuration that contains the old IG, this function may still be available. |

The IG can be used as a gateway. It is possible to transmit single messages and the whole bus communication from one bus to the other one.The signal values can be changed during the transfer.

You can make all the necessary settings to use the IGs as gateways in the CAN view of the IG configuration dialog.

|  | Step by Step Procedure to transmit a message from one bus to another Select the CAN view in the transmit list. Select in the transmit list the messages to be transmitted. Select in the Message Parameters column in the Channel list the channel (output) for the bus communication. Activate the Gateway check box in the Triggering column.Now the gateway functionality is activated. All other fields of the Triggering column become inactive in the current row. Select the (input) channel for the bus communication in the Gateway pull down list of the Triggering column.When trigger channel and message channel are the same, the channel field of the gateway will be displayed red. |

|  | Example Example for transmitting a message from one bus to another Note The following example uses the CANSystemDemo sample configuration. The EngineData Messages message shou

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_IG_highload


# IG: Generating a High Load Situation

IG » Configuration » Transmit List » Triggering: High Load

With the High loadtrigger condition in the CANview of the IG configuration dialog you can induce a high load situation.

A high bus load is reached when messages follow right after one another on the bus. To achieve this, after successful transmission of one message the next transmit request of the same message must follow immediately.

The number set in Burst indicates the current number of messages in the transmit queue.

With this trigger condition you should not do the following:

- Simultaneously initiate the same message by Send, Key or Cycle time, since the queue would then overrun, and a data loss could occur.

- Filter out the receive acknowledgments to Tx or TXRQ events before the Interactive Generator in the data flow plan, since the queue would then not be refilled.

|  | Example: Generating a high load situation The trigger condition High load for a message with CAN identifier 100, on CAN channel 1 and with Burst 5 can be represented by the following CAPL program: variables {int Burst = 5;message can1.100 msg100 = {dlc=4, word(0)=0x1234}; } on start {for (i=0; i < Burst; i++) {output(msg100);}} on message 100 {if (this.dir == TX) { // Reaction to own transmissions;// if TxRequest enabled: (this.dir == TXREQUEST)if (this.can == 1) { // only on the proper busoutput(msg100);}}} |

The IG as a Signal Generator | The IG as a Gateway | Further Application Examples

| Ve

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_IG_message_list


# IG: Transmit List

IG » Configuration » Transmit List

|  | Note For displaying the transmit list the views Standard,CAN, LIN, J1939, J1587,A429, are available. |

Each message to be sent by the Interactive Generator must be added to the transmit list.The following procedures are available:

- Click the [New] button and select the message that should be inserted into the transmit list from the Symbol Selection dialog that appears.

- Use the [Special Frame] button to select the CAN Error Frame to send an error message.

- Double-click in an empty field in the Message Name column to open the symbolic selection dialog. Then select the message that should be inserted into the transmit list.

- Enter the identifier of the desired message directly into the Identifier field in the Message Parameter column.

You can configure trigger conditions both before and during the measurement run.

## Setup of the transmit list

Message name

Shows the symbolic name e.g. PowerTrain::EngineData.

If a symbolic name is not available, the identifier is displayed.

## Message parameter

You can enter the following message parameters in the Transmit List:

| Identifier | Serves to uniquely identify a message. |
| --- | --- |
| Channel | Indicates the CAN channel on which the message is sent. |
| Frame | Message format: Data Frame: To transmit a message. Remote Frame: To request a message. |
| DLC (Data Length Code) | Indicates the number of data bytes in the message. |
| BRS (Bit Rate Switch) |

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] ohid_ig_most_shortcut_menu


# MOST IG: Context Menu of the Transmit List

MOST IG » Transmit List » Context Menu

Open the context menu of the MOST IG transmit list using the right mouse button; the following functions are available:

Rename Rename the [Send] button

Set FBlockID… Sets the FBlockID for the selected message/messages. Value range: 0x00…0xFF

Set FunctionID… Sets the FunctionID for the selected message/messages. Value range: 0x000…0xFFF

Set OpType… Sets the OpType for the selected message/messages. Value range: 0x00…0x0F

Set InstanceID… Sets the InstanceID for the selected message/messages. Value range: 0x00…0xFF

Set Destination… Sets the destination for the selected message/messages. Value range: 0x0000…0xFFFF

Set Channel… Sets the MOST channel for the selected message/messages.

Read configuration from file… Opens a file dialog to select the file (.igm) from which the configuration (transmit list) should be read. Beside all messages, sequences and keyboard triggers are stored, too.

Write configuration to file… Opens a file dialog to select the file (.igm) to which the configuration (transmit list) should be written. At first, the transmit list has to be stored in a file with the above mentioned command.

Create message sequence Generates a transmission sequence from several selected lines. A title line is inserted above the selected lines and identified by both color as well as symbols in the first column. All messages in the sequence can now be transmitted in one step using the se

[...truncated for brevity...]

# IG: Keyboard Operations

IG » Key Combinations

The following key combinations relate to configuration of the Interactive Generator:

| Command | Key Combination |
| --- | --- |
| Press the chosen button Opens the Symbol Selection dialog to the message name fields Makes a selection in the case of check boxes | <Spacebar> |
| Switches between the various buttons Switches between the various input boxesAn explanation of the selected box appears in the status bar. | <Arrow keys> |
| Incrementing or decrementing of the selected box By the value 1 for timers and data bytes in the Transmit List By the smallest possible raw value difference in the Signal List | <Ctrl>+<Up><Ctrl>+<Down> |
| Logarithmic adjustment of the slider in the transmit list | <Ctrl>+<Right><Ctrl>+<Left> |
| Incrementing or decrementing of the selected box by the preset step width (phys. step) in the signal list | <Page Up><Page Down> |
| Press the chosen button Opens a selection list in the Transmit Lis | <Enter> |
| Exits the boxes to be edited, and any changes are canceled.Attention: <Esc> stops a running measurement! | <Esc> |
| Starts the measurement | <F9> |

|  | Note As long as the configuration dialog of the Interactive Generator is opened and active, all keyboard entries except <F9> and <Esc> are used to edit the dialog. |

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_IG_signal_list


# IG: Signal List

IG » Configuration » Signal List

The signal values of a message are configured in the Signal List.

- If you are working with an associated database, those signals appear which were assigned to the selected message in the Signal List.

- If you are working without an associated database the Signal List remains empty.

## Each signal is described by the following boxes

| Field name | Comment |
| --- | --- |
| SB | Start bit of the signal, taken from the database |
| Signal name | Name of the signal, taken from the database |
| Raw value | Raw value of the signal, user-configurable |
| Phys Value | Physical value of the signal, user-configurable Converting the raw value into the physical value and vice versa is completed using the conversion formula: [physical value] = ( [raw value] * [factor] ) + [offset] |
| Unit | Unit of the signal, taken from the database |
| Dec | Decrementing of the physical value by the physical step |
| Phys Step | Physical step, calculated with values of the database. User-configurable |
| Inc | Incrementing of the physical value by the physical step |
| Wave form generation | Open the configuration dialog for the Signal Generator using the Define button. A signal chart can be defined for the signal in this dialog. |
| Modification enabled | Only available if the IG is used as a Gateway. If you activate this option, changed signal values will be sent via the gateway. If this option is inactive, changed signal values remain uncons

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/IG] OHID_IG_without_database


# IG: Working without a Database

IG » Configuration » Transmit List » Working without a Database

Even if you have not associated a database with your CANoe configuration you can send any configurable messages you wish.

## To generate a new message in the transmit list:

- Enter the message's Identifier

The message type depends on the current entry mode.The entry mode is shown in the status bar of the configuration dialog.

The right part of the [New |V] button activates a menu in which the default entry mode can be changed.Furthermore new messages can be inserted.A different entry is available for each message type.

- Enter the channel over which the message should be sent.

The default value 1 can be changed.

- Enter the desired number of data bytes (DLC)

- Enter the data bytes themselves in the data box

|  | Note The configuration dialog's signal list will remain empty. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINDisturbanceBlock] LINDisturbanceBlock


# LIN Disturbance Block

Test Setup for Test Modules » LIN Disturbance Block

Context Menu of the Test Environment folder in the Test Setup: Insert LIN Disturbance Block

### [FunctionBlocks/LINDisturbanceBlock] LINDisturbanceBlockBusDisturbanceView


# LIN Disturbance Block: Bus Disturbance View

LIN Disturbance Block » Views » Bus Disturbance

Bus level disturbances are configured in the Bus Disturbance view.These can be started and stopped as needed using the button in the Action column.

## Configuration

- Disturbance Level Specifies whether the bus is to be disturbed with a dominant or recessive level.

- Disturbance Length Specifies the disturbance length in bit times.

|  | Note Please note hardware-related restrictions! |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINDisturbanceBlock] LINDisturbanceBlockDisturbanceList


# LIN Disturbance Block: Disturbance List

LIN Disturbance Block » Disturbance List

Each disturbance to be executed by the LIN Disturbance Block needs to be added to the Disturbance List.

## Configuration

- Click the New icon in the toolbar to add a new entry for a Header, Response or Bus disturbance.

- Click the Delete icon in the toolbar to remove the selected item(s).

- Click the Up or Down icon in the toolbar to move an item up or down in the Disturbance List.

- You can also select one or more items and press the <Del> key to remove them.

- Click the right mouse button to access the context menu.To remove the selected item(s), select Remove.

## Columns/Fields

- Action Click the [Activate] button to activate the configured disturbance on the LIN bus.The button title reflects the currently configured functionality. The title changes dynamically as you modify the configuration. This button is only enabled during a measurement.

- Exec. Specifies the number of runs (executions) for the configured disturbance.

- Type Specifies whether the disturbance is a Header, Response or Bus disturbance.

- Byte Specifies which byte or interbyte space of a Header or Response is affected.

- Disturb on Header LIN frame ID (6 bits); the symbolic name is shown if a database is available.The disturbance is executed for the configured header.

- Bus Level Specifies whether the disturbance is recessive or dominant.

- Bit Count Specifies the length of the configured disturbance.

- Ch

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/LINDisturbanceBlock] LINDisturbanceBlockHeaderDisturbanceView


# LIN Disturbance Block: Header Disturbance View

LIN Disturbance Block » Views » Header Disturbance

You can configure Header disturbances in the Header Disturbance view.

## Configuration

- Byte without disturbance Value of the data byte for disturbance configuration, displayed as a bit sequence with additional start and stop bits.If Interbyte or PID byte is selected as the Byte in the Disturbance List, you can select the available PID data byte values from an existing database via their symbolic names.

- Byte If Sync or PID byte is selected as the Byte in the Disturbance List, the byte to be disturbed is displayed as a bit sequence.You can use the context menu to configure one dominant or recessive disturbance per data bit.You can configure only one dominant disturbance for the stop bit, and none for the start bit.

- IB (Interbyte)If Interbyte is selected as the Byte in the Disturbance List, the interbyte space to be disturbed is represented by a full bit length.You can use the context menu to configure a dominant disturbance for this interbyte space.The data byte that follows is also displayed as a bit sequence, but cannot be disturbed in addition.

- Byte with disturbance If Sync or PID byte is selected as the Byte in the Disturbance List, the byte value resulting from the disturbance is displayed.This is derived from the original byte value prior to the disturbance and the configured disturbance.If a disturbance is configured for the stop bit, no prediction is displ

### [FunctionBlocks/LINDisturbanceBlock] LINDisturbanceBlockResponseDisturbanceView


# LIN Disturbance Block: Response Disturbance View

LIN Disturbance Block » Views » Response Disturbance

Response disturbances are configured in the Header Disturbance view.

## Configuration

- Byte without disturbance Value of the data byte for disturbance configuration, displayed as a bit sequence with additional start and stop bits. If a database is available, the value is automatically derived from the initial signal values. If symbolic resolution is not possible, the value is set to 0xFF.

- Byte If a Data byte is selected as the Disturbance byte in the Disturbance List, the data byte to be disturbed is displayed as a bit sequence.You can use the context menu to configure one dominant or recessive disturbance per data bit.You can configure only one dominant disturbance for the stop bit, and none for the start bit.

- IB (Interbyte)If an Interbyte is selected as the disturbance byte in the Disturbance List the interbyte space to be disturbed is represented by a full bit length.You can use the context menu to configure a dominant disturbance for this interbyte space.The data byte that follows is also displayed as a bit sequence, but cannot be disturbed in addition.

- Byte with disturbance If a Data Byte is selected as the disturbance byte in the Disturbance List, the byte value resulting from the disturbance is displayed.This is derived from the original byte value prior to the disturbance and the configured disturbance.If a disturbance is configured for the stop byte,

### [FunctionBlocks/LINDisturbanceBlock] LINDisturbanceBlockRestrictions


# LIN Disturbance Block: Hardware-Dependent Disturbance Limitations

LIN Disturbance Block » Restrictions

Limitations apply with

- LIN Hardware (XL, XLe, VN)For Header and Response disturbances, there needs to be at least one undisturbed bit between two consecutive disturbance positions.

- XL Hardware (CANcardXL, CANcaseXL, CANboardXL)Bus disturbances shorter than 64 bit times may fail, if the hardware simultaneously transmits on this channel.

- VN1611 VN1611 does not support disturbance functionality. The Stress IG therefore can not be used.

|  | Note A disturbance position is a bit index with a sequential number of bits that are disturbed with identical bus levels. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveMaster] LINIMConfiguration


# LIN Interactive Master - Configuration Dialog

LIN » Interactive Master - Configuration Dialog

This dialog allows you to configure filtering of certain CAPL functions that might disturb normal behavior of their LIN Interactive Master (LIN IM) block. The settings only apply to a LIN channel used by the LIN IM. If the LIN IM is disabled or inactive the settings are ignored.

You can also use this dialog to configure the starting state of the LIN Scheduler.

## Start with stopped LIN Scheduler

If this option is selected, the LIN Scheduler will not be started when the measurement starts.

## Filter CAPL functions for controlling the LIN Scheduler

Deactivate CAPL functions which influence the behavior of the LIN Scheduler.For example:

- LINStartScheduler

- LINStopScheduler

- LINChangeSchedTable

- etc.

## Filter CAPL functions for controlling the LIN Interactive Master

Deactivate CAPL functions which influence the behavior of the LIN Interactive Master.For example:

- LINInitSetMaster

- LINInitSetBaseBaud

- LINWakeup

- etc.

The system ignores calls to the deactivated functions and a suitable warning message appears in the Write Window.

LIN Interactive Master - Scheduling

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveMaster] LINIMScheduling


# LIN Interactive Master - Scheduling

LIN » Interactive Master - Scheduling

## General

The LIN Interactive Master can be inserted in the Simulation Setup and becomes active when CANoe simulates the LIN Interactive Master node of a LIN network. This feature allows the interactive control of the internal LIN scheduler. Buttons are also provided for sending SleepMode and wake-up commands interactively.

The use of the LIN Interactive Master can be combined with a CAPL implementation of the LIN Interactive Master functionality. It is however not recommended to use CAPL functions that influence the behavior of the LIN Interactive Master or the internal LIN scheduler. By default such CAPL functions will be filtered out automatically. If necessary, the filtering of such CAPL functions can be deactivated in the Settings dialog.

## Context Information

The top-half of the LIN Interactive Master provides the following information about the context of the LIN Interactive Master:

- Master node The name is taken from the associated database.Whether this node is currently being simulated by CANoe is displayed in brackets next to the node’s name e.g. LIN_Master (simulated). Otherwise the text LIN_Master (not simulated) is displayed.To simulate a LIN Interactive Master node, the corresponding network node must be inserted in the Simulation Setup and be set to active.

- Database This is the name of the database associated to the bus channel being simulated.

- Baud rate Baud rate (in k

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveScheduler] LINiscConfiguration


# Configuration

Simulation Setup » User Interfaces » LIN ISC » Configuration

## Toolbar

| Icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Activate or deactivate filtering of CAPL commands for controlling the LIN scheduler for the assigned network. If filtering is active, CAPL functions such as linStartScheduler or linStopScheduler are ignored. If a command was filtered, this is indicated by a corresponding notification in the Write Window. |
|  | Activate or deactivate filtering of CAPL commands that are used for controlling LIN Master functions for the assigned network. If filtering is active, CAPL functions such as linGotoSleep are ignored. If a command was filtered, this is signaled by a corresponding notification in the Write Window. |
|  | During a running measurement, changes the master mode for the assigned network. Note: This function is only active if the assigned hardware channel supports the changeover of the master mode during a running measurement. |
|  | Inserts a new schedule table in the Schedule Tables view. |
|  | Inserts a slot with a frame from the database into a user defined schedule table. |
|  | Inserts a slot with a raw frame into a user defined schedule table. |
|  | Add LIN frame from an assigned database to the LIN Interactive Scheduler. |
|  | Add LIN frame to the LIN Interactive Scheduler. |
|  | Remove selected LIN frame from the LIN Interactive Scheduler. |
|  | Undoes the last action. |
|  | Redoes the last action. |

### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveScheduler] LINiscFrames


# Frames

Simulation Setup » User Interfaces » LIN ISC » Frames

Displays the configured Frames of the network that was selected in the Network view.

| Symbol | Frame Type |
| --- | --- |
|  | Raw Frame |
|  | Database Frame |
|  | Event Triggered Frame |
|  | Status Frame |

Via this view you configure frames, that you want to send on the bus. You can add LIN frames from an assigned database via the symbolic selection dialog and you can customize the properties of that frames. Alternatively you add LIN raw data frames and modify their ID or further properties.

| Column | Option | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Frame | — | Name of the frame. This input box is only editable for raw data frames. |
| Active | — | Determines whether the frame is sent or not. |
| Frame ID | — | ID of the frame. |
| DLC | 0-8 | Number of payload bytes This input box is only editable for raw data frames. |
| Send Type | Only Response | Only the response of the frame will be updated. |
| Only Header | Only the header of the frame is sent. |
| Header and Response | The response data is set and the header is sent. |
| Send |  | if the trigger condition type is manual, this button sends the frame. if the trigger condition type is periodic, this starts the periodic transmission of the frame |
|  | If the trigger condition state is periodic and currently active, this button is displayed. Activating this button stops the periodic transmission of the frame. |
| Trigger | manual/ on key | After click

### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveScheduler] LINiscNetwork


# Network

Simulation Setup » User Interfaces » LIN ISC » Network

The Network view displays the network configuration in the form of a tree. The Schedule table configuration of the last selected list entry is displayed in the Schedule Tables view.

The following functions are available for the network; the functions can be controlled using the buttons next to the network name:

The properties of the selected network are displayed in the Properties view.

| Function | State | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Master Simulation |  | The LIN ISC performs the master simulation. Provided an LDF database with at least one schedule table is configured for the network, scheduling can be performed. Click to change the node to slave mode. |
|  | The LIN ISC network node is in slave mode. Scheduling is deactivated. Click to activate master simulation . |
| Channel state |  | The LIN network is in the awake state. Click to send a sleep mode frame. If the measurement is stopped, the function defines the start state of the network. In this state, the network starts in the awake state. Note: Sleep mode frames can only be sent if master simulation is active. |
|  | The LIN network is in the asleep state. Click to send a wake-up signal. If the measurement is stopped, the function defines the start state of the network. In this state, the network starts in the asleep state. |
| Scheduler state |  | The LIN scheduler is started. Click to stop the scheduler. If the measurement is stopped, th

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveScheduler] LINiscRawData


# Raw Data

Simulation Setup » User Interfaces » LIN ISC » Raw Data

The Raw Data view displays the payload of the frame selected in the Frames view, that is sent on the bus.

The view is a byte view of the frame. Depending on the configured payload length, the row contains up to 8 payload bytes.

You can navigate between the bytes using the keyboard. Activate the edit mode of a byte with a double click on the byte value or use the <F2>key.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveScheduler] LINiscSignals


# Signals

Simulation Setup » User Interfaces » LIN ISC » Signals

The Signals view shows all signals of a selected LIN frame from an assigned database of the Frames view. It is also possible to toggle between the Signals view and the Raw Data view.

Response_Error-Signale are marked with the symbol.

|  | Note If the frame is no LIN frame from an assigned database or not connected to the database the Signals view is disabled. Only the Raw Data view is available in this case. |

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | The database name of the signal. |
| Raw value | The raw value of the signal can be increased or decreased by the defined step size via the arrow buttons. |
| Raw step | The raw step size. Default is 1/20 of the raw value range. |
| Phys. value | Physical value of the signal, user-configurable Converting the raw value into the physical value and vice versa is completed using the conversion formula: [physical value] = ( [raw value] * [factor] ) + [offset] |
| Phys. step | The physical value step size. Default is 1/20 of the physical value range. |
| Unit | Physical unit of the signal value, taken from the database. |
| StartBit | Start bit of the signal inside the frame payload, taken from the database. |
| Length | The length of the signal. |
| DB comment (Not displayed by default) | The comment for the signal in the database. |

## General

You only need to enter the raw value or physical value, since the corresponding value is always also calculated b

### [FunctionBlocks/LINInteractiveScheduler] LINiscTables


# Schedule Tables

Simulation Setup » User Interfaces » LIN ISC » Schedule Tables

Displays the configured schedule tables and slots of the network that was selected in the Network view. Furthermore, the view offers functions for dynamically changing the schedule table. The configuration of the schedule tables is taken from the LDF database that is assigned to the network. In the context menu, existing tables can be duplicated via copy/paste and then modified.

The properties of the selected schedule table / of the selected slot are displayed in the Properties view.

| Column | Options | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Table/slot | Name | Name of the table/slot |
| — |  | Click to send the table once. Note: One-time sending is only possible if the measurement is running and master simulation is activated by the LIN ISC node. |
| — |  | Click to send the schedule table cyclically. If the measurement is stopped, the function defines the start table of the scheduler. Note: Cyclical sending is only possible if master simulation is activated by the LIN ISC node. |
| Index | — | Index of the table/slot |
| Frame ID | — | ID of the frame |
| Active | — | Defines whether or not the slot is active. Inactive slots are not sent while scheduling is running. Note: Cyclical sending is only possible if master simulation is activated by the LIN ISC node. |
| Type | Unconditional | The frame assigned to the slot is an unconditional frame |
| Sporadic | The frame assigned to the slot is a

### [FunctionBlocks/LINStressIG] LINStressIGDiagnosticDataView


# LIN Stress IG: Diagnostic Data view

LIN Stress IG » Diagnostic Data View

Data of diagnostic frames (MasterReq, SlaveResp) can be configured in the Diagnostic Data view.To simplify the configuration different symbolic selection dialogs are provided here.This view allows two interpretation modes:

- Interpretation As node Configuration Simplifies the manual creation of node configuration commands, e.g. AssignNAD, ReadByIdentifier, etc…

- Interpretation As Diagnostic Simplifies the manual creation of diagnostic frames according to the LIN transport layer.

To toggle between those modes use selector appearing in the header of the Field column.

## Fields

- Field (read-only)Name of data field.Depends on the current interpretation mode.It is updated automatically.

- Raw Value Raw value of data field.

- Symbolic Value (read-only)Symbolic interpretation of data field value.Depends on the current interpretation mode.It is updated automatically.

- Select Push button to open the Symbolic Selection dialog for the concerned data field.Whether this field is enabled depends on the current interpretation mode and current data field values.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINStressIG] LINStressIGHeaderSettings


# LIN Stress IG: Header settings

LIN Stress IG » Header Settings

Header components of a frame can be configured in the Header Settings view.

## Fields

- Header Length (read-only)Total length of the configured LIN Header in bit and millisecond units.This field is updated on each modification of header component values.

- Break Length Length of the Break field (dominant bits) in bit units.

- Delimiter Length Length of the Break Delimiter field (recessive bits) in bit units.

- Sync Byte Value of Sync Byte field.

- Inter-byte Length of inter-byte space between Sync Byte field and Protected Identifier fields in bit units.

- PID Byte Value of Protected Identifier field (8-bit).

- Parity Bits Parity bits of the Protected Identifier field.

|  | Note To activate/deactivate the transmission of a certain header component set/clear the option belonging to that component. If an error is configured to be sent the corresponding stressed fields are indicated with a special background color and a tooltip text. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINStressIG] LINStressIGRawDataView


# LIN Stress IG: Raw Data view

LIN Stress IG » Raw Data View

The data byte and checksum values of a frame can be configured in Raw Data view.

## Fields

- D1…Dn Data byte value.Number of configurable bytes is defined by the Length Trans. value in the Transmit list.

- Checksum Calculation Checksum calculation mode.

- Automatic The checksum value is calculated fully automatically from the LIN channel context and updated automatically on each modification of data byte values.

- Manual The checksum value has to be set manually.

- Predefined CS model The checksum model has to be configured manually, after this the checksum value is calculated automatically and updated automatically on each modification of data byte values.

- Checksum Model Checksum model – Classic or Enhanced.This field may become read-only depending on the value of the Checksum Calculation field.

- Checksum Value This field may become read-only depending on the value of the Checksum Calculation and Checksum Model fields.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINStressIG] LINStressIGResponseSettings


# LIN Stress IG: Response settings

LIN Stress IG » Response Settings

Response timing values of a frame can be configured in the Response Settings view.

## Fields

- Resp. Length (read-only)Total length of the configured LIN Response.This field is updated on each modification of response timing values.

- Resp. Space Length of inter-byte space in front of the first data byte.

- Interbyte D1-D2, … , Dn-Dn+1 Length of inter-byte space between data bytes with corresponding indexes.Number of configurable values is defined by the Length Trans. value in the Transmit list.

- Interbyte CS Length of inter-byte space in front of the checksum byte.

|  | Note If an error is configured to be sent the corresponding stressed fields are indicated with a special background color and a tooltip text. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [FunctionBlocks/LINStressIG] LINStressIGSignalDataView


# LIN Stress IG: Signal Data view

LIN Stress IG » Signal Data View

The signal values of a frame can be configured in the Signal Data view.If no symbolic frame matches the configured FrameId or if the symbolic frame contains no signals this view remains empty.

## Fields

- Name (read-only)Name of the signal, taken from the database.

- Raw Value Raw value of the signal.

- Phys. Value (read-only)Physical value of the signal.Converting the raw value into the physical value is completed using the conversion formula:[physical value] = ( [raw value] * [encoding_scale] ) + [encoding_offset]taken from the database.

- Offset (read-only)Least significant bit position of the signal in the frame, taken from the database.

- Length (read-only)Signal size in bits, taken from the database.

- Info (read-only)Unit of the signal or name of the used encoding, taken from the database.

|  | Note If an error is configured to be sent the corresponding stressed fields are indicated with a special background color and a tooltip text. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/LINStressIG] LINStressIGTransmitList


# LIN Stress IG: Transmit list

LIN Stress IG » Transmit List

Each frame to be sent by the LIN Stress IG block must be added to the transmit list.

## Setup

- Click the [New|Symbolic Frame] toolbar command and select the frame that should be inserted into the transmit list from the Symbol Selection dialog that appears.

- Click the [New|Raw Frame] toolbar command and enter the identifier of the desired frame directly into the Frame Id column of the new row.

- Select one or multiple rows and press the <Del> key to remove frames from the transmit list.Alternatively use the Remove toolbar command.

- Click with the right mouse button in a row to display the context menu and to remove the row.

## Fields

- Serial (#) Serial number of the item in the transmit list.To modify this value use toolbar buttons Move Up / Move Down.

- Action Push button to transmit the specified frame (or error) over the LIN bus.The title of the button indicates the currently configured functionality and updated dynamically on each configuration change.This button is enabled during the measurement only.

- Frame Id LIN frame Identifier (6 bits).

- Frame Name Shows the symbolic name matching FrameId value.If no matching name found this field remains empty.

- Length Exp. Indicates the expected number of data bytes in the frame.If there is symbolic frame matching FrameId value this field becomes read-only, that is since the frame length specified in the database is used here.

- Length Trans. Indicat

### [FunctionBlocks/Logging] LoggingComment


# Logging Comment

Logging Block » Logging File Configuration » Logging Comment

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Logging|Logging File Configuration |Logging Comment|Set Current Comment...

The Logging Comment dialog allows you to specify the logging comment. Settings you make here apply only to this single logging block.

Information on global settings for all logging blocks can be found on the Global Logging Comment help page.

Logging comments can consist of several attributes. The set of these attributes is the same for each logging block and can only be set in the Global Logging Comment dialog.

## Field Codes

Inserts a field code into the currently selected input field. Field codes can be used to generate parts of the comment automatically. The values are determined each time the logging file is closed and inserted into the comment text. In the preview fields you get a preview of the comment text with resolved field codes.

The Logging Comment Field Codes page contains a detailed list of the available field codes.

## Columns

| Entry Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Attribute Name | The name of the attribute. This value can only be changed in the Global Logging Comment dialog. |
| Attribute Selection | Sets whether the locally defined, the globally defined, or no value at all for this attribute is written to the logging file. You set globally defined values in the Global Logging Comment dialog. |
| Default Value | The default value, which is written by the logging blocks. Th

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Logging] LoggingCommentEdit


# Change Logging Comment

Logging Block » Logging File Configuration » Change Logging Comment

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Logging|Logging File Configuration |Logging Comment|Change Last Comment...

This dialog is opened via a logging block and allows to change the logging comment after a measurement.

You can change the comment of the last logging file written by this block. The [Apply] and [OK] buttons write the new value directly to this file.

To change the comment of the last logging file written by all blocks, refer to the help page Change Global Logging Comment.

## Field Codes

Inserts a field code into the currently selected input field. Field codes can be used to generate parts of the comment automatically. The values are determined each time the logging file is closed and inserted into the comment text. In the preview fields you get a preview of the comment text with resolved field codes.

The Logging Comment Field Codes page contains a detailed list of the available field codes.

## Columns

| Entry Field | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Attribute Name | The name of the attribute. |
| Value of last Measurement | Here the value is displayed which is currently in the logging file. |
| Current Value | The value to be written to the logging file. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Logging] LoggingCommentFieldFunctions


# Logging Comment Field Codes

Logging Block » Logging File Configuration » Logging Comment » Logging Comment Field Codes

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Logging|Logging File Configuration |Logging Comment|Set Current Comment...

With the field codes parts of the logging comment can be generated automatically. The values are determined at the end of the logging file and inserted into the comment text.

## System

| Field Code | Field Function | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| User Name | {Username} | Windows log-in name. |
| Computer Name | {Computername} | Name of the computer. Current local system time. |
| Local System Time | {LocalTime[|Format]} | The default timestamp format can be selected in the global options. Within the field function the default can be overwritten. |

## CANoe

| Field Code | Field Function | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Measurement number | {MeasurementIndex} | The measurement number can be set in the global options and increases automatically with each start of the measurement. Starting with CANoe 9.0, the measurement number is stored in a separate file for system parameters, independent of the configuration. |
| Measurement start | {MeasurementStart[|Format]} | Timestamp for the start of the measurement. The default timestamp format can be selected in the global options. Within the field function the default can be overwritten. |
| Configuration name | {ConfigName} | Name of the configuration file. |
| Program version | {Version} | CANoe program

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Logging] LoggingEthernet


# Logging Block: Ethernet

Logging Block/Replay Block » Ethernet

You can log the bus traffic in ASC, BLFBinary Logging Format, MF4, PCAP Packet Capture and PCAPNG format to logging files. Afterwards you can replay theses logging files in the offline mode or by using the Replay Block.

## Extended Ethernet Logging

When Vector network interfaces are used (e.g. VN5610), you can log additional information. Only the BLF and ASC logging formats support this. For this, you must activate the Extended Ethernet Logging (Options dialog).

The following additional information is taken into account in the extended Ethernet logging:

- Ethernet frame checksum

- Transmission duration of an Ethernet frame [ns]

- Hardware channel number if the network interface is configured accordingly

- Forwarded Ethernet packets if the network interface is configured accordingly

## Overview of Ethernet Logging Formats

With Ethernet you can use the following logging formats:

| Format | Basic Logging Format | Extended Logging Format |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BLF | • | • |
| ASC | • | • |
| MF4 | • | — |
| PCAP | •Ethernet packages only, no error events.1, Channel number is not supported. Eth 1 is used for read in.2, Transmit direction (Rx, Tx) is not supported. RX is used for read in.3 | — |
| PCAPNG | •Ethernet packages only, no error events.1, Transmit direction (Rx, Tx) is not supported. RX is used for read in.3 | — |
| TTL | Read onlyEthernet packages only, no error events.1 | — |

See Also

| Ver

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Logging] LoggingFileExternalComponents


# Logging Block: External Writer/Reader

Logging Block/Replay Block » External Writer/Reader for Logging Files

You can specify external writers and readers for any number of log formats in order to write and read them in CANoe.

These logging files can then be read in Replay Blocks, as offline source files or as source files for export and conversion. Furthermore they can be written in logging blocks or as a target file for export or conversion.

These external writers and readers take the form of COM components that are implemented by the IStorage COM interface, and which transfer log data from a specific format into the BLF format.

A few settings must be adjusted in order to use external readers in CANoe.

|  | Step by Step Procedure Adjust the [LOGGING_READER] section of the CAN.INI file as follows: <file format>=<descriptive string>|<external reader>|<read/write flag> Example [LOGGING_READER]CCO=MOST CCO|{1715a11b-d3b9-4e3e-ad9c-07451164f494}|Mostlog.dll|readOP2=MOST OP2|{1715a11b-d3b9-4e3e-ad9c-07451164f494}|Mostlog.dll|readwrite With the Read/Write flag you determine whether the component has only read access or write access as well. The following flags are available: read Component supports read access only write Component supports write access only readwrite Component supports both read and write access. Register the library in which the reader is to be found using the following system command. To do this, open an MS-DOS prompt and change to the directory in which 

[...truncated for brevity...]

- Destination folder Here you select the folder to which the logging file is to be written.

- Destination file Here you specify the name of the logging file. Field functions support you when specifying the logging file names.

- Existing files can be protected against overwriting.

- After the end of measurement you don't have to enter new file names constantly.

- The logging recording can be split into several (smaller) logging files

- which are easier to be sent

- which are easier to be analyzed

- The {IncMeasurement} field code has been replaced by the {MeasurementIndex} field code.

- Only for Logging Blocks:The {Date} {Time}{YYYY}{MM}{DD}{hh}{mm} and {ss} field codes have been replaced by the {MeasurementStart} field code.

- Days (d): 0-99

- Hours (h): 0-23

- Minutes (m): 0-59

- Single Trigger: SingleTrigger

- Toggle Trigger: ToggleTrigger

- Entire Measurement: NoTrigger

- Test Trigger: Name of the corresponding test element that activates the trigger.Example: Suppose you have a test configuration PowerTrain with a test unit GearBox and a test case GearCheck that activates the trigger. Then, the field code is resolved to PowerTrain_GearBox_GearCheck. If the trigger would react on test units, the field code would be resol

### [FunctionBlocks/ProgramNode] help_caplboxan_1


# Program Node: Compile

Program Node » Compile

Context Menu of a Program Node: Compile

Before the start of measurement the programs belonging to the CAPL nodes must be compiled. The CAPL compiler can be run for each node individually using this button. You can also use in the Environment ribbon tab.

An error message may appear on the screen at the start of measurement if program blocks are not configured completely. This occurs if no file was defined for a program block (or for CAPL Logging), the file does not exist yet, or the user forgot to compile after creating the file.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/ProgramNode] help_caplboxan_2


# Program Node: Edit

Measurement Setup » Function Blocks » CAPL Nodes » Edit

Context Menu of a Program Node: Edit...

When this menu command is activated the program opens in the CAPL Browser.To do this all set-up and measurement windows are closed, and the Browser window is opened.

Browser is an easy-to-use tool for creating, modifying and CAPL programs.

The old screen contents are reinstated after ending the Browser session.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/ProgramNode] ProgramNode


# Program Node

Measurement Setup » Function Blocks » Program Node

A program node is a universal function block whose characteristics the user defines by writing a CAPL program. A detailed description of CAPL program blocks and instructions on creating CAPL programs can be found in the Manual in the chapter on CAPL programming. An example of an important use of program blocks is to specify transmit messages in the transmit branch. Nevertheless, very simple program blocks can be written too, e.g. for data reduction (Example: Only pass every tenth message) or for monitoring.

Program blocks appear in the data flow plan as small blocks with the inscription P.

With the right mouse button you can open the context menu of the selected function block. The following functions are available:

Configuration...

Compile

Edit...

Debug...

Comment... Opens the Comment: Function block dialog. Here you can insert a comment for the function block and choose the font settings of this comment.

Rename... Opens a dialog field to enable the renaming of the chosen block.

Block active If this option is active the function block is connected with the bus.

Copy Copies the function block to the clipboard. Choose a new bus via the tabs on the above margin of the Simulation Setup and paste the copied block with the right mouse button as shortcut. Now you can use the copied block as gateway.

Cut Cuts the function block to the clipboard.

Remove... Deletes the block out of the configuration.

| V

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/ProgramNode] ProgramNodeConfiguration


# Program Node: Configuration

Program Node » Configuration

Context Menu of a Program Node: Configuration...

- File name This field displays path and name of the current CAPL source code (*.can).

- With [Select…] you get a dialog where you can select a (new) CAPL file for the current CAPL node.

- With [Edit…] you start the CAPL Browser; the current CAPL source code is automatically loaded.

- With [Compile] you start a (new) compilation of the current CAPL source code.

- Modified Last modification date of the current CAPL source code (*.can).

- Binary Name and path of the (compiled) CAPL binary (*.cbf).

- Modified Last modification date of the current CAPL binary (*.cbf).

It is permissible to reference the same programs in different program blocks.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] help_envar_replay


# Replay of Environment Variables

Replay Block » Replay of Environment Variables

The Replay Block provides you with the option of feeding data into the simulation configuration from both binary and ASCII files.

Thus, you can edit ASCII logging files with a conventional text editor before you play them back in the Replay Block. In particular, you can also generate your own ASCII files with the sequences of values of certain environment variables, in order to feed them into the measurement through the Replay Block.

The Replay Block reads an ASCII file line-by-line. You enter the number format in the first line.

- If the integer values in your file are to be interpreted as decimal values you write:

- If you wish to enter the numbers in hexadecimal format you write:

You can comment out any line in the file with two slashes followed by a space character (// ). For lines which are to be fed back into the measurement from a Replay Block, you first enter the time of transmission in seconds (after start of measurement) in the first column. To play back an environment variable you write - in the second column - the name of the environment variable followed by a space character, the assignment operator :=, another space character and the variable value.

|  | Example of an ASCII Replay File // Number format: Decimal base dec // Time Name of EV Value1.0000 LightSwitch := 12.0000 LightSwitch := 24.0000 MotorSwitch := 14.1000 SpeedEntry := 100.04.2000 SpeedEntry := 150.004.3000 Spe

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] idd_rb_can_cfg_page


# Replay Configuration: CAN Options

Replay Block » Configuration » CAN Options

| General | Channel Mapping | CAN Options | LIN Options | MOST Options | FlexRay Options |  |

## Send

Here you indicate which Rx or Tx messages contained in the file should be sent.

In simulation mode if it is needed to read and display unchanged time stamps from file, activate the option Do not send messages using CAN simulator. In this case, messages are not considered in bus statistics.If the option is not activated, time stamps can be slightly adjusted by the CAN simulator according to time conditions and transmission protocol definitions (e.g. baud rate settings and bus load).

Are there used several Replay Blocks (Simulation Setup, Test Setup, etc.) the option has to be activated for only one Replay Block.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] IDD_RB_CHANNELMAPPING_CFG_PAGE


# Replay Configuration: Channel Mapping

Replay Block » Configuration » Channel Mapping

| General | Channel Mapping | CAN Optios | LIN Options | MOST Options | FlexRay Options |  |

Here you configure the allocation of the channels found in the logging file to the channels available in the configuration. The possible option settings are identical for all supported bus types.

The Source column shows all channels that are configurable. These correspond to the channels found in the logging file, whereby not all channels named in the list need to also exist in the logging file.

In the Target column you can configure the channels of the active configuration on which the messages should be output. Channel selection is limited to the number of channels set in the configuration. Ignore may be selected here so that the messages of certain channels will not be sent.

If the number of channels used for a bus system is reduced in a configuration, even though the channel allocation shows mappings to channels which are eliminated by this action, then these mappings are highlighted in yellow in the "Channel Mapping" dialog and are automatically set to ignore, since it is not possible to send on unconfigured channels. However, these new values are not saved in the configuration until you confirm them with [OK] in the dialog.

## Options

The [As in original] button causes all channels of the logging file to be mapped to the corresponding channels of the configuration (Channel1 -> Channel

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] idd_rb_flexray_cfg_page


# Replay Configuration: FlexRay Options

Replay Block » Configuration » FlexRay Options

| General | Channel Mapping | CAN Options | LIN Options | MOST Options | FlexRay Options |  |

|  | Note The FlexRay Replay Block can only be inserted into the Simulation Setup. |

## Functionality

The FlexRay Replay Block reads a logging file and sends the recorded FlexRay frames onto the assigned bus. The following points have to be considered:

- In order to send FlexRay frames by the Replay Block, the appropriate Tx buffers must be enabled. The node-based configuration therefore has to be made in the page "FlexRay Options" of the Replay Block's configuration dialog (see below).

|  | Note Because this configuration is made database-oriented, the currently assigned FlexRay database of the Replay Block's bus must be the same as the database, which was used while recording the logging file. |

- In order to send FlexRay frames, the FlexRay bus must be synchronous. Sync-Frames in the logged recording will not be replayed with the sync attribute in the FlexRay header!

- The replay for FlexRay happens cycle-oriented and not time stamp-oriented.

- Starting the replay needs some preliminary lead, resp. start-up time in order to synchronize with the current cycle.

- Environment and system variables will not be replayed.

## Options

## Replay Configuration

In this list you can select those nodes, whose transmitted messages should be replayed from the logging file.

## Behavior

- Stop at

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] IDD_RB_GENERAL_CFG_PAGE


# Replay Configuration: General

Replay Block » Configuration » General Settings

| General | Channel Mapping | CAN Options | LIN Options | MOST Options | FlexRay Options |  |

A Replay Block is controllable by CAPL calls automatically even if the start/stop and pause/resume keys (see bottom of the page) are not defined.

You can start, suspend, resume or stop the Replay Block during the measurement by buttons of the Replay Block in the Simulation Setup:

- - Start / Resume button

- - Pause (suspend) button

- - Stop button

## General

- Replay name Replay Block name used as a parameter for CAPL replay functions.

- [Select file…] You can select the logging file to be replayed here using the file selection box.The file name is displayed in the line that follows.You can enter the message based file types like BLF Frame Logging File (format BLF), MDF Frame Logging File (format MF4) or ASCII Frame Logging File (format ASC) as file formats.

|  | Note If the messages you save in a MDF Frame Logging File (format MF4), have a time distance of more than 240 seconds they cannot be replayed with their correct time stamps (e.g. in offline mode and in the Replay Block). MOST objects will be discarded on importing CLF files. Here you find further notes for logging file formats. |

You can use ASCII Frame Logging Files (ASC format) in the Replay Block to Feed environment variable sequences into the simulation configuration in CANoe.

## Output modes

- Standard Events are replayed with

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] IDD_RB_LIN_CFG_PAGE


# Replay Configuration: LIN Options

Replay Block » Configuration » LIN Options

| General | Channel Mapping | CAN Options | LIN Options | MOST Options | FlexRay Options |  |

## Replay mode

Here can be configured the way LIN events are replayed. The list of supported LIN events follows:

- Unconditional frame

- Event-triggered frame – No Response

- Event-triggered frame - Single Response

- Dynamic frame

- Configuration Request / Response

- Diagnostic Request / Response

- Wake-up Request

- Transmission Error, i.e. header without any response

To send headers of LIN frames the Replay headers option has to be selected. Responses for the sent headers can be configured in one of the following ways:

- Take all responses from the Replay file

- Take responses from the Replay file only for frames published by the Master node

- Ignore responses from the Replay file for all frames

- Use break timings from logfile By enabling this option, the break length and the delimiter length from the first LIN event appearing in the replay file on a specific channel will be used for sending all subsequent LIN headers on the same channel. These modified timings will be effective until replay stops.

|  | Note In order to send headers LIN has to be in the Master mode. See: Master mode of the LIN hardware for more information. |

## Replay filter

Here can be configured a filter for certain events from the Replay file. Available settings:

- Filter responses from Replay file for frames wi

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] IDD_RB_MOST_CFG_PAGE


# Replay Configuration: MOST Options

Replay Block » Configuration » MOST Options

| General | Channel Mapping | CAN Options | LIN Options | MOST Options | FlexRay Options |  |

## Send

Here you indicate which Spy, Tx node or Rx node messages contained in the file should be sent.

## Types/Channels

In addition to 'normal' messages here the user inputs whether system messages (RemRead/RemWrite or Alloc/Dealloc) found in the file should be sent.

## Target address

Here the user may specify whether the messages should always be sent with the target addresses saved in the logging file, or whether the target address should be determined by the address handler from FBlock & Instance ID for command OpTypes (0…8).

## Sender address

Since each node in a MOST ring has exactly one device address, it is quite possible that the sender address of a message in the logging file may not agree with the device address of the connected MOST hardware. Here you can decide whether these messages should be ignored (i.e. not sent) by the Replay Block or whether or whether they should be sent with the device address of the connected MOST hardware.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Replay] ReplayBlock


# Replay Block

Simulation Setup » Function Blocks » Replay Block

The Replay Block provides the means for reproducing measurement sequences which have already been recorded. To do this, the user indicates a logging file. Messages contained in it are introduced into the data flow.

An important application is the replay of a logged data stream onto the bus. This involves setting the Replay Block to a specific bus type (Supported bus types: CAN, LIN, MOST, FlexRay, Ethernet).

With CANoe you can also Feed environment variable sequences into the simulation configuration.

For data replay the Replay Block must be entered in the simulation configuration. When the [Select file] button is activated a file selection box appears for the replay file which is to be reproduced. Below this the user can specify how transmission is to occur. This area contains a selection of messages and time behavior. The file can be transmitted once or cyclically. With cyclic transmission the first message is started again after the end of file is reached.

|  | Note Effective with version 6.0 it is possible to use long symbolic names (> 32 characters). These are logged in the ASCII Frame Logging file (format ASC) if in the standard toolbar the icon sym is active.The produced ASCII Frame Logging files can be read in older versions of the program. However, messages with long symbolic names are ignored, because older program versions do not support long symbolic names.If you like to read ASCII Frame Loggi

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [FunctionBlocks/Statistics] dlg_histogramm_fkt


# Frame Histogram: Histogram Output

Frame Histogram (Measurement Setup) » Histogram Output

Context Menu of the Frame Histogram in Measurement Setup: Histogram Output...

On the left side all acquired data are shown in a tree view, sorted either by message or by acquisition range. Any desired entries can be selected from this list and copied to the list on the right side using the [Add»] button. This list contains all data intended for further processing. Selected entries can be deleted from this list by [Remove].

The selected data can be saved as CSV files (Character Separated Values). You can then use these files to utilize the data in other programs, whereby it is possible to append the data to a file that already exists.

Furthermore, you have the options of processing the data directly via Java and VB scripts, and e.g. representing them as a chart in Excel. Please use the enclosed sample script \Script\ToExcel.js(in the program directory) for this purpose.

|  | Note Script functionality can only be utilized if the Windows Script Host has been installed. If execution of the script fails please check the installation and obtain any necessary updates from the Microsoft Internet web site at: http://msdn.microsoft.com/scripting/ |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Statistics] dlg_statistic


# Frame Histogram: Configuration

Frame Histogram » Configuration

Context Menu of the Frame Histogram: Configuration...

In the text input box Averaging Time the user can define the interval over which values are averaged. A short interval can lead to a display that fluctuates severely. Very long averaging times make the display "sluggish".

## Expanded statistical functions

If the Activate check box of the expanded statistical functions is selected, a Statistics report is generated for at least one acquisition range during the measurement. After the end of the measurement this can be output to the Write window by a command in the context menu of the Statistics Window. As an alternative the output can be made automatic. Additional acquisition ranges can be logged via the Histogram functions.

Acquisition ranges that have already been recorded can be reset by pressing the lower button.

If a Higher Layer Protocol has been installed as an option, protocol-dependent interpretations can be displayed.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Statistics] HELP_STATCONF_2


# Statistics Report

Frame Histogram (Measurement Setup) » Statistics Report

Context Menu of the Frame Histogram in Measurement Setup: Display Statistics Report

If Statistics Report is activated, after a measurement has been run a report of statistical data can be requested via the context menu of the Frame Histogram in the Measurement Setup. This report is output in the Write window.

Output in the first line are the last valid acquisition range and the time at the measurement start.

Output in the first two columns are the identifiers and message attributes of the received messages.

The other columns have the following meanings:

| N | Number of received messages with this ID and attribute |
| --- | --- |
| Aver | Mean value of the transmit interval in ms |
| StdDev | Associated standard deviation in ms |
| MIN | Shortest transmit interval between two messages of this type in ms |
| MAX | Longest transmit interval between two messages of this type in ms |

Shown in the last column is the CAN channel over which the specific event occurred.

The Delay line represents a statistic for the transmit interval (Time between Tx and TXRQ).

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Statistics] HELP_STATISTIKBLOCK


# Frame Histogram: Measurement Setup

Measurement Setup » Function Blocks » Frame Histogram

|  | Note The Bus Statistics window for CAN, LIN and FlexRay has been replaced by the new bus-specific Statistics windows.To clarify and distinguish the features of the former Statistics window from these Statistics windows the former Statistics window has been renamed to Frame Histogram. Note the differences between the opened Frame Histogram an the Frame Histogram window in the Measurement Setup. |

All data that reach this block are included in the statistical evaluation. The online evaluation is output to the Statistics window in the form of bar chart diagrams. Furthermore, a statistics report is maintained which can be output in the Write window. Moreover, the histogram functions can be used for further statistical evaluations.

Various settings and actions can be executed from the context menu:

Configuration General options for the statistics report and the Frame Histogram window can be configured here.

New Evaluation Starts a new acquisition range for evaluation by the histogram function.

Stop Evaluation Ends an acquisition range.

Histogram Output Opens the dialog for the output of statistical data.

Display Statistics Report Outputs the statistics report in the Write window.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Trigger] TriggerConditionCAPL


# Trigger Condition: CAPL

Trigger Configuration » Trigger Condition CAPL

With the CAPL trigger condition triggering is initiated by a CAPL program.

The configured pre-trigger and post-trigger times define the logging time period.

|  | Note With the mechanisms of CAPL programming, conditions of any desired complexity may be formulated which may depend on the occurrences of various events. Triggering is initiated as soon as the trigger() function is called by a CAPL procedure.The pre-trigger and post-trigger times can be set with the setPreTrigger(long preTriggerTime) and setPostTrigger(long postTriggerTime) functions. |

|  | Example Trigger should be initiated on the nth occurrence of a specific message. Implementation of an edge trigger for the Single Trigger mode. Triggering might occur, for example, when a signal changes from A to B. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [FunctionBlocks/Trigger] TriggerConfiguration


# Trigger Configuration

Measurement Setup » Function Blocks » Trigger Block/Logging Block » Configuration

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Logging|Trigger Configuration

In this dialog you can configure the trigger conditions for logging measurement data to logging files.

|  | Note Whenever you activate the logging function the trigger is activated as well, automatically.Additionally you can place the trigger anywhere (hot spot) in the analysis branch of the measurement window. InStandalone Mode, only one Trigger Block with limited functionality is available (toggle trigger triggered with CAPL). To make the results of a measurement easier to understand, you should create multiple Logging Blocks in the evaluation branch, each with a different trigger condition. This results in several compact files, each of which is customized to address a special analytical question. If necessary, you can also configure a Logging Block to record the entire measurement. For test triggers, it is strongly recommended to use the Increment at each Named Trigger field code in the Logging File Configuration dialog for generating the file names. In this way, each test element is logged into a single file with a unique and meaningful name. |

|  | Note Filters in the Measurement Setup Filters in the Measurement Setup are tailored to specific (superordinate) objects such as frames, PDUs, messages. Note that subsequent CAPL blocks usually address subobjects such as signals directly via the signal server and are 

[...truncated for brevity...]

| == | equal |
| != | not equal |
| < | less than |

| > | greater than |
| >= | greater than or equal |
