### [ISO11783/procedures] UseNodelayerDLL


# Interaction Layer Integration

ISO11783 » Interaction Layer Integration

In the CANoe option. ISO11783 different interaction layer can be used to implement the functionality of a simulated node. Each interaction layer provides you with many other context-specific CAPL functions that can be used to influence the behavior of the simulated node. Assign the interaction layer to the node in the CANoeSimulation Setup so that it can be used there. To do this, use one of the methods described on the Assigning Modeling Libraries page.

## Using an Interaction Layer in vTESTstudio

The CAPL functions of an interaction layer are not available in vTESTstudio by default. To use it in vTESTstudio, you have to insert the DLL from the CANoe folder Exec32 as modeling library in the Test Unit configuration.

Copying the Interaction Layer DLL from the CANoe folder Exec32 and storing it in a subfolder of the vTESTstudio project has proven successful. If the Test Unit is executed in CANoe, the Interaction Layer DLL from CANoe is used.

## Available ISO11783 Interaction Layers

The table lists the interaction layers of option .ISO11783 and the corresponding DLL files:

| Interaction Layer | DLL |
| --- | --- |
| ISO11783 Interaction Layer | ISO11783_IL.dll |
| Virtual Terminal Interaction Layer | ISO11783_VT_IL.dll |
| Task Controller Interaction Layer | ISO11783_TC_IL.dll |
| File Server Interaction Layer | ISO11783_FS_IL.dll |
| GNSS Node Layer | GNSS_NL.dll |
| J1939 GB/T 27930 Interaction L

### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminal] VTOverview


# Virtual Terminal (VT)

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal (VT)

| General |
| --- |
| In an ISO11783 network one of the important components is the Virtual Terminal (VT). CANoe Option .ISO11783 offers the whole functionality of such a terminal. Besides, big value was put on the flexibility with the description of the qualities of the terminal. So it can be configured, e.g. whether the simulated terminal supports color or black and white representation. The number of the Softkeys as well as the resolution are configurable freely. With it all possible terminals are available to the user with one tool. Manufacturers of Virtual Terminals can check therefore without big expenditure different operating drafts. With only a few steps you can activate a Virtual Terminal and work with it. Additional options are available to analyze an object pool, so all objects of the object pool can be displayed and analyzed in an additional view. Within the passive mode the bus traffic of a real Virtual Terminal can be monitored and the object pool can be analyzed in detail in the CANoe Virtual Terminal if any errors occur. Starting with version 6, the VT adds a transaction number (TAN) to the Activation Messages and controls the corresponding Response Messages. |
| Virtual Terminal Window |
| is immediately ready for use no possibility for fault injection has the graphical user interface is controlled by the CANoe user | Virtual Terminal Video Tutorial Virtual Terminal Window VT Diagnostic Requests Vir

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminal] VTtan


# TAN

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal (VT) » TAN

Starting with version 6, the VT adds a transaction number (TAN) to the Activation Messages and controls the corresponding Response Messages.

|  | Note Exceptions are the VT Change Active Mask, VT Change Soft Key Mask and VT Change String Value, which do not contain a TAN. |

The VT manages a separate TAN list for each working set. The TANs are assigned in ascending order starting with 0. After TAN 15, the list starts again at TAN 0.

If the VT Interaction Layer (VT IL) is used, the next TAN can also be set with the VTIL_SetNextTAN function or queried with VTIL_GetNextTAN. function can also be used, which can be used to falsify the arbitrary bits of a Virtual Terminal message.

To falsify the TAN values, the VTIL_ManipulateMessage function can also be used, which can be used to falsify the arbitrary bits of a Virtual Terminal message.

After the VT has sent an Activation Message, the VT expects a response from the Working Set with the same TAN within 250 ms. If the response is not received by the VT, the VT issues an error notification. If the VT IL is used, the callback function VTIL_OnError is also called with the error code -2124 (if the function is implemented). The TAN of the Activation Message is passed as an additional parameter.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminalWindow] PageAuxiliary


# Page Auxiliary Function

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal Overview » Virtual Terminal Window » Page Auxiliary Function

Auxiliary functions can be assigned to the auxiliary inputs on this page. All auxiliary functions of all working sets are graphically displayed in this list. To establish a connection between a function and an input, click on a selected line. A list appears with the available auxiliary inputs. The assignment is made by means of an entry from the list.

## Assignment Storage

### VT Version 2

At the end of measurement, assignments are saved in the file VTAuxAssign.INI. This file is loaded with the next measurement and the assignments are executed again.

### VT Version 3 or higher

The assignment are not saved by the Virtual Terminal but by the Working Set, providing the auxiliary functions. The Working Set sends a Preferred Assignment command during the initialization phase, that initiate the rebuilding of the assignments.

## Learning Mode

If the Virtual Terminal version is 3 or higher, you can activate the learning mode with the correspondent button. The learning mode allows the assignment of an auxiliary input to an auxiliary function by using the input. Before you have to select the auxiliary function on this page.

## Restrictions for Auxiliary Functions and Inputs

For Virtual Terminal Version 3 or higher the auxiliary functions and inputs may contain some restrictions. The Virtual Terminal uses overlay icons to show the kind of restriction for the fun

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminalWindow] PageDataMask


# Page Data Mask

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal Overview » Virtual Terminal Window » Page Data Mask.

On this page the data mask and soft key masks are displayed. The number of soft keys and the position of them can be changed on the Configuration page.

## Tooltip and Context Menu

If the mouse pointer remains on an object a short time, a tooltip with the object-ID is displayed. The settings allow the configuration of the tooltip, so that additional information about the object are displayed and the object is framed.

The right mouse button opens the context menu. With the entries Go to... it can be switched to page Object Pool. The designated object is selected and its attributes are displayed.

## Keyboard Shortcuts

The following shortcuts are available for navigating and editing in this page:

| Key(s) | Action (Command) |
| --- | --- |
| <F2> | opens a window for editing the selected input object |
| <Return> | confirmation of a alarm mask |
| <+> (Num-Block) | incrementation of the value of the selected numeric input object |
| <-> (Num-Block) | decrementation of the value of the selected numeric input object |
| <Tab> | selection of the next Working Set |
| <Tab>+<Shift> | selection of the previous Working Sets |
| <Left> | selection of the next left input object |
| <Right> | selection of the next right input object |
| <Down> | selection of the next input object below |
| <Up> | selection of the next input object above |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminalWindow] PageObjectPool


# Page Object Pool

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal Overview » Virtual Terminal Window » Page Object Pool

Here you can see the object pool of the current working set. On the left side the name and the identifier of the objects are displayed. If an object is selected then you can see a preview and the properties of it on the left side of the page.

## Search Function

The search box is above the table with the available objects. If a text is entered an incremental search is executed. All objects and their attributes are searched for the entered text.

If the search box is opened and the command Search ID and Reference is selected, it is searched for the object-ID and references to this ID.

It is also possible to display objects of a selected type (e.g. data mask or alarm mask) only.

### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminalWindow] PageSettings


# Page Configuration

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal Overview » Virtual Terminal Window » Page Configuration

On this page you can edit the settings of the Virtual Terminal.

## Properties of the Virtual Terminal

| VT version | Here you can select the VT version used by the Virtual Terminal. |
| --- | --- |
| Error handling | The Virtual Terminal has a mechanism to handle errors in the object pool (i.e. references to unknown objects). How the Virtual Terminal should react in error situations can be set with this list. Delete Object Pool on error: In error cases the Working Set is deleted from Virtual Terminal and an error message is displayed. This is conform to the behavior of a real Virtual Terminal. Suspend Working Set on error: In error cases the Working Set is deactivated and cannot be addressed from the bus. For the Virtual Terminal it seems as if the Working Set is deleted. However the Working Set can be selected and displayed. This setting can be used for later analysis. Tolerant error checking: The object pool is checked for errors less restrictive. Error messages are displayed in the information view at the bottom of the Virtual Terminal Window. |
| Mode |
| Enable passive mode | The passive mode is enabled and the Virtual Terminal only monitors the bus traffic. If the two following settings are not used, the Virtual Terminal is monitored and displayed that has the same address as defined at Device Name. |
| Auto detect address of the VT | The Virtual Terminal that r

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminalWindow] VTErrorcodes


# Virtual Terminal Error Codes

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal Overview » Virtual Terminal Window » Error Codes

The following errors can be output by the Virtual Terminal.

| Error Code | Error Text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 500 | Delete working set of node ... after timeout. | The maintenance message of the Working Set Master was not received within three seconds. The Working Set is deleted. |
| 501 | Initiating flag of the working set maintenance message of node ... is still 0. | After deleting the Working Set cause by time-out of the maintenance message, all further maintenance messages are responded with the NACK message until the initializing bit of it is set. |
| 502 | 'Graphics context' (object ...): Value of 'number of polygon points' (...) is bigger than the number of following points. | The command for drawing a polygon contains less points than specified. |
| 503 | 'Graphics context' (object) ...: Value of 'number of bytes' (...) is bigger than the number of following bytes. | The command for drawing text contains less bytes than specified. |
| 504 | Cannot assign auxiliary input (...) to auxiliary function (...) because single-assignment flag is set. | An auxiliary function cannot assigned multiple because the single-assignment of the auxiliary input is activated. |
| 505 | Virtual Terminal changes function instance of the device name from ... to ... because original function instance is already used by another Virtual Terminal. Virtual Terminal is stop

### [ISO11783/VirtualTerminalWindow] VTRequests


# VT Diagnostic Requests

ISO11783 » Virtual Terminal » Virtual Terminal Window » VT Diagnostic Requests

The following information of the Virtual Terminal can be queried using a diagnostic request:

| Message | PGN | Content of the Response |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Product identification | 0xFC8D | Character string 001*CANoe Virtual Terminal*<current CANoe version>*. |
| Control function functionalities | 0xFC8E | Functionality Universal Terminal Server with configured VT version. |
| ECU identification information | 0xFDC5 | Character string 001*<current CANoe version>*Unknown*Virtual Terminal*Vector Informatik GmbH*01*. |
| Diagnostic protocol | 0xFD32 | Note, that no further diagnostic protocols are supported. |
| ISOBUS certification | 0xFD42 | Note, that no certification exists. |
| Language command | 0xFE0F | Language settings, date and time format and units. |
| Active diagnostic trouble codes (DM1) | 0xFECA | Note, that there are no errors. |
| Previously active diagnostic trouble codes (DM2) | 0xFECB | Note, that there are no errors. |
| Diagnostic data clear/reset previously active DTCs (DM3) | 0xFECC | As response to this request a positive acknowledgment message is sent. |
| ECU software identification | 0xFEDA | A software identification field with following content Virtual Terminal <current CANoe version>. |
| Time/Date | 0xFEE6 | Current date and current time. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

To activate the Virtual Terminal Window you have to open the context menu in the Simulation Setup by right-clicking on the bus symbol. Pressing command Insert Virtual Terminal inserts a Virtual Terminal node. By double clicking it the window is opened.

## Layout of the Virtual Terminal Window [▲ back]

In the top part of the window the symbols of the Working Sets, which has sent by an object pool to the Virtual Terminal, are displayed. The active Working Set is selectable here. The context menu of the window part offers the following commands:

- Delete Working Set The Working Set is deleted from Virtual Terminal.

- Export Object Pool... The actual object pool of the Working Set is saved to an IOP file.

- Import Object Pool... An object pool is read from an IOP file and displayed. The imported object pool can only be monitored.

- Remove stored versions Removes the stored object pool versions of the Working Set.

In the middle part of the window there are some property pages:

- Page Data Mask

- Page Object Pool

- Page Auxiliary Function

- Page

### [J1587] J1587HardwareConfiguration


# Hardware Configuration

J1587 Network Hardware » J1708

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|J1587

To configure J1587 hardware, a J1708 channel must be configured before.

Via the [Driver...] button you open the Vector Hardware Config dialog, where you can configure a J1708 channel. See link below.

The Setuppage shows the baud rate. For J1708 a baud rate of 9600 baud is configured and cannot be modified.

The setting Rx - max. Inter-Character Bit Time sets the maximum allowed distance between data bytes within a J1587 message. The specified value for J1708 is 2 bit times.

### [J1587] J1587ParameterDefinition


# Parameter Definition

J1587 » Parameter Definition

A J1587 parameter can be defined in the CANdb++ Editor. A J1587 parameter is equivalent to a CAN message in the CANdb++ Editor, the CAN identifier is equivalent to the PID. The PID contains the parameter identifier and the page, see Display J1587 PID.

A parameter of type J1587 can have a CAN identifier of 2047 at a maximum (eight pages x 256 PIDs). For a parameter of type J1587 Proprietary a CAN identifier in the range of 0x1000000..0x1FFFFFFF is necessary.

The DLC of a J1587 parameters can be 3825 at maximum.

Via the context menu of a J1587 parameter (or a CAN message) and via the button […] the dialog J1587 Properties can be opened. In this dialog the PID and the page can be edited separately.

## Message List

The message list contains some additional columns for J1587:

| Column | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| J1587 PID | shows the PID with page |
| J1587 Page | shows the page of the PID |

Proprietary extensions can be activated with the network attribute J1587ProprietaryFormat. The following values are possible:

- undefined: The PID 254 is not evaluated

- Volvo: The PID 254 is evaluated (Volvo specific)

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1587] J1587PID


# Display of J1587 PID

J1587 » Display J1587 PID

The parameter identifier (PID) is build with the PID and the page of the PID. CANoe and CANdb++ Editor show the page and PID as one value.

|  | Example PID 84 (54h) – PID 84 of page 1 PID 340 (154h) – PID 84 of page 2 |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1587] J1587TransportProtocol


# J1587 Transport Protocols

J1587 » Transport Protocols

## Overview

J1587 specifies two transport protocols, whose main characteristics are explained here in short.

- Multisection – PID 192 (Page 1) / PID 448 (Page 2) Using the multisection parameter it is possible to transmit a parameter, whose message length would exceed the maximum of 21 bytes (as specified by J1708). The parameters' data is split up into several fractions, applied a sequence number, and then transmitted consecutively.The multisection protocol allows the transmission of a single, "long" J1587 Parameter. Its length (data portion - without MID, PID, length byte & checksum) must in between 18 to 239 bytes (page 1 parameter) and 17 to 226 bytes (page 2 parameter) respectively.

- Transport Protocol with flow control (RTS / CTS) — PID 197 (CMP – Connection Management PID) & PID 198 (CDP – Connection Mode Data Transfer PID) This protocol provides for a handshake mechanism between two bus sharing units, where the receiving node acknowledges the submitted packets and requests the send node to continue its transmission.This transport protocol permits sending of any form-free byte sequences, which are interpreted by the respective receiver.The length of the data sequence fractioned and submitted with this transport protocol is limited to 3825 bytes.

## Treatment and Usage of Transport Protocols within CANoe

- Parameter Monitor, Diagnostics (DC) Monitor Parameter submitted via the multisection protocol are tre

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1587] J1708ChannelConfiguration


# Channel Configuration

J1587 » Channel Configuration

## Channel-Hardware Mappings

To ensure CANoe can access J1708 hardware, an application channel must be configured using Vector Hardware (see below) in the Control Panel of Windows.

The J1708 channel must be assigned to a J1708 capable hardware (in this example a CANcardXL with a J1708cab) by right-clicking the transceiver the channel should be assigned to.

|  | Note In case there is no menu command for J1708 within the context menu, increase the number of available channels by double-clicking Application J1708 channels after selecting Application|CANoe in the tree view. |

## Channel Usage

In order to enable a J1708 channel for use in a CANoe configuration, set the amount of desired channels in the Options dialog (Measurement|General|Channel Usage).

On Hardware ribbon tab|Network Hardware the available J1708 channels are listed. For each of these the maximum inter-character bit time when receiving can be configured. Other hardware parameters cannot be changed due to regulations by the J1708 specification.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939] conceptJ1939


# J1939 for CANoe | Concept and Functions

J1939 » J1939 for CANoe » Concept and Functions

| Analysis |
| --- |
| Option J1939 supports and extends the basic analysis of CANoe. Existing analysis windows are extended to interpret J1939 transport protocols and the Fast-Packet transport protocol, and to check for protocol violations. Signals of proprietary Parameter Groups can be analyzed and interpreted. Additional analysis windows allow the analysis of the J1939 specific Address Claiming and the J1939 Device Name. Dynamic address handling can be displayed as well as GPS data. The analysis of data can be made more comfortable by using filter. Data that are not interesting for analysis can be filtered out of the data stream. Option J1939 allows filtering for J1939 Parameter Groups, static and dynamic addresses by using the J1939 Device Name, and filtering depending on priorities. |
| Simulation and Stimulation |
| Option J1939 allows to simulate J1939 nodes (ECUs) and their sending behavior. J1939 nodes can be simulated signal based and message based. GNSS receiver can be simulated with predefined models and logging files or by using CAPL. Depending on the requirements the programming of CAPL source code is completely not necessary. The bus can be stimulated with Interactive Generators. Option J1939 offers J1939 specific message parameter to configure the messages and the creation of the message send list. |
| Diagnostics |
| Option J1939 offers several extensions for the J193

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks] filter


# CAN Filter

J1939 » CAN Filter

The filter of the standard CANoe is extended with the option J1939. The configuration is analogous to the standard filter.

|  | Cross Reference Functions that are not J1939 specific are described in the online help of CANoe. |

## Buttons and Options

| Button | Command |
| --- | --- |
| [Events...] | Messages from a J1939 database are filtered by their PGN instead of the message ID. |
| [Raw data...] | You can enter the numeric value of a PGN by adding a P, e.g. Start ID FF00P and Last ID FFFFP for all non-specific proprietary PGNs. For PGN format 1 (00xxP..EFxxP) you have to set the destination address xx to 0. |
| [Nodes...] | You can select J1939 network nodes and filter them by their J1939 addresses. |

## Filter Settings

On page J1939 in the upper right part of the filter you can set the J1939 message and node attributes that are to be filtered.

To define a filter you have two possibilities. The static method filters by the defined Address, the dynamic method however observes the Address Claim messages and filters by the 64 bit device name. You can toggle between theses two methods with the check box in the column Dynamic Filter.

For a filter defined with the button [Events...] you can freely set the address, the database name and the node name for both the receive direction and the transmit direction. For a filter defined with [Nodes...] you can replace the wildcards (*) in the columns of the device name with specific values, or r

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks] generatorblock


# Generator

J1939 » Generator

The operation of the Generator occurs analogous to the layer 2 send list. Differences arise only in the structure of the send list.

Each line of the list consists of 9 columns.

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| <number> | ascending number of lines |
| PGN | Parameter Group number of the J1939 message followed by a p, or symbolic name |
| SRC | address of sender, range 0.255 |
| DST | address of receiver, range 0..255 The global receive address of 255 can be specified symbolically with all. |
| R1 | not set by default, can be changed and analyzed in the Generator only |
| DP | not set by default, can be changed and analyzed in the Generator only |
| Prio | priority of the J1939 message, range 0..7 |
| DLC | number of data bytes of the J1939 message In principle, up to 1785 bytes can be transmitted. In the Generator the number is limited to max. 8 data bytes since for the transmission of more than 8 data bytes a fragmentation protocol (with handshake) is required. This cannot be implemented with the line-oriented structure of the Generator. A simple broadcast data transfer sequence can nevertheless be set up easily. |
| CAN | selection of the CAN controller used for the transmission of the J1939 message |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks] interactivegeneratorblock


# J1939 Interactive Generator

J1939 » Interactive Generator

The operation of the configuration dialog occurs analogously to the original send list. Differences arise only in the structure of the send list.

Additional J1939-specific message parameters:

| Parameter | Note |
| --- | --- |
| PGN | Parameter Group Number |
| Prio | set the priority of the J1939 message, permissible values: 0…7 |
| SRC | Station address of the transmitter, address range: 0…255 |
| DST | Station address of the receiver, address range: 0…255 |
| R1 (Reserved) | Field can only be changed and evaluated in the Interactive Generator |
| DP (Data Page) | Field can only be changed and evaluated in the Interactive Generator |

Extended identifier of a message

- can be calculated with the parameters above or

- entered directly: The correspondingly-calculated values appear in the parameter fields.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/diagmemaccess] dmaCyclicObjAccess


# Cyclic Object-Oriented Memory Access

J1939 » J1939 Diagnostic Memory Access » Cyclic Object-Oriented Memory Access

In addition to manual access, memory objects can also be read cyclically. But for the column Duration and two missing buttons, this page is identical to the manual object-oriented memory access page.

For objects with an activated check box in the first column, a cyclic read access is executed during the measurement. During the measurement the read access to an object can be activated or deactivated by using the check box. The cycle time in the column Duration (in milliseconds) and the object ID in the column ID can be changed too. In contrast it is not possible to change the environment variable which is assigned to an object, if the cyclic read access is activated.

If several objects have the same cycle time, only one timer is created for these objects.

In addition, the following fields are available:

| Button | Action |
| --- | --- |
|  | Starts or stops broadcast messages of a single ECU or all ECUs |
|  | Deletes the content in the Output Window |
|  | The window synchronization is activated and mark that instant of time at which the current selected message occurred. |

Diagnostics memory access | Manual direct memory access | Manual object-oriented memory access

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/diagmemaccess] dmaManualDirectAccess


# Manual Direct Memory Access

J1939 » J1939 Diagnostic Memory Access » Manual Direct Memory Access

On this page it is possible to read out a memory area, write to it or delete it; it is also possible to execute a boot load procedure.

The data to be read or written is also displayed in the memory window. The window is divided into three columns. The left column displays the offset with respect to the specified address. The offset always begins with zero. The middle column displays the data in hexadecimal form and the right column in ASCII form. The data can be edited in the middle and right columns. After editing, the data is displayed in red. With the help of the context menu or the key combination <Ctrl>+<Z> operations can be undone and restored with <Ctrl>+<Y>. Using the context menu, the number of data bytes per line can be set.

The result of the action carried out is specified in the Output Window. In case of errors that are reported by the ECU with the DM15, the error number is also displayed next to the error text.

In addition, the following fields are available:

| Button | Action |
| --- | --- |
|  | Load a file into the Data Window. The previous content is lost. |
|  | Store the data shown in the Data Window in a file. |
| Address | Memory access occurs at this address in the ECU. From the list box, one of the last used addresses can be selected. |
| Length | Length of the data in bytes that should be read, written or deleted. |
|  | Read data of the specified 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/diagmemaccess] dmaManualObjAccess


# Manual Object-Oriented Memory Access

J1939 » J1939 Diagnostic Memory Access » Manual Object-Oriented Memory Access

On this page objects can be specified and their values written to or read from the memory with the touch of a button.

Each line describes an object. Absolutely necessary values in the object table are the object ID and the length of the object. The name, unit, and an assigned environment variable are optional. The column Space specifies how the object ID is to be interpreted. If value 0 (SPN SPACE) or a SPACE defined in the database is selected, an object can be selected from a list of possible IDs or from a list with names.

If an object is selected from the list, it is marked as "connected with the database". Different icons show if an object is connected to a database or not:

| Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | object is connected to a database |
|  | object is currently not connected to a database |

If an object is connected to a database, the object length, unit, and conversion values (factor, offset) are then extracted from that database. The object length and the unit cannot be changed then. If the object is not connected to a database, the unit and the physical value cannot be displayed. The raw value and the object length are modifiable.

A connection to the database can be established or released with a right mouse click on the icon in column DB. For this the object ID for the specified SPACE must be defined in the database.

If objects 

[...truncated for brevity...]

# J1939 Diagnostic Memory Access

J1939 » Diagnostic Memory Access (J1939)

Ribbon|Diagnostics & XCP Tab|J1939|Diagnostic Memory Access|View / New

| General |
| --- |
|  | With the J1939 Diagnostic Memory Access in the Measurement Setup you can read and write parts of the memory as well as execute a boot load procedure. For this the diagnostic messages DM14, DM15, DM16, and DM18 are used. |
| Structure | Note |
| The window consists of four page. In addition to direct access to a memory area, object-oriented access is also possible (manual or cyclic). The settings that apply for all types of memory access, you can make on the Configuration page. | To minimize the network traffic, you can stop the transmission of broadcast messages of a single ECU or all ECUs. For this the diagnostic message 13 is sent to the ECUs. Subsequently a status messages is cyclically sent to the ECUs to avoid that these ECUs change to their previous states. If required you can start the transmission of the broadcast messages. The ports to be used you can set on page Configuration. |

Manual direct memory access | Manual object-oriented memory access | Cyclical object-oriented memory access | Configuration | Shortcuts

### [J1939/blocks/diagmemaccess] dmaSettings


# Settings

J1939 » J1939 Diagnostic Memory Access » Settings

This page is divided into various areas, which will be described below:

## Tester

Here the address of the Memory Access Window and the channel on which the messages are sent/received is specified. In CANoe the CAN channel is assigned in the Simulation Setup and it cannot be changed here.

If the Use address claim check box is activated, then on measurement start, the Memory Access Window tries to log onto the network with the specified address (Address Claiming). If this fails, then no messages can be sent. In this case, a new address must be selected and the measurement started anew. Additionally the J1939 device name must be specified. Open the appropriate dialog with the button [Device Name...] and enter the values that should be used for the device name.

## Node

In the selection field, the address of the ECU is selected whose memory should be accessed, or whose transmission of broadcast messages should be started or stopped. This address can be changed even during measurement (it does not influence the cyclical reading of objects).

## Objects

Objects that should be read or written are identified using a number (ID). These numbers are interpreted depending on the so-called SPACE. J1939 specified previously only the SPACE for SPNs (value 0).

The value entered here is used as default value for new objects on pages Manual direct memory access and Manual object-oriented memory access.

## Used Ports (DM13)


[...truncated for brevity...]

| <End> | Cursor in the memory window jumps to the end of the line |
| <Ctrl>+<End> | Cursor in the memory window jumps to the end of the memory |
| <Shift>+<End> | Data in the memory window is marked from the cursor to the end of the memory. |
| <F2> | Edit the current field in the memory window. |

### [J1939/blocks/diagmonitor] dmConvertSpn


# SPN Conversion

J1939 » J1939 DTC Monitor » SPN Conversion

## SPN Conversion Method

The Suspect Parameter Number (SPN) in a diagnostic trouble code (DTC) can be interpreted differently. The SPN is a 19 bit signal. With the Conversion Method Bit the SPN cannot be decoded always. If the bit is not set the conversion method 4 is used (SAE J1939-73). If it is set the method 3 is used.

To modify the conversion method for specific nodes, the node attribute J1939SPNConversionMethod can be defined. The attribute is of type integer and can have the values 1 to 4. The attribute must be defined in the database for specific nodes..

J1939 DTC Monitor | Data Export

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/diagmonitor] dmExportData


# Data Export

J1939 » J1939 DTC Monitor » Data Export

The J1939 DTC Monitor offers the possibility to copy the displayed data into the clipboard for further processing or archiving. If you copy data, no diagnostic messages are requested. So you can use the copy function if the measurement is stopped or if CANoe is not connected to a network.

To copy the data of a single ECU, select this ECU and press <CTRL>+<C>. Alternatively you can open the context menu of the ECU and select the entry Copy. To copy the data of multiple ECUs, select the ECUs by holding the <CTRL> key pressed. Then press <CTRL>+<C> or select the entry Copy of the context menu.

You can also select one or more DTCs (Diagnostic Trouble Code) in the lower part of the J1939 DTC Monitor. Follow the instructions described above for the ECUs to select the DTCs.

If you copy data, the current column selection and sorting as well as the number format is considered. The data in the clipboard are available for text, rich text (RTF) and CSV. So you can use the data within software for word processing or spreadsheets.

| Format | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text | For text format standard spaces are used to format tables. Use a non-proportional font, e.g. Courier New. |
| RTF | For RTF format tabulators are used to format table. Eventually you must modify font size or the tabulator space to get an optimal table format. |
| CSV | Numeric values are always displayed in decimal format for the CSV export and separated f

[...truncated for brevity...]

Additionally the J1939 device name must be specified. Open the appropriate dialog with the button [Device Name...] and enter the values that should be used for the device name.

### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/functions] gmGraphicDisplay


# Graphical Display of GNSS Data

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Graphical Display of GNSS Data

Graphical display of longitudinal and latitudinal degrees takes place in a two-dimensional Graphics Window. In the settings you can select whether differences in angles or the converted longitudinal differences will be displayed. The values can be determined with the aid of the grid.

The coordinate system in the Graphics Window extends to the directions north and east.

In addition, a third variable can also be displayed with the color of the position point. This addition variable can be either the altitude, the speed or an environment variable. The color bar underneath the Graphics Window displays the minimum and maximum values that have been set.

The first position is drawn in the middle of the Graphics Window. All additional positions are displayed in terms of this reference position.

The recording of data begins with the start of the CANoe measurement. Message filtering can be used to adjust which messages will be displayed.

You can use the mouse to move the graphic within the graphics area or to zoom in on a particular area.

Numeric Display of GNSS data | See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/functions] gmLogging


# Logging of Received Positions

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Logging

Received GNSS positions can be recorded in a logging file along with other data. This data can be further processed by evaluation tools, for example to send received sequences back to the bus again or to facilitate an external display.

Data is saved in the form of an ASCII file with the file ending ".gns". One position is saved per line, in the following format:

where

| ID: | The format ID of the file line (previously, exclusively 'A', which stands for NMEA2000®) |
| --- | --- |
| PGN: | The Parameter Group number of the GNSS message (decimal number) |
| TIME: | Time stamp of the GNSS receiver (floating point number, in seconds starting from midnight, UTC) |
| LAT: | Degrees latitude (floating point number, in degrees) |
| LON: | Degrees latitude (floating point number, in degrees) |
| ALT: | Altitude (floating point number, in meters) |

Since altitude is not supported by all messages, this field may contain a minus sign (-) instead of a number. If the time stamp is of no interest, a zero (0) should be entered there.

The path of the file to be used can be specified on page Logging. Data will not be logged unless the button is pressed and the measurement is started.

To prevent the file from being inadvertently overwritten, a query can be performed each time before an overwrite takes place (see logging settings).

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/functions] gmNumericDisplay


# Numeric Display of GNSS Data

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Numeric Display of GNSS Data

Numeric display of degrees longitude and latitude is shown in degrees (°), minutes (') and seconds (''). The altitude display depends on the selected unit.

The recording of data begins with the start of the CANoe measurement. Message filtering can be used to adjust which messages will be displayed.

Graphical Display of GNSS Data | See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/functions] gmWndSync


# Window Synchronisation

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Window Synchronisation

If the measurement was stopped, the function Synchronize windows can be activated in the context menu of the GNSS Monitor window or with the icon in the CANoe Home ribbon tab. The measurement cursor is shown with the left mouse button and makes simultaneous scrolling in all Trace, Graphics and Map Windows available. This allows a direct assignment of the displayed data in all windows.

|  | Note The window synchronization only jumps to the point of time in the trace or Graphics Window if the data is contained in the window buffer. |

|  | Cross Reference Further information on the window synchronization are available in the CANoe online help. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Notifications

| Notification | Unable to open file …. |
| --- | --- |
| Possible Cause | It was not possible to open the file selected for logging. |
| Remedy | If the file is write-protected, the write protection must be removed in order to be able to use it for logging. If you have entered an invalid path for the file, it must be corrected. |

| Notification | Please specify a valid file path. |
| --- | --- |
| Possible Cause | The file path for the logging file is invalid. |
| Remedy | Specify a valid file path. |

| Notification | Please specify a valid source address. |
| --- | --- |
| Possible Cause | An invalid source address was specified in the settings of the message filter. |
| Remedy | Specify an address between 0 and 253. It is also possible to enter an asterisk. This will cause messages to be analyzed before all nodes. |

### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/procedures] gmMeasurement


# Measurement

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Measurement

Before starting the CANoe measurement, you can change the settings of the GNSS Monitor. For example, measurement filtering can be used to adjust which messages will be evaluated. This is no longer possible after the measurement starts.

The recording of data begins with the measurement start.

Logging can be turned on and off during the measurement. It should be noted that any existing file may be overwritten when logging is turned on or at the beginning of the measurement. For this reason, the user is able to turn on a warning message in the program settings that will appear each time before a file is overwritten.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/procedures] gmShiftGraphic


# Move the Graphic

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Move the Graphic

You can use the scroll bars to display different segments of the graphics area in the Graphics Window. Positions that are outside the viewable area remain invisible.

Positions that are outside the graphics area cannot be displayed until the graphic is moved (unless the scale is reduced to display more on the screen). To move the viewable area, hold the left mouse button down and drag the cursor to the desired location. After you release the mouse button, the graphic will be redrawn in the new location you have moved to.

The process can be canceled by clicking with the right mouse button before releasing the left mouse button.

The move (and scrolling) can then be reset with the button in the main window.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/procedures] gmZoomGraphic


# Display a Segment of the Graphic

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Display a Segment of the Graphic

A specific segment of the graphic can be displayed at a larger scale. To do this, click with the right mouse button and move the cursor. As you move the cursor, you describe a rectangle representing the desired segment. After the mouse button is released, the scale is adjusted and the graphic is moved. The scroll bars will be reset so that they are located in the middle of the graphic.

The process can be interrupted by clicking with the left mouse button before releasing the right mouse button.

The move (and scrolling) can then be reset with the button in the main window. The scale can be changed again with the buttons and .

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

# Configuration

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Configuration

The Configuration dialog you open via the context menu command with the same name or via the Configuration button in the toolbar:

In this dialog, you can change the settings of the GNSS Monitor. There are the following pages available:

- Graph

- Units

- Message Filter

- Logging

- Optimizations

|  | Note Help for the selected page can be started with the [Help] button. |

See Also

### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/settings] gmSettingsGraphic


# Graph

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Configuration » Graph

| Graph | Units | Message Filter | Logging | Optimizations |  |

## North/East Axis

Either the angle different between longitudinal and latitudinal degrees or else the corresponding paths that have been traversed can be displayed in the two-dimensional Graphics Window.

## Third Dimension

A third dimension can be made visible on the two-dimensional Graphics Window with the aid of color. The user can select between displaying altitude values, speed values or environment variable values for this third dimension.

To obtain a favorable range and allocation of colors, the anticipated maximum and minimum values can be indicated. The minimum value will then appear in blue in the Graphics Window, the maximum value in red. If a value falls outside this interval, the corresponding point will be shown in gray.

If the Update range on fit check box is selected, the maximum and minimum values of the third dimension will also be adjusted when the graphic is adjusted to the Graphics Window. Maximum and minimum values for altitude and/or speed recorded since the beginning of the measurement are used to do this. The adjustment can be made either with the button in the main window or by activating automatic adjustment (which can also be turned on in the main window).

## Use Fix Grid

If this check box is deactivated, the grid network will also be moved when the graphic is moved. To determine a length from the graphic, however, it is m

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/settings] gmSettingsLogging


# Logging

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Configuration » Logging

| Graph | Units | Message Filter | Logging | Optimizations |  |

In order to log position data that is received, a logging file can be selected. If you select the check box Ask before overwrite existing file, a dialog will appear before the file is overwritten with which the user can give consent to the process or prevent logging from occurring.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/settings] gmSettingsMsgfilter


# Message Filter

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Configuration » Message Filter

| Graph | Units | Message Filter | Logging | Optimizations |  |

NMEA2000® specifies three messages and J1939 specifies one message that can be used to transfer position information. You can indicate which of these messages will be represented and/or post-processed by the GNSS Monitor for the Message Filter.

Specifying the source address means that only messages of the desired sender will be received. If you want messages of all GNSS devices on the network to be received, you should indicate so with an asterisk (*) in the editing box instead of a concrete device address. The specified address is displayed in the main window in the Node Filter area.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnssmonitor/settings] gmSettingsOptimization


# Optimizations

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Configuration » Optimizations

| Graph | Units | Message Filter | Logging | Optimizations |  |

If GNSS data is to be displayed in a different way in the Graphics Window, the data must be saved. The size of the buffer limits the number of positions that can be saved. Each position requires 300 bits to be saved. This means that a buffer with a maximum size of 3000 kB is capable of storing 10,000 messages.

The User-defined refresh input box specifies how often the display of the Graphics Window will be updated. Small values will result in a continuous display of the change of position over time, but will require a higher level of computer performance on the other hand.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

# Units

J1939 » GNSS Monitor » Configuration » Units

| Graph | Units | Message Filter | Logging | Optimizations |  |

The units of variables can be selected from one of the following systems of units of measure:

- Meters (m), kilometers (km), kilometers/hour(km/h)

- Feet (ft), yards (yd), miles (mi), miles/hour (mph)

- Feet (ft), yards (yd), nautical miles (nmi), knots (kn)

Information about altitude is always in meters or feet. Routes in the Graphics Window are specified in meters and kilometers, yards and miles or yards and nautical miles (nautic system). Speed information also depends on the system of measurement units that is selected.

After the button is activated again, the speed is set back to the value that is displayed and the process of traversing the selected route continues. |
| Start with measurement | When this check box is set, the GNSS simulation

### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator] gsOverview


# GNSS Simulator

J1939 » GNSS Simulator

Context Menu of the CAN Bus in the Simulation Setup|Insert GNSS SimulatorRibbon|Simulation Tab|GNSS Simulator|View

| General |
| --- |
|  | The GNSS Simulatorcan be used to simulate a GNSS receiver. To do this, a geometric figure or a position file is used to determine a path that will be traced at a variable speed when the simulation is started. GNSS positions thereby generated are transferred cyclically with configurable messages. |
| Window | Functions and Control |
| The main window consists of the following parts: Main Window/Toolbar Graphics Window for graphic display of positions to be sent and labeling for them area for controlling the simulation Pressing <F1> allows the user to start online help. | Generating GNSS Data with Models: File Line Rectangle Meander Graphical Display of GNSS Data Altitude Information Playing back Logging Files with GNSS Positions File Format |
| Procedures | Configuration |

### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator] gsPositionDataDlg


# GNSSPositionData

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Dialog GNSSPositionData

In this dialog you can edit additional values of the NMEA2000® message GNSS Position Data:

| Value | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| Sequence ID | Either an upward counting number used to tie related information together between different PGNs or a fix value of 255. |
| Type of system | This value indicates the type of GNSS system. |
| GNSS Quality | This value indicates the quality of GNSS information. |
| GNSS Integrity | This value indicates the GNSS integrity. |
| Number of SVs | This value indicates the number of satellites used in solution. |
| HDOP | This value indicates the horizontal dilution of precision with a resolution of 0.01. |
| PDOP | This value indicates the positional dilution of precision with a resolution of 0.01. |
| Geoidal Separation | This value indicates the distance of the geoid to the geodetic ellipsoid (Geoidal Separation), resolution: 0.01 m. |
| Number of Reference Stations | This value is always set to 0. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/functions] gsFileformat


# File Format

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » File Format

A GNSS logging file is in ASCII format so that positions can be inserted or changed manually.

One position is saved per line, in the following format:

with

| ID: | The format ID of the file line (previously, exclusively 'A', which stands for NMEA2000®) |
| --- | --- |
| PGN: | Number of the Parameter Group (decimal number) |
| TIME: | Time stamp of the GNSS receiver (floating point number, in seconds starting from midnight, UTC) |
| LAT: | Degrees longitude (floating point number, in degrees) |
| LON: | Degrees longitude (floating point number, in degrees) |
| ALT: | Altitude (floating point number, in meters) |

|  | Note Since altitude is not supported by all messages, this field may contain a minus sign (-) instead of a number. If the time stamp is of no interest, a zero (0) should be entered there. Commends must be preceded by an asterisk (*) or a semicolon (;) in the first column of the line. '.gns' is recommended as the file name extension. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/functions] gsFileReplay


# Playing Back Logging Files

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Playing Back Logging Files

You can use GNSS Simulator to play back GNSS logging files with position information, for example information that was created with the GNSS Monitor. Position data can be modified with a text editor using the simple file format.

There are two types of playback:

### Playback at a Specified Speed

Only the positions of the file that have a time factor > 0 are used to calculate the positions to be sent. The current speed at any given time can be calculated by using the difference in space and time between any two successive points. The speed can also be set when it reaches a given position.

### Playback at a user-defined Speed

All positions of the file are used to calculate the positions to be sent. The instantaneous speed is controlled in the main window.

To play back a position file, select the path on the Model page. The file path can be selected there and the playback type specified.

The advantage compared to playing back a CANoe logging file is that variables such as speed, the parameters of messages and the magnitude of the jitter can be changed for each simulation.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/functions] gsGeneratePosData


# Generate GNSS Data with Models

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Generate GNSS Data with Models

Generating GNSS data is performed in two steps:

- Calculation of waypoints:

- Generating and sending position data

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/functions] gsGraphicDisplay


# Graphical Display of GNSS Data

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Graphical Display of GNSS Data

Graphical display of longitudinal and latitudinal degrees takes place in a two-dimensional Graphics Window. In the settings you can select whether differences in angles or the converted longitudinal differences will be displayed. The values can be determined with the aid of the grid.

The coordinate system in the Graphics Window extends to the directions north and east.

In addition, altitude is also represented by the color of the position point. The color bar underneath the Graphics Window displays the minimum and maximum values that have been set.

The first calculated position is drawn in the middle of the Graphics Window. All additional positions are displayed in terms of this reference position. The positions are represented by a small cross and are connected by a straight line.

The mouse can be used to move a graphic in the graphics area or to zoom in on a specific area In addition, the button can be used to adjust the zoom factor, the offset and the range of values of the altitude, so that the entire graphic is visible.

When the simulation begins, a small gray diamond appears that identifies the current position. After the simulation is complete, this diamond disappears.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/functions] gsHeightData


# Altitude Information

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Altitude Information

The altitude information for positions in the GNSS Simulator refers to the rotation ellipsoid of the WGS84 reference system.

The altitude in the Graphics Window of the main window and in the model dialogs is specified in either meters or feet (depending on the setting on the Units page). The altitude in a GNSS logging file is always specified in meters.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/messages] gsMsg


# Notifications

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Notifications

The different messages of the GNSS Simulator are displayed in dialogs:

| Notification | The file … was not closed. |
| --- | --- |
| Possible Cause | It is not possible to close the file selected in the model File after reading. |
| Remedy | No. |

| Notification | Invalid file line(s) … in file … .Data of these line(s) is not used. |
| --- | --- |
| Possible Cause | While reading the file specified in the model File one or more lines with invalid format are found. These lines are ignored and are not used by the GNSS Simulator. |
| Remedy | Check with a text editor whether the specified file contains GNSS position data with the correct format. See also: Playing back logging files |

| Notification | No GNSS position data found in file ... |
| --- | --- |
| Possible Cause | The file specified in the model File contains no GNSS position data. |
| Remedy | Check with a text editor whether the specified file contains GNSS position data with the correct format. See also: Playing back logging files |

| Notification | No GNSS position with latitude information found in file … . The altitude is set to 0. |
| --- | --- |
| Possible Cause | The specified file in the model File contains only GNSS position data without altitude information. |
| Remedy | To calculate the simulation data the longitude and latitude as well as the altitude of the positions is necessary. If the altitude is missing for all positions, the altitude 0 is

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/models] gsModelFile


# File

J1939 »GNSS Simulator » Models » File

You can select a GNSS protocol file here that the GNSS Simulator will replay. You can specify the path of the file to be used either in the input box, with the input box list or with the [...] button.

If you select the option box Use route, only longitudes and latitudes as well as the altitude of the positions will be used to specify the route to be traversed. The speed is controlled in the main window.

When you select the option box Use route and speed, the instantaneous speed is calculated from the time information of the positions as well. This makes it possible to traverse the route at the same speed that was used to create the protocol. Please note that positions that have no time information are not taken into consideration during this type of playback.

|  | Cross Reference For more information see Playing back protocol files. |

Model | Line | Rectangle | Meander | See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/models] gsModelLine


# Line

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Models » Line

Specifying a beginning point and an end point describes a line. The two points are specified by latitude, longitude and altitude. The values can either be specified directly or the position can be selected from the corresponding list on page Positions (where it can also be edited).

If the Connect with straight line check box is marked, a straight line will be traversed from the beginning point to the end point without taking into account the curvature of the earth.

Otherwise a number of interpolation points will be introduced at fixed distances (currently 1000 m) to allow for the curvature of the earth.

Model | File | Rectangle | Meander | See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/models] gsModelMeander


# Meander

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Models » Meander

A meander can be specified by a reference point, the width and height of a loop, the radius of the rounded off corners and the number of loops.

The illustration below shows the position of the reference point (point) and the direction of circulation (arrow) for different meanders. Both variables depend on the prefixed sign of the width (w) and the height (h). The radius in this example is zero.

The units used to specify the length, width, and radius may be meters, feed or nautical miles (depending on the setting on page Units).

Model | File | Line | Rectangle | See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/models] gsModelRect


# Rectangle

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Models » Rectangle

Specifying a reference point as well as the width, height and radius of the rounded corners makes it possible to define a rectangle.

The reference position can either be specified directly with longitude and latitude, along with the altitude, or it can be selected from the list on page Positions (where it can also be edited). The illustration below shows the position of the reference point (point) and the direction of circulation (arrow) for different rectangles. Both variables depend on the prefixed sign of the width (w) and the height (h). The radius in this example is zero.

The units used to specify the length, width, and radius may be meters, feet or nautical miles (depending on the setting on page Units).

With this model, a circle can also be described in which the height is equal to the width and the radius is equal to one half the height.

Model | File | Line | Meander | See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/procedures] gsFitGraphic


# Adjust the Graphic to the Graphics Window

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Adjust the Graphic to the Graphics Window

Clicking on the button in the main window adjusts the scale and offset so that the entire graphic is visible. Scrolling is reset during this process. If automatic adjustment of the maximum and minimum altitude values was selected in the graphic settings, these values will be adjusted as well.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/procedures] gsShiftGraphic


# Move the Graphic

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Move the Graphic

You can use the scroll bars to display different segments of the graphics area in the Graphics Window. Positions that are outside the viewable area remain invisible.

Positions that are outside the graphics area cannot be displayed until the graphic is moved (unless the scale is reduced to display more on the screen). To move the viewable area, hold the left mouse button down and drag the cursor to the desired location. After you release the mouse button, the graphic will be redrawn in the new location you have moved to.

The process can be canceled by clicking with the right mouse button before releasing the left mouse button.

You can use the button in the main window to reset the offset (and scrolling).

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/procedures] gsSimulate


# Simulation

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Simulation

The procedure for performing a simple simulation is described in the following steps.

|  | Before the measurement starts the Configuration dialog is opened with the button [] of the main window. Then you can select a model on the Model page and set parameters appropriately. The figure of the model will later be traversed during the simulation and GNSS messages will be generated. The settings of the Parameter Groups to be sent should then be adjusted on the Messages page. After the dialog closes and the measurement is started, the simulation can be started with the [Start] button in the main window. If you want the simulation to start at the same time as the measurement, the Start during measurement check box in the main window must be activated. When the simulation starts, the GNSS Simulator also begins to calculate positions and to transmit selected Parameter Groups cyclically. The speed at which the route of the selected model is traversed can be varied in the Speed input box or with the slide control under it. The simulation can be ended with the [Stop] button in the main window or when the measurement ends. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/procedures] gsZoomGraphic


# Display a Segment of the Graphic

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Display a Segment of the Graphic

A specific segment of the graphic can be displayed at a larger scale. To do this, click with the right mouse button and move the cursor. As you move the cursor, you describe a rectangle representing the desired segment. After the mouse button is released, the scale is adjusted and the graphic is moved. The scroll bars will be reset so that they are located in the middle of the graphic.

The process can be interrupted by clicking with the left mouse button before releasing the right mouse button.

In the main window, you can use the button to display the whole graphic. You can change the scale with the and buttons. You can use the button to reset the offset (and scrolling).

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsDlg


# Configuration

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration

The Configuration dialog you open via the context menu command with the same name or via the Configuration button in the toolbar:

In the Configuration dialog, you can change the settings of the GNSS Simulator. There are the following pages available:

- Model

- Graph

- Units

- Messages

- Parameters

- Positions

- Network Management

|  | Note Help for the selected page can be started with the [Help] button. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsGraphic


# Graph

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration » Graph

| Model | Graph | Units | Messages | Parameters | Positions | Network Management |  |

## North/East Axes

Either the angle differences between longitudinal and latitudinal degrees or else the corresponding paths that have been traversed can be displayed in the two-dimensional Graphics Window. Angle differences are given in degrees, while the way in which routes are specified depends on the selected unit (see page Units).

## Altitude

Altitude values can be made visible on the two-dimensional Graphics Window with the aid of color. To obtain a favorable range and allocation of colors, the anticipated maximum and minimum values can be indicated. The minimum value will then appear in blue in the Graphics Window, the maximum value in red. If a value falls outside this interval, the corresponding point will be shown in gray.

If the Update when adjusting check box is activated, the limit values for altitude will also be adjusted to the current graphic when the button in the main window is activated.

## Use Graphic

If this check box is deactivated, there will be no graphic display. This avoids long waits that may occur while the screen is refreshed, especially when large files are replayed.

## Fixed Grid Network

If this check box is deactivated, the grid network will also be moved when the graphic is moved. To determine a length from the graphic, however, it is more practical if the grid network remains fixed and the gr

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsMessages


# Messages

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration » Messages

| Model | Graph | Units | Messages | Parameters | Positions | Network Management |  |

Here you can select parameters of messages (Parameter Groups) of the NMEA2000® and J1939 protocol with which GNSS positions will be transferred. The selection of the message to be sent is made in the list box.

In the upper part of the dialog there is a list of possible messages. Only the activated messages will be sent. Down below this list are the message-specific parameters. There are displayed the properties of the currently selected message.

| Used Messages |
| --- |
| List of different Messages | All messages that are activated are sent with the set cycle time. The message Vehicle Direction/Speed (J1939, PGN 65256) is not sent cyclically per default. If this message is activated it can be required with a J1939 request. |
| Priority | J1939 priority of the selected message. |
| Cycle Time | Time in ms at which the selected message is sent cyclically. |
| [More data...] | With this button you can open a dialog in which you can enter further message-specific data. This is possible for the following two messages: GNSSPositionData (NMEA2000) COG&SOG, Rapid Update (NMEA2000) The button is deactivated for all other messages, as these messages cannot transmit any further data. Information: The message Position Delta, High Precision Rapid Update (NMEA2000) uses the sequence ID (SID) of the last GNSS Position Data message sent and

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsModel


# Model

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration » Model

| Model | Graph | Units | Messages | Parameters | Positions | Network Management |  |

In the list Model you can choose between different models that can be used to generate GNSS data.

## Possible Models

- File

- Line

- Rectangle

- Meander

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsNM


# Network Management

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration » Network Management

| Model | Graph | Units | Messages | Parameters | Positions | Network Management |  |

Here you can select the channel through which the messages of the GNSS Simulator are sent and received. If the GNSS Simulator is used in CANoe, the channel is already defined by the Simulation Setup.

Also defined here is the J1939 address that the GNSS Simulator should use and the device name that is used in the address claimed message.

The GNSS Simulator sends the address claimed message when the measurement is started. However, it does not evaluate address claimed messages from other participants.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsParameter


# Parameters

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration » Parameters

| Model | Graph | Units | Messages | Parameters | Positions | Network Management |  |

The jitter for longitude/latitude and altitude can be specified here. Calculated position values include an error factor specified by these values to simulate the deviations of a GNSS receiver.

In addition, the maximum and minimum value of the speed can be set on this page. During the simulation, the speed can only be changed in the main window in this area.

The units of the variables depend on the configuration on page Units.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsPositions


# Positions

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration » Positions

| Model | Graph | Units | Messages | Parameters | Positions | Network Management |  |

Reference positions to be used for models on page Model can be defined in the table of this page. When the position name is selected, there is no need to explicitly specify altitude, longitude and latitude.

Reference positions are saved in the CAN database that is specified at the top of the display field. The [...] button can be used to open a dialog in which another file can be selected. To modify the database load it in the CANdb++ Editor and open the Attribute Definitions Window from the View menu.

Each location is specified by three related environmental variables each of type Float. They all start with the same name but end on _Alt (for altitude), _Lon (longitude) and _Lat (latitude). The valid range for longitude is -180..180 and for latitude it is -90..90, negative values mean west/south, positive values mean east/north.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/gnsssimulator/settings] gsSettingsUnits


# Units

J1939 » GNSS Simulator » Configuration » Units

| Model | Graph | Units | Messages | Parameters | Positions | Network Management |  |

The units of variables can be selected from one of the following systems:

- Metric system: Meters (m), kilometers (km), kilometers/hour(km/h)

- English system: Feet (ft), yards (yd), miles (mi), miles/hour (mph)

- Nautic system: Feet (ft), yards (yd), nautical miles (nmi), knots (kn)

The altitude and jitter information for altitude, longitude and latitude are always specified in meters or feet. Lengths on page Model are specified in meters (metric system), in feet (English system) or in nautical miles (nautic system). Routes in the Graphics Window are specified in meters and kilometers (metric system), yards and miles (English system) or yards and nautical miles (nautic system).

|  | Note Conversions and equivalences are as follow: 1 kilometer 1 foot 1 yard 1 mile 1 nautical mile 1 mile/hour 1 knot = 1000 meters = 0.3048 meters = 3 feet = 1.609 kilometers = 1.852 kilometers = 1.609 kilometers/hour = 1.852 kilometers/hour | 1 kilometer 1 foot 1 yard 1 mile 1 nautical mile 1 mile/hour 1 knot | = 1000 meters = 0.3048 meters = 3 feet = 1.609 kilometers = 1.852 kilometers = 1.609 kilometers/hour = 1.852 kilometers/hour |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 kilometer 1 foot 1 yard 1 mile 1 nautical mile 1 mile/hour 1 knot | = 1000 meters = 0.3048 meters = 3 feet = 1.609 kilometers = 1.852 kilometers = 1.609 kilometers/hour = 1.852 kilometer

### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter] j1939FilterBlock


# J1939 Filter

J1939 » J1939 Filter

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Filter|J1939 Filter

| General |
| --- |
|  | The J1939 Filter lets you filter J1939 messages. It has a number of advantages over the CAN filter. filtering for J1939 parameters groups filtering for static addresses filtering for dynamic addresses by using the J1939 device name filtering for priority |
| Window | Usage |
| The J1939 Filter window is divided into three areas: Menu Bar Here you configure the filter (Pass or Stop Filter, filter active/inactive) and the view (depending on the currently displayed filter group), and you can add/remove filters. Filter View All filter types are shown here: PG Filter Node Filter The main view lists all filter. The columns are configurable depending on the filter type.The properties view displays the settings of the currently selected filter. Configuration Overview Here, in the lower part of the window, an overview of all configured and activated filters is shown. | Requirements: the J1939 option has to be installed at least one CAN application channel has to be configured The filter is used in the analysis branch of the Measurement Setup. To insert a J1939 Filter, use Insert Event Filter | J1939 command from the context menu of a hot spot that precedes the relevant evaluation block. The main function of this filter is to serve as either a Pass Filter or a Stop Filter. A Pass Filter that is not configured (i.e. is empty) does not allow any J1939 events to pass through, and there

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter] j1939FilterBlockColumns


# Column Configuration

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Column Configuration

In dialog Column Configuration you can select the settings that are displayed in the main view of the filter. With that you can display the selected settings for all configured filter. The properties view still display all settings of the current selected filter.

The available columns depend on the filter type: PG Filter or Node Filter. Which columns are available is described on the help pages of the PG Filter and Node Filter.

The column configuration is set individually for each filter and is stored in the configuration file of your CANoe configuration.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter/j1939filterNode] j1939FilterNode


# Node Filter

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Node Filter

On this page you can define filters for the communication of specified nodes with distinction whether the nodes is sender, receiver or both. To identify the node the source and destination address of the message is evaluated. This address must match with the address of the node. Assignments of messages to nodes (Tx/Rx messages) in the database are not considered.

You can select the nodes from the database or define them freely. You can set further restriction for the filters by using additional settings.

Insert Node Filter | Node Filter in older Configurations | See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter/j1939filterNode] j1939FilterNodeInsert


# Insert Node Filter

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Node Filter » Insert

You can insert a node filter

- via commands of the context menu or

- via commands of the menu Edit.

The following commands are available:

- Add From Database…: opens a node selection dialog that you can use to insert nodes from a database to the node filter.

- Add Node Filter...: opens a dialog to configure a node filter using the address (without NMT) or the J1939 device name (with NMT), and the CAN application channel.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter/j1939filterNode] j1939FilterNodeParameter

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Node Filter » Settings

| Filter Settings | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Common |
| Enabled | activates and deactivates a filter |
| Channel | number of the application channel The available selections depend on the number of configured channels. |
| Database |
| Database | the name of the database is displayed for messages that are configured in the database (not editable) |
| Name | the name of the messages as configured in the database is displayed for messages that are configured in the database (not editable) |
| Message Flags |
| Data | Data Remote Filter yes no filter message if RTR bit is set to Data no yes filter message if RTR bit is set to Remote yes yes ignore RTR no no don't use, no message can match this condition | Data | Remote | Filter | yes | no | filter message if RTR bit is set to Data | no | yes | filter message if RTR bit is set to Remote | yes | yes | ignore RTR | no | no | don't use, no message can match this condition |
| Data | Remote | Filter |
| yes | no | filter message if RTR bit is set to Data |
| no | yes | filter message if RTR bit is set to Remote |
| yes | yes | ignore RTR |
| no | no | don't use, no message can match this condition |
| Remote |
| Tx Request | yes: messages are filtered regardless if direction is Rx, Tx or TXRQ no: messages with direction TXRQ are not filtered |
| J1939 |
| Direction | Tx: messages with node address as source address are filtered Rx: messages with node address as destinat

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter/j1939filterPG] j1939FilterPG


# PG-Filter

J1939 » J1939 Filter » PG-Filter

On this page you can define filter for Parameter Group numbers (PGNs) and for PGN ranges.

You can select PGNs from the database or define them freely. You can set further restriction for the filters by using additional settings.

On the following page you will find examples for the use of filters: Samples (J1939 PG Filter)

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter/j1939filterPG] j1939FilterPGInsert


# Insert

J1939 » J1939 Filter » PG-Filter » Insert

You can insert a PG filter

- via commands of the context menu or

- via commands of menu Edit.

The following commands are available:

- Add From Database…: opens a symbol selection dialog that you can use to insert Parameter Groups from a database to the PG filter.

- Add PG Filter…: opens a dialog to configure the PG filter using the Parameter Group number (PGN) and the CAN application channel.

- Add PG Range Filter…: opens a dialog to configure a PG filter using a range of Parameter Group numbers (PGN) and the CAN application channel.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

| Remote |
| Tx Request |
| J1939 |

| Priority | priority of the message |
| NMT Activated | Network Management deactivated: the address is used to identify network nodes activated: the Device Name in ACL messages is used to identify network nodes The ACL messages are evaluated how they enter the Measurement Setup. Modifications that are made later, e.g. by CAPL nodes, are not considered. | J1939 Source Node |
| Src Name | name of the so
