### [J1939/blocks/j1939filter/j1939filterPG] j1939FilterPGSample


# Samples for Specific Filter Configurations

J1939 » J1939 Filter » PG-Filter » Samples

## Mixed PDU-Format 1/PDU-Format 2 Filter

A PG range filter can filter from a PGN of PDU format 1 to a PGN of PDU format 2. In that case the destination address can configured as follows:

| PGN | Source address | Destination address | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0hp..FF01hp | * | 2 | The destination address is considered only if the Parameter Group that should be filtered has PDU format 1. This configured filter would filter a Parameter Group with PGN 100hp and destination address 2, but not a Parameter Group with PGN FF02hp. |

## Node Filter as PG Filter

By using PG range filters you can configure specific node filters that are not mappable on page Node Filter. Some of such specific filters are described next.

| PGN | Source address | Destination address | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0hp..FFFFhp | 1 | global | This filter is a mixed PDU-Format 1/PDU-Format 2 filter. All broadcasts with source address 1 are filtered. |
| 0hp..EFFFhp | 1 | 2 | This filter filters Parameter Groups in PDU format 1 only. The source address has to be 1, the destination address has to be 2. |
| 0hp..FFFFhp | 1 | 2 | This filter filters Parameter Groups of source address 1 and in PDU format 1 if the destination address is 2. Broadcasts of source address 1 in PDU format 2 are filtered too. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939nodefilter] nfChannelSelection


# Channel Selection

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Node Filter » Deprecated Node Filter » Channel Selection

In the dialog Channel selection you can define, which channels are filtered during measurement additionally to the filter rules for PGs. For each channel you can define a separated setting. With the button [Reset] you can restore the default settings.

A colored icon in column Channel shows the state of the node filter for the appropriate channel:

| Icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
| green | The node filter affects the channel. |
| red | The channel is blocked. |
| green/red with a cross | If a J1939 database is assigned to the channel, the node filter affects the channel per default. If no J1939 database is assigned to the channel, the channel is blocked. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939nodefilter] nfMainWnd


# Node Filter (Deprecated)

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Node Filter » Deprecated

With the J1939 Node Filter you can filter on PGs that are sent or received by specific nodes.

|  | Example Three filter rules are listed in the filter displayed above. The filter type is set to "Pass filter". Rule 1: All PGs that are send by the node with address #5 and received by the node with address #10 are allowed to pass through. Rule 2: All PGs that are sent globally (destination address 255) by nodes with addresses #220, #221, #222,...., #230 are allowed to pass through. Rule 3: All PGs having address from #20 through #30 as a destination address are allowed to pass through. The sender address is ignored (*). |

[Add filter rule...]: A new filter rule will be added to this filter. This rule will be configured using the Node selection dialog.

[Delete filter rule]: To delete a rule, first select it under Source. Then click on the [Delete filter rule] button.

Edit filter rule: To be able to edit a filter rule, open the Node selection dialog by double clicking on the rule to be changed and then edit the rule.

Filter type: To adjust the filter type, choose the selection Pass filter (for a pass-through filter) or Stop filter (for a block filter).

[Channel...]: This button opens the dialog for the channel selection.

[OK]: With this button you can close the dialog.

[Help]: Press this button if you need help about the J1939 Node Filter.

|  | Note If no rules are specified for the filter, then

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/j1939nodefilter] nfNodeSelection


# Node Selection

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Node Filter » Deprecated Node Filter » Node Selection

Rules can be defined for the filter in this dialog. The following possibilities are available for setting the source address and the destination address:

- Ignore: The setting will be ignored. If this setting is selected for the source address, for example, the sender address will not be taken into consideration during filtering. The Ignore setting is displayed in the main window by an asterisk (*).

- Range: A range can be specified. For example, if you want to allow all PGs to pass through that are sent by nods with addresses 5-10, this can be specified by using a range.

- Node address: The address of an individual node is specified: If you want to allow all PGs to pass through that are sent/received by a specific node, the address of the node can be specified under this setting.

- Global: (recipient address only): Filtering for PGs whose recipient address is GLOBAL (255). This setting is displayed in the main window by "GLOBAL".

|  | Example All nodes that are sent by the node with Address #10 will be filtered. Source address: Select Node address and enter Address 10. Destination address: Select Ignore. |

|  | Example All nodes whose recipient address is “GLOBAL" (255) will be filtered. Source address: Select Ignore. Destination address: Select Global. |

|  | Example All PGs that are sent by nodes 2, 3, 4, and 5 to node 10 will be filtered. Source address: Select Range an

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/j1939nodefilter] nfRangeSelection


# Range Selection

J1939 » J1939 Filter » Node Filter » Deprecated Node Filter » Range Selection

Specify the starting value in the Start field and the ending value of the range in the End field. If the starting value of the entry is greater than the ending value, an error message will appear.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner] scannerOverview


# J1939 Scanner

CANoe » Analysis Windows » J1939 Scanner

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|System Scanner|View / New

| Allgemeines |
| --- |
|  | With the J1939 Scanner in the Measurement Setup active nodes in the network can be displayed and analyzed. The source addresses of the received Parameter Groups and the Address Claiming messages are evaluated by the J1939 Scanner. From this information the J1939 Scanner detects the active nodes and the state. |
| Pages | Functions |
| Systems Nodes Network Configuration | For selected nodes more detailed information can be displayed, e.g. the J1939 device name. It is also possible to send Request and Commanded Address messages. |
| Sections | Configuration |
| The view on the right side depends on the selected element (node or channel) on the left side. The sections can be elapsed/collapsed with a click in the title bar. The following sections are available: Request Address Claim Device Name Set Device Name Commanded Address Request Parameter Group ECU Information(only with option ISO11783) Working Set Information | To insert a J1939 Scanner function block in the Measurement Setup, click on a function block (data sink) with the right mouse button. Then select the context menu command Insert J1939 Scanner. The window will be opened. |

Option .J1939

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] commadedAddress


# Commanded Address

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » Commanded Address

The button [Change now] sends a Commanded Address Parameter Group (FED8h) to the selected node. The new address must be entered to the input field, then the button is enabled.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] deviceName


# Device Name

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » Device Name

In this section the J1939 device name of the selected node is displayed. For nodes which has not sent the address claim Parameter Group (EE00h), no device name is displayed.

## NAME-Management

If the Parameter Group 9300h for NAME-Management (NM) of J1939-81 is used in the network, the columns Previous-Name and Pending-Name provide the following information:

- Pending-Name: future device name that is confirmed last with a NM-Set-Pending-Name-ACK

- Previous-Name: device name that the device had before the last successful NM-Adopt-Pending-Name

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] ecuInfo


# ECU Information

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » ECU Information

This section allows to request and display of information of an ECU as specified in ISO11783-12 (Diagnostic) and J1939-71 (Vehicle Application Layer). The information of the following Parameter Groups is displayed:

- ECU Identification Information (FDC5h)

- Software Identification Information (FEDAh)

- ISOBUS Conformance Certification (FD42h)

- ECU Diagnostic Protocol (FD32h)

- Vehicle Identification (FEECh)

The type of request is selected in the context menu of the button:

- Request ECU Information: specific request with the destination address of the selected node

- Request ECU Information (Global): global request

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] info


# Information

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » Information

In this section warnings and notes are displayed with the time stamps, which the J1939 Scanner has detected.

The button [] activates the window synchronization and sets the marker to receive of the message.

|  | Note The window synchronization only jumps to the point of time in the Trace Window or Graphics Window if the message is in the buffer of the window. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] requestAcl


# Request Address Claiming

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » Request Address Claiming

With the button in the Request Address Claim section, a diagnostic request for the Address Claiming Parameter Group (EE00h) can be sent. With the context menu of the button a request on the selected channel or all channels can be selected.

All nodes which responds to the request are added to the list.

The button [] activates the window synchronization and sets the marker to the moment of the last Address Claim Parameter Group (EE00h).

|  | Note The window synchronization only jumps to the point of time in the Trace Window or Graphics Window if the message is in the buffer of the window. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] requestPG


# Request Parameter Group

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » Request Parameter Group

In this section any Parameter Group can be requested (EA00h) from the selected node. The response is shown in the table.

In the context menu of the button [Request PG] the kind of request can be selected:

- specific request with the destination address of the selected node

- global request

- specific Request2 Parameter Group (C900h)

- global Request2 Parameter Group (C900h)

The button [] activates the window synchronization and sets the marker to the response of the request (EA00h).

|  | Note The window synchronization only jumps to the point of time in the Trace Window or Graphics Window if the message is in the buffer of the window. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] setDeviceName


# Set Device Name

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » Set Device Name

In this section the J1939 device name can be changed using the NAME-Management (NM) Parameter Group (9300h) defined in J1939-81.

In the column Value you can set the new values for the name segments. Then confirm the values with [Change Name]. The scanner then sends a Set-Pending-Name message to the node.

If the node answers with a Set-Pending-Name-ACK, the scanner sends an Adopt-Pending-Name message to initiate the node to process an Address-Claim (ACL) with the new name. If the node answers with a Set-Pending-Name-NACK, the scanner displays the reported error. Timeouts are displayed too.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/sections] workingSet


# Working Set

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Sections » Working Set

This section shows, if the node is part of a Working Set and which other nodes belong to the same Working Set. The J1939 Scanner evaluates the Working Set Master (FE0Dh) and Working Set Member (FE0Ch) Parameter Group.

The button [Request Working Set] sends a request for the Working Set Master Parameter Group.

The button [] activates the window synchronization and sets the marker to receive of the Working Set Master Parameter Group (FE0Dh).

|  | Note The window synchronization only jumps to the point of time in the Trace Window or Graphics Window if the message is in the buffer of the window. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/tabs] network


# Network

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Pages » Network

| Systems | Nodes | Network | Configuration |  |

The nodes are ordered by channel. If the nodes are active, their states are displayed with different arrows. If they are inactive, no arrows are displayed and the nodes are framed with different colors.

## Display With Arrow

The node is active on the bus and has a valid node address.

| Arrow | In the last second the node has sent ... |
| --- | --- |
|  | nothing |
|  | less than 10 messages |
|  | between 10 and 19 messages |
|  | 20 or more messages |

## Display Without Arrow

The node has the null address and does not participate on the bus.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/tabs] nodes


# Nodes

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Pages » Nodes

| Systems | Nodes | Network | Configuration |  |

This view shows the nodes in a list and provides the following information:

| Column | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| Name | name of the J1939 node |
| Address | address of the J1939 node |
| Channel | channel on which the node sends and receives messages |
| First Change | time stamp on which the node appears on the bus for the first time This time stamp is recognized by any message with a valid address. If that message is an ACL message, the device name of the node is displayed in the column Device Name. |
| Last Change | time stamp on which the address of the node has changed for the last time This time stamp is recognized by an ACL message of the node. |
| Device Name | internal device name of the J1939 node |

|  | Cross Reference Further information for naming and displaying nodes in J1993 Scanner you find here: Page Systems. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/tabs] settings


# Configuration

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Pages » Configuration

| Systems | Nodes | Network | Configuration |  |

The address of the scanner can be modified on this page. Request messages, which are sent from the J1939 Scanner are sent with this source address.

Furthermore the usage of the Address Claiming can be activated. If this setting is active, the J1939 Scanner performs the Address Claiming procedure before sending a message. By default this is disabled. Additionally the J1939 device name must be specified. Open the appropriate dialog with the button [Device Name...] and enter the values that should be used for the device name.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/j1939scanner/tabs] systems


# Systems

J1939 » J1939 Scanner » Pages » Systems

| Systems | Nodes | Network | Configuration |  |

This view shows the nodes hierarchical, ordered by channel, industry group, and system. Next to the channel the number of active nodes are displayed, nodes without address are not considered.

The names of the nodes are formed as follows:

- If a node with fitting J1939 device name or address is defined in the database, this name is used, e.g. N1.

- If there are J1939 device names from several nodes (transmitted with the corresponding ACL messages) that fit to the same node definition of the database, the node name of the database gets a postfix <consecutive number>, e.g. N1, N1<2>, N1<3> etc. This is the case when one or more components of the J1939 device names are present as wildcards ("*").

- If an Address Claiming Parameter Group is received, the name is formed of parts of the J1939 device name, e.g. VT:Vector Cantech.

- Otherwise the label ECU is used together with the address, e.g. ECU 5.

The nodes can be sorted to the columns ECU and Address.

| Node symbol | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | The node has a valid address and the Address Claiming was executed. |
|  | The node has a valid address and the Address Claiming was executed. Additional information are available in section Information. |
|  | The node sends with an address without having executed the Address Claiming or no Address Claim message has been seen on the bus. |
|  | The node sends with an address

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/obdimmonitor] obdimmDiagMsg


# Diagnostic Messages

CANoe » J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor » Diagnostic Messages

| Diagnostic Message | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| DM10Non-Continuously Monitored Systems Test Identifiers Support | It reports the list of non-continuously monitored system tests supported by the ECU. This information is used in the command section. If the test type is set to Manufacturer Specific Test, the content of the combo-box in the column TID shows if the selected test is supported or not supported. |
| DM5Diagnostic Readiness 1 | It reports the number of Active DTCs the number of Previously Active DTCs the OBD compliance of the ECU (visible in the node view of the monitor) the support and state of some continuously and non-continuously monitored systems |
| DM21Diagnostic Readiness 2 | It reports the distance traveled while MIL is activated the distance since DTCs were cleared the minutes run by engine while MIL is activated the time since DTCs were cleared These information have to be used in relation with those from DM5. The response to request for DM21 is visible in Trace Window. |
| DM26Diagnostic Readiness 3 | It conveys the pending status of OBD system monitors for the current drive cycle. This is useful in determining whether the OBD system has a defect or not. This DM plus the DM5 and DM21 formulate the OBD system readiness. The response to request for DM26 is visible in Trace Window. |

|  | Cross Reference For detailed information about the discussed diagnostic messages, see SAE Stan

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/obdimmonitor] obdimmOverview


# J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor

CANoe » J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor

Ribbon|Diagnostics & XCP Tab|J1939|OBD-I/M Monitor|View / New

| General |
| --- |
|  | The J1939 On-Board Diagnostic Inspection and Maintenance Monitor (short: J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor) allows in the Measurement Setup to configure diagnostic messages of type DM7 and send them to ECUs in the network. The DM7 is interpreted by the ECUs as a test command. Depending on the parameters defined in the DM7, the ECU receiving the command executes one or more tests and send back the test results with diagnostic messages of type DM8 or DM30. |
| Structure | Diagnostic Messages |
| The J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor has two pages: Test to configure and send test commands Configuration to configure the functionality of the monitor | Furthermore the OBD-I/M Monitor is capable of sending some request for the following diagnostic messages: DM5, DM10, DM21, and DM26. Those DMs return particular information relative to the test capability of the ECUs under test. The requests can be addressed globally or destination specific. The table on page Diagnostic Messages shows the purpose of each diagnostic message. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/obdimmonitor/sections] command


# Command

J1939 » J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor » Section » Command

This section shows all defined test commands. You can add, configure and send test commands via DM7 messages. If the test commands are not longer used you can delete them.

If you have selected an ECU in the monitor section you will only see the test commands that are specifically defined for this ECU. You can select several ECUS by holding the key <Ctrl> pressed and select the ECUs. In this case you will see the test commands of these ECUs. If you don't select any of the ECUs you will see all test commands that are defined.

The following table shows the available information about test commands:

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | name of the command |
| Address | destination address of the command |
| CAN | channel of the receiver ECU |
| Test Type | additional parameter that helps you to configure a defined type of command |
| TID | Test Identifier identifies the type of test that is to be processed by the ECU |
| SPN | Suspect Parameter Number If the test is of type Manufacturer Specific this value is not configurable. The default value of 0x7FFFF is used. |
| FMI | Failure Mode Identifier If the test is of type Manufacturer Specific this value is not configurable. The default value of 0x1F is used. |

|  | Note The columns Name and CAN are hidden per default. With a click with the right mouse button and the opening context menu you can show these two columns as well as hide and show each of the 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/obdimmonitor/sections] monitor


# Monitor

J1939 » J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor » Section » Monitor

In this section all ECUs are displayed that send active on the bus. The information about each ECU is shown in the table for which the following columns are available:

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | name of the ECU as it is defined as source address in the database |
| Address | address with which the ECU sends its messages |
| CAN | channel on which the ECU sends its messages |
| OBD Compliance | level of the compliance of the ECU with the OBD standard This information is sent back in response to a request for DM5. |
| MIL | Malfunction Indicator Lamp The lamp is controlled by the information provided by the cyclic DM1. It informs if an error occurred. |

|  | Note You can sort all columns ascending and descending. |

With a click on the right mouse button you can open a context menu. There you can choose if diagnostic messages should be sent and which diagnostic messages. The following diagnostic messages are available to send: DM5, DM10, DM21 and DM26.

If you have selected a single ECU the request is valid for this ECU and sent to a specific destination address. If you don't have selected any ECU the request is sent globally.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/obdimmonitor/sections] result


# Result

J1939 » J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor » Section » Result

In this section all diagnostic messages of type DM8 and DM30 are displayed that have been sent on the bus. If you have selected an ECU in the monitor section only the test results of this ECU are displayed. If you don't have any ECU selected, then all test results are displayed.

|  | Note On measurement start all results of previously executed tests are deleted. |

The tabular view shows the following information:

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Time | Rx time of the message |
| PG | Parameter Group, DM8 or DM30 |
| Src | source address of the message |
| Dest | destination address of the message |
| TID | Test Identifier identifies the kind of test that has been executed by the ECU |
| CID | differentiates between several components that use the same TID to trigger tests |
| SPN | identifies the system that has been tested, does not exist for DM8 |
| FMI | identifies the type of failure that has been testes, does not exist for DM8 |
| Verdict | shows the result of the executed test : Test passed : Test failed : Test was not completed or could not be performed |
| Value | result value of the test |
| Minimum | minimum limit of the result value |
| Maximum | maximum limit of the result value |
| Unit | unit of the result value |

The button bar on the right side offers the following functions:

| Button | Command |
| --- | --- |
|  | synchronizes windows and jump to diagnostic message Parameter Group |
|  

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/blocks/obdimmonitor/tabs] obdimmTabSettings


# Configuration

J1939 » J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor » Configuration

| Configuration | Test |  |

On page Configuration of the J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor you can configure the functionality of the J1939 OBD-I/M monitor.

If you activate the check box Use Address Claiming procedure the monitor will send an ACL message before trying to send the first message on the bus. If the Address Claiming fails the monitor will not be able to send any messages anymore. In this case you have to restart the measurement.

Additionally you can set the address and after a click on button [Device Name...] the J1939 device name with which the monitor will send the ACL message.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/blocks/obdimmonitor/tabs] obdimmTabTest


# Test

J1939 » J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor » Test

| Configuration | Test |  |

On page Test of the J1939 OBD-I/M Monitor you can configure and send test commands. The test results are displayed on this page too. To represent the information more clearly the page is divided in several sections:

- Monitor Section

- Command Section

- Result Section

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/ComplianceChecks] a10overview


# Network Management Test

J1939 » J1939 Test Service Library » Checks » J1939-82 Compliance Test (2008) » Network Management Test

### Reference Table in the SAE Specification: A10

| Row | Test Name | Test Flow | Limitations |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | ECU NAME | The test node sends a requests for the "Address Claimed" message to the DUT. The DUT has to send an "Address Claimed" message whose data matches with the data configured in the database. | — |
| 2 | System Initialization | The user is requested to execute a "power-up system initialization" process with the DUT. The request dialog is shown 60 seconds and is closed automatically if the user doesn't close it. An "Address Claimed" message to the global address is expected within the next 120 second.This is checked with the occurence_count check. | — |
| 3 | Non-Configurable Address ECU | The test node sends an "Address Claimed" message from the address of the DUT. The NAME of this message has a higher priority as the NAME of the DUT defined in the database. If the DUT supports the dynamic address handling, an "Address Claimed" message with another address is expected from the DUT. If it doesn't support the dynamic address handling, a "Cannot Claim" message is expected from the DUT. During the test the network management is monitored with the J1939 ACL Violation check. | This test is generated for nodes only that have the "Commanded Address" message in its Rx list. |
| 4 | Commanded Address | The test node sends 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/ComplianceChecks] a3overview


# Data Link Transmit Test - General

J1939 » J1939 Test Service Library » Checks » J1939-82 Compliance Test (2008) » Data Link Transmit Test - General

## Reference Table in the SAE Specification: A3

| Row | Test Name | Test Flow | Limitations |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Position and order of PGN in the 29-bit Frame Header(DUT Device under Test as Source) | The test waits 10000 ms to analyze all messages from the DUT. All messages on the Tx list are requested to stimulate the DUT. During the observation time, all messages of the DUT run through the J1939 check j1939_messages_known in the "pgn" context. The check looks up all messages in the Tx list of the database node that have the same source address and priority as a received PG from the DUT. The check fails if the PGN part of the CAN identifier of the received PG does not match any database message. | — |
| 2 | Use of EDP Bit to identify PGN(DUT as Source) | The test waits 10000 ms to analyze all messages from the DUT. All messages on the Tx list are requested to stimulate the DUT. During the observation time, all messages of the DUT run through the J1939 check j1939_messages_known in the "edp" context. The check ensures that all messages sent by the DUT have the EDP bit set to "0". | — |
| 3 | Use of DP Bit to identify PGN(DUT as Source) | The test waits 10000 ms to analyze all messages from the DUT. To stimulate the DUT all messages on the Tx list are requested. During the observation time, all messages of the D

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/ComplianceChecks] a4overview


# Data Link Receive Test - General

J1939 » J1939 Test Service Library » Checks » J1939-82 Compliance Test (2008) » Data Link Receive Test - General

## Reference Table in the SAE Specification: A4

| Row | Test Name | Test Flow | Limitations |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Standard Frame Message Tolerance | The test sends five CAN messages with 11-bit identifiers. Afterwards, the test waits 5000 ms. During this entire time the data traffic on the bus is monitored for Error Frames. In addition, a check is made of whether the cycle times for periodic messages are maintained. A tolerance of ±5% is permitted. | No check can be made of whether the DUT was not affected, as required in the SAE J1939-82 document. The lack of Error Frames on the bus as well as any existing periodic messages are insufficient to indicate that the DUT's operation was unaffected. |
| 2 | Device not a CAN 2.0A Device | The test sends two CAN messages (Request message and Address Claimed message) with 29-bit identifiers. Afterwards, the test waits 5000 ms. During the entire time period the bus traffic is monitored for Error Frames. | — |
| 3 | Independence of Priority Bits in PGN Receive(DUT Device under Test as Recipient) | The test sends eight diagnostic requests with different priorities and waits for arrival of the requested messages or an associated NACK message. FECAh with priority 3 FDD4h with priority 1 FED3h with priority 4 FDB5h with priority 7 FDB6h with priority 0 FF00h with priority 2 FF01h

### [J1939/ComplianceChecks] a5overview


# Data Link Transmit Test - TP BAM

J1939 » J1939 Test Service Library » Checks » J1939-82 Compliance Test (2008) » Data Link Transmit Test - TP BAM

### Reference Table in the SAE Specification: A5

| Row | Test Name | Test Flow | Limitations |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | BAM Protocol: BAM is valid (content and format)(DUT Device under Test as Originator) | For all messages in the DUT's Tx list that have a DLC>8, the test sends a diagnostic request via the j1939_request_response test pattern. A response is expected in the form of the requested message or an ACK message. It is acceptable too to receive no because a NACK message mustn't be sent after a global request. During run-time, the node under test is monitored by the j1939_BAM_violation check in the "inconsistentBAM" context. The parameters min and max are set to 0 and 60000. Therefore, a violation of the timeout is essentially untested. | — |
| 2 | BAM Protocol: BAM is sent before data packets(DUT as Originator) | For all messages in the DUT's Tx list that have a DLC>8, the test node sends a diagnostic request via the j1939_request_response test pattern. A response is expected in the form of the requested message or an ACK message. It is acceptable too to receive no because a NACK message mustn't be sent after a global request. During run-time, the node under test is monitored by the j1939_BAM_violation check in the "sequenceNumber" and "timeouts" contexts. The parameters min and max are set to 50 and 200. | — |
|

The DUT has to answer by TPCM.Abort message. The test waits for 1300 ms for timeout of the connection. | The test is only generated if the DUT has TPCM and TPDT messages in its Rx list of the database. |

# J1939-82 Compliance Test (2008)

The tests listed here cover test cases of the J1939 Compliance specification. For more information, see the specification. The table and line numbers given here match those of the specification.

| Reference Table in the SAE Specification (2008) | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Table 3 (A3) | Table 3 contains principally test cases that analyze the basic sending behavior of the test object. The prerequisite for generation of individual test cases is often the presence of messages in the Tx list of the test object that have attribute GenMsgRequestable = true and are not system messages.This is because the test object must be able to send messages in order to test the sending behavior, e.g., the correct value in the EDP bit. |
| Table 4 (A4) | Table 4 contains principally test cases that analyze the basic receiving behavior of the test object. There are various prerequisites for generation of these test cases. In particular, various tests of the response of the test object to a Request message are generated if for PDU1 and PDU2 messages there is both a single frame and multipacket message with attribute GenMsgRequestable = true present in the Tx list of the test object and these are

### [J1939/ComplianceChecks] compliancetestGlossary


# Glossary: Compliance Test

J1939 » J1939 Test Service Library » Checks » J1939-82 Compliance Tests (2008) » Glossary

| Term | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Cyclical messages | Cyclical messages have the attribute GenMsgSendType = "cyclic" in the underlying DBC file. |
| Proprietary messages | Proprietary messages have the parameter groups 0xEF00, 0xFF00 - 0xFFFF. |
| System messages | System messages are parameter groups that are generally not sent or received by the application of the test object without further context. For example, the TPDT message (0xEB00) serves only as a container for the transported message. The application of the test object tends not to send or receive this message without the context of a transport protocol transmission. These messages are explicitly for the J1939-82 Compliance Test: Acknowledge (0xE800) Request (0xEA00) Request2 (0xC900) Transfer (0xCA00) AddressClaimed (0xEE00) TPCM (0xEC00) TPDT (0xEB00) ETPCM (0xC800) ETPDT (0xC700) CommandedAddress (0xFED8) | Acknowledge | (0xE800) | Request | (0xEA00) | Request2 | (0xC900) | Transfer | (0xCA00) | AddressClaimed | (0xEE00) | TPCM | (0xEC00) | TPDT | (0xEB00) | ETPCM | (0xC800) | ETPDT | (0xC700) | CommandedAddress | (0xFED8) |
| Acknowledge | (0xE800) |
| Request | (0xEA00) |
| Request2 | (0xC900) |
| Transfer | (0xCA00) |
| AddressClaimed | (0xEE00) |
| TPCM | (0xEC00) |
| TPDT | (0xEB00) |
| ETPCM | (0xC800) |
| ETPDT | (0xC700) |
| CommandedAddress | (0xFED8) |

| Version 15© Vector Info

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/ComplianceChecks2015] compliancetest2015


# J1939-82 Compliance Test (2015)

J1939 » J1939 Test Service Library » Checks » J1939-82 Compliance Test (2015)

|  | Multimedia Link This online video tutorial will show you how you can generate a J1939-82 (2015) compliance test module. You will see a step by step sample which is assisted by text explanations for further information to the steps. |

The tests serve to cover test cases from the J1939 Compliance Specification (2015).For further information, please refer to the specification.

With the new requirements and test cases since 2015, the technology and strategy of the test have been changed.

## Advantages

## Configuration

With the Compliance Test (2015) a test unit is created via vTestStudio that was programmed with CAPL. It depicts the content of the Compliance Tests 2015. The J1939.CT_Configurator panel serves to configure the data to be used by the test, for example, which messages support the DUT.

With the Compliance Test (2008) the tests were generated via the CANdb++ Editor for a DUT (Device Under Test) in order to be performed during the measurement via a test module.

## Test Results

The error description in the test reports was extended to simplify the interpretation of the test results. The HTML report has a detailed description of individual test cases and provides links to them.

## Test Strategies

|  | Note On failure, especially on inconclusion, there is no need to diagnose a wrong behavior of the DUT too early. Try to repeat the test isolated 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/gnssNL] gnssNLDbAttributes


# Database Attributes

J1939 » GNSS Node Layer » Database Attributes

In the database used by the node the following node attributes have to be defined. The values of these attributes are used while sending the message GNSSPositionData (PGN 129029).

| Attribute Name | Data Type | Object Type | Default Value | Minimum Value | Maximum Value |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GNSSPosData_SequenceID | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 1 |
| GNSSPosData_TypeOfSystem | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 15 |
| GNSSPosData_MethodGNSS | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 15 |
| GNSSPosData_GNSSIntegrity | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 3 |
| GNSSPosData_NumberOfSVs | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 252 |
| GNSSPosData_HDOP | Float | Node | 0 | -327.64 | 327.64 |
| GNSSPosData_PDOP | Float | Node | 0 | -327.64 | 327.64 |
| GNSSPosData_GeodialSeparation | Float | Node | 0 | -21470000 | 21470000 |
| GNSSPosData_NumOfRefStations | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 252 |
| Dependent on the number of reference stations (equivalent to the value of the attribute GNSSPosData_NumOfRefStations), further attributes are to be defined for every reference station. Besides the placeholder N is replaced by the number of the reference station (starting with 1). |
| GNSSPosData_RefStationTypeN | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 15 |
| GNSSPosData_RefStationIDN | Integer | Node | 0 | 0 | 4096 |
| GNSSPosData_AgeOfDGNSSRefStatN | Float | Node | 0 | 0 | 655.32 |

For Parameter Group COG & SOG, Rapid Update the following node attributes are necessary in t

### [J1939/gnssNL] gnssNLOverview


# GNSS Node Layer

J1939 » GNSS Node Layer

## Function

The GNSS Node Layer (J1939) (Global Navigation Satellite System) is used to simulate a GNSS receiver. These virtual receivers can be used to supply other devices on the network with position data. GNSS positions are transferred cyclically with configurable messages.

## Setting up the Node Layer

A node layer can be assigned to each node in the simulation structure of CANoe. CAPL functions can be used to obtain access to node layer.

For a node of GNSS to be able to use the node layer, a few settings must be made in the simulation structure and database:

- A node attribute must be inserted in the database with the name NodeLayerModules and with type String. A node must then be inserted in the same database. The attribute NodeLayerModules must be set to GNSS_NL.DLL for this node. If a number of node layers will be used by a single node, the names of the individual ones must be separated by commas.

- A node must be inserted in the simulation structure. The node from the database must be assigned in the configuration dialog.

You can check which node layers are being used by a node in the configuration dialog under Module.

## Using the Node Layer in a CAPL Program

The GNSSStartUp function can be used to set up a node in the network. From this time on, settings can be made, waypoints can be added and the speed and course of the GNSS receiver can be determined.

The GNSSStartSimulation function is used to start the simu

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/gnssNL] gnssNLUnits


# Measurement Units

J1939 » GNSS Node Layer » Measurement Units

The GNSSSetUnits function can be used to specify the system of units of measure for GNSS node layer. Three different systems can be used, metric, english and nautical. When the node is set up, the metric system for units of measures is set.

The following table returns the units of the possible function parameters:

| System | Metric | English | Nautic |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Absolute altitude measurement | Meters | Feet | Feet |
| Lengths and altitudes | Meters | Yards | Yards |
| Jitter | Meters | Yards | Yards |
| Speed | Kilometers/hour | Miles/hour | Knots |
| Latitude and longitude | Degrees | Degrees | Degrees |
| Course and gradient | Degrees | Degrees | Degrees |

|  | Note The unit of measure for altitude measures of a GNSS protocol file is always meters. |

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [J1939/j1939basics] defineCommRelations


# Define Communication Relationships

CANoe » J1939 » Define a J1939 Database » Define Communication Relationships

The setup of communication relationships is absolutely necessary for the definition of a network and the code generation. Here it is specified which messages are sent and received by the nodes on the network.

The content of a Parameter Group is identified with the Parameter Group Number (PGN). On a J1939 network it is possible that a Parameter Group is sent the same PGN by different nodes. These Parameter Groups distinguish themselves in their transmitter addresses and can be assigned to a send node using these.

Furthermore, it is possible to specify whether a Parameter Group is only received if it was sent by a particular node or whether a Parameter Group is accepted regardless of the sender.

Which of these possibilities is used depends on the particular application.

With the assignment of messages to a node, the selection and display of Parameter Groups in CANoe/CANalyzer is simplified and it is clearer.

## Assigning send Parameter Groups

If a Parameter Group should be sent by a node, the Parameter Group is inserted into the list of Tx messages. During the assignment of send Parameter Groups, two possibilities can be distinguished:

- The sender address of the message is defined: A Parameter Group with defined sender address can only be sent by the node with the corresponding sender address.

- The sender address of the message is not defined: The Param

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/j1939basics] defineJ1939DB


# Define a J1939 Database

CANoe » J1939 »Define a J1939 Database

For the definition of a J1939 network, several particularities must be considered, e.g. does the CAN identifier contain J1939 addresses that do not exist with pure CAN. Thus there are more configuration possibilities that must be taken into account for a J1939 network.

Various programs in the Vector tool chain use a J1939 database in special ways. To be able to use a database with J1939, the value J1939 must be assigned to the network attribute ProtocolType. This attribute is already defined in the included database J1939.dbc. If a database is created new, the attribute must be defined as described in the following table:

| Attribute definition | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Attribute type | Network |
| Name | ProtocolType |
| Type | String |
| Value | J1939 |

In a J1939 database, additional settings can be made for messages, signals, and nodes. These are described in more detail in the following chapters:

- Define a J1939 Parameter Group

- Define a J1939 Signal

- Define a J1939 Node

- Define Communication Relationships

- Overview of the J1939 Database Attributes

- Define Caterpillar Parameters

| Attribute Definition | Value |
| Attribute Type | Signal |
| Name | CAT_PID |
| Type | Hex |
| Value | PID |

You do not need to map a Caterpillar parameter signal to a CAN message (J1939 Parameter Group). This is done by CANoe.J1939/CANalyzer.J1939 if an appropriate configuration message is received.

Currently Caterpillar parameters are supported in Trace Window, Data Window, Graphics Window and in the State Tracker. In the Data and Graphics Window you can specify the address of the sender and/or receiver of the parameter.

### [J1939/j1939basics] defineJ1939PG


# Define a J1939 Parameter Group

CANoe » J1939 » Define a J1939 Database » Define a J1939 Parameter Group

A J1939 Parameter Group is essentially defined the same way as a normal CAN message. However, there are three particularities to heed:

- The message type of a J1939 Parameter Group is "J1939 PG"

- The DLC of a J1939 Parameter Group can be larger than 8 bytes. A J1939 Parameter Group with a DLC greater than 8 must be transmitted with a suitable transport protocol.

- A J1939 Parameter Group has an extended CAN identifier that must correspond to the J1939 identifier format.

The signals can be assigned to the message as usual. However, signal attributes can also be defined . If the DLC is greater than 8, the signals can also begin at a bit position that is greater than 64.

For J1939 messages whose length is variable, the DLC should be set to the maximum possible value.

The PGN of the Parameter Group must be defined. In the CANdb, the data page to the PGN is counted in, so that the PGN includes 18 bits. In addition it is also necessary to specify the priority of the message. The priority consists of 3 bits.

The transmitter address of a Parameter Group does not absolutely have to be defined. It is possible to use the null address (FEh) here. '*' is then displayed for the address. Thus you can see that the transmitter address of the Parameter Group is not defined. For specific Parameter Groups (PDU Format 1) the same applies for the recipient address. For many applicat

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/j1939basics] defineJ1939Signal


# Define a J1939 Signal

CANoe » J1939 » Define a J1939 Database » Define a J1939 Signal

For J1939 signal values are only valid within a defined value range. Particular values are reserved and identify an invalid or not available signal.

For J1939 signals, there are two additional signal attributes available:

## Signal Attribute SigType

The valid value range and the interpretation of the signal value is specified with the attribute SigType. By using SigType it is possible to display only valid signal values in the Graphics Window. If the attribute is not defined the entire value range is valid.

The attribute is defined as follows:

| Attribute Definition | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Attribute type | Signal |
| Name | SigType |
| Type | Enum |
| Value | DefaultRangeRangeSignedASCIIDiscreteControlReferencePGNDTCStringDelimiterStringLengthStringLengthCtrlMessageCounterMessageChecksum |

The order of the enumeration values must not be changed. The values have the following value range and interpretation:

- Default: normal signal, no protocol-specific interpretation

- Range

Signals with the attribute value Range are interpreted according to SAE J1939-71. The available value range is restricted due to coding additional information. The attribute value can only be used for signals of value type Unsigned.

|  | 1 Byte | 2 Byte | 4 Byte |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| valid signal | 0..FAh | 0..FAFFh | 0..FAFFFFFFh |
| parameter specific | FBh | FB00h..FBFFh | FB000000h..FBFFFFFFh

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [J1939/j1939basics] j1939Basics


# J1939 for CANoe/CANalyzer | The J1939 Protocol

CANoe » J1939 » J1939 for CANoe/CANalyzer » J1939 Protocol

J1939 is a CAN-based layer 7 protocol that has its origin in the diesel control of commercial vehicles. Among the typical properties of J1939 are:

- based on 29-bit CAN identifier

- point-to-point and broadcast communication

- no limitation of the data length to 8 bytes - with transport protocols, a transmission of up to 1785 bytes is possible

- network management for the management of node addresses and device names

- definition of the Parameter Group (PG) as data bundle

- definition of the Parameter Group number (PGN) as identification characteristic

- autonomous priority assignment regardless of the PGN although based on the CAN identifier.

Usage of the J1939 Protocol

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |
