# Installation: OptoLyzer (MOST50)

MOST » Network Interfaces » Installation OptoLyzer (MOST50)

Install the OptoLyzer G2 3050e or OptoLyzer G2 3050e production by following the directions in the OptoLyzer user manual.

|  | Notes Note that a license for the Vector OptoLyzer Integration Package (OIP) is required, either on Vector hardware or a USB dongle, or as a license key. The OptoLyzer G2 is addressed on the following Ethernet ports. If you are using a firewall, it must not block these ports. Spy: 27998 Node: 27999 The computer automatically assigns the ports. If you want to use specific ports, you need to adapt the CAN.INI file. The following code is valid for the OptoLyzer G2 3050e (MOST50) on channel 1: [OptoLyzerG2_50_1]MyPortNode= MyPortSpy= Using TCP/IP improvement software (e.g. Vodafone Mobile Client) can lead to data losses during communication between the OptoLyzer G2 and the computer. If necessary please remove this software. |

## Sync line for spy channel time stamps

If you want to synchronize the OptoLyzer time stamps of the spy channel with the time stamps of any Vector hardware interfaces that are connected in parallel, you will need a sync line. It connects the Trigger Extension Box of the OptoLyzer with the sync input of the Vector hardware.

OptoLyzer OL3050eVector Sync +-----+ +-----+ | 9 7 5 3 1 | GND (brown) o----o GND (Pin7) | [] [] [] [] [] |Signal (green) o----o Trigger Input (Pin8) | [] [] [] [] [] | | 10 8 6 4 2 | +----------------+ T

### [MOST/MOST_Hardware] MOST_Installation_VN2600


# VN2640 MOST Interface Installation

MOST » Network Interfaces » VN2640 Installation

### Requirements

- VN2640

- External power supply unit (included in delivery)

- Vector Driver Disk with driver for VN2640

- Free USB port (USB 2.0 or - with limitations on performance/throughput - USB 1.1)

- Windows 7 (32/64 bit)

- Administrator rights

|  | Note In Windows 7 it is not possible to install the drivers from a network drive. If you got your update from the Vector product page in the Internet, please copy the files to your local hard drive. |

|  | Step by Step Procedure Install your drives before connecting the hardware. If you have already connected your hardware, close the Hardware Assistant with [Cancel]. Insert the delivered driver CD in your computer.The installation setup will be started automatically. If the installation is not started automatically please start the program by your own. You can find the installation program here: \Drivers\32_Bit\setup.EXE (for Windows 7 32 Bit) \Drivers\64_Bit\setup.EXE (for Windows 7 64 Bit). Select on the driver page the hardware for the driver installation. Start the installation with [Install]. After successful installation close the setup with [Close]. Connect your hardware with the computer. |

|  | Cross Reference See further information in the VN2640 manual. |

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept] most_fuction-blocks_database-interface


# Database Interface of the Function Blocks

MOST » Simulation Concept » Database Interface of the Function Blocks

For proper functionality of the Application Socket in CANoe it is helpful, if the system's function blocks are described as network nodes in a CANdb database.

## Node attribute MostApFBlockID:

The function block's FBlockID is defined by the node attribute MostApFBlockID. At the measurement start the node is automatically registered with this ID in the Local FBlockList.

## Node attribute MostApInstIDDefault:

The node attribute MostApInstIDDefault indicates which InstID is preferred by the function block for registration. If a device with an identical functional address is already registered in the local FBlockList then the InstID is incremented.

|  | Notes Function blocks in the Simulation Setup must be assigned to network nodes of the database to allow the Application Socket to evaluate node attributes. The necessary node attributes are already defined in the CANdb database template MostTemplate.dbc. Automatic registration of a function block at the measurement start can be inhibited by calling the function MostApUnregister() in on prestart. For example this may be done to simulate delayed readiness of a function block. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept] most_simulation-concept


# Simulation Concept

MOST » Simulation Concept

| General |
| --- |
| For the simulation of one MOST device in CANoe typically one MOST channel is used. The main window of the Simulation Setup exactly shows one MOST device with its function blocks (CAPL nodes). The function blocks communicate via the Application Socket with other function blocks. The Application Socket and further services are represented by the "device" symbol in the main window of the Simulation Setup. Depending on the configuration of the Application Socket, CANoe provides further function blocks (NetBlock, MOST NetworkMaster, MOST PowerMaster). The system view of the Simulation Setup gives an overview of all simulated MOST devices and other networks (CAN, LIN) respectively. Notes Unlike real bus networks (cp. CAN), a node in the main window of the MOST Simulation Setup represents only a part (e.g. a function block) of a device (ECU). When deactivating a node only this part becomes inactive. If a device shall completely be removed from the simulation, it can be deactivated in the context menu of the device symbol in the main window. The Application Socket in CANoe only processes AMS messages, i.e. CAPL message variables of type mostAMSMessage. |  | Notes Unlike real bus networks (cp. CAN), a node in the main window of the MOST Simulation Setup represents only a part (e.g. a function block) of a device (ECU). When deactivating a node only this part becomes inactive. If a device shall completely be removed

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept] most_simulation_device_multiple


# Simulation of Multiple Devices

MOST » Simulation Concept » Simulation of multiple devices

CANoe/DENoe can simulate up to 16 MOST devices simultaneously.

A precondition is the definition of the devices in the System view of the Simulation Setup in accordance with section Simulation of a Device.

In the real ring for this various MOST hardware interfaces are needed. In pure simulation mode a system can be modeled in the virtual MOST ring without hardware interfaces.

|  | Notes When simulating multiple devices it has proven to be advantageous to describe all of the system's network nodes (function blocks) in a database and to allocate them to all MOST channels in the Simulation Setup. A function block can then be exchanged between devices by moving its associated CAPL node to another bus. The sample CANoe configuration for the Application Socket shows a simulation with multiple devices. On application level more than one device can be simulated per MOST Interface, by attaching the convenient function blocks of additional devices to the MOST Interface in the Simulation Setup. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept] MOST_Simulation_Device_Single


# Simulation of a Device

MOST » Simulation concept » Simulation of a Device (MOST Simulation Concept)

The following sections describe the procedure for simulating a MOST device in CANoe with the help of the Application Socket.The sample configuration for the MOST Application Socket that is installed with CANoe can serve as an example.

|  | Creating the device in the Simulation Setup Open the dialog for entering MOST devices in the system view of the Simulation Setup. (Select Add... from the Network root element context menu.) In this dialog you can specify a name for the new MOST device. Select MOST as the bus system. When you close the dialog, the new device will be added to the MOST Devices in the system view. Allocate a MOST channel.(Select Add... from the Channels sub-tree context menu.) Activate the Application Socket for the allocated MOST channel (see MOST Application Socket configuration) and specify the services and supplementary function blocks to be provided by CANoe for the device. |

|  | System definition The MOST system can be described in a CANdb database.CANoe evaluates this system description to simulate the Application Socket. Open the CANdb++ Editor Create a new database for the MOST bus system.Use the MostTemplate.dbc template for this purpose. Define the network nodes in the CANdb database.Each network node is a function block (e.g., Telephone, Display). Define the FBlockIDs for the function blocks, each with the node attribute "MostApFBlockID". Save

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept] most_troubleshooting


# Troubleshooting

MOST » Simulation Concept » Troubleshooting

## MostApRegister() registers no function block.

- Has an associated network node been defined in the CANdb database?

- Has the node attribute MostApFBlockID been defined as greater than 0?

- Does channel allocation agree with the CANdb database?

- Has the CAPL node been associated to the CANdb?

- Is the Application Socket active for the allocated channel?

## The Application Socket sends out unauthorized error messages 'FBlock not available'

- The Command Interpreter of the Application Socket sends out these error messages if the function block is addressed by a command but is not registered in the Local Registry. Utilize the CAPL function MostApRegister() or MostApRegisterEx() to register the function block.

Troubleshooting (MOST)

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] MOST_ApplicationSocket_Notification-Service


# Notification Service

MOST » Simulation Concept » Notification Service

The communication partners of a MOST function block can use the MOST notification service to stay informed about changes in the function block states (properties).

The integrated notification services is of great advantage in simulating a function block using CANoe.

The service in the CAPL program for the function block must be activated using the MostAsNtfEnable() or MostAsNtfEnableEx() commands. MostAsNtfFunctionEnable() or MostAsNtfFunctionEnableEx() can be used to define which function block functions are to be bound to the notification service.

CANoe maintains the notification matrix for these functions. CANoe processes and replies to all commands issued via Notification.Set/Get and NotificationCheck.Set/Get messages.

|  | Example This example demonstrates the use of the notification service The DeckStatus function (function ID = 0x200) for the notification service is activated in the CAPL program for the AudioDiskPlayer function block. on preStart{// Activate notification service for this FBlockMostAsNtfEnable();// Enable DeckStatus function and set the callback for status reportingMostAsNtfFunctionEnable(0x200, "SendStatus_DeckStatus");} A device with the account address 0x0102 registers as a notification client for the AudioDiskPlayer.DeckStatus by sending the command AudioDiskPlayer.Notification.Set(SetFunction, 0x0102, 0x200) to the AudioDiskPlayer CAPL function block. CANoe receives the 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] MOST_Bus_Registry


# Bus Registry

MOST » Simulation Concept » Bus Registry

The Bus Registry contains all function blocks of a MOST system and the assignments of function blocks to devices. This involves storing the functional address of the function block {FBlockID, InstID} in a table together with the logical device address (RxTxLog).

The MOST NetworkMaster ensures that the functional address {FBlockID, InstID} occurs only once in the total system.

The Application Socket in CANoe mirrors the Bus Registry during the transition to network status ConfigOk. To achieve this it sends the request NetworkMaster.CentralRegistry.Get to the MOST NetworkMaster and evaluates its status report.

|  | Note This mechanism utilizes the control channel on each status change to ConfigOK. For this reason, it is deactivated by default and must be activated in the hardware configuration, if required (Hardware ribbon tab|Network Hardware|Application Socket). Alternatively, you can scan only the parts of the registry that are required for the respective application. The shadow service can be used for this purpose. This option is typically used in real control units, as well. |

Read-Out of Registries (under Application Socket API)

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] MOST_Local-FBlockList


# Local FBlockList

MOST » Simulation Concept » Local FBlockList

The Local FBlockList contains the functional addresses {FBlockID, InstID} of all of the device's function blocks. It is ensured that the combination {FBlockID, InstID} occurs only once within the device.

CANoe/DENoe .MOST offers the following options for registering/unregistering function blocks in the Local FBlockList:

- The function block is automatically registered before the measurement start if a node in a CANdb database is assigned to the CAPL node and this database has defined the node attribute MostApFBlockID > 0.

- Blocks can be unregistered and reregistered with CANdb database reference using the CAPL functions MostApUnregister() and MostApRegister().

- Function blocks can be registered in the Local FBlockList without CANdb database reference using the CAPL functions MostApRegisterEx() and MostApUnregisterEx().

Changes to the Local FBlockList are propagated within the device by registry events (OnMostAsRegistry() or OnMostApInstID()).

Read-Out of Registries (under Application Socket API)

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] MOST_Simulation_Concept_Application_Message_Service


# Application Message Service

MOST » Simulation Concept » Application Message Service

The Application Message Service (AMS) is based on the control message service (CMS) and used for transmitting messages, which user data size exceeds the maximum size of one control frame (12 bytes with MOST25). For transmitting and receiving AMS messages you have to activate it in the hardware configuration dialog.

MOST150: The user data may contain a maximum of 45 bytes, before it is segmented. For transmissions that exceed a length specified by the OEM, a message is initially sent with TellD=4 and the length information of the user data as a parameter. The recipient can use this to reserve the appropriate memory space.

## The AMS offers the following functionality:

- The send buffer has a default size of 1000 AMS messages. The size can be configured via CAN.INI - set the switch 'MaxTxMsgs' in section [MOSTAMS] to the desired size (e.g. 'MaxTxMsgs=200').

- Clearing the send buffer (CAPL function MostAmsClearTxQueue())

- Automatic clearing of the send buffer configurable via CAN.INI:

The automatic clearing can be activated in section [MOSTAMS] by setting the switch 'AutoClearTxQueue=1'. The send buffer is then cleared on one of the following events:

- SystemState ’NotOk’

- Critical Unlock

- Light off

- Automatic handling of segmentation errors. This can be activated cia CAN.INI in the section [MOSTAMS] by setting the switch 'HandleSegError=1'. In case the receiver of an AMS mess

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] MOST_Simulation_Concept_Function_Service


# Function Service

MOST » Simulation Concept » Function Service

The function service simplifies the creation of simulated function blocks. The service performs the following tasks:

- Responds to the FBlock.FktIds.Get() request with FBlock.FktIds.Status(RLE-coded list with function IDs)

- Sends error messages if requests are made to function-block functions or operations, which have not been implemented

The service is enabled for the function block with MostAsFsEnable(). The MostAsFsFunctionEnable() command can then be used to define the functions and operations implemented by the function block.

MOST Simulation Concept: Command Interpreter | MOST Simulation Concept: Notification Service | MOST Application Socket API

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] MOST_Simulation_Concept_Notification_Shadow_Service


# Notification Shadow Service

MOST » Simulation Concept » Notification Shadow Service

The notification shadow service automatically registers the simulated device in the notification matrix of a function block. The MostAsNtfShdFunctionEnable(fblockId, instId, functionId) function specifies the "Property" type MOST function; if this is changed, the simulated device must be notified.

The service sends the FBlockId.InstId.Notification.Set(SetFunction, functionId) message as soon as the function block is known in the bus registry of the simulated device. If the network status changes from ConfigOK, the service registers the device for notification again in the next ConfigOk phase.

MOST Simulation Concept: Shadow Service | MOST Simulation Concept: Notification Service | MOST Application Socket API

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] MOST_Simulation_Concept_Shadow_Service


# Shadow Service

MOST » Simulation Concept » Shadow Service

The shadow service can be used to locate an appropriate function block in the network. Applications with controller functionality, which send command messages to a function block, obtain the logical address of the device containing the function block with the help of this service.

If a function block has been registered with MostAsShdEnable(FBlockId, InstId), the service sends a NetworkMaster.CentralRegistry.Get(FBlockId, InstId) request if the following events occur:

- The network status switches from ConfigNotOk to ConfigOk (see OnMostNetState()).

- The MOST NetworkMaster signals that the registered function block is newly present in its central registry (NetworkMaster.Configuration.Status(New, FBlockIdInstId List) message).

- The NetworkMaster.Configuration.Status(Ok) message arrives while the network status is ConfigOk. The MOST NetworkMaster thus signals that a major change has been made in its bus registry.

- MostAsShdEnable is called when the network status is ConfigOk and the device address of the function block is not yet known in the simulated device.

The MOST NetworkMaster's response message (NetworkMaster.CentralRegistry.Status(RxTxLog, FBlockId, InstId)) adds the new function block instance and its device address to the copy of the bus registry in the simulated device. This causes a message to be sent to the CAPL program indicating that the bus registry has been changed (see OnMostAsRegistry()).

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] option_most_address_handler_


# Address Handler

MOST » Simulation Concept » Address Handler

The Address Handler is responsible for send requests where the node address is not given. That is, when the destination node address of the send request is undefined (RxTxLog=0xFFFF) the Address Handler searches for the correct node address based on the functional address {FBlockID, InstID} from the Bus Registry.

## Please observe the following requirements for proper functionality of the Address Handler:

- The destination address (Selector DA of the message variable mostAMSMessage) for the send request is 0xFFFF (This is the default when creating a mostAMSmessage variable).

- The send request is a MOST command (OpType 0 - 8).

- The InstID in the send request is nonzero and is not equal to 0xFF.

- The FBlockID in the send request is is not equal to 0xFF.

- The MOST network finds itself in the status 'ConfigOk'.

For the case where the function block to be found is located in the same device as the sender, the message is sent internally to the device (not via the MOST ring). In this case source and destination addresses are identical at the receiver.

MOST Application Socket API

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] option_most_command_interpreter


# Command Interpreter

MOST » Simulation Concept » Command Interpreter

The Command Interpreter checks whether the functional address (FBlockID+InstID) of an incoming message is located in the device. If it could not be found, the Command Interpreter answers with a corresponding error message (Error Code: 0x01 FBlockID not available and 0x02 InstID not available respectively). This error message is only send, if the OpType of the received message is a MOST Command and is not broadcasted.

The Command Interpreter recognizes which function blocks are available based on the Local FBlockList.

MOST Application Socket API

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] option_most_netblock


# NetBlock

MOST » Simulation Concept » NetBlock

The following functions of the NetBlock function block are made possible by CANoe implementation of the Application Socket:

- 0x000 FBlockIDs

- 0x002 NodePositionAddress

- 0x003 NodeAddress

- 0x004 GroupAddress

- 0x005 AbilityToWake

- 0x007 RetryParameters

- 0x010 Version

- 0x008 Sampling Frequency

- 0x401 Notification

- 0x402 NotificationCheck

- 0xA03 Boundary

## Function: FBlockIDs

When polling for the device's function blocks (NetBlock.FBlockIDs.Get) the Local FBlockList is returned with the status report NetBlock.FBlockIDs.Status(FBlockIDList).

See Application Socket API - "Registering/ Unregistering of function blocks" with Local FBlockList

If a change to an InstID is initiated by the message NetBlock.FBlockIDs.SetGet(FBlockID, OldInstID, NewInstID) the Local FBlockList changes. The Application Socket then lets the MOST NetworkMaster know the new status by the message NetBlock.FBlockIDs.Status(FBlockIDList). Within the simulated device the change is propagated by a registry event (OnMostAsRegistry() or OnMostApInstID()).

All functions described in the following provide device-, manufacturer-, or version-dependent information. For this reason, the CANoe NetBlock does not implement these functions. Simply no response message FktID not available is sent. If a response with an error message is required, or the application behavior is to be simulated, you can implement these functions in CAPL, as in the exampl

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] Option_MOST_Network_Master


# NetworkMaster

MOST » Simulation Concept » MOST NetworkMaster

The MOST NetworkMaster is not part of the Application Socket. Nevertheless, CANoe provides for an implementation of a MOST NetworkMaster to permit fast setup of a MOST network.

The following properties apply to CANoe implementation of the MOST NetworkMaster:

- The MOST NetworkMaster function block can be activated on every device. This is possible with adding the NWM directly in the Simulation Setup or via the configuration page of the Application Socket in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog (Hardware ribbon tab|Network Hardware).

- Late registration or unregistration of a function block in the network results only in a complete system scan by the Network Master if an invalid registration was made. Otherwise the function block is integrated into or removed from the Central Registry

- The MOST NetworkMaster function block's functions SaveConfiguration and Boundary are not available.

- The number of attempts of the NWM to resolve a duplicated functional address can be defined in the CAN.INI:

[MOSTApplicationSocket]; Default: 20 attemptsNMAttemptsChangeInstID=20

## MOST150

- The Boundary function no longer exists.

- The SystemAvail (0xA10) function is new for MOST150; accordingly, no error message FktID not available is returned and the user can simulate the functionality in CAPL.

- If MOST150 is configured as a speed grade for the channel on which the NWM was placed, the CANoe implementation auto

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/Application_Socket] option_most_power_master


# PowerMaster

MOST » Simulation Concept » MOST PowerMaster

In the MOST network the MOST PowerMaster handles portions of Network Management. First and foremost it performs the steps for shutting down the MOST network (see MOST Specification 2.3 Section 3.2.4).

The .MOST option offers an implementation of the MOST PowerMaster which has the following properties:

- The MOST PowerMaster (FBlockID 0x04) can be activated for any device (see configuration of the Application Socket).

- Network shutdown is initiated either by the CAPL command MostPMShutDownStart() or by the MOST message NetBlock.ShutDown.Result(0x03: Temperature Shut Down).

- When performing a temperature shutdown, the "AbilityToWake" flag is set in each device in the ring (s. MOST Specification 2.3 chp. 3.2.6).

- MostPMShutDownCancel() stops an ongoing ShutDown procedure.

- For simulation of an over temperature behavior, there are the CAPL functions MostPMSetOverTemperature () and MostPMResetOverTemperature ()

MOST Application Socket API

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/FaultInjection] FaultInjection


# Fault Injection

MOST » Simulation Concept » Fault Injection

Fault injection allows you to change the MOST side behavior of simulated applications (CAPL node and node layer modules in the Simulation Setup).

The main component of the fault induction are the following two CAPL callbacks, which you must define in the CAPL program of the application and which only affect this node in the Simulation Setup.

- OnMostFiAmsPreReceive is called before the simulated application receives an AMS message.

- OnMostFiAmsPreSend is called before the simulated application sends an AMS message.

Within the callbacks, you can suppress messages or forward them as unaltered or altered messages. The callbacks contain the complete CAPL function block for use in defining complex scenarios for fault injection.

|  | Notes OnMostFiAmsPreReceive and OnMostFiAmsPreSend cannot be used in test modules. Fault injection should not be a component of normal implementation of a simulated MOST application. |

## Activation

You can activate the fault injection for each simulation channel in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog.

Only when injection is active are the OnMostFiAmsPreSend and OnMostFiAmsPreReceive procedures called.

## Application Examples

- You can send outgoing messages of a simulation node systematically to another address.

- You can use CAPL timers to send outgoing messages after a delay.

- You can ignore/manipulate incoming commands of a real controller without having to adapt t

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/FaultInjection] FaultInjectionApplicationSocket


# Application Socket

MOST » Simulation Concept » Fault Injection » Application Socket

Fault injection allows you to influence the services of the simulated MOST application socket. Because these CANoe-internal services are not integrated as CAPL nodes in the Simulation Setup, you must explicitly assign a CAPL program to them with the MostAsFiEnable command. This CAPL program receives the AMS messages in OnMostFiAmsPreReceive before the services of the application socket and can use OnMostFiAmsPreSend to alter outgoing messages.

|  | Note MostAsFiEnable is only active when the fault injection of the relevant channel is active (see Network HardwareConfiguration). |

Effect on the MOST NetworkMasters

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Simulation_Concept/FaultInjection] FaultInjectionNetworkMaster


# NetworkMaster

MOST » Simulation Concept » Fault Injection » NetworkMaster

You can use a set of CAPL functions to influence the behavior of the CANoe MOST NetworkMaster for test purposes, in order to confront real MOST control units with network management faults in a targeted manner.

For example, you can

- use MostNwmFiSetConfigState to set the Configuration status to ConfigOk or ConfigNotOk, irrespective of the actual network status.

- use MostNwmFiEnableRingScan to suppress the automatic network scan of the MOST NetworkMaster

- use MostNwmFiSetParameter to change the values of certain timing parameters (e.g., tWaitBeforeScan, see MOST Specification)

|  | Notes All functions of the fault injection should only be used in limited test scenarios, as otherwise the correct behavior of the CANoe MOST NetworkMaster cannot be ensured. The functions can only be applied in a CAPL program that is assigned to the MOST NetworkMaster in the Simulation Setup. For this function to be active, the switch for the channel-specific activation of fault injection must be activated in the Network HardwareConfiguration dialog. Incoming and outgoing AMS messages of the MOST NetworkMaster can be altered using the OnMostFiAmsPreReceive and OnMostFiAmsPreSend functions. |

Fault Injection of the Application Socket

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Troubleshooting] MOSTTroubleshootingAccessHugeAMSMessages


# Problems accessing huge AMS Messages

MOST » Troubleshooting » Problems accessing huge AMS Messages

| Reason | Solution |
| --- | --- |
| AMS message variable was not correctly initialized. In CAPL an AMS message variable has a default size of 200 bytes. | Initialize the message variable with a specific length: e.g. mostAMSMessage * msg = { DLC = 1000 }; For further information have a look at: Initializing message variables in CAPL. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Troubleshooting] MOST_Troubleshooting_XML-Function-Catalog


# MOST Troubleshooting: The Integration of an XML Function Catalog fails

MOST » Troubleshooting » The Integration of an XML Function Catalog fails

The program aborts the loading of a MOST XML function catalog.

The logging file created when the XML file is loaded provides further information about the nature of the error. The path for this logging file is exactly the same as the path for the XML file, except for the file extension (.log).

In certain circumstances, the parser may not be able to find the referenced DTD file. You can comment out the DTD reference in the XML file. The reference is located under the DOCTYPE tag at the beginning of the XML file.

|  | Example of a DOCTYPE tag that has been commented out: <!-- <!DOCTYPE FunctionCatalog SYSTEM 'fcat.dtd'> -->t |

Loading of the file can also fail if the XML file contains special characters that are not covered by the specified encoding system (e.g., UTF-8). This problem can be eliminated by changing the encoding. This is done by modifying the first line of the XML file.

|  | Example of the use of alternate encoding: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="ISO-8859-1"?> |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [MOST/MOST_Troubleshooting] Option_MOST_Troubleshooting_AMS_messages


# AMS Messages are not shown in the Trace Window

MOST » Troubleshooting » AMS Messages are not shown in the Trace Window

| Reason | Solution |
| --- | --- |
| AMS Service was not activated | Activate the AMS Service in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog. |
| MOST Filters are not letting AMS messages pass through | Check to see whether the MOST Filters in front of the Trace Window were properly configured |
| By default CAPL nodes block all messages in the Measurement Setup | In the CAPL program pass the messages,e.g. by on mostAMSmessage {output(this);} |
| Trace Filter blocks AMS Events. | Open the configuration dialog of the Trace Window. Select the MOST Filter page and verify whether the AMS check box is activated. The AMS atoms check box filters as the case may be the single control frames of a segmented transmission. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Troubleshooting] Option_MOST_Troubleshooting_Configuration_long


# Option .MOST Troubleshooting:Long Loading Time for a MOST Configuration

MOST » Troubleshooting » Long Loading Time for a MOST Configuration

It is very likely that the configuration is using a very large XML function catalogs.

When the configuration is loaded the XML files are automatically imported which may take a number of seconds depending on computer performance.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Troubleshooting] Option_MOST_Troubleshooting_Timestamps


# Option .MOST Troubleshooting:Timestamps of CAN and MOST drift apart

MOST » Troubleshooting » Timestamps of CAN and MOST drift apart

Ensure that Synchronize Hardware is enabled in the Vector Hardware Config dialog (Hardware ribbon tab |Bus Hardware|[Driver...]|General Information|Settings)

Time synchronization only works with CAN driver versions from 3.4 and later.

Please install the drivers enclosed in this distribution, or visit our web site (http://www.vector.com) to download the drivers.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [MOST/MOST_Troubleshooting] Option_MOST_Troubleshooting_Trace-Window_Events


# The Trace Window shows a number of "Events"

MOST » Troubleshooting » The Trace Window shows a number of "Events"

| Reason | Solution |
| --- | --- |
| The AS is active | Deactivate the AS in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog. |
| No MOST Filters were set | Insert suitable MOST Filters before the Trace Window to filter out internal events |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

- Endpoints of a participant can be distributed in the system setup via drag and drop.

- Elements from the graphical representation of the communication relationship can be distributed in the system setup via drag and drop.

- Simplified import of changed data sources.

- Distributed Objects (DO)

- Signal generators now directly support distributed objects. No mapping to system variables is necessary any more.

- Python can be used for programming application models with distributed objects (DO).

- With the new data type bytes a variable number of opaque bytes can be transferred.

- In CAPL and C#, handlers for interface members can also be used (e.g. on any_value_change or [OnAnyCall]) and thus the same implementation can be used for many objects.

- Using C# binding, a special transfer can be implemented for individual members.

- Logging

- Different attributes can now be entered i

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [NewFeatures/Domain] NewFeaturesDomainIndustrialIoT


# New Features for Industrial / IoT

CANoe » New Features » Industrial / IoT

## Version 15

- Connectivity

- Support of new CANoe hardware IoT Enabler for OTA (Over-the-Air) protocols (WLAN, Bluetooth, …).

- CANoe internal MQTT broker for testing IoT devices without an external broker.

- Binary serialization of MQTT via Google Protocol Buffers (GPB) as alternative to JSON.

- Option CANopen

- Support of Configuration Master functionality:

- Master simulation supports configuration of devices of the network.

- New CMT page in the CANopen Configuration window allows the selection of the NMT and CMT master.

- On the CMT page a concised data stream with the configuration of each device can be generated and loaded into the CMT master.

## Version 14 SP2

- Option CANopen

- CANopen DBC databases do not require a CANopen license any more. The CANopen columns in the Trace Window are filled when a CANopen license is available.

## Version 13.0 SP2

- Option CANopen

- Full support of the distributed mode.

- The new CAPL functions CANopenDomainServerOpenFileRead and CANopenDomainServerSetFileContents can be used to access domain data files.

- Performance improvements accessing files and displaying values in the CANopen Configuration window.

## Version 13.0

- Connectivity

- Support of the MQTT protocol version 4

- MQTT client modeling using vCDL and distributed objects (DOs)

- Simulation of PUBLISH messages from different sensors

- Subscription to different MQTT topics

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [NewFeatures/Domain] NewFeaturesDomainPanelDesigner


# New Features for Panel Designer

CANoe » New Features » Panel Designer

## Version 15

- Panel Designer

- For better clarity in the Toolbox, the controls have been divided into groups.

- With the Event Control you can now also receive events.

- In the Hex/Text-Editor, the byte display in hex format can now also be copied and pasted.

- Files can now also be opened with drag and drop on the Vector Tools Environment.

## Version 14

- Panel Designer

- With the Event Control you can send events.

- The length of a dynamic array (CO/DO) can be dragged onto a panel and connected to an Input/Output Box.

- The LED Control can now display more than two states in color.

## Version 13.0

- The Path Dialog can now also be displayed as a link.

## Version 12.0 SP3

- Graphical User Interface (GUI) The user interface of the Panel Designer is now High-DPI capable. On high-resolution monitors, symbols and texts are adapted to selected resolution and scaling.

- Input/Output Box In addition to the description, you can also display defined min/max values and a defined unit.The display can be set as default.

- Method Call Control Complex structured data types can be displayed.

- Switch/Indicator and Picture BoxFor both controls an error message will be displayed if an image that is to large is selected. A maximum image size (height/width) of 32767 pixels is supported. For larger images, the image size must be reduced in an image processing program.

- Improved Loading Time for Confi

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [NewFeatures/Domain] NewFeaturesDomainSecurity


# New Features for Security

CANoe » New Features » Security

## Version 15

- Security

- Secured PDU support in Replay Block.

- Use of synchronized freshness.

- Automatic recalculation of Message Authentication Code.

- X.509 certificates CAPL API (X509API.vmodule)

- Extensive CAPL functions to modify certificates for tests:

- Evaluation of object contents

- Verification

- Signature generation

- Generation of certificates

- Support of Certificate Signing Requests (CSR)

- Provision of certificates by the Security Manager.

- Extension of the COM interface for automated assignment of security profiles for SecOC, TLS and IPSec.

- Simple profile search in the dialog Security Configuration.

- Backend status display in status bar, available for OEM security packages with backend access.

## Version 14

- Observer for Transport Layer Security (TLS) - Available since version 14:

- Display of the decrypted data in the Trace Window

- Configuration of the Observer

- Automatic (CANoe is terminal)

- Input of the Master Secrets

- Evaluation of a special UDP frame

- Offline analysis of TLS communication

- Observer configuration analog to real mode

- For end point simulation, CANoe writes the Master Secret to the log file, e.g. for later analysis.

- Support of Ethernet frame protection via IPsec Authentication Header (AH) - Available since version 14:

- Different IPsec cipher suites available and configurable via Security Profiles

- Compatible with higher layer proto

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [NewFeatures/Domain] NewFeaturesDomainSIL


# New Features for Software in the Loop

CANoe » New Features » Software in the Loop

## Version 15

- Software in the Loop

- The full functional range for CANoe4SW is now directly available within CANoe.

- In addition to C++, Python is now supported as a programming language for the software under test.

- RPC-like functionality to call SUT functions directly from CANoe and vice versa.

## Version 14

- Software in the Loop

- First release

- Comprehensive tool for development, test and analysis of software in cyber physical systems

- SIL testing by virtual execution environments

- Test software for any targeted OS based on Windows or Linux, e.g., Ubuntu, CentOS, SUSE

- In the first step available as separate product, that can be operated with a CANoe license

- Download: http://vector.com/yTcFHB03LAvS20J81dX8aev1bvwC07

- Trigger possibilities: manually, periodically and via system variable.

- Global signal generators can also be configured directly in the PDU IG.

- PDUs can also be triggered when receiving a PDU.

- Retrieve all DDIs of an Implement with just one click.

- Different representation of supported and unsupported Measurement Commands.

- ISO11783 GNSS Simulator

- Improved performance.

- PDU Interactive Generator (PDU IG)

- Global signal generators can also be configured directly in the PDU IG.

- PDUs can also be triggered when receiving a PDU.

- LIN Interactive Scheduler (LIN ISC)

- Signals

## Version 15

- Option SmartCharging

- GB/T 27930

- The new sample configuration GB/T 27930 (2011) Simulation simulates a charging station and a battery management system communicating with each other according to GB/T 27930 (2011).

### [NewFeatures/Domain] NewFeaturesDomainTesting


# New Features for Testing

CANoe » New Features » Testing

## Version 15

- Test

- 64-bit support for test units and test modules. Note on Compatibility: Test units that were built with a vTESTstudio version prior to 2.2 are not supported any longer.

- Together with vTESTstudio 6

- Extended support of DO (Distributed Objects) within test commands in the Test Table Editor and Diagram Editors, e.g. support of waveform command.

- Test Table Editor: Support of background checks within loops (For, For Each, While).

- State Diagram Editor: New element Composite State enables the comfortable description of sub states for complex state diagrams.

- .NET Framework 4.7 can be selected as target framework, e.g. for the usage of external libraries.

- CANoe Execution Adapter and execution plans from vTESTstudio now support the selection of variant properties for test units.

- CANoe Test Report Viewer

- Test Cases can be annotated with "Reassessments":

- A reassessment consists of a verdict and justification text.

- New window with an overview of all reassessments in the report, including the ability to navigate to reassessed test cases, sorting and filtering.

- Reassessments are included in all export formats and can be read, created or modified through the .NET API.

- VT System / I/O Hardware

- Introduction of a COM Interface for VT System.

- Improvements of the time synchronization.

- Extension of the system variable interfaces, e.g. access on channel variables in CAPL

- Option AFDX

- The Vector network driver can be installed or updated by means of a button provided in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog.

## Version 10.0 SP3

- Option AFDX

- Performance improvement for scheduling.

- AFDX VL Statistics

- On high load the statistic values are calculated once per second (instead of once per 100 ms).

- The output of manually configured AFDX messages (not configured in the database) by means of function A664TriggerMessage and mode Immediate has been corrected.

- The initialization of selector SubVLid in FDS mode has been corrected.

- The call of event procedures (on a664Frame and on a664Message) has been revised.

- Trace Window

- Highlighting of protocol errors in SNMP and TFTP has been corrected.

- The detection of fragmentation errors at the end of a fragmented AFDX message has been corrected.

- The column filters offer selection of multiple values now. This allows displaying parameters from several folders simultaneously.

- EPK check is now available for CCP devices, too.

# New Features for Option CAN

CANoe » New Features » Automotive » Option CAN

## Version 14 SP2

- Option CAN

- If a CAN frame is destroyed by an error frame, the payload data still transmitted can be queried via CAPL (getPayloadData).In addition, the data can be queried via selectors of the error frame event.

## Version 12.0 SP3

- Option CAN

- The C Library now supports CAN FD.

## Version 11.0 SP3

- Option CAN

- CAN Interactive Generator (CAN IG)

- Triggering on frame reception is possible.

- Triggering on system variable update or change is possible.

## Version 11.0

- Option CAN

- CAN Interactive Generator (CAN IG)

- A new signal generator "Variable" is available for the CAN interactive generator.

## Version 10.0

- Option CAN

- CAN Disturbance Interface

- With the new CAN Disturbance Interface VH6501 you can disturb the CAN bus. The disturbances are e.g. freely definable sequences of dominant or recessive disturbance pulses. The disturbances are activated via a trigger.

- CAN FD

- Improved support of non-ISO and ISO CAN FD.

- For CAN FD, the following attributes can be used for configuration via a DBC or ARXML file.To use the attributes it is required to activate Use database settings in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog.(required ARXML version ≥ 4.2)

- Baudrate

- BaudrateCANFD

- TimeQuantaMin

- TimeQuantaMax

- SamplePointMin

- SamplePointMax

- SyncJumpWidthMin

- SyncJumpWidthMax

- TimeQuantaCANFDMin

- TimeQuantaCANFDMax

# New Features for Option Car2x

CANoe » New Features » Automotive » Option Car2x

## Version 15

- Option Car2x

- C-V2X Hardware

- Support of the Autotalk Craton2 EVK Revision F.

- Possibility to communicate via C-V2X (Cellular-V2X, 3GPP-PC5 Link).

- Network Hardware Configuration

- Adjustments for C-V2X support.

- WLAN default settings are predefined depending on the region (CN/EU/US).

- Car2x Stack / Interaction Layer

- Additional functionality is supported for the Chinese protocol:

- The frame content is automatically set according to the application message (e.g. the BSM).

- GNSS data is automatically used in the frame if available.

- It is possible to sign application messages in the Security Header now.

- Automatic certificate change can be configured in the database.

- Setting of protocol fields in the OnPreTx callback function has been improved.

- In case of the EU protocol, the lowFrequencyContainer can be removed in the OnPreTx callback function.

- In case of the EU protocol, no lowFrequencyContainer is sent for stationType = roadSideUnit.

- Security

- IEEE 1609.2 US root certificates with NIST-384 are supported.

- EU certificates with Brainpool-256 and Brainpool-384 are supported.

- ETSI TS 103 097 V1.4.1 (EU) is supported.

- Certificates and private keys can also be imported in binary format.

- Map Window

- The pathHistory is displayed for CAM and BSM (even without an event).

- Image files can be used as map material.

### [NewFeatures/Domain] NewFeaturesOptionEthernet


# New Features for Option Ethernet

CANoe » New Features » Automotive » Option Ethernet

## Version 15

- Option Ethernet

- Core Functionality

- The Ethernet Network Monitor is replaced by the Protocol Monitor. It supports a graphic representation of connections between endpoints for the following protocols:

- [ISO/OSI Level 2] Ethernet, ARP, NDP

- [ISO/OSI Level 3] ICMPv4, ICMPv6, IGMP, IPv4, IPv6

- [ISO/OSI Level 4] TCP, UDP

- [ISO/OSI Level 6] DHCP, DHCPv6, HTTP, SOME/IP

- It is now possible to let external virtual ECUs participate in the network over a virtual (TUN/TAP) connection.

- Support of AUTOSAR E2E Profil 44

- The use of extended Ethernet logging is now recommended.

- Communication Concept

- Support of Container PDUs and Header-less PDUs

- Network-based Access

- The Port object has additional methods in CAPL and .NET

- to get the port name

- to get the network name

- to get the segment name

- to get the information if it is a physical port and if it is measured at the port

- Added an option to disable forwarding of all simulation port events to the Measurement Setup, making it easier to focus exclusively on the measurement ports.

- TC10:

- Sleep/Wakeup events can be saved to BLF, MF4 and ASCII.

- Sleep/Wakeup events can be handled in the new CAPL callback on ethPhyState.

- New test functions: TestWaitForEthernetPhyState, TestWaitForEthernetLinkStatus

- Trace Window

- Show more details of the IKEv2 protocol in the detailed view.

## Version 14 SP2

- Option For EtherCAT

- Option For EtherCAT can now also be used on VN8900 devices.

- Option LIN

- Support of new specification SAE2602:2020.Extended functions:

- LDF Explorer

- LIN Interactive Scheduler (LIN ISC)

- The LIN Interactive Scheduler (LIN ISC) now allows you to configure and send individual frames:

- raw frames

- frames from the LDF description

- The checksum model of the LIN-Reserved-Frames (ID 0x3e/f) can now be configured using the linSetChecksumModel function.

- Option LIN

- LIN Interactive Scheduler

- LIN Interactive Scheduler allows to configure valid sync break limits.

- J2602 conformance tests

- The VH1150 test hardware automatically adjusts changing voltage levels.

### [NewFeatures/Domain] NewFeaturesOptionScope


# New Features for Option Scope

CANoe » New Features » Analysis » Option Scope

## Version 11.0 SP3

- Option Scope

- Support of oscilloscope Vector PicoScope5444D-034. For usage with CANoe the Option .Scope is needed.

- The scope can also be used with standard software from manufacturer PicoTech.

- Vector PicoScope 5444D-034 with 4 analog channels and 200 MHz bandwidth

- Fast data transfer with USB 3.0

- USB-powered for 2 channels (1 CAN/FR or 2 LIN/IO)

- External power supply needed for 4 channels (2 CAN/FR or 4 LIN/IO)

- 500 MS/s sampling rate (for 2 channels) with up to 512 MS buffer

- Bus connection via Scope Bus Probe (x10) with DSUB bus connector

- Time synchronization via Scope Trigger Y-Cable with Vector HW sync connector

## Version 11.0 SP2

- Option Scope

- New Scope Classes

- New Scope Test Functions

## Version 11.0

- Option Scope

- Support of bitmask analysis for LIN

- New sample configuration LINScope

## Version 10.0

- Option Scope

- Bitmask Definition with Background Color and Alpha Blending as well as Import and Export function in XML format for detailed Bitmask settings.

- Trigger configuration with new Online Filter Settings.

- Eye diagram with new Online Options as well as Import and Export function in CEYE format.

- Data Acquisition with Pre-Trigger as default setting.

- Option Scope

- Eye diagram and voltage edge measurement for LIN.

- Export format CSFX The new export format increases the load/save performan

- Option Sensor

- Support of SENT extension SPC (Short PWM Code).

- SENT tick time can be set via CAPL (VT2710 only).

- New function SwitchSupplyVoltage enables or disables the sensor supply voltage of a simulated ECU.

## Version 14 SP2

- Option Sensor

- For sensors that are controlled via SENT or PSI5, the supply voltage can be switched on and off via CAPL (Switch Supply Voltage).

## Version 13.0

- Option Sensor

- New I2CBasic.cfg sample configuration.

- New CAPL functions ClearQueue/sensorClearQueue for sensor simulation that clears the send queue of a simulated sensor. Available for all supported sensor protocols.

## Version 12.0 SP3

- Option Sensor

- The tree view in the Sensor Configuration dialog was updated to a more recent design.

## Version 12.0 SP2

- Option Sensor

- The SENT tick time can now be configured in the configuration dialog and in CAPL to minimum 1.5 μs.

## Version 12.0

- Option Sensor

- Support for the new SENT piggyback

- The SENT piggyback is available for the network interfaces VN1640/VN1640A and VN1530/VN1531.

## Version 11.0

- Option Sensor

- Support of the User FPGA on the VT2710 module for high performance and time critical sensor applications (since CANoe 10.0 SP4).

## Version 15

# New Features in Version 9.0

CANoe » New Features » Version 9.0

## New Features in Version 9.0 SP4

### General

- I/O Hardware

- Redesign of the Vector I/O Configuration dialog:

- Simplification of the configuration.

- Improved visualization.

- Harmonization with other configuration dialogs.

- Replay / Offline

- If a system variable cannot be updated from a log file, the name will be stored in the automatically created system variable UnknownSysVar.

- Ribbon

- The animation factor can now be set directly in the ribbon.

- Improved logic for displaying the tooltips.

- Customizations can now be taken over after service pack installation.

- Symbol Explorer and Symbol Selection dialog

- The search can be canceled with a double click to show sub-items of the selected element.

- A running search operation is visualized better now.

- Hyperlinks can now be operated with the keyboard (space key).

- Optional Ethernet signals are highlighted now ().

- Various performance improvements.

- Templates

- Files names of logging blocks in templates are now predefined with field codes.

- XIL API

- Improvement of the EXAM integration.

- The ASAM XIL API Interfaces Installer is now already included in the CANoe XIL API Installer.

- Improved error behavior when loading MATLAB files.

# New Features in Version 10.0

CANoe » New Features » Version 10.0

## New Features in Version 10.0 SP4

### General

- Introduction of a new communication concept for service-oriented architectures (SoA) and AUTOSAR adaptive.

- FDX Protocol The FDX feature, that used UDP/IPv4 as transport layer, now supports even TCP/IPv4, TCP/IPv6 and UDP/IPv6 as transport layer.

### CAPL / CAPL Browser

- CAPL

- New/Extended AUTOSAR CAPL Function

- The DBLookUp CAPL function now supports the dynamic search of AUTOSAR PDUs in databases.

- New/Extended Car2x CAPL Functions

- The C2xGetStationColor CAPL function returns the configured color of an ITS station in the ITS Station Manager.

- The C2xSendMsg CAPL function now automatically uses current time.

- New Test Service Library Check

- The check ChkCreate_CRCObservationAsrPDU, ChkStart_CRCObservationAsrPDU monitors the CRC of a PDU or a single signal group of a PDU.

- New/Extended Ethernet CAPL Functions

- The AREthGetSecurityValidationState CAPL function returns the Security-Validation-State of a received SOME/IP message.

- The SomeIpGetSecurityValidationState CAPL function returns the Security-Validation-State of a received SOME/IP message.

- New/Extended Security CAPL Functions

- .NET Programming

- Visual Studio 2017 is also supported as an editor.

### Panel Designer

- The new Method Call Control is used to send a function/method. You can set parameters and receive the corresponding return value.

### Analysis

- Graphi

### General

- All configuration dialogs have a maximize button now.

- In the Endpoint Selection dialog you can select for which objects C# APIs will be generated.

- Change of the order of parameters, PDU signals as well as members of the data types struct and union via drag and drop.

## New Features in Version 14 SP2

## General

- General

### [NewFeatures/Version] CANoeCANalyzerNewFeatures150


# New Features in Version 15

CANoe » New Features » Version 15

## New Features in Version 15

## General

- General

- C Library 2.0

- Support for the platforms Windows_x64 and Linux_x64 and compiler support for Mingw-w64.

- Communication Concept (SOA)

- New Application Panel for manual stimulation of application layer objects. On the consumer side Get and Set calls can be made on fields Methods can be called On the provider side an event can be triggered

- Get and Set calls can be made on fields

- Methods can be called

- an event can be triggered

- To improve the clarity, bindings can be deactivated via the ribbon.

- Endpoints of a participant can be distributed in the system setup via drag and drop.

- Elements from the graphical representation of the communication relationship can be distributed in the system setup via drag and drop.

- Simplified import of changed data sources.

- Distributed Objects (DO)

- Signal generators now directly support distributed objects. No mapping to system variables is necessary any more.

- Python can be used for programming application models with distributed objects (DO).

- With the new data type bytes a variable number of opaque bytes can be transferred.

- In CAPL and C#, handlers for interface members can also be used (e.g. on any_value_change or [OnAnyCall]) and thus the same implementation can be used for many objects.

- Using C# binding, a special transfer can be implemented for individual members.

- Logging

- Differ

### [Ribbon] Ribbon


# Ribbon

CANoe » Ribbon

Ribbon

In CANoe ribbon the following tabs are available:

| File | Home | Analysis | Simulation | Test |  |

| Diagnostics & XCP | Environment | Hardware | Tools | Layout |  |

## Quick Access Toolbar

As default in the Quick Access Toolbar the following functions are available:

- Start measurement

- Start measurement without Logging

- Stop measurement

- Load configuration

- Save configuration

- Save configuration as

- Open Options dialog

You can use the button on the right in the toolbar to activate/deactivate functions after they have been added.

|  | Note You can use the context menu of the symbols and group titles in the ribbon to add each symbol or group to the toolbar. You can remove symbols again using the context menu of the Quick Access Toolbar. |

## Window-dependent Toolbars

Window-dependent toolbars are arranged within the Write, Data, Trace, Graphics and Map Windows. These toolbars cannot be configured.

Ribbon and Toolbars are saved in the Windows Registry.

## Context Menu of the Ribbon

Customize the Ribbon... Opens the dialog Ribbon Customization

Minimize the Ribbon The functions of the single tabs will be hidden. With a click on a tab the respective functions will be displayed.

Customize the Ribbon | Menu ⇒ Ribbon: Where to find What?

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Ribbon] RibbonCustomization


# Customize Ribbon

Ribbon » Customize

Ribbon: Context Menu

In the Ribbon Customization dialog you can adapt the ribbon.

You can create new user-defined tabs or groups and add commands for the groups. Thus you can configure the commands that are most important to you on a separate tab or expand an existing tab to include new groups and commands.

## Choose Commands

In this area you can choose individual commands that you want to display on a ribbon tab.

You can have the commands displayed sorted by commands (All Commands) or by tabs (All Tabs).

## Customize Ribbon

In this area, all configured tabs are displayed.

Here you can:

- Activate/deactivate Ribbons and tabs via the selection field

- Add or remove new tabs

- Add or remove new groups

- Add and remove commands for user-defined groups

- Rename user-defined categories, tabs, or groups

- Import/export file with customizations

|  | Notes To make the ribbon easier to read, commands that are not available in the current configuration are hidden. An example would be commands for a bus system that is not being used in the configuration. If, as a result of this, a group or tab does not contain any commands, this element is also hidden. All elements that are hidden in the current configuration are marked with (*). |

Please note the following restrictions:

- Commands cannot be renamed.

- Predefined standard tabs and groups cannot be renamed.

- Predefined standard tabs and groups cannot be removed. However, it is 

[...truncated for brevity...]

# Menu ⇒ Ribbon

Where to find What?

Ribbon

With CANoe 9.0 classic menu and toolbar are replaced by a ribbon. The ribbon is divided into the application areas of CANoe.

|  | Cross Reference You will find additional information on the application areas here: Analysis | Concept Simulation/Stimulation | Concept Testing with CANoe | Concept Diagnostics with CANoe | Concept |

## Where to Find What

Up to CANoe 8.5 (including all service packs): Menu bar with toolbar

Starting with CANoe 9.0: ribbon

| --- | --- |
| New Configuration Load Configuration Save Configuration Save Configuration As Configuration Overview Files used Database Usage <list of recently used configurations> Exit | File ribbon tab |
| Import AMD Configuration | Diagnostics & XCP Ribbon Tab |

The windows have been sorted by application areas.

| Menu | Ribbon |
| --- | --- |
| Write Panel | Home ribbon tab |
| Measurement Setup Event Filter (e.g. CAN, LIN) Trace Graphics Data State Tracker FlexRay Cluster Monitor Scope Video Statistics Windows (e.g. CAN, LIN) Bus Statistics Frame Histogram MOST Status MOST Central Registry MOST Audio Routing MOST FBlock Monitor MOST System Viewer MOST Symbol Explorer Car2x Certificate Explorer | Analysis ribbon tab |
| Simulation Setup Interactive Generator (IG) MOST IG LIN Master LIN Network Management FlexRay Frame Panel FlexRay PDU Panel Automation Sequences Signal Generators and Signal-Replay Visual Sequences MOST Stress |

### [Ribbon/Analysis] MOSTHighObserverConfiguration


# MOST High Observer Configuration

CANoe » Analysis Windows » MOST High Observer Configuration

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|High Observer

The MOST High Protocol Observer detects violations of the MOST High Protocol (MHP). It analysis all MHP connections and generates MHP Error events in case of timeout and retry violations or illegal communication flows. These events can be shown in the Trace Window or received in CAPL programs placed in the Measurement Setup. The Observer also collects statistical information about NegAcks as well as block and frame requests.

The MHP Combiner collects all payloads which are transported over a MOST High connection. After completion of a block (see MOST High specification for the terminology) an MHP Block event is shown, which contains some statistical data about the transmission as well as the time stamp of the last frame belonging to this block.

A MHP Packet event is generated after transmission of a MHP packet. It contains the payload of the transmission if the Combiner was active and statistical information about errors and warnings if the Observer was active. The Trace Window can display a disassembly of the user data of the MHP transmission if the message is described in the function catalog and as long as certain limitations in size are not violated (see Technical Background). A MHP Connection event is generated at the end of a MHP connection, which contains statistical information about all packets and blocks that were transmitted.

|  | 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Environment/Mapping] Mapping


# Symbol Mapping

CANoe » Symbol Mapping

Ribbon: Environment Tab|Mapping

| General |
| --- |
|  | Using the mapping dialog, you can map system variables, environment variables, signals, values of communication objects or members of distributed objects and namespaces of system variables. When the value of a source variable changes during measurement, the value of the destination variable is automatically set. Optionally, you can apply a linear conversion formula. When mapping system variable namespaces, only system variables with the same name and data type located directly under the two namespaces are considered. Symbol Mapping is not available in offline mode for system variables created with CAPL |
| Functions and Control | Use Case: Value Conversion |
| Columns Groups Toolbar Context Menus Editing the Mapping List Formula Assistant COM | A measurement hardware uses the system variable TempC to provide temperature values in degrees Centigrade. A test module that uses the measurement hardware expects the temperature values in degrees Fahrenheit (system variable TempF). By using the following mapping relation you get a conversion without programming skills: 32 + TempC * 1.8 → TempF Source Variable: TempCOffset: 32Factor: 1.8Target Variable: TempF |

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation | If a mapping relation is marked active, the source and destination of the mapping relation are mapped on measurement start, dependent on the settings in the Mapping column. |

| Destination/Source | The Destination and Source columns contain the two symbols of the mapping relation that are mapped on measurement start.The following symbols are available: System variables Environment variables Signals Values of communication objects Values of distributed objects Namespaces of system variables |
| Mapping | In this column you define when the source symbol should be linked to the target symbol: OnChange: The source symbol will be only mapped on the target symbol, if the source value changes and will be different to the target value.Valid for signals, environment and system variables, namespaces of system variables. OnUpdate: The source symbol will be always mapped on the target symbol. It is irrelevant, if the symbol values (source or target) are identical or different.Valid for signals, system variables, namespaces of system variables. # Symbol Mapping: Editing the Mapping List

Symbol Mapping » Editing the Mapping List

|  | Note Cyclical assignments are only allowed for system variables and environment variables. The following conditions have to be met: The assignments have to be done with Factor=1 and Offset=0. At least one mapping must contain the OnChange mapping relation. |

## Groups and Mapping Sets

You can insert and delete groups and mapping sets via the toolbar.

You can move and copy groups within the dialog with drag and drop. To copy them press additionally <Ctrl>.

With a double click in the Name column, you can rename each group and each mapping set.

To create mapping sets, you must activate them in the Options dialog.

## Adding/Deleting Mapping Relations

You can insert variables, signals, values of application layer objects or system variable namespaces in the list as follows:

- Keyboard Start entering (a part of) the name of the symbol in the Destination or Source columns. A list of all available symbols containing the typed text string is displayed. You can select the desired symbol using the up and down arrow keys. The symbol is inserted with the <Tab> or <Enter> key. With <Del> you can delete all selected mapping relations from the mapping list.With <Ctrl>+<C>, <Ctrl>+<X>, <Ctrl>+<V> you can copy and cut configured mapping relations and add it to a new line in the mapping list.With <Ctrl>+<a> you can select all lines of the mapping list.

- This button is used in the Destination a

### [Ribbon/Environment/Mapping] MappingFormulaAssistant


# Symbol Mapping: Formula Assistant

Symbol Mapping » Formula Assistant

Context Menu: Formula Assistant

You can open the Formula Assistant dialog via the context menu of the mapping list or via in the toolbar of the Symbol Mapping dialog. The Formula Assistant provides various input options and automatic calculations for factor and offset. When you confirm the dialog with [OK], the calculated values are applied to the mapping list.

## Input by Sampling Points

Here, you can enter two sampling points that will be used automatically as the basis for calculating factor and offset.

Example: The output voltage of a power section can be set using a control voltage. It is known that the control voltage can be set between 0 V and 3 V and that this yields an output voltage between 0.2 V and 12.2 V. The two resulting sampling points for the linear conversion are thus:

Point 1: 0 and 0.2Point 2: 3 and 12.2

If you enter these sampling points at the bottom of the formula assistant, the values for offset 0.2 and factor 4 appear on the right.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/Environment/Mapping] MappingGroups


# Symbol Mapping: Groups

Symbol Mapping » Groups

## Groups

You can combine the mapping relations in groups in order to improve the overview. Each group can be assigned an individual name.

If a group is activated, all activated mapping relations of this group will be executed. If a group is deactivated, all of its mapping relations will be regarded as deactivated and therefore not be executed.

You can create groups using in the toolbar.

## Mapping Sets

You can use mapping sets to configure various mapping relations in groups and change them during the measurement.

Active mapping relations that have been configured in the Static Mapping Set will always be executed.

Active mapping relations that have been defined in a Dynamic Mapping Set can be activated during measurement using the CAPL function SymbolMappingSetDynamicMappingSet. There can be only one dynamic mapping set active at the same time.

You can create mapping sets using in the toolbar. You need to activate the mapping set in the Options dialog in order to create it.

|  | Note If you activate the dynamic mapping set in the Options dialog, all previously created groups will be moved to a static mapping set. All newly created mapping sets are dynamic. |

## Binding Groups

Binding groups are used especially for members of distributed objects whose binding is set to Mapping. With this binding you can conveniently link the DO member value to e.g., a VT System system variable or an XCP parameter.

With such a DO

# Symbol Mapping: Context Menus

## Context Menu within the Table

Add Mapping Relation... Adds a new (empty) mapping relation to the mapping list.

Add Application Layer Object... Opens the Symbol Selection dialog. Here you can select a value of a application layer object as source or destination for a mapping relation.

Add Signal... Opens the Symbol Selection dialog. Here you can select a signal as source or destination for a mapping relation.

Add Variable... Opens the Symbol Selection dialog. Here you can select a variable as source or destination for a mapping relation.

Add System Variable Namespace... Opens the Symbol Selection dialog. Here you can select a namespace as source or destination for a mapping relation.

Activate Activates the selected lines.

Deactivate Deactivates the selected lines.

Deactivate All Invalid Mappings Deactivates all invalid mappings (with error icon). This function is especially useful because the dialog can only be closed (using the [OK] button) if all active mapping relations are valid.

Delete The selected lines are removed from the list.

Delete All All lines are removed from the list.

Cut Cuts the selected lines and copies into the clipboard.

Copy Copies the selected lines into the clipboard.

Paste Pasts the content of the clipboard above the selected line.

Formula Assistant Opens the Formula Assistant, that supports the input of Factor and Offset.

### [Ribbon/File] CANoe4ServerExport


# Export CANoe4SW Server Edition Configuration

CANoe » Configuration

Ribbon: File Tab|CANoe4SW Server Edition Export

In the Export CANoe4SW Server Edition Configuration dialog, you can export the current configuration for CANoe4SW Server Edition.

## Target Platform

Here you can select the target platform (Windows or Linux) on which the CANoe4SW Server Edition configuration should take place.

Press the [Configure...] button to open the dialog CANoe4SW Server Edition Configuration Settings. Here you can configure the settings for the logging in the CANoe4SW Server Edition environment.

Press the [Export…] button to open the dialog Save under where you can define the storage location and the name of the configuration file (*.rtcfg).

|  | Note In the CANoe configuration, DLLs / module description files (*.vmodule) that match the selected target platform must be integrated. CANoe4SW Server Edition only supports the work mode Simulated Bus. If a real bus is configured, a switchover to the simulated bus takes place during the export. In Linux not all functions are supported (see RT Setup with Linux). |

Errors and warnings are shown in the Write Window.

A frequent error during the export for Linux: The encoding in CAPL scripts must be set to the value 65001 in order to guarantee that UTF-8 is supported. The setting is realized via the first line in the respective CAPL source code.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File] CANoe4ServerExportLogging


# CANoe4SW Server Edition Configuration Settings

CANoe » Configuration

Ribbon: File Tab|CANoe4SW Server Edition Export|Configure...

In the CANoe4SW Server Edition Configuration Settings dialog, you can configure settings for the logging in the CANoe4SW Server Edition environment.

|  | Note The settings configured here are saved in your CANoe configuration. At the export to a CANoe4SW Server Edition configuration, all these settings are also saved in the configuration file (*.rtcfg). |

## Logging Block

Here you can activate or deactivate a Logging Block for CANoe4SW Server Edition and configure the logging properties (e.g. the name of the logging file) themselves ([Configure...] button).

When Use CANoe4SW Server Edition logging has been activated, Trigger Blocks and CAPL Blocks can be inserted optionally.

## Trigger Block

With this Trigger Block logging can be started and stopped with CAPL (trigger, startLogging, stopLogging).

When the Trigger Block is configured using the [Configure...] button, you can specify a pre-trigger time (logged events before occurrence of the trigger condition) and a post-trigger time (logged events after completion of the Trigger Block). Furthermore you can use Stop after n Trigger Blocks to specify that the measurement will be stopped after a certain number of trigger starts. In the Buffer size input box, you can limit the number of messages that will be saved during the pre-trigger or post-trigger time.

### [Ribbon/File] LoadConfiguration


# Load configuration

CANoe » Configuration

Ribbon: File Tab|Open

A previously saved program configuration can be read from a file.

In doing so, the currently active configuration (measurement configuration, simulation configuration, window positions, DPRAM setup) is lost.

With a link to the CANoe Demos in the sidebar of the Open dialog you can navigate directly to the folder, where the CANoe sample configurations are stored.

|  | Note The display of this link can be deactivated within the CANoe Options dialog. |

You can also open configurations using drag and drop. To do this, simply pull the configuration file from the file manager to the CANoe main window.

|  | Note Standard type windows (e.g., panels) and non-modal dialogs (e.g. macro configuration dialog) are exceptions to this rule. You cannot use them to open configuration files with drag and drop. |

|  | Note on Names/Descriptors and on Zip/Unzip Some Zip programs have problems with certain characters, e.g., umlauts (mutated vowels; ä, ö, ü), "ß", space. These characters are altered during a zip/unzip operation without any feedback/confirmation. This results in configurations that do not work (properly) any longer after unzipping. Please use only names/descriptors that do not contain any of these characters. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File] NewConfiguration


# Create Configuration

CANoe » Configuration

Ribbon: File Tab|New|Create Configuration/Delete Template/Save as Template

## Selecting a Template

Under Available Templates you can select a predefined (empty) template of a standard configuration. You can chose among different bus systems/protocols.

|  | Creates a new CANoe configuration file (CFG) in which e.g. the number of channels is configured. When saving, select a name for the configuration and a storage location. To create a configuration you can also use the Simulation Setup Assistant. Therefore select a template and click on . |
| --- | --- |
|  | Copies a configuration file (including all required files and directories) into a selected directory and opens the configuration file (CFG). |

In the Options dialog you can specify a default configuration that will be loaded by selecting Create Configuration (Default Template) or by a double-click on the template.

## Create/Save New Template

With CANoe it's easy to create and save new templates. These templates are configuration files that are stored with a different file extension (TCN). All templates have to be stored in the templates directory of your CANoe installation.

You can create templates with

- Save as Template on the File ribbon tab. The template is automatically saved in the templates directory.

- Save As (e.g in the Quick Access Toolbar). Select CANoe template as storage format and save the file in the templates directory.

### [Ribbon/File] SaveConfiguration


# Save Configuration

CANoe » Configuration

Ribbon: File Tab|Save

The entire program configuration can be saved to file. The configuration is comprised of the following information:

- Measurement configuration for online mode

- Measurement configuration for offline mode

- Simulation configuration

- Positions of all windows

- DPRAM setup

|  | Note on Names/Descriptors and on Zip/Unzip Some Zip programs have problems with certain characters, e.g., umlauts (mutated vowels; ä, ö, ü), "ß", space. These characters are altered during a zip/unzip operation without any feedback/confirmation. This results in configurations that do not work (properly) any longer after unzipping. Please use only names/descriptors that do not contain any of these characters. |

|  | Note for System State Starting with version 9.0 CANoe stores certain system parameters independent of the configuration file. These include, for example, the measurement index and the counter for the test execution. The file for the system parameters is created in the same directory as the CANoe configuration with the same name but with extension STCFG. If this file is present, it is automatically used in the CANoe configuration. If you copy or move a configuration, ensure that the file for the system parameters is also copied. |

Saving File Paths

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File] SaveConfigurationAs


# Save Configuration As

CANoe » Configuration

Ribbon: File Tab|Save As

The active configuration can be saved to a new directory and/or with a new name, together with all sub-files such as CAPL files, logging files, etc.

When you expand the lop level entry (Vector CANoe) in the sidebar you can directly navigate to the sample configurations folder.

|  | Note The display of this link can be deactivated within the Options dialog. CAPL programs containing new CAPL functions of the current CANoe version cannot be compiled and executed with older program versions. This effects all new CAPL features, e.g. new functions. If you want to use the configuration for older compiler versions (older CANoe versions) you shall not use the new features. The source code remains using Save as… function. Older CANoe versions cannot read the file format of the active configuration. If you still wish to edit active configurations with an older program version, you can save the configurations in the following format that is compatible with the version indicated. Older CANoe versions may offer older versions in the Save as… dialog. Older CANoe versions prior to version 12.0 SP3 do not yet support module description files (*.vmodule). Modules that are integrated in the network nodes of the simulation setup are automatically replaced by the file name of the 32-bit DLL. Modules that are specified in databases (DBC) or in CAPL scripts (#pragma library) are not replaced and are therefore not supported

[...truncated for brevity...]

CANoe » Configuration

- With [Zip Files] a ZIP archive is created using the file list.
