Under Used Files in this Configuration a list of all used files is displayed. Open the Pack Files into Archive dialog via [Zip Files] button to select an archive file. Select a name for the ZIP file and the storage folder. Additionally to the used files in the configuration a TXT file is stored in the ZIP file. It contains system information, driver information, installed components and the license. In the ZIP file the path structure remains the same. |

|  | Note on Names/Descriptors and on Zip/Unzip Some Zip programs have problems with certain characters, e.g., umlauts (mutated vowels; ä, ö, ü), "ß", space. These characters are altered during a zip/unzip operation without any feedback/confirmation. This results in configurations that do not work (properly) any longer after unzipping. Please use only names/descriptors that do not contain any of these characters. |

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Appearance] AppearanceProgram


# Configuration: Program

Options » Appearance » Program

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Appearance|Program

|  | Note These settings are configuration-independent settings of CANoe. |

## Language

You can configure the language for all program functions.CANoe must be reopened before the new language setting takes effect.

## Date and Time Settings

Via the Format list you can set the format of the ASCII and Write Window time stamps independent of the selected GUI language.

There are three formats:

- Die Dez 17 15:06:04.278 2013 (German 24h)

- Tue Dec 17 03:06:04.278 pm 2013 (English 12h am/pm)

- Tue Dec 17 15:06:04.278 2013 (English 24h)

## Application Window

If you activate the Hide the program window, when it is minimized option the CANoe program window will be hidden, if it is minimized.

Instead of that a tray icon is displayed in the status bar.

## Windows Settings

- Fit windows automatically You can activate this menu command to have the window layout automatically adjusted to the new size of the main window if the size of the main window is changed. If this function is disabled, the sizes of subordinate windows remain unchanged, even if the size of the main window is changed.

- Magnetic windows When you activate this function the individual windows of a configuration automatically attempt to dock to one another after being moved.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File/Options/Appearance] AppearanceUnitConversion


# Configuration: Unit Conversion

Options » Appearance » Unit Conversion

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Appearance|Unit Conversion

## Unit Conversion for Display

If using standardized databases the signal description including its physical unit is firmly defined. Usually the definition of the unit is oriented on the international SI units.

In the Data and Graphics Window you can display your own defined units.

For this conversion you have to set the Source Unit and the Destination Unit and to mark the corresponding column.

|  | Note The conversion descriptions and the activating/deactivating of the conversions depend on the program, not on the configuration. The conversion descriptions have only an effect on the display in the corresponding windows. The original data will not be changed. |

The conversion description is stored in the UnitConversion.XML file. You can find this file in the application data folder of your installation (Options dialog|General|File Locations|Location of application data)

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File/Options/BussystemsProtocols] BussystemsProtocolsAFDX


# Configuration: AFDX

Options » Bus Systems/Protocols » AFDX

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Bus Systems/Protocols|AFDX

## Strictness - AFDX protocol violations

Sets the strictness of error messages in the AFDX messages analysis.

The strictness can range over three levels:

- Low: base checks

- Medium: additional checks for address errors

- High: additional checks for MAC address details

The strictness has an effect on the performance of the CANoe system. If the CANoe system is running at full load, it is advisable to reduce the strictness. This reduces the level of information detail accordingly.

## AFDX VL Statistics

With the check box Activate you activate or deactivate the calculation of AFDX VL Statistic values.

The statistic values are recalculated every 100 ms based on the so called "rolling average". The epoch duration defines the integration period for average values and standard deviation. The update is carried out independently every second.

The default value for the epoch duration is 1000 ms.

|  | Note The calculation of AFDX VL Statistic values depends on the state of the Calculate statistics check box on page Measurement | Performance under Bus Statistic. If the check box is active the state of check box Activate and the epoch duration can be changed. If the check box is inactive the check box Activate and the input box for the epoch duration are inactive too. |

## AFDX Interactive Generator

The initial standard IP address is used as the preset IP addre

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/BussystemsProtocols] BussystemsProtocolsBussystems


# Configuration: Bus Systems

Options » Bus Systems/Protocols » Bus Systems

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Bus Systems/Protocols|Bus Systems

## CAN Default Interaction Layer

CAN signals can only be sent when the send node is contained in the Simulation Setup and there is an interaction layer configured for it.If there is no interaction layer configured, you can switch on the default interaction layer here.

## Transmit Behavior CANoeILNLVector.dll and CANoeILNLDefault.dll

- The Default Interaction Layer (CANoeILNLDefault.dll) sends each message based on event triggering. Thus, on any signal update the message will be sent.A message can not be sent cyclically with the Default IL.The default interaction layer ignores NM, TP and diagnostic messages.

- The CANoe Interaction Layer (CANoeILNLVector.dll) sends a message cyclically and/or even triggered according to the message and signal attributes in the database. Also the cycle period can be switched (if defined) according to the activity state of any signal.A message can be sent with the CANoe Interaction Layer:

## Start-of-Frame Time Stamp Calculation for CAN

### In online mode

- If SOF time stamps are provided by the hardware, you do not need this option. It has no effect.

|  | Notes The calculation of SOF can negatively affect the throughput performance. The following hardware does not provide SOF time stamps: CANCardXL, CANCaseXL, CANBoardXL |

- If the CAN hardware does not provide SOF time stamps, you must activate this

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/BussystemsProtocols] BussystemsProtocolsCANopen


# Configuration: CANopen

Options » Bus Systems/Protocols » CANopen

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Bus Systems/Protocols|CANopen

## CANopen Settings

SDO Timeout You can use this input box to enter the maximum wait time in milliseconds that is to be used to wait for responses from the SDO command.

Set NMT status to PreOperational before SDO Downloads If several objects of one or more nodes are written via SDO download (e.g. the entire PDO configuration), the CANopen network is set to the PreOperational state via NMT message before the download.

Save DCF compact The CiA306 specification optionally provides a more compact storage format for DCF files, which you can activate via this check box.

[EDS Directories] The EDS Directories button allows you to specify where CANoe searches for EDS files in order to display them in selection dialogs and assign devices during scanning.

## Name Scheme for CANopen System Variables

The naming scheme for the generation of the CANopen system variables is freely configurable. You can use your own characters and combine the available field codes as you like.

| Input Box | Available Field Codes |
| --- | --- |
| Namespace for Nodes | Node ID, Node Name |
| Namespace for Structured Objects | Index (hexadecimal), Object Name (Name of the main object of the structured oject) |
| Variable for Objects | Index (hexadecimal), Sub Index (hexadecimal), Object Name |

The generated names must be C identifiers so that you can access the variable in CAPL.

### [Ribbon/File/Options/BussystemsProtocols] BussystemsProtocolsCar2x


# Configuration: Car2x

Options » Bus Systems/Protocols » Configuration Car2x

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Bus Systems/Protocols|Car2x

The following options are available.

## Physical Layer

The choice of the physical layer of the frame determines which technology is used in the construction of the frame and in the wireless transmission:

- IEEE 802.11p DSRC

- C-V2X PC5

This setting is valid for all CANoe configurations until it is changed again. Select C-V2X PC5 when using a connected C-V2X device when measuring with CANoe on the real bus.

## Station Manager

## Assign frames to stations based on

- Signer HashedID8 and Source MAC Address: Attempt to assign a message to a station primarily by means of the HashedID8. Only if no HashedID8 is available, the tool will attempt to assign the message to a station by means of the MAC address.

- Source MAC Address:Attempt to assign a message to a station primarily by means of the MAC address.

- Signer HashedID8: Attempt to assign a message to a station primarily by means of the HashedID8.

|  | Note There must always be at least one option activated, therefore it is not possible to deactivate all options. |

## Packet assignment based on station movement

If you activate this check box, an algorithm is used supporting the automatic recognition of a vehicle based on its position data if a certificate change occurs. This helps e.g. to maintain a continuous display in the Map Window as well as to analyze signals in the Graphics and

[...truncated for brevity...]

## Settings for Diagnostic Console

- Warn if length of primitive is not as specified The length of bus primitives is defined in the CANdela diagnostic description file.If the length of actually transmitted primitives differs from their specified length, the console outputs a warning that can be suppressed by this option.

- Show gap (reserved) parameters in Diagnostic Console To fill up bit fields to complete bytes, CANdelaStudio inserts non-visible gap parameters in the primitive definition.For usual applications they are not relevant and by activating this option you can inhibit that they will be displayed in the Diagnostic Console.

### [Ribbon/File/Options/BussystemsProtocols] BussystemsProtocolsEthernet


# Configuration: Ethernet

Options » Bus Systems/Protocols » Ethernet

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Bus Systems/Protocols|Ethernet

## AVB Settings

AVTP version: Select the AVTP version here.

In the table, you can assign RTP Payload Types to media types. The default values are the encodings of certain payload types specified by the RTP standard profile.

When the corresponding RTP data streams are received, the encoding information from this table is used to interpret the sample data accordingly.

## Network Access

With the network-based access option you activate the network-based mode of operation in CANoe.

Please note that network-based mode of operation is not supported by all Vector interfaces.

Update signals without port qualification

- always Events of all ports are used to update a signal without qualified Ethernet ports.

- RX packages only Only RX packages received by the interface are used which leads to less duplicated updates

- never Signals without qualified Ethernet ports are not updated.

## Optimize the required hardware resources (reduce number of used ports)

The VN Ethernet interfaces allow only a limited number of ports that can be opened for each network or segment. To avoid that a simulation cannot be executed due to this hardware restriction, the number of simulation ports used can be optimize, by combining ports into one port. Only simulation ports that are connected to the same switch segment are combined

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/BussystemsProtocols] BussystemsProtocolsJ1939


# Configuration: J1939

Options » Bus Systems/Protocols » J1939

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Bus Systems/Protocols|J1939

## Network Management

This setting configures the functionality of the J1939 filter.

## Track Dynamic Address Changes

If network management is enabled, the ACL messages with its contained device names are monitored to identify network nodes. If a node changes its address the assignment is hold up. The ACL messages are evaluated how they enter the Measurement Setup. Modifications that are made later, e.g. by CAPL nodes, are not considered.

If network management is disabled the address is used to identify network nodes. If an address of a network node is claimed by another network node, these two network nodes can't be distinguished.

## Send ACL Request On Measurement Start

If nodes change their addresses dynamically, the node name is only displayed correctly in the Trace Window, if the AddressClaim messages are seen by all active nodes of CANoe.

However, this is only automatically guaranteed if the measurement is started before the first node reports with its AddressClaim message. To avoid ambiguities, it is therefore necessary to have all nodes report again. To do this, activate this option.

If this option is activated, CANoe sends an AddressClaim Request on all connected J1939 channels right after the measurement .

The default setting is deactivation of this option.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Extensions] ExtensionsCANstress


# Configuration: CANstress

Options » Extensions » CANstress

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Extensions|CANstress

The settings on this page are relevant, if CANstress CAPL commands or the CANstress XML test function is used. You can find general information on page CANstress access.

## Settings Serial Port

If a CANstress device is connected via a serial (RS232) interface, the connection parameters are specified here. No connection parameters have to specified for the USB interface. Any (CANstress) device connected via the USB interface will be automatically detected and used.

## CANstress Devices

If a device specified via an index or alias is to be accessed using CAPL commands, the utilized devices must be configured beforehand. This is always necessary if multiple CANstress devices are connected in parallel. If only one device is connected, specification of the utilized device via an index or alias is not necessary.

All connected and configured devices appear in the list of devices. Configured devices are represented in the list with a pin:

- Configured devices that are connected to the computer

- Configured devices that are not connected to the computer at the moment

- Devices without a pin are not configured. To configure a device, you must select it in the list and press the button . To delete the configuration for a device, press the button .

You can use the arrow keys to change the sequence and, therefore, the assigned device numbers of the configured CANstress dev

# Configuration: Connectivity

Options » Extensions » Connectivity

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Extensions|Connectivity

## Mode

In this area, you control the connectivity mode via three options:

- Disabled Via this option you can switch off the CFS completely.

- Backend Via this option you can activate a connection to the CFS cloud.

- Local Network Via this option you activate a connection to a local broker, but there is no connection to the CFS cloud.

## Proxy

In this area, you control the Proxy Mode via three options:

- None No proxy is used.

- Default The system proxy is used.

- User-Defined If you select this option, you must specify a URI in the URI input box and, if necessary, user name and password.

|  | Note The proxy access data is not saved in the configuration. These settings are only available as long as CANoe is not restarted. |

## Export/PEM Certificate Path

In this input box, you have to define a Path that applies to the PEM certificates when an instance is created. This path also defines where certificates are stored when they are exported from the Windows Certificate Store.

## Authentication

Use the button with the yellow arrow to import the owner certificate received from Vector Support. After successful import, you can create an instance.

Possible actions:

| Action | Description | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| New Instance | A new instance is created in the cloud. The PEM certificates for the new instance are stored under the export/PE

## XIL API Settings

- With the Enable XIL API server option you can set, if the XIL API server is activated for the current configuration.

- In the Port number input box you can set the number of the TCP port that should be used for the XIL API (TCP/UDP Ports used by CANoe).

## FDX Settings

- With the Enable FDX check box you can set, if the FDX protocol is activated for the current configuration.

- With the Transport Layer list you configure the protocol to be used as transport layer for the FDX protocol: UPD/IPv4, UDP/IPv6, TCP/Ipv4 or TCP/IPv6.

- In the Port number input box you set the number of the UDP port or TCP port to be used for the FDX protocol. (Table of the TCP/UDP ports used by CANoe).

## FDX Description Files

To enable data exchange via FDX, you must specify in CANoe before start of the measurement which signals or variables are to be exchanged and the manner in which this is to occur (e.g., with regard to grouping).

- With the [Add…] button you can add description files in XML format to the list of FDX description files. The files selected in the list are read at the start of the measurement and an assignment is made to the signals and variables from the CANoe configuration or the databases used therein.

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Extensions] ExtensionsMultiCANoe


# Configuration: Multi CANoe

Options » Extensions » Multi CANoe

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Extensions|Multi CANoe

Multi CANoe allows several computers with CANoe to be connected and synchronized. Each CANoe instance is a participant of the Multi CANoe Configuration. All network interfaces are connected via the sync line. Data exchange takes place via Ethernet (FDX).

## Multi CANoe Settings

The Multi CANoe settings are independent from the configuration and are saved only for your work station:

Enable Multi CANoe Via this check box you activate the Multi CANoe function for this CANoe.

Abort measurement on error If this check box is activated, the measurement is aborted as soon as an error occurs in Multi CANoe mode.

Start timeout in seconds Depending on the configuration, the start of the measurement on the individual instances of CANoe can take different amounts of time. In this input box you can set how long CANoe waits until all participants have successfully started the measurement.

Table In the table you can configure the participants of the Multi CANoe Configurations. For a Multi CANoe Configuration, you must configure at least one master and one slave. You can use the Alias to give the participant a freely definable name. The IP-Adresse is used for communication between the participants. If CANoe is operated in distributed mode the IP address of the RT server has to be specified here.

| Icon | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | The current CANoe instance. |
|  | Con

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Extensions] ExtensionsUserFiles


# Configuration: User Files

Options » Extensions » User Files

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Extensions|User Files

## User Files

## Scope

If you want to read files in distributed mode using the corresponding CAPL functions (e.g. openFileRead, openFileWrite or getProfileInt) in the Simulation Setup or in the Test Setup for Test Modules programs, you have to first define these files here. The files specified and activated in this dialog are transferred to the remote device at the start of measurement (if available). CAPL then accesses the files on the remote device during measurement. Files that are only created during measurement may also be pre-defined as mentioned above. If they are not pre-defined they will be implicitly registered as user files for the current measurement. All files created or changed via CAPL functions on the remote device are transferred back to the pre-defined location on the user computer once measurement ends. In case of implicitly registered user files the file path given as parameter to the CAPL file function is interpreted as the file path on the user computer to which the file is copied on measurement end (with non-absolute paths being considered as relative to the configuration directory).

|  | Cross Reference For more information on using user files in a distributed environment see VN8900: Model and Configuration Requirements. |

If there is no distributed simulation environment the definition of user files in this dialog is not required. Howev

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Extensions] ExtensionsVirtualLink


# Configuration: Virtual Link (TUN/TAP)

Options » Extensions » Virtual Link (TUN/TAP)

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Extensions|Virtual Link (TUN/TAP)

CANoe can use technology provided by TUN/TAP device drivers to link an external (virtual) network device to the simulation. Communication is bi-directional and operates at the data link layer in both real and simulated mode. Network packets originating from the CANoe simulation are relayed to the device and vice-versa.

Currently this feature is available for Ethernet only. The device is linked to the first network of the CANoe configuration.

The TUN/TAP server can run in the operating system of a virtual machine or on a real computer. The server is independent of the specifically used virtualization solution (e.g. HyperV, VirtualBox, VMware).

## Functionality (schematic)

## Setting up the TUN/TAP Servers

A sample program is supplied with CANoe that can be compiled for Linux and is a fully functional TUN/TAP server driver. Minor modifications may be required to compile the program for the particular Linux distribution.

- Source file: tapserver.cpp (open folder)

- The C++ Boost library is required (https://www.boost.org/).Version 1.67 of the library was used for the sample program. (https://dl.bintray.com/boostorg/release/1.67.0/source/boost_1_67_0.tar.bz2)

- The file CMakeLists.txt (open folder) contains help to set up the compiler.

After compiling the program code, the following instructions are to be entered in the cons

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/ExternalPrograms] ExternalProgramsActions


# Configuration: Actions

Options » External Programs » Actions

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|External Programs|Actions

On this page you can define external applications to be executed automatically on certain events.

## Actions for Current Configuration

You can choose among the following events:

- Configuration was opened

- Configuration was saved

- Configuration is closing

- Measurement is starting

- Measurement was stopped

The external application will be executed parallel to the CANoe application. This means that the measurement might already be running and the configuration might already be closed before the application finished.

If the chosen application cannot be started a message will be displayed in the Write Window.

For each event you can make the following settings:

- Selection of the application to be started.Whether the application shall be started or not can be set with the check box in the selection list.

- Selection of the working directory for the application (optional).

- If/which command line parameters (program arguments) shall be passed to the application (optional).

- Whether the Console for Batch Files shall be displayed or not.

|  | Note The application itself must be executable, e.g. an *.EXE file.To execute a script, just set the interpreter as the application (e.g. wscript.EXE or perl.EXE) and pass your script as the parameter. These settings are specific settings of the currently loaded configuration. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik

### [Ribbon/File/Options/ExternalPrograms] ExternalProgramsToolsCAPLEditor


# Editing CAPL Code with External Editors

Options » External Programs » Tools » CAPL File Editor

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|External Programs|Tools|CAPL File Editor

If you enter in CAPL file editor a Visual Basic script (*.vbs), CANoe runs the script instead of starting the editor.

## Passed Parameters:

The name of the Browser project file is passed as a parameter. This file contains all the settings required to open or compile the CAPL program. The name of the CAPL program to open is contained in the setting

[Command1]File=<FileName>

To compile the CAPL program, call up the compiler CAPLcompExec.EXE.CAPLcompExec.EXE accepts the following parameters:

- Name of the Browser project file (/f<name>).

- Name of a logging file (/p<name>).

If this parameter is not passed the CAPL compiler messages are written to the standard output.

- Output format for messages (/t<format>).

By default the messages are output in CAPL Browser format.Format: <Function name> (<Line>,<Column>),Message.

If msdev or cw32 is used as the format parameter, the messages are written in this format: <File name>(<Line>) : Message

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File/Options/General] FieldCodes


# Field Codes

Options » General » Field Codes

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|General|Field Codes

### Format Settings

- Number of leading zeros for Measurement Index Number of digits to be used for field functions.The measurement index can be set in the Options dialog (Measurement|General|Values on Measurement Start).

- Default date/time format Here you define the default format of the field codes {MeasurementStart}, {TestStart}, {TestStop} and {LocalTime}:

- YYYY-MM-DD_hh-mm-ss

- YYYY-MM-DD

- hh-mm-ss

Any format can be used within the field code. To do so, please use the following descriptors:

- Year: YYYY

- Month: MM

- Day: DD

- Hour: hh

- Minute: mm

- Second: ss

Apart from the descriptors you can use any other character in the field code format.

Example

{MeasurementStart|on DD.MM. at hh.mm}

### Logging Format Settings

- Number of digits for auto increment Number of digits to be used for field codes with auto-numbering.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

## Display Signal and Signal Group Names

With I-Signal (default setting) or System Signal you can set from where in the network description file the signal and signal groups get their symbolic names.

With the symbolic name signals and signal groups can be qualified and configured in all analysis windows, CAPL programs, Interaction Layer DLLs,…

The selected option

- will be effective after reloading the configuration

- is independent of the configuration file and is stored on the user copmuter

The consequences are: already existing CAPL programs cannot be compiled and must be adapted the affected signals must be configured again in the analysis windows |

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|General|Save/Open

## Configuration

If the When saving the configuration save a backup of the old configuration is activated, whenever the configuration is saved a backup copy is made with the file extension BAK. No backup copy is made with the Save as function.

Pressing the [Delete] button deletes the list of Last Used configurations on File ribbon tab.

The option Always start with the default configuration prevents CANoe from loading the last used configuration when starting the program. Instead, CANoe starts with the default configuration. This is normally a simple empty configuration with all activated bus systems.

You can also use the option Use the following configuration as default configuration to define a default configuration, which will be loaded when the Create Configuration command (Default Template) is called, with a double-click on the template or (if the option Always start with the default configuration is activated) when the program is started.

|  | Note Up to now it was possible to save a template with the name default template within the template folder of the CANoe installation. This template was loaded when selecting Create Configuration (default template). This procedure is no longer supported because of the Use the following configuration as default configuration option. |

## Save Configuration Additionally in Version

Here, you can specify whether an olde

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/General] GeneralStart


# Configuration: Windows/Program Start

Options » General » Windows/Program Start

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|General|Windows/Program Start

|  | Note These settings are configuration-independent settings of CANoe. |

## Windows Start

Here you can configure the action that has to be performed when you start Windows.

- Perform no action

- Activate CANoe quick start feature If activated, parts of CANoe are copied to the main memory when you start Windows.Thereby all subsequent starts of CANoe are accelerated.

- Start CANoe and measurement When you activated this option CANoe will be started automatically next time you start Windows. The last opened configuration will be loaded/used and the measurement will be started. When you exit Windows, the measurement will be stopped and CANoe will be shut down regularly. A possible use case is using a PC installed in a vehicle as a data logger that shall start logging every time the vehicle is started.This setting remains active until you manually deactivate it (here in this dialog) or you install a new version of CANoe.

Save configuration when exiting Windows When you activate this check box the configuration will automatically be saved on exiting windows after changing the configuration with activated Start CANoe and measurement option. This may be useful for automated measurement, e.g. when you configured a Logging block to increment the logging file name automatically, the configuration has to be saved to avoid that your logging f

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/General] GeneralTestFeatureSet


# Test Feature Set Configuration

Options » General » Test Feature Set

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|General|Test Feature Set

## Verdict Concept for Test Units

Here you define the verdict concept of the current configuration. The default setting for new configurations is 5-valued.

When you open an existing configuration

- that contains Test Units and

- that has been created/edited with CANoe up to and including version 8.5 SP2

the 2-valued verdict concept is activated due to compatibility reasons. You can change this setting at any time.

## Reporting File Format

Here you select the file format for reports:

- CANoe Test Report Viewer (recommended)Using CANoe Test Report Viewer you can visualize and search test reports intuitively.Several possibilities for filter, grouping and navigation as well as user-defined views provide easy analysis of test reports.

- XML/HTML Format (previous format)XML/HTML output format allows creating test reports in XML format. Using an XSLT stylesheet file transforms the XML test reports to HTML. Thus you can adjust the look of your test report to your needs.

# Application Channel Mapping

Options » Measurement » General » Application Channel Mapping

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Mapping

## General

- In the Application Channel Mapping dialog you define the mapping of application channels to hardware interface channels or the mapping of application network names to hardware network names. Additionally you can deactivate networks and their assigned channels.

- This dialog automatically appears if a channel mapping error is detected on measurement start.

- If you leave the dialog with [OK] the activation state of the networks will be transferred to the configuration and all channel mapping changes will be stored locally on your computer.

- Channels that are not assigned to a network can be temporarily deactivated for the following measurement. This modification will not be saved in the configuration.

- Network name mapping modification will not be saved in the configuration.

|  | Supported Hardware The assistant supports Vector XL- and VN-hardware families. VN8900 devices are supported in interface mode as well as in distributed operation mode. The displayed channel mapping refers to the currently used execution environment. In distributed mode the channels set and usable on the device are displayed (see also Home ribbon tab|Group Measurement|Vector Tool Platform|CANoe/CANalyzer|Channel Mapping), while in interface mode the channels are displayed on the local computer. Automatic or manual reassignment to other hardware devices is not

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementDataHistory


# Configuration: Data History

Options » Measurement » Data History

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|Data History

Here you can make settings for the data history of the analysis windows. CANoe saves measured data from Trace, Test Trace and Graphics Window in a global buffer. The data can be swapped to the hard disk here.

## Usage of Memory(RAM)

With the slider, you can specify the size of the data history in the memory:

short – middle – long – very long – Custom use

Depending on the operating system and the real available main memory, CANoe uses a maximum of the displayed amount of RAM. The RAM requirement of CANoe increases continuously during the measurement until the data history reaches the set maximum size. When the maximum size is reached, older elements in the data history are overwritten.

|  | Note With a very large use of the main memory, the next measurement start can take considerably longer. If other programs with large main memory requirements are executed, you should set the usage so that enough real main memory is available for all programs. Otherwise, the operating system will attempt to move memory areas to the hard disk. |

## Usage of Swap File

|  | Note If high data rates are involved, you should select the fastest hard disk possible with sufficient space for the swapping. Do not place the swap file on an external storage medium connected to a slow USB port, for example. Please do not create the pagefile on the system partition (usually "C:\")

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementDataHistorySwapFileFull


# Swap File Full

Options » Measurement » Data History » Swap File Full

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|Data History

CANoe stores data for the history of the analysis window in a global buffer on the hard disk.

Your data buffer is now full, and old data is deleted from it bit by bit. This slows down the system.

You can now deactivate swapping to increase the system speed. The previous history is deleted here, and the data is then no longer available in the windows. Deletion of the data takes some time, and data could be lost during this time, e.g. for Logging Blocks as well. The speed will increase when this is complete.

If you do not want to deactivate swapping, select [Continue Swapping].

The settings for the swap file can be changed in the Options dialog. Here you can also select a faster hard disk or a larger file, for example, so that the system is fast enough.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementEventSorting


# Configuration: Event Sorting

Options » Measurement » Event Sorting

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|Event Sorting

Configure here the event sorting.

If you are using several hardware interfaces simultaneously (such as a CANboard XL and a VN3600), you can configure the simulation behavior here:

## Simulation Time Source

Every hardware device usually has its own integrated clock. This clock is used to produce a time stamp for messages, frames, and other data received from the network. In order for these time stamps to match, you should use time synchronization.

In addition, there is an internal simulation time in the program that you can query, for example, with the timeNow function in CAPL. This time should be established by exactly one of the hardware clocks to ensure that it increases evenly and monotonically. Here, you can select the channel whose hardware clock establishes the simulation time.

If you have activated event sorting, this channel is simultaneously the reference channel for sorting. Therefore, the events received on this channel are regularly processed with a time delay according to the Reference delay setting.

If you have deactivated event sorting, only the simulation time for events of the selected hardware is guaranteed to match the time stamp of the event (timeNow() == this.time).

In most cases, you can leave the setting at Auto and this will automatically result in selection of a good time source.

- Select a different channel, for example,

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementGeneralSettings


# Configuration: General

Options » Measurement » General

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|General

## Channel Usage

In this dialog you can configure the number of channels you wish to work with in your active configuration. Besides affecting the measurement, the channel definition also affects the input options of the various configuration dialogs. Only defined channels are offered to you for selection.

In case you do not need more than one channel per bus system in the current configuration, please set the respective channel count values to 1.

- In online mode the defined channels correspond to the channels of the current measurement (hardware or simulation).

- In offline mode the defined channels correspond to the channels on which the replayed messages were logged in the logging file.

- In simulation mode (online mode, simulated bus) each channel corresponds to a simulated bus.

|  | Note The number of channels is configuration-specific. It is saved in the configuration file and is restored when the configuration is loaded. |

|  | User Defined Application Name If CANoe is started with a user defined application name not equal to CANoe, the name of this group includes the user defined application name. |

CAPL blocks represent a special case. With these program blocks the check to determine whether all referenced channels are valid is not made until the compiling process. A warning is output if invalid channels are detected. It is advisable to compile all nodes

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementGeneralSettingsChannelsOfflineMode


# Channels in Offline Mode

Options » Measurement » General Settings » Channels in Offline Mode

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|General

In offline mode messages from a previously recorded logging file are replayed into the Measurement Setup.

This involves playing-in each message on the channel on which it was logged. Thus, the channels in offline mode match the channels used during logging to the logging file. The number of channels should be defined such that it matches the number of channels configured when logging to the logging file.

## Behavior when using undefined channels:

- The channel setting does not result in any filtering of messages in the data flow plan.

- If messages assigned to an undefined channel are played in, they are passed through the Measurement Setup.

- This is also the case when transmitting from a Generator or Replay Block in the Measurement Setup on an undefined channel. The transmit request is passed through.

- CAPL blocks represent an exception. These program blocks do not send messages to which an undefined channel is assigned.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementGeneralSettingsChannelsOnlineMode


# Channels in Online Mode

Options » Measurement » General » Channels in Online Mode

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|General

In online mode with working mode Real Bus, messages are transmitted from the Simulation Setup to one or more real buses, and they are received in the Measurement Setup by one or more real buses.

Thus, the defined channels match the controller chips in the real buses. A chip is allocated to each defined channel in order to permit a start of measurement.

|  | Allocating Channels to Controller Chips In the CAN hardware configuration of your computer's Control Panel the channels are allocated to the CAN chips that are registered in the system. The chip allocation is only relevant in online mode. In offline mode, where the messages are played in from a file, it is irrelevant. If you wish to modify the standard chip allocation, then go to your computer's control panel (via the Start menu path Settings|Control Panel) and open CAN Hardware. The CAN Hardware Configuration Dialog then appears, in which you can change the chip allocation of the channels. The chip allocation is also shown in the Hardware Configuration dialog. |

## Behavior when using undefined channels

- The channel setting does not result in any filtering of messages in the data flow plan.

- When receiving on chips that are not allocated to a defined channel, the received messages are passed through the Measurement Setup.

- This is also the case when transmitting from a Generator or 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementGeneralSettingsChannelsSimulationMode


# Channels in Simulation Mode

Options » Measurement » General » Channels in Simulation Mode

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|General

In online mode with working mode Simulated Bus/Slave Mode buses and network nodes are completely simulated.

Each channel corresponds to a simulated bus. Set up your channel definition according to how many buses you wish to simulate.

## Behavior when using undefined channels:

- The channel setting does not result in any filtering of messages in the data flow plan.

- When transmitting from a Generator or Replay Block in the Measurement Setup to an undefined channel, the messages are passed through.

- When transmitting from a Generator or Replay Block in the Simulation Setup to an undefined channel, an error is reported in the Write Window as soon as the transmit request is on the undefined channel. In this case the message is not transmitted.

- CAPL blocks represent an exception. These program blocks do not send messages to which an undefined channel is assigned.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementGeneralSettingsMissingChannelAllocation


# Missing Channel Allocation

Options » Measurement » General » Channels

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|General

You get this warning message if you attempt to use more channels than are allocated in your system or application.

Essentially, only those CAN channels that your hardware supports may be registered. Additionally, a maximum of two virtual CAN channels are available.

To allocate additional real or virtual CAN channels to your application, start CAN Hardware Driver Configuration dialog by replying [Yes] in the message window, or from the menu path Configure|CAN|Driver.

Channel Usage Configuration | Hardware Configuration | CAN Hardware

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Measurement] MeasurementPerformance


# Configuration: Performance

Options » Measurement » Performance

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Measurement|Performance

## Statistics

- Calculate bus statistics Here you can activate/deactivate the calculation of all bus statistics values.

- Enable performance monitor Here you can activate/deactivate the performance monitoring of critical system parameters.

## Optimization

- Optimize Performance This function executes a performance optimization and a report is generated in the Write Window.

- Undo Performance Optimization This function cancels the changes made in a previous optimization of configuration options. The operations necessary for this are summarized in a report. Please note that only those values can be canceled which were not changed in specific configuration dialogs since the optimization.

- Analyze Performance Optimization This function outputs the report on the last performed optimization again.

## Throughput

Here you can optimize the throughput by filtering events in the real-time area (RT server) of the application. This means that the data will not be transmitted to the analysis area (user computer).

- Early filtering of events

With activated option a Channel Filter and a logging file will be included in the Measurement Setup in front of the Online/Offline switch.The filtered data will not be displayed nor could they be logged in the Measurement Setup.

With the Channel Filter you can filter messages and events of all available bus systems.

If you w

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Programming] ProgrammingCAPL


# Configuration: CAPL

Options » Programming » CAPL

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Programming|CAPL

## CAPL Compiler

When you create a new configuration, both check boxes are not activated by default.When you load an old configuration (program version 3.2 and earlier), both check boxes are activated by default.The configuration behaves automatically like in program version 3.2 (or earlier).

- Suppress compilation to work without CAPL sources (.CAN) Activate this check box, if you want to relay the configuration without CAPL source files (.CAN). If activated it is not possible to compile any CAPL programs in the configuration. If databases or other components have changed, on measurement start a compilation is required. In this case the measurement start is aborted with the following message: CAPL compilation is turned off in this configuration.To relay a configuration that works without compiling the following conditions must be met:

- Same CANoe version, same service pack as in the preparation of the CFG file, thus the CAPL compiler is identical.

- Identically configured databases (their time stamps and on which networks they are configured).

- Identically configured modeling libraries (DLL files) and their time stamps.

- Identically defined system variables (name, type, value table; but not the value).

- Time stamp of the source file and all include files that are referenced by it (only if the source file exists).

- Identical execution target (computer, CAPL-on-Board, 

[...truncated for brevity...]

## .NET Debugging

## .NET Debugger

In this list you can select an internal debugger or a Visual Studio debugger. In the list all Visual Studio versions that are supported from CANoe as .NET Debugger are displayed. The selected Visual Studio must be installed on your computer.

If you have selected a Visual Studio debugger, the following options are available:

## Load required .NET sources automatically

The .NET program will be loaded automatically in the selected Debugger.

You can open the Debugger via

- the context menu command Debug... of a test module in the Simulation Setup

- the context menu command Debug... of a test module in the Test Setup

- in the test execution dialog

If a test module references only one .NET assembly, the source files should be in the same folder as the assembly. The debugger will not start if this is not the case.

If the folder structure can't be arranged as described above, you will have to deactivate this option. In this case, you will need to manually load the source files in Visual Studio at the start; they will remain open during the rest of the debugging process.

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/Programming] ProgrammingNETNamespaces


# Configuration: .NET

Options » Programming » .NET

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Programming|.NET

## .NET Target Framework

- .NET 4.7 (recommended)

- .NET 4.0 (version compatible to earlier CANoe versions)

- .NET 2.0 (version compatible to very old .NET DLLs)

Newly created CANoe configurations use the .NET 4.7 Runtime and the corresponding CANoe API. Existing configurations (created before CANoe version 13.0) use the .NET Target Framework 4.0 with the .NET 4.7 Runtime for compatibility reasons. Even older configurations (created before CANoe version 8.2 SP3) use the .NET 2 Framework and the .NET 2 Runtime.

Information about conversion of configurations to the .NET 4 Runtime can be found here: Migrating from .NET 2 API to .NET 4 API

## Type Libraries Creation Path

Here you find the following three options:

- Create Type Libraries in the database folder

- Create Type Libraries in a folder for use with the current configuration only

- Create Type Libraries in a folder shareable with multiple configurations

Classes are generated automatically for access to symbols in .NET. The type libraries are normally stored with the corresponding configuration or database (option 1). However, you can also select a different directory here (option 2 and option 3).

In case the target directory is used with this configuration only, you should select option 2. With this selection the path is stored as relative path to the configuration.

In case you want to share the target directory b

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/WindowsBlocks] WindowsBlocksGraphicWindow


# Configuration: Graphics Window

Options » Windows/Blocks » Graphics Window

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Windows/Blocks|Graphics Window

## Default Lines and Samples for Graphs

Here you can specify the standard connection type for symbols to be added to a Graphics Window.

- Automatic Here, the connection type is determined according to the symbol definition.If it is assumed that the symbol accepts only a few different values, Step is used as the connection type. Otherwise, Line is used. The interpolation nodes are drawn as points.

- User-defined Here you can define which connection type and interpolation point type are to be given to new symbols. The preview shows you how a signal is drawn with such settings in the Graphics Window.With [Reset], you can reset the settings to the standard Line and Point values.

## Optimization

Here you can set the standard drawing accuracy for new Graphics Windows.

The display accuracy setting is used to optimize the Graphics Window display. Using Pixel or Peak settings a delayed display can be avoided by a high amount of data.

### Display accuracy: Pixel

If multiple measurement points fall within the time range of one screen pixel, only those measurement points at the ends of this range are displayed. This leads to faster output when there are many measurements in a small space. However, individual peaks in a signal response might not be displayed if the measurement point with the extreme value lies within this range.

### Display accur

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/File/Options/WindowsBlocks] WindowsBlocksLoggingCommentEdit


# Change Global Logging Comment

Options » Windows/Blocks » Logging » Configuration: Logging » Change Global Logging Comment

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Windows/Blocks|Logging|Logging Comment|Change Last Comment...

This dialog can be opened via the Options dialog and allows to change the logging comment after a measurement.

You can change the comments of the logging files last written by all logging blocks. The [Apply] and [OK] buttons open another dialog where you can select the logging files to which the new comment is to be transferred.

To change the comment of the last logging file written by a single block, refer to the help page Change Logging Comment.

## Field Codes

Inserts a field code into the currently selected input field. Field codes can be used to generate parts of the comment automatically. The values are determined each time the logging file is closed and inserted into the comment text. In the preview fields you get a preview of the comment text with resolved field codes.

The Logging Comment Field Codes page contains a detailed list of the available field codes.

## Columns

| Entry Field / Control | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Attribute Name | The name of the attribute. |
| Value of last Measurement | Here the value is displayed which is currently in the logging files. |
| Current Value | The value to be written to the logging file. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

# Global Logging Comment

Options » Windows/Blocks » Logging » Configuration: Logging » Global Logging Comment

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Windows/Blocks|Logging|Logging Comment|Set Current Comment...

The Global Logging Comment dialog (Options dialog) can be used to specify the logging comment. Settings that you make here apply to all logging blocks that have not been explicitly configured differently.

For information on local settings for an individual logging block, see the Logging Comment help page.

Logging comments can consist of several attributes. The set of these attributes is the same for each logging block and can only be set in the Global Logging Comment dialog.

## Toolbar

| Symbol | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Adds a new attribute to the comment. This functionality is only available outside the measurement. |
|  | Deletes the currently selected attribute. This functionality is only available outside the measurement. |
|  | Moves the currently selected attribute up. This functionality is only available outside the measurement. |
|  | Moves the currently selected attribute down. This functionality is only available outside the measurement. |
| Field Codes | Inserts a field code into the currently selected input field. Field codes can be used to generate parts of the comment automatically. The values are determined each time the logging file is closed and inserted into the comment text. In the preview fields you get a preview of the comment text with resolved fi

## Digits after Decimal Point

Here you can specify the number of digits after the decimal point with which time stamps should be displayed in the Trace Window.

- The default value is 6 digits after the decimal point.

- The maximum number is 9 digits after the decimal point.

Furthermore you can specify the number of digits after the decimal point with which floating-point numbers should be displayed in the Trace Window.

- The default value is 4 digits after the decimal point.

- FlexRay signal groups

- Signal multiplexing information

# Configuration: Write Window

Options » Windows/Blocks » Write Window

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Windows/Blocks|Write Window

## Display Options

### Number of lines in the Write Window

Here you can enter the number of lines per page. If the number of messages displayed on the page exceeds the configured number of lines, the oldest messages are discarded. The number of lines must be between 100 and 20000 lines.

### Clear Write Window on measurement start

If you activate this option, the content of all pages in the Write Window is deleted at the start of the measurement.

### Click here to change the font

The link takes you to the options for the fonts.

## Record Messages in a File

### Record messages

Activate this option to record the messages of the Overview page.Select a file to which the messages are to be written.

### Clear logging file on measurement start

Activate this option to delete the content of the file at the start of the measurement.

### Message Filter

You can filter the messages according to their classification (notifications, information, warnings, errors).

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/Hardware] NetworkHardware


# Network Hardware Configuration

CANoe » Network Hardware

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware

At the left of the Network Hardware Configuration dialog is an overview of the channels defined in the current configuration (the number of channels can be set in the Options dialog).

You can enter/modify the settings for the following bus systems:

- CAN

- LIN

- FlexRay

- MOST

- J1708

- Eth (Ethernet)

- AFDX

- Ath (WLAN)

- ARINC429

- Hardware Sync

- Hardware Configurations

## Buttons

- [Driver…] With this button you start the Vector Hardware Configuration dialog (Windows Start Menu Path: Control Panel|Vector Hardware).

- [OK] With this button you confirm all entries.

- [Cancel] With this button you discard all entries.

- [Undo Page] If you only wish to undo entries in one of the sub-dialogs mentioned above, press the [Undo Page] button in the particular sub-dialog.

- [Undo] If a channel symbol is selected [Undo] will cancel all changes (Setup / Filter/ Options) for this channel.

|  | Note Starting with CANoe 11.0 the distributed operation for VN8910, VN8910A, VT6010, VT6050, VT6050A and older RT Racks is no longer supported. VN8910 and VN8910A can still be used in interface mode. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/Hardware] VectorIO


# Vector I/O Configuration

Vector I/O Configuration

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Vector I/O

The Vector I/O Configuration dialog is used to configure the IOpiggy and VN IO modules.

As long as no device is configured, blue links are available in the right window to configure a standard device.

## Add Devices

Via the Devices menu you can add, delete and disable devices.

In the left window of the dialog all added devices are displayed. Here you can also select the mode (standard, disabled, custom) in the Mode column.

## Mode

- Disabled The I/O device is disabled and will not be used.

- Standard The I/O device is in default mode. All pins use preconfigured settings. The Device Connector page shows which pin is in which mode.

- Custom All pin functions and names are fully user configurable. Additional settings (e.g. trigger configuration) are possible.

## Configure Devices

Via the Configuration menu you can

- create a new configuration, already configured devices will be deleted

- adapt device settings to the hardware, already existing settings will be overwritten

- import a device configuration (IOCFG), already configured devices will be deleted

- export a device configuration (IOCFG)

In the right window of the dialog different pages are displayed depending on the selected device and mode.

- IOpiggy Configuration

- VN I/O Module Configuration

VN0601 | VN1630/VN1640 | VN7570/VN7572 | VN8900 | IOpiggy

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

The columns in the table contain the following information:

| Column | Description |

| Group | Signal group of the pin. |
| Pin | Pin number on the connector. |
| Name | Name of the pin, which you can modify.The pin name is used to generate the system variables. |
| Disabled | If you select this option, the corresponding pin is not used. |
| Function | Function of the pin.The following functions are available for the various pins: Function System Variable Note Input <Pin name> Analog: Voltage in volts The pin acts as a digital or analog input. Digital: Level (Low = 0, High = 1) Output <Pin name> Output level (Low = 0, High = 1) The pin acts as a digital or analog output. Output (oc) <Pin name> The pin acts as a digital output with open collector behavior. Output (pp) <Pin name> The pin acts as a digital output with push pull behavior. Output (pp) / Activation Line <Pin name> The pin acts as a digital output wit

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareCAN


# CAN Network Hardware Configuration

Network Hardware » CAN

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|CAN

On the left side of the dialog all channels used in the current configuration are listed in a tree view.

For CAN the following pages are available:

| Setup | Acceptance Filter | Options | CANopen | J1939 |  |

## CAN Channel

Here you can find information about the connected hardware.

The channel assignments of Vector hardware interfaces are configured in the Vector Hardware Config dialog. Click on [Driver…] to open this dialog.

## CAN Controller

Mode

You can operate the channel either in CAN or in CAN FD mode.

You can activate the CAN FD mode when using Vector Informatik interfaces with CAN FD support (CAN with flexible data rate).

## Baud rate

When you change the baud rate, the CAN controller parameters are automatically re-calculated. However, you can set specific values at any time on the Setup page.

CAN mode: If you use a database that contains baud rate information you can select the Use database settings option to accept this values.

Another option is to use the Baud Rate Scanner to calculate the baud rate automatically. Click [Scan…] to start the Baud Rate Scanner.

Bus System Protocols

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareCANBaudRateScanner


# Baud Rate Scanner

Network Hardware » CAN » Hardware Interface Parameters » Baud Rate Scanner

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|CAN|Setup|[Scan...]

The baud rate scanner attempts to determine the baud rate of a network automatically. It does this by using variable baud rates and checking whether a message can be processed successfully via the given channel.

A scan is registered as successful if it was possible to send or receive a message correctly, depending on the mode used. If it was not possible to process a message successfully within the given time span, the scan continues with the next baud rate in the given baud rate interval.

## Mode

## Active Mode

Active mode is used when scanning the baud rate of a network on which there is currently no communication. In this case, CANoe is an active participant (transmitter) in the network, sending messages via the given channel. The process ends when a message has been sent successfully and thus the correct baud rate is known. If the wrong baud rate is used the other devices on the bus will not be able to receive the message. In this case, CANoe (as the transmitter) does not receive an acknowledgment and therefore does send an Error Frame in turn. The scan then continues with the next baud rate.

## Passive Mode

Passive mode is used when scanning the baud rate of a network on which there is already communication going on. In this case, CANoe is an passive participant (receiver) in the network, receiving messages via

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareCANControllerAcceptanceFilter


# Acceptance Filter

Network Hardware » CAN » Acceptance Filter

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|CAN|Acceptance Filter

| Setup | Acceptance Filter | Options | CANopen | J1939 |  |

The acceptance filter controls which received messages are filtered and not passed on to CANoe.

You can define the acceptance filter either by using an ID bit mask or by using Code and Mask parameters.

|  | Note An acceptance filter influences the calculation of bus statistics! |

## ID Bit Mask

All messages are being blocked, whose identifiers don't match the bit mask on each single bit position. An X in the bit mask (don't care) is not taken into consideration. A bit mask with X at each bit position would let pass all messages.

Press [Select ID...] to open a dialog where you can specify a single message with a certain identifier that should be allowed to pass.

The bit mask is used for the automatic calculation of the Code and Mask values.

## Code and Mask

- Code and Mask are compatible to the corresponding registers of the Philips SJA1000 controller.

- When you change a parameter the bit mask is automatically adjusted.

- Code and Mask are hexadecimal values.

|  | Example (of Standard IDs) Code = any, Mask = 000All messages are passed. Code = 008, Mask = 7F7Only messages with IDs whose fourth bit is 1 are passed, e.g. the ID 8. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareCANControllerConfiguration


# CAN Controller Configuration

Network Hardware » CAN » CAN Controller Configuration

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|CAN|Setup

| Setup | Acceptance Filter | Options | CANopen | J1939 |  |

On this page you define the bit timing; additionally you can change the CAN controller mode.

The bit timing is displayed graphically in consideration of the bit timing parameters.A bit time is shown that is subdivided into three segments: SYNC (orange), TSEG1 (blue) and TSEG2 (green).

## Mode

Here you can select between CAN and CAN FD. Depending on the selection the following different settings are available.

## CAN Controller Mode

You can set the CAN controller mode via the list field. The following settings are available:

- Normal The CAN controller participates in bus communication.

- Ack Off The bus is influenced by the measurement since the CAN controller on the interface card represents the interface between the analysis software and the CAN bus. Particularly the CAN controller sends its acknowledge for correctly recognized messages by sending a dominant level (on the bus) in the corresponding slot of the CAN message. To reduce the influence on the system you can switch off this functionality. Thereby the node is no longer visible on the bus. Please keep in mind that – when you switched off acknowledge – communication on the bus only may happen if at least one other network node sends an acknowledge.

- Restricted This mode is only supported by CAN FD enabled hardware

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareCANControllerConfigurationSamplePoint


# Sample Point

Network Hardware » CAN » Setup » Sample Point

The Sample Point is the point of time at which the bus level is read and interpreted as the value of that respective bit. Its location is at the end of TSEG1.

| Value | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | The bit is only sampled at the end of TSEG1. |
| 3 | The bit is sampled three times. |
| 1st sample point | Two time quanta (Tq) before end of TSEG1. |
| 2nd sample point | One time quantum (Tq) before end of TSEG1. |
| 3rd sample point | At the end of TSEG1. |

TSEG1 = PROP_SEG + PHASE_SEG1

TSEG2 = PHASE_SEG2

|  | Note on CAN FD Sampling will only ever occur once at the end of TSEG1 for CAN FD. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareCANControllerOptions


# Options

Network Hardware » CAN » Options

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|CAN|Options

| Setup | Acceptance Filter | Options | CANopen | J1939 |  |

## Channel specific

## Tx Request

By activating TxRq a time stamp is preserved for each message that is written in the transmit register of the CAN controller. The message is reported to CANoe as a TXRQ message. This makes it possible to perform difference measurements between the transmit request and time of transmission.

## Transmission Error Notification

When sending a message you can set the number of transmission attempts (single shot mode). If it wasn't possible to send the message and transmission error notification is active you will be notified in CAPL with a TxReq message and the TxFailed message selector.

## CAN FD specific

## Protocol Exception

With activated protocol exception support the CAN controller switches to bus integration state when receiving a recessive res bit; additionally a message will be displayed in the Write Window. It will not send an Error Frame.

## Edge Filter

IN CAN FD mode the edge filter is activated by default. It prevents that short dominant phases (<2 BTL-Zyklen) during bus integration are being interpreted as synchronization edge.

## SSP-Offset

The Secondary Sampling Point Offset (SSP) is used for the compensation of the Transmitting Node Delay (TD). To compensate the TD the transmitter must be able to compare its transmitted bit to the current bit on the CAN network.Th

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareConfigurations


# Hardware Configurations

Network Hardware » Hardware Configurations

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|Hardware Configurations

Only available for network-based access.

It is possible to link VN Ethernet interface configurations with a CANoe configuration. When loading the CANoe configuration, a check is performed to verify whether the configurations stored in the CANoe configuration are identical to the configurations on the interfaces (including the interface serial number) . If not, you will be asked if you want to write the interface configurations to the connected interfaces.

You can define multiple configuration sets with interface configurations. This can be useful if you have a CANoe configuration that can be used with different hardware setups.

Only one configuration set can be active at a time. If no configuration set is active, no check is performed when the CANoe configuration is loaded.

## Buttons

The buttons in the section Set can be used to create and change a configuration set.

With [Add] you add a new configuration. You can add single files (interface configurations) with File: [Add] to this group.

With [Remove] you remove the selected configuration set.

With [Description] you change the name of the selected configuration set to give it a more descriptive name.

With [Activate]/[Deactivate] you activate or deactivate the selected configuration set. Activated configuration sets are marked bold.

With [Apply] you write the selected configuration 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareEthernet


# Ethernet Network Hardware Configuration

Ethernet » Use Cases » Ethernet Network Hardware Configuration

An Ethernet interface is required to enable measurements in an Ethernet network in real mode by means of CANoe. Vector offers for Ethernet various network interfaces which have a different number of channels and different configuration options.

## Procedure for Network-Based Access

VN Ethernet interfaces from firmware version 11.1 support a network-based access.

Application note AN-IND-1-023_Ethernet_VN_Family_From_Firmware_Version_11.1.pdf describes how the concept behind the Ethernet VN interfaces was changed.

VN Ethernet interfaces with network-based access support flexible definition of networks, segments and ports. This allows you to use these devices in combination with CANoe for a wide range of use-cases such as analysis, tests and simulation.

The configuration of the CANoe can be done in the in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog.

|  | Step by Step Procedure Open the Network Hardware Configuration dialog via the Hardware ribbon tab|Network Hardware. Press the [Drivers…] button to open the Vector Hardware Config.All connected Vector interfaces are displayed under Hardware.(Alternatively, you can open Vector Hardware Config via Windows Control Panel with Vector Hardware.) Select an Ethernet interface, information on the selected interface will be displayed on the right side. In the Ethernet Mode Configuration dialog you can check if the Ethernet interf

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareEthernetGlossary


# Term Definition

Ethernet » Network Hardware Configuration » Term Definition

Figure: Sample drawing. This configuration is not possible (9.0 SP3).

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| Hardware segment | A hardware segment is used for gaining access to an Ethernet network. A hardware segment may comprise several hardware channels and provides an application channel for the application (e.g. CANoe). The following hardware segments are available: Switch Comprises one or more hardware channels. The switch learns MAC addresses and transfers packages according to the learned rules. TAP Comprises two hardware channels and is a simple measurement point (see below for additional information). Direct link Comprises exactly one hardware channel and is typically used for diagnosis applications. |
| TAP(Test Access Point) | A TAP basically provides the functionality of a Y-cable, which you may be familiar with from CAN applications. I.e., a TAP is (usually) a measurement point between two end points.You can configure one (e.g. VN5610) or several (e.g. VN5640) TAPs on the Vector network interfaces. When doing this, the following options are available: PHY Bypass Packages are transferred between the two hardware channels with merely any latency. It is not possible to send packages using CANoe. MAC Bypass Packages are transferred between the two hardware channels with minimum latency. In addition, packages can be sent using CANoe. Bypass Off Packages are not transferred automatically b

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareEthernetPageEthFilter


# Network Hardware Configuration: Filters

Ethernet Network Hardware » Eth » Filters

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|Eth|Filters

| Eth | Settings | Filter |  |

Only available with channel-based network access.

On this page you can define independent acceptance filters for the IEEE 802.3 fields Destination MAC Address, Source MAC Address and Ethertype to see only relevant packets and/or improve the performance. Further you can use IPv4 addresses and TCP/UDP ports as filter criteria.

## Acceptance Filters

With the button [Add...] you open the dialog Add filter, in which you can define new filters and add them to the list of active filters. For a new filter you have to specify the following values:

- Field: select the type of the filter in the

- Codelist: acceptance code, the relevant bits (specified by the acceptance mask) of the received packet are compared with that code

- Mask: acceptance mask, which specifies the bits of the acceptance code that are compared

|  | Note During the measurement the defined filters are applied in the same order as they are defined in the filter list. |

With the button [Edit...] you can modify the values of the current selected filter in the list.

If the list is empty, no acceptance filter are defined so that all network packets on this channels are blocked and no incoming network data can be received.

You can remove one or all defined filters with the buttons [Remove] and [Remove all].

If you activate the Accept all packetsc

Select one of the following options: OFF: Ethernet packets are not forwarded. PHY Bypass: Ethernet packets are forwarded., packets cannot be sent from CANoe, but the lag of time during forwarding is minimal. MAC Bypass: Ethernet packets are forwarded., packets can be sent from CANoe that are integrated to the current network traffic. If you select Bypass the destination channel of the bypass is displayed next to the list. |
| Ethernet channel | Here you set the configuration of the Ethernet channel. Select one of the following options: BR, Slave, 100MBit: Physical Layer BroadR-Reach®, Clock Mode Slave, transmission speed 100 Mbit/s BR, Master, 100Mbit: Physical Layer BroadR-Reach®, Clock Mode Master, transmission speed 100 Mbit/s IEEE, Auto, 100BASE-TX: Physical Layer IEEE802.3, active Auto-Negotiation, transmission speed

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareIPConfiguration


# VN8900: IP Configuration

VN8900 » IP Configuration

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|IP Configuration

The VN8900 has one or two network interfaces with an RJ-45 connector, that enable communication with other devices in the network using TCP/IP functions in CAPL or via FDX, for example. Moreover, the VN8900 can be operated as a RT server via this interface. That means the device can be selected in Options dialog and via Home ribbon tab|Group Measurement|Vector Tool Platform|CANoe/CANalyzer|Standalone Mode via its IP address and the connection between computer and VN8900 is based on TCP/IP instead of USB in this case.

In order to communicate with the VN8900 via IP, you will need to set up the corresponding IP configuration for the VN8910 as follows:

- If the device is currently connected via USB:

- [Device…] button of the Network Hardware dialog (Hardware ribbon tab)

- Edit IP Configuration… menu command

- If the device is currently connected via Ethernet and an IP address was advised:

- [Edit…] button of the Options dialog (File ribbon tab)

- Edit IP Configuration… menu command

- In Standalone Manager in the System Settings page.

The configuration applies to the selected network adapter. Please note that only adapters built into the VN8900 are available. You can then choose from the following configuration options:

- Obtain IP address automatically The adapter participates in the Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) based IP assignment service config

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareOSUpdate


# VN8900: Operating System Update

VN8900 » Operating System Update

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|Operating System Update

In certain rare cases, when a software or driver update doesn't suffice, it can be necessary to update the operating system (including all pre-installed drivers) running on the VN8900. This is done by loading a memory image (provided by Vector) to the internal flash memory of the device.

To prepare for this operation, unplug the VN8900 from the power supply. Open the device (for details, see the device manual) and remove the network hardware insert.

- In case of a VN8910/VN8910A you have to connect the Mini-B USB port of the device with a USB port on the user computer using an appropriate USB cable.

- In case of a VN8911/VN8912/VN8912A you have to remove the CFast card from the drive of the device and connect the card to the user computer using the provided card reader.

|  | Updating the Operating System In CANoe, the memory image can only be loaded via the Network Hardware Configuration dialog (Hardware ribbon tab). If at least one of the application channels currently in use is assigned to a hardware channel on the VN8900 in the driver configuration, the [Device] button appears in the corresponding dialog. Click on this button and select Update Operating System... from the menu. This opens the Vector VN8900 Flash Program dialog. Proceed as follows: In the upper part of the window, enter the path to the image file provided by Vector support

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Ribbon/Hardware/NetworkHardware] NetworkHardwareSync


# Hardware Synchronization

Network Hardware » Hardware Synchronization

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Network Hardware|Hardware Sync

Enable the Activate check box, to switch on the Hardware synchronization.

By default use the automatic configuration option.

If the Automatic Configuration option doesn't work, click the Manual configuration option. After that you are able to select a specific channel as »master clock and »sync source respectively. The master clock will then be used for all channels.

Activate the Monitoring active check box, to display all channels that are synchronized in the Write Window. If this check box is deactivated, the Write Window puts out one global message, that the synchronization is generally successful or not.

On the following page you get more information about use cases for hardware synchronization:Time stamp synchronization.
