### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSendA429Word


# Send A429 Word

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Send A429 Word

## Description

Sends an ARINC429 word that is defined the database.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 8.5 SP4 | not available for OnBoard |
| CANalyzer | 8.5 SP4 | not available for OnBoard |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ARINC429 Word | ARINC429 word from the database. The send channel is defined by the assignment of the database to a specific network and cannot be set separately. The signal values of the ARINC429 word can be set with the Set command or indirectly with the = operator from byte values. |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| = | Assigns a byte string consisting of four bytes to the ARINC429 word and sends the ARINC429 word. |
| <no operator> | Sends an ARINC429 word whose signals were previously parameterized with the Set command. |

## Operands

| Operand/with Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| byte string (only with operator =) | The value of the ARINC429 word in the form of a byte string can contain four bytes in hexadecimal notation. The last byte is reserved; its value has no effect on the data content of the ARINC429 word. Example AA BB CC 00 – Byte "00" is ignored, and the rest is assigned to bytes 1 to 3 of the ARINC429 word. |  | Example AA BB CC 00 – Byte "00" is ignored, and the rest is assigned to bytes 1 to 3 of the ARINC429 word. |
|  | Example AA BB CC 00 – Byte "00" is ignored, and the

Use the Set command to set the signals of the frame.

| CANalyzer | 7.5 | — |

| Object | Description |

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSendCANRawFrame


# Send CAN Raw Frame

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Send CAN Raw Frame

## Description

Transmits a CAN frame that has been parameterized without database support.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 7.5 SP2 | — |
| CANalyzer | 7.5 SP2 | — |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CAN Identifier | E.g. 100 for an 11-bit identifier In the absence of other settings, this command applies to frames on channel CAN1You can address other channels by prepending a channel qualifier (e.g. CAN2::100) If you want to use extended identifiers (29 bit), you need to append an x to the identifier (e.g. CAN2::12000x). |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| = | Parametrizes the CAN frame with a byte sequence and sends the frame. |
| <no operator> | Sends a CAN frame that has been parameterized previously with Set CAN Raw Frame. |

## Operands

| Operand/with Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Byte sequence (only with operator "=") | The byte sequence may contain 0 to 8 bytes in hexadecimal representation. |

## Example

|  | Example 1: Operator "=" The following example writes the values 0xAA and 0xBB to byte 0 and byte 1 of a CAN frame with ID 0x100 and sends the frame on channel 1. Command Object Operator Operand Wait [ms] Send CAN Raw Frame CAN1::0x100 = AA BB | Command | Object | Operator | Operand | Wait [ms] | Send CAN Raw Frame | CAN1::0x100 | = | AA BB |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --

Example 005 Channel 1, Label 5o A4292::377 Channel 2, Label 377o A4294:012 Channel 4, Label 12o |  | Example 005 Channel 1, Label 5o A4292::377 Channel 2, Label 377o A4294:012 Channel 4, Label 12o | 005 | Channel 1, Label 5o | A4292::377 | Channel 2, Label 377o | A4294:012 | Channel 4, Label 12o |
|  | Example 005 Channel 1, Label 5o A4292::377 Channel 2, Label 377o A4294:012 Channel 4, Label 12o | 005 | Channel 1, Label 5o | A4292::377 | Channel 2, Label 377o | A4294:012 | Channel 4, Label 12o |
| 005 | Channel 1, Label 5o |
| A4292::377 | Channel 2, Label 377o |
| A4294:012 | Channel 4, Label 12o |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| = | Assigns a byte string consisting of four bytes to the ARINC429 word and sends the ARINC429 word. |
| <no operator> | Sends an ARINC429 word whose data were previously assigned with the Set Raw A429 Word command. |

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSet


# Set

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Set

## Description

Sets the value of a signal, a system variable or an environment variable.

The new signal value is effective on the bus as soon as the message containing the corresponding signal is transmitted.

When the signal layer is used, this message is transmitted automatically.

If the signal layer is not used, you can use the Set CAN Cyclic Frame command to simulate it for the corresponding message (see example).

The functions of the signal layer are currently not supported for bus type ARINC429. To send a new signal value to the bus, the e.g. command Send A429 Word must be active in the sequence after setting the signal value.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 7.5 | not available for OnBoard |
| CANalyzer | 7.5 | not available for OnBoard |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ARINC429 signal | ARINC429 signal definition from the database, via the assignment of database to a network the send channel is defined indirectly. |
| CAN signal | — |
| GMLAN signal | — |
| LIN signal | — |
| System variable | — |
| Environment variable | — |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| = | Assigns a constant value. |
| = [raw] | Assigns a constant value without using the conversion formula from the database. (only with signals and members of system variables) |
| inc | Increments by a constant value. |
| inc [raw] | Increments the raw signal value by a const

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSetCANCyclicFrame


# Set CAN Cyclic Frame

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Set CAN Cyclic Frame

## Description

Starts or stops cyclical transmission of a CAN frame. Use the Set command to set the signals of the frame.

The command is only available if the usage of the signal layer is switched off.

This command overwrites settings (for the selected CAN frame) for cyclical transmission of previous commands.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 7.5 | — |
| CANalyzer | 7.5 | — |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CAN frame | CAN frame from the database. |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| cycle time (ms) | Starts cyclical transmission. |
| stop | Stops cyclical transmission. |

## Operands

| Operand/with Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| In the case of cycle time (ms) | Transmission interval in milliseconds |

## Example

|  | Transmits the EngineState message every 200 milliseconds for a total of 5 seconds. Command Object Operator Operand Wait [ms] Set CAN Cyclic Frame EngineData cycle time (ms) 200 Wait 5000 ms Set CAN Cyclic Frame EngineData stop | Command | Object | Operator | Operand | Wait [ms] | Set CAN Cyclic Frame | EngineData | cycle time (ms) | 200 |  | Wait | 5000 | ms |  |  | Set CAN Cyclic Frame | EngineData | stop |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Command | Object | Operator | Opera

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSetCANCyclicRawFrame


# Set CAN Cyclic Raw Frame

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Set CAN Cyclic Raw Frame

## Description

Starts/stops cyclical transmission of a CAN frame that has been parameterized in raw format (without database support).

You can define the content of the frame with the Set CAN Raw Frame command. This command overwrites settings (for the selected CAN frame) for cyclical transmission of previous commands.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 7.5 SP2 | — |
| CANalyzer | 7.5 SP2 | — |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CAN identifier | E.g. 100 for an 11-bit identifier. In the absence of other settings, this command applies to frames on channel CAN1. You can address other channels by prepending a channel qualifier (e.g. CAN2::100). If you want to use extended identifiers (29 bit), you need to append an x to the identifier (e.g. CAN2::12000x). |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| cycle time (ms) | Starts cyclical transmission. |
| stop | Stops cyclical transmission. |

## Operands

| Operand/with Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| In the case of cycle time (ms) | Transmission interval in milliseconds. |

## Example

|  | Sends the frame with ID 100 every 200 milliseconds for a total duration of 5 seconds on channel CAN 2. Command Object Operator Operand Wait [ms] Set CAN Cyclic Raw Frame CAN2::100 cycle time (ms) 200 Wait 5000 ms Set CAN Cyclic Raw Fram CAN2::100 stop | Command | Object | Op

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSetCANRawFrame


# Set CAN Raw Frame

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Set CAN Raw Frame

## Description

Parametrizes a CAN frame without accessing a database.

In the absence of other settings, this command applies to frames on channel CAN1. You can address other channels by prepending a channel qualifier (e.g. CAN2::100)

If you want to use extended identifiers (29 bit), you need to append an x to the identifier (e.g. CAN2::12000x).

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 7.5 SP2 | — |
| CANalyzer | 7.5 SP2 | — |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| CAN Identifier | Choose this object type to fill the frame completely (all data bytes); e.g. 100. |
| Frame length field | Use this object type if you only want to modify the DLC of a frame; e.g. 100.dlc. |
| Frame data byte | This object type addresses a single data byte in the frame. The other data bytes remain the same. In the case of CAN frames, you can address data bytes "byte0…byte7"; e.g. 100.byte2. |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| = | Assigns a constant or a byte sequence. |

## Operands

| Byte sequence, if the object is a CAN identifier. The byte sequence can contain 0 to 8 bytes in hexadecimal representation. |
| --- |
| Constant (0 <= Operand <= 8), if the object is a length field (DLC). |
| Constant (0 <= Operand <= 255), if the object is a data byte. |

## Example

|  | Example 1 The following example writes the values 0xAA and 0xBB to byte 0 and byte

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSetCyclicA429Word


# Set Cyclic A429 Word

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Set Cyclic A429 Word

## Description

Starts or stops the cyclic sending of an ARINC429 word that is defined in the database.

This command is executed by the ARINC429 hardware scheduler. The content of the word can be defined with command Set (for A429 signals). This command overwrites the settings for cyclic sending of preceding commands for the selected ARINC429 word and the setting for the cycle time from the database.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 8.5 SP4 | not available for OnBoard |
| CANalyzer | 8.5 SP4 | not available for OnBoard |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ARINC429 Word | ARINC429 word from the database. |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| cycle time (ms) | Starts the cyclic sending; the cycle time is specified in milliseconds. |
| stop | Stops the cyclic sending for the selected ARINC429 word. |

## Operands

| Operand/with Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| In the case of cycle time (ms) | Cycle time in milliseconds. |

## Example

|  | Cyclic sending of the same ARINC429 words in two time segments with different cycle. Command Object Operator Operand Wait [ms] Set Cyclic A429 Word Label_337 cycle time 100 1001 Set Cyclic A429 Word Label_337 cycle time 50 1001 Set Cyclic A429 Word Label_337 stop | Command | Object | Operator | Operand | Wait [ms] | Set Cyclic A429 Word | Label_337 | cycle time | 100 | 100

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandSetRawA429Word


# Set Raw A429 Word

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Set Raw A429 Word

## Description

Defines the content and properties of the ARINC429 word independent of the database.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 8.5 SP3 | not available for OnBoard |
| CANalyzer | 8.5 SP3 | not available for OnBoard |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| gap | Overwrites the value of the minimum transmission interval (gap) that was previously set by the channel presetting for the selected ARINC429 word. Example A4293::002.gap: addresses the gap for ARINC429 word on channel 3 with Label = 002o |  | Example A4293::002.gap: addresses the gap for ARINC429 word on channel 3 with Label = 002o |
|  | Example A4293::002.gap: addresses the gap for ARINC429 word on channel 3 with Label = 002o |
| byte | This object type addresses an individual data byte of the ARINC429 word; the other data bytes remain unchanged. The data bytes "byte 0…byte3" can be addressed. Byte3 is reserved; an assigned value has no effect. Example A4291::002.byte1: addresses byte 1 in ARINC429 word 002o on channel 1 |  | Example A4291::002.byte1: addresses byte 1 in ARINC429 word 002o on channel 1 |
|  | Example A4291::002.byte1: addresses byte 1 in ARINC429 word 002o on channel 1 |
| all data | This object type addresses all four bytes of the selected ARINC429 word . The last byte is reserved; its value has no effect on the data content of the ARINC429 word. |

## Operators

### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandWait


# Wait

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Wait

## Description

Waits a specified length of time.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 7.5 | — |
| CANalyzer | 7.5 | — |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| ms | — |

## Operands

—

## Example

|  | In the following example network LIN1 will be sent to sleep for 1 second and waked up afterwards. After the wake-up nothing will be sent on the bus (as long as the is no active scheduler). After another second the bus will be sent to sleep anew for a second. Finally, after the wake-up a LIN Frame with ID 0x0B will be sent. ### [VisualSequencer/VSCommands] VSCommandWaitFor


# Wait For

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Wait For

## Description

Pauses the execution of the sequence until a variable meets a certain condition.

| Availability | Since Version | Restrictions |
| --- | --- | --- |
| CANoe | 8.0 SP4 | — |
| CANalyzer | 8.0 SP4 | — |

## Objects

| Object | Description |
| --- | --- |
| System variable | — |

## Operators

| Operator | Description |
| --- | --- |
| =, !=, >, <, >=, <= | Checks the value of the object and the operand according to the specified operation. |
| in range | Checks whether the value of the object falls within or is at the limits of the specified range (lowlimit <= value <= highlimit). |
| outside range | Checks whether the value of the object falls outside of the specified range. |
| Raw value operators (only with signals) | Operators with the addition [raw] compare the signal value without using the conversion formula from the database: = [raw] != [raw] > [raw] < [raw] >= [raw] <= [raw] in range [raw] outside range [raw] | = [raw] |  | != [raw] |  | > [raw] |  | < [raw] |  | >= [raw] |  | <= [raw] |  | in range [raw] |  | outside range [raw] |  |
| = [raw] |  |
| != [raw] |  |
| > [raw] |  |
| < [raw] |  |
| >= [raw] |  |
| <= [raw] |  |
| in range [raw] |  |
| outside range [raw] |  |

## Operands

| Description |
| --- |
| in the case of =, !=, >, <, >=, <=: constant value |
| in the case of in range, outside range: range limits |

## Example

|  | Example 1 The following sequence waits until the system variab

Visual Sequencer » Commands » Wait For A429 Word

| CANalyzer | 8.5 SP4 | not available for OnBoard |

—

—

| Command | Object | Operator | Operand | Wait [ms] |
| Wait For A429 Word | Label_003 |  |  |  |
| Wait For A429 Word | Label_004 |  |  | 150 |
| Wait For A429 Word | Label_005 |  |  |  |

| Object | Description |

| ARINC-429 Label | Octal value of the label (0o…377o) Without the optional information of the ARINC429 channel, the command acts on channel 1. Other channels can be addressed by putting a channel qualifier in front (example: A4292::377, A4294:005). |

## Operators

—

## Operands

—

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Command | Object | Operator | Operand | Wait [ms] |
| Wait For Raw A429 Word | A4295::002 |  |  |  |
| Wait For Raw A42

### [VTPPlatformManager] AdvancedSearch


# Advanced Search

Connection » General / Connection Settings » Advanced Search

The advanced search can be used to find devices that are physically connected to the user computer, but are not in the same subnet. All found devices are automatically added to the selection list and displayed.

Use the [Apply] button to set a new IP address and subnet mask for the selected device. If the device is then recognized by the Platform Manager as an available device, it is removed from the list of found devices in this dialog and appears instead under Connections in the Platform Manager.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTPPlatformManager] ConnectionAddNetworkDevice


# Edit Connection Parameters

Connection Settings » Edit Connection Parameters

The Edit Connection Parameters dialog contains the tabs LAN Connection and Remote Connection.

| LAN Connection | Remote Connection |  |

Manual configuration allows you to add, delete or change additional IP network devices that are not automatically found on the network. You can specify the following:

- The IP address

- The computer name of the device

- The TCP port number

These settings are retained after the end of the program, i.e. the network devices always appear in the list, regardless of whether they are found in the network or not.

| LAN Connection | Remote Connection |  |

## General

In this tab you create or edit remote connections. A remote connection enables remote administration of a device in the Vector cloud. This additional service can only be booked for VN8810 devices. Select the corresponding connection, enter the VN8810 session to be connected and configure the server.

## Session Configuration

A session identifies a connection to a Vector VN8810 hardware. The Session Identifier corresponds to the device type (e.g. VN8810) and the serial number of the device (e.g. -54) without leading zeros.

## Server Configuration

To configure the connection to the Vector Cloud for remote administration, the server URL must be specified. You will receive this after booking the additional service for remote administration.

Please contact us at VN88-support@vector.com for more inform

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager] ConnectionGeneral


# General / Connection Settings

Vector Tool Platform » Connection » General / Connection Settings

| General |  |

## Device Selection

### Selected Device

Via this list you can select a device. In the device list all found devices are displayed that are supported by the Vector Tool Platform.

- Devices connected to the computer via USB

- IP network devices found in the local network

- All IP network devices, added by the user manually ([Edit...] button)

With [Edit...] you open the Edit Connection Parameters dialog. Here you can add and delete IP network devices or change their connection parameters. These settings are retained even after you exit the program, which means these network devices will always be listed, regardless of whether or not they are found in the network.

With [Advanced Search…] you open the Advanced Search dialog. In this dialog you can find devices that are not in the same address range and configure their IP address.

After selecting a device from the list, the connection is established. The current state of the device is displayed in the status bar. Now you can make other device settings under Tool Platform.

## Vector Tool Platform and Drivers

### Update Components

With [Update…] you can update the Vector Tool Platform and drivers on the connected device.

### Installation Source Path

This is where you enter the current location (on your computer) of the drivers you want to install. You can download an installation package with the current d

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager] UsingVTPControlServer


# Installation of the Vector Tool Platform on a Computer

Vector Tool Platform » Procedures » Installation of the Vector Tool Platform on a Computer

## General

CANoe offers the possibility to execute real-time relevant simulation parts on any computer (RT server), independent of the graphical CANoe user interface on the actual user computer (distributed mode).

This page explains how to run any computer on which the Vector Tool Platform (VTP) has not yet been preinstalled as an RT server.

## Installation of Vector Tool Platform

In order to be able to operate any computer as an RT server, it is necessary to install the Vector Tool Platform on this computer. The following prerequisites are mandatory and restrictions apply.

## Prerequisites

The following technical requirements are necessary to install VTP on a (standard) computer:

- (Standard) computer with Windows operating system ( ≥ Windows 7).

- Correctly installed drivers for any bus hardware connected to the (standard) computer.

- Microsoft .NET Framework (at least version 4.7.1) and Visual C++ redistributable for Visual Studio 2013 and 2017 (available as a download from Microsoft or by installing the VTP Platform Manager on the computer).

- At least the system partition (C:) and a data partition (D:) exist on the (standard) computer.

- The user on the (standard) computer has administrator rights.

- The (standard) computer is connected to the same network as the user computer.

## Installation

The following s

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager] VN8800Series_AppControl


# Applications

Vector Tool Platform » Tool Platform » Applications

| General | Device Settings | Applications | Channels/Network Interfaces | Network Settings |  |

## Application Control

The Vector Tool Platform allows you to install multiple Vector software products on an intelligent network interface. The table shows the software packages installed on the connected device.

| Name | The Name of the software package. |
| --- | --- |
| Version | The installed version of the software package. |
| Active | here you can activate/deactivate the installed software package. |

With [Add…] you can install further software packages.

With [Remove…] you remove the installed package from the connected device.

With [Enable/Disable] you can decide whether an application can be started. Each application can register executable files. An application can only be enabled if at least one executable file is registered. Disabling an application means that the application will not start even if an executable file is registered. Disabled applications can still be used by other enabled applications.

With [Start/Stop] you can control enabled applications.

### Web API Server

The Vector Tool Platform contains an integrated web API server. Running the web server allows unrestricted access to the web services provided by the installed applications. If the web API server is running and the RT hardware is configured with an IP address, that IP address can be used to access the Open API documenta

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager] VN8800Series_ChannelMapping


# Channels/Network Interfaces

Vector Tool Platform » Tool Platform » Channels/Network Interfaces

| General | Device Settings | Application | Channels/Network Interfaces | Network Settings |  |

| Image | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | This page gives you an overview of the channel configuration of the currently connected Vector device as well as related hardware information. The window is divided into the following parts: Channel Mapping Tree view (on the left) Details view (on the right) Table view (below) USB Driver Settings |

## Channel Mapping

With the Channel Mapping you can configure the hardware channels that are used to transmit and receive data in a specific Vector application (e.g. CANoe, CANalyzer, CANape, ...). Before a message can be transmitted or received, you have to specify the hardware channels that have to be used by the Vector application.

With the Vector Platform Manager you configure the channels on the connected device. Using the Vector Hardware Configuration in the Control Panel you configure the channels on your local computer.

### Procedures in the Tree View and Details View

- Select a listed device to see its general information.

- Add an application channel to a hardware channel by right-clicking the hardware channel and selecting an application channel in the context menu.

- Delete an application channel by right-clicking the application channel followed by Delete in the context menu.

- Delete all application channels by right-clickin

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager] VN8800Series_DeviceSettings


# Device Settings

Vector Tool Platform » Tool Platform » Device Settings

| General | Device Settings | Applications | Channels/Network Interfaces | Network Settings |  |

## Date and Time

Here you see the date and time on the connected device. With [Synchronize] you can set date and time of the connected device to the currently set values of the computer.

## Device Mode

|  | Note This section is not available on all devices supported by the Vector Tool Platform. |

The device is powered through the OBD connector’s power PIN. So it is automatically rebooted when it is connected to the vehicle. Since the device's operating system has to be booted first it takes a while until the device is ready for usage. To reduce this boot time the Fast Boot Mode is available, in contrast to the Configuration Mode which is required for updating the system.

Booting in Configuration Mode takes about 35 seconds, booting in Fast Boot Mode takes about 20 seconds only.

Switching between the modes may take up to 2 minutes.

|  | Note To update the data of the device application – such transferring new flash packages to the device – the device can remain in the Fast Boot Mode. |

## Configuration Mode

The Configuration Mode is used to update the drivers, update the system and install new applications. The Configuration Mode is the default mode. So the device is immediately configured to be updated with new applications, etc.

When the configuration is completed it is highly recommended switc

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager] VN8800Series_General


# General

Vector Tool Platform » Tool Platform » General

| General | Device Settings | Applications | Channels/Network Interfaces | Network Settings |  |

## Device Information

In this section you get information about the connected hardware. Here e.g. the serial number, the hardware type, and different version information are displayed.

## Device Description

The host name of the VTP devices is automatically set by default by the device image. However, you also have the possibility of giving a device an individual description in order to identify it quickly. For example, a device could be given the name of the ECU it is to simulate, or a description of the test bench.

### Features of the device description:

- May contain Unicode characters.

- Is also the primary identifier of a device in the display of available connections under Connection.

## Device Control

With [Restart…] you can reboot the operating system of the connected device. All running applications are terminated immediately, and unsaved data is lost.

## Support

With [Save Report…] you can save the status information of the connected device in a file. The data will be compressed and stored on the local computer. On request you can send this data to the Vector support for analysis.

Supported Devices

- On devices of type VN8911 (fr

### [VTPPlatformManager] VTP


# Vector Tool Platform (VTP)

Vector Tool Platform » Vector Tool Platform (VTP)

The Vector Tool Platform (VTP) is a software environment that is installed on various devices (e.g. VN8800, VN8900, VT6000, CANoe RT Rack) that run applications with increased real-time requirements, especially in standalone operation.

|  | Note Starting with CANoe version 13.0 the VTP version 3.0 becomes mandatory for installation. |

VTP offers various services that can be used in Vector applications (e.g. CANoe). On the device side, VTP consists of the components Extended Real Time (ERT) and the VTP Control Server, which are closely linked to the operating system image and the Vector drivers. ERT is not supported on all devices.

VTP also includes the Platform Manager, which can be installed as a standalone application on a user computer and allows access to device functions via IP/USB. Application-specific settings can also be made there via corresponding plug-ins (e.g. for CANoe).

## Extended Real Time

With Extended Real Time as a part of the Vector Tool Platform the latency and determinism of CANoe and CANape are improved. To achieve this, the device is logically divided into two areas. A new area provides Extended Real Time in which predefined functions can be executed under real-time conditions.

Extended Real Time is supported by the Vector Hardware VN8912A, VN8911, VN8914 and VT6051A.

## VTP Control Server

The VTP Control Server allows the installation of drivers and applications 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager/CANoePlugIN] CANoePlugIn


# CANoe/CANalyzer Plug-in

Vector Tool Platform » CANoe/CANalyzer Plug-in

In CANoe, the distributed mode as well as the standalone mode is controlled by a dialog that can be opened via Home ribbon tab|Group Measurement|Vector Tool Platform. If you have installed the Vector Tool Platform, this dialog automatically loads the plug-in from the connected device.

As an additional tool, the Vector Platform Manager is supplied with CANoe, which you can install on any other computer. Here, the CANoe/CANalyzer plug-in can also be displayed.

The CANoe/CANalyzer plug-in is supported by all devices.

## Installation of the CANoe/CANalyzer Plug-in

New devices are automatically shipped with the current CANoe version and installed CANoe/CANalyzer plug-in. The device can be used immediately. To be able to use the new functions with existing devices, the CANoe/CANalyzer plug-in must be installed automatically during a CANoe software update:

- For devices that are used in CANoe, the software version is checked during connection setup in distributed mode and updated automatically if necessary. This check also takes place when a device is selected via ribbon tab Home|group Measurement|Vector Tool Platform dialog in CANoe.

- For devices that can only be reached via the Standalone Manager and running a CANoe version < 11.0 SP3, a CANoe software update must be performed once via the Standalone Manager. To do this, proceed as described in the Standalone Manager help.Subsequent software updates

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager/CANoePlugIN] CANoePlugInDeviceControl


# Standalone Mode / Device Control

CANoe Plug-in » Standalone Mode / Device Control

| Standalone Mode / Device Control | Distributed Mode | Remote Files | Notifications | Execution Environment | vFlash |  |

## Device Status

Displays the current status of the device with respect to standalone mode.

| Status | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Standalone mode deactivated | The standalone mode is not activated on the device. |
| Standalone mode activated | The standalone mode is activated on the device. This means that the measurement can be started via CANoe/CANalyzer ([Start] button) or via a keypad connected to the device. This operating mode is also activated immediately when the device is switched on. |
| Standalone measurement running | The standalone mode is activated on the device and the measurement runs there (using the real-time relevant part of the configuration loaded on the device). |
| Standalone measurement completed | The standalone mode is activated on the device and the measurement there is finished. |

The following buttons are available for controlling the standalone mode on the selected device.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [Activate] | The standalone mode is activated on the device. The measurement with the standalone configuration loaded on the device can now be started either via CANoe/CANalyzer or with the aid of a keypad connected to the device. Note: No physical connection to the computer is required during standalone mode. If a physical

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager/CANoePlugIN] CANoePlugInDistributedMode


# Distributed Mode

CANoe Plug-in » Distributed Mode

| Standalon Mode / Device Control | Distributed Mode | Remote Files | Notifications | Execution Environment | vFlash |  |

In this page you can set where the simulation should be executed.

|  | Note These settings are configuration-independent settings of CANoe. |

|  | Note Starting with CANoe 11.0 the distributed operation for VN8910, VN8910A, VT6010, VT6050, VT6050A and older RT Racks is no longer supported. VN8910 and VN8910A can still be used in interface mode. |

## RT Server

CANoe offers the possibility to execute the real-time relevant simulation parts on a separate computer (RT server), i.e. separate from the graphical user interface (user computer), refer to distributes mode.

### Current RT Server

If an RT server is currently configured, the server and connection type (USB or TCP/IP) are displayed next to Current RT server.

Under Current occupation of selected device as RT server, you can see whether the device selected under Connection|Device Selection is already occupied as RT server, so that the current instance of CANoe cannot connect to the RT kernel on the device. Possible assignment states are here:

- The device is currently not used as RT server and is therefore available for the current instance of CANoe.

- The device is used by another instance of CANoe/CANalyzer (on the same or another user computer) as RT server in distributed mode. In addition, the user (user name, computer) that occupies the

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager/CANoePlugIN] CANoePlugInExecutionEnvironment


# Execution Environment

CANoe Plug-in » Execution Environment

| Standalone Mode /Device Control | Distributed Mode | Remote Files | Notifications | Execution Environment | vFlash |  |

## Update RT Kernel

Under Current Version of the CANoe/CANalyzer RT kernel on the device, the software version that is currently installed on the selected device is displayed.

The software version on the device can be updated in different ways:

- In CANoe/CANalyzer:

- Update the RT kernel to the currently used CANoe/CANalyzer version: Click on the [Update] button.

- In addition, CANoe checks the software version when a device is selected via Home ribbon tab|Group Measurement|Vector Tool Platform and suggests an update if necessary.

- A check of the software version with suggested update also takes place when establishing a connection to the RT server in distributed mode in CANoe.

- When using Platform Manager or CANoe/CANalyzer:

- Update based on a packed installation: Click on the [Update] button and select the desired archive file.

- When using Standalone Manager:

- In the case of devices that are not accessible from CANoe and on which a CANoe version < 11.0 SP3 is running, a CANoe software update must be performed once via the Standalone Manager using an RTENV file. Please refer to the corresponding notes on the help pages for the Standalone Manager. Subsequent software updates can then be performed using one of the above methods.

|  | Notes When you start the software update, 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTPPlatformManager/CANoePlugIN] CANoePlugInNotifications


# Notifications

CANoe Plug-in » Notifications

| Standalone Mode/Device Control | Distributed Mode | Remote Files | Notifications | Execution Environment | vFlash |  |

## Notifications

This page can be used to display Write Window notifications recorded on the selected device while CANoe is running in standalone mode. To do this, select the file to be displayed and the content of the file is then displayed in the window.

You can select parts or all messages in the window and transfer them to other programs via the clipboard. Alternatively, you can save the messages in a file. Use the commands in the context menu or the corresponding key combinations.

If you have selected the current message file, new messages are automatically displayed. If Activate autoscrolling is activated, the window always shows the last entries of the file.

With many messages it can happen that a part of it gets lost. In this case, a corresponding error message appears in the list.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTPPlatformManager/CANoePlugIN] CANoePlugInRemoteFiles


# Remote Files

CANoe Plug-in » Remote Files

| Standalone Mode/Device Control | Distributed Mode | Remote Files | Notifications | Execution Environment | vFlash |  |

## Remote File Browser

This page provides a file browser that enables access to various types of files located on the selected device. The available file types are displayed in a tree view.

Depending on the file type, different operations are possible.

- [Add]: One or more standalone configuration files that were generated from CANoe using Export beforehand can be loaded onto the device. It is also possible to load user files on the device, that are used in a standalone configuration.

- [Delete]: The selected files are deleted on the device.

- [Save]: The selected files are transferred to the user computer and saved there in the specified local directory.

- For configuration files, the standard configuration as well as the currently loaded configuration (only with activated standalone mode) can be changed in the context menu.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

# vFlash

CANoe Plug-in » vFlash

| Standalone Mode/Device Control | Distributed Mode | Remote Files | Notifications | Execution Environment | vFlash |  |

On this page you manage the vFlash projects of the vFlash tool installed on the selected device.

|  | Cross Reference You can find further information in the document Automated flashing and testing with CANoe, vFlash and VN8900 family/VT System. |

## vFlash Application

Under Current vFlash Version on the device, the software version currently installed on the selected device is displayed. The software version on the device can be updated using a VTPAPP file in the Tool Platform Application Control. You can save a support report on your computer by clicking [Save].

## vFlash Projects

All vFlash projects available on the selected device are listed here with a time stamp of the latest change. You can use the [Add] button to transfer additional vFlash projects to the selected device. You delete the selected vFlash projects from the device with the [Remove] button.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTPPlatformManager/CANoePlugIN] StandaloneModeLoggingConfiguration


# Standalone Mode: Configuration Settings

Standalone Mode » Logging in Standalone Mode

|  | Note Note that the settings you make in this dialog are configuration settings. These are therefore saved in the configuration open in CANoe. In order for these settings to take effect on the standalone device, you must therefore first download the current configuration to the device – just like for all other configuration settings. |

## Logging Block

Here you can activate or deactivate an upstream Logging Block for standalone mode and configure the logging properties (e.g. the name of the logging file) themselves ([Configure...] button).

When Use standalone logging has been activated, Trigger Blocks and CAPL Blocks can be inserted optionally.

## Trigger Block

With this Trigger Block logging can be started and stopped with CAPL (trigger, startLogging, stopLogging).

When the Trigger Block is configured using the [Configure...] button, you can specify a pre-trigger time (logged events before occurrence of the trigger condition) and a post-trigger time (logged events after completion of the Trigger Block). Furthermore you can use Stop after n Trigger Blocks to specify that the measurement will be stopped after a certain number of trigger starts. In the Buffer size input box, you can limit the number of messages that will be saved during the pre-trigger or post-trigger time.

## CAPL Filter

This CAPL block can be used, in particular, for filter purposes and is added upstream of t

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystem


# VT System

Connecting to External Hardware » VT System

|  | The VT System is a modular hardware system that connects an ECU's I/O lines for testing purposes. The VT System, for instance, records the voltages of the ECU's output lines, pre-processes them (average, RMS value, PWM measurement) and makes them available to test cases in CANoe. It can also be used to apply signals from CANoe to the ECU's input lines. The sensors and actuators are simulated electronically on the VT modules, e.g. via an electronic load or a decade resistor. Original sensors and actuators can also be connected via the VT module. The module's relays can be used to generate diverse line failures and short circuits. |

| Quick Access New Features for Testing Available Modules Available Extensions Configuration Setup Configuration Calibration Ground Connection Activating the Alternative Timebase Integration Time and Transfer Cycle Time Timestamps Safety Functions Graphical VT System Control User-Programmable FPGA | Quick Access | New Features for Testing Available Modules Available Extensions | Configuration | Setup Configuration Calibration Ground Connection Activating the Alternative Timebase Integration Time and Transfer Cycle Time Timestamps Safety Functions Graphical VT System Control User-Programmable FPGA | Functions and Control Basic procedures for use with CANoe Using the VT System Offline Mode Notes for use with distributed mode VT System and Extended Real Time Time Stamps Firmware Update Ma

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemAlternativeTimeBase


# Activating the Alternative Timebase

VT System » Activating the Alternative Timebase

An alternative timebase is implemented in CANoe, which makes a more precise time available using the processor timer. This substantially improves adherence to cycle times when values are retrieved from the VT System hardware.

|  | Note on Editing You can further improve the time stamps by making the following adjustment in the CAN.INI file: [PCBOARD]TimerRate=25 |

These settings should only be used if you are running the VT System. However, if you are running it, you should activate these settings.

Integration Time and Transfer Cycle Time | VT System – Timestamps

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTSystem] VTSystemBusBar


# Using the Bus Bars

VT System » Using the Bus Bars

The VT1004/VT1104 and VT2004 VT System modules have two bus bars each.

Lines a and b can be laid separately onto the internal bus bar lines via corresponding relays. These can be used to transmit the signal to an external device via the bus bar connections. Several channels can be bundled via one bus bar.

The image illustrates a bus bar on the VT1004/VT1104 or VT2004. All modules include two such bus bars.

Bus bar 1 typically carries Vbatt and Gnd (battery voltage and device ground), so that Vbatt and Gnd based short circuits of individual channel lines can be generated via the corresponding bus bar relays. However, this means that line a can initially only be connected to Vbatt and line b to Gnd.

By swapping the bus bar lines (i.e. both swap relays for bus 1 are on), you can also create ground based short circuits on channel line a and Vbatt based short circuits on channel line b. Bus bar line swapping is a module property, not a property of the individual channels. For this reason, you need to adjust the corresponding settings in the module namespace, not in the channel namespace.

Bus bar 2 remains freely available and can be used for customization. For instance, it can be used to connect an external load in case the internal electronic load is insufficient. It can also be used to generate short circuits between the lines connecting different channels.

## Connecting the Bus Bars to other VT Modules

If there are n

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemCalibration


# VT System - Calibration

VT System » Calibration

## Calibration

To improve the accuracy of measurements and output several supported VT modules can be calibrated using the VT System Calibration Manager. The tool is located via Start menu|<…>|<CANoe>|Tools.For further information on calibrating VT modules see VT System Calibration Manager.

## Test Specification

For modules or functions that cannot be calibrated a test specification is available with which the compliance with tolerances can be tested against the requirements of the VTSystem Manual (PDF).

- The test specification describes the setup and execution of the different tests. Usually these are executed using a measurement device (e.g. oscilloscope, multimeter etc.), a small cable set and the VT System Control Window in CANoe.The test specification is located in CANoe's documents directory: Test Specification (PDF).

- The test report consists of multiple Excel sheets. During the test execution all measured values are inserted into these tables. The comparison between the measured deviations and the allowed tolerances is performed automatically. All of the test results are displayed in a separate column.The test report is located in CANoe's documents directory: Test Report (XLS).

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTSystem] VTSystemCANoe


# Notes for Usage with CANoe

VT System » Notes for Usage with CANoe

## Usage in CANoe

The measurement and output values, along with many settings (e.g. for the relays), are made available in CANoe via system variables. The VT System configuration creates these automatically. They use the reserved VTS namespace.

A VTS sub-namespace is created for each VT module channel and for each VT module. The corresponding channel and module system variables are created in these sub-namespaces. You can specify the name of each channel and module; however, names must be unique across all modules and channels. The names are saved to the VT System configuration.

|  | Example 1: System Variables and Namespaces VTS::LowBeamRight::Avg System variable Avg of the LowBeamRight channelVTS::Light_Modul Namespace of the Light_Module module |

To prevent creating dependencies in the test script related to the way channels are distributed onto modules, channels are not created as sub-namespaces within the module namespaces.

In addition, a number of settings are adjusted using special CAPL functions that use a method syntax to call the system variables and namespaces of the VT System's modules and channels.

|  | Example 2 sysvar::VTS::LowBeamRight::Avg.SetIntegrationTime (0.1);sysvar::VTS::LowBeamRight.ResetMinMax (); |

The vtsystem_configure command is used to performed these tasks for XML test modules. You can also use existing patterns in XML test modules to access the VT System's system vari

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemCANoeRT


# Notes for Usage with Distributed Mode

VT System » Notes for Usage with Distributed Mode

In principle there are no restrictions when running the VT System with distributed mode.

The VT System is connected to the RT server, i.e. the computer on which the CANoe real-time component is executed and to which the network interfaces are connected.

|  | Caution! A separate Ethernet interface must be used for the VT System on the RT server. In principle it is often possible to use the same Ethernet interface also for connections to the RT server and the user computer. However, the two systems (internal CANoe communication and communication with the VT System) will steal bandwidth from one another, which greatly limits the practicable running of the system. |

|  | Note The VT System's interface settings are saved in the user computer configuration file. If you intend to use it without the RT server, you must adjust the network interface settings accordingly in the Options dialog (Extensions|VT System). |

Activating the Alternative Timebase | Integration Time and Transfer Cycle Time

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTSystem] VTSystemConfiguration


# VT System Configuration

VT System » Configuration

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Configuration

In this dialog you can create or modify a VT System configuration. You can add modules to or remove modules from your configuration (context menu). In addition, you can change the order of modules in the configuration via drag and drop.

Each module is represented as a node in the tree. The leaves of the node represent the module's different channels, whereas the module node itself represents the complete module. You can change the names of modules and channels. The channel name serves as the namespace for the active measurement values and settings of the channel. Likewise, the module name is used as the namespace for module-wide variable settings.

The available measurement values are shown for each channel. These can be activated and parameterized. A system variable is created for each of the activated measurement values.

|  | Example The Avg measurement value (this name is preset and is specified in the dialog) of the LowBeamRight channel (this name was set by the user for the given channel) can be read via the system variable VTS::LowBeamRight::Avg. |

In addition there are comparable system variables in this namespace for writing values. These system variables are not displayed in the configuration dialog, however, since they cannot be configured or deactivated. They are always created automatically.

It is also possible to create or modify a VT System configuration and to create 

[...truncated for brevity...]

# Configuration – Hardware

VT System » Configuration » Connected VT System

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Configuration – Hardware

In this dialog you can choose the network adapter your VT System is connected to. You can also adapt the current VT System configuration to the actually connected hardware.

To operate a VT System, the module configuration has to correspond exactly with the currently connected modules (with respect to the type, order and number of modules). In order to operate an existing configuration with a different hardware setup, the configuration has to be adapted accordingly.

## [Network Adapter]

Here you can choose the network adapter your VT System is connected to. When you change the selection, the current adapter will be scanned for connected modules. If modules are found, they will be shown in the list below. You can also trigger the scan manually by pressing Scan for modules.

## [Connected VT System Modules]

This list shows the currently connected VT System modules. If no modules are shown, please make sure that

- the VT System is turned on

- you have selected the correct network adapter

- no other instances of CANoe or the VT System Utilities are running in the background

- Auto Match – CANoe tries to automatically match your configured VT System modules to the actually connected modules.

- For connected modules that have no matching counterpart in your configuration, a new module configuration will be created.

- Configured modules that cannot be

VT System » Configuration » Relay Constraint Editor

On the top of the dialog setting and operation are displayed. For these you can set here a condition. The selected operation can only be executed if the condition is met.

### [VTSystem] VTSystemExtendedRealTime


# Extended Real Time

VT System » Extended Real Time

If Extended Real Time (ERT) is available, additional transfer cycle times of 200µs and 500µs can be set for certain values. Those transfer cycle times make it possible to achieve higher sampling rates.

Of the VT6000-modules ERT is currently supported by the VT6051A. To use ERT with a VT6051A you have to enable the feature in Options dialog. Use the button [Edit...]|Edit Extended Real Time Configuration....

Additional lower transfer cycles are available for certain values of the VT1004, VT7001 and VT2816 modules. Those values are marked in the respective lists of available values.

The additional transfer cycle of 500µs is also available for Avg128us of the VT1004 module and Bits of the modules VT1004, VT2516 and VT2848. For those values sampling rates > 1kHz are accomplished by transferring multiple values of different age at the same time. Hence no higher sampling rate is achieved here. Instead it is possible to avoid spontaneous short gaps in value transmission.

|  | Caution! Using transfer cycle times < 1ms cause a high load. Therefore they should be used for very few values at the same time. Preferably they should be used for short spans of time only (see page Integration Time and Transfer Cycle Time) |

|  | Caution! Transfer Cycle Times < 1ms will only work in a sensible way if ERT is available. If those transfer cycles are configured on systems without ERT support or if you are loading such a configuration there

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemFirmwareUpdate


# Firmware Update

VT System » Firmware Update

## Introduction

In order to use the expanded functionality, you may have to update the firmware of your VT modules.

If CANoe requires a new firmware version, you are notified of this in an error message in the Write Window. Without this update, the respective module cannot be used in CANoe.

A standalone tool is available for updating the firmware, which can be found in the CANoe installation directory:[CANoeInstallationFolder]\Exec32\VTSFirmwareUpdater.EXE

|  | Note Before starting the firmware update, you must close CANoe, because otherwise the network adapter may be blocked, thus preventing the firmware update. |

In order that the firmware update can establish a connection to the VT System, the VT System backplane has to be connected directly to the computer on which the tool is executed. Especially when using a Realtime Module (e.g. VT6011) it is necessary, to modify the Ethernet connection temporarily while the firmware update tool is executed on the computer on which the CANoe GUI is running. Generally this is essential when using distributed mode, what means that CANoe runs distributed in two computers. You have to take care of this even when a VN8900 is used together with the VT System. The connection between the VT System backplane and the VT6011 and VN8900 (or similar) respectively is replaced by a connection to the computer on which the firmware update tool is executed. After executing the update the original Eth

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemGroundConnection


# Ground Connection

VT System » Ground Connection

## General

Correct grounding is absolutely essential when operating a VT System. In a test setup that includes a VT System, a distinction is made between the following ground lines, which are usually separate:

- DGND:The ground for the power supply unit that powers the VT System itself.The power supply unit is connected to the backplane.If several racks are in use, the grounds for all power supply units are connected.

- AGND:The reference ground for all VT System measurements and input voltages.AGND can be connected to the backplane (power supply plug).

- GND/clamp 31:The ground for the ECU power supply.This comes with the power supply unit that powers the ECU.

## Please Note the Following Rules

- GND/clamp 31 and AGND need to be connected. The VT System only works when ECU ground is connected to AGND. If you are using the VT7001, the VT7001 automatically generates a connection from GND1 to AGND.

- AGND and DGND need to be connected at exactly one point.This can be done in two ways:

- Bridging the AGND and DGND ground pins at the power supply plug on the backplane.You can only do this if you are using a single backplane. If you are using several backplanes, you would typically do this at the first one, with AGND itself also connected to ECU ground.

- When you are using the VT7001, AGND and DGND are connected automatically.In this case, AGND and DGND should not be bridged at the power supply plug on the backplane.



[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemIntegrationTimeTransferCycleTime


# Integration Time and Transfer Cycle Time

VT System » Integration Time and Transfer Cycle Time

The VT System modules function as follows when making measurement signals available:

- The measurement electronics scan the signal with great frequency (on the VT1004, for instance, with 250 kSamples/s). These measurement values are raw values that cannot be transmitted to CANoe in their original form.

- The VT module pre-processes these raw values; for example, it can calculate an average over a given time span. This time span, which can be adjusted, is called the integration time in CANoe. This is a gliding time span. An average with an integration time of 10 ms, for instance, is recalculated every 1 ms using a time span of the past 10 ms. The 10 ms window thus glides over the measurement values every 1 ms (this ratio of 10:1 applies to all values calculated).

- CANoe cyclically collects the values thus calculated on the system and uses them to refresh the contents of the corresponding system variables. The transfer cycle time, which can be adjusted, is independent of the frequency with which the values are recalculated (see 2.). This collection cycle time is called the transfer cycle time.Please note that measurement values not be updated more often than calculated by the hardware. An average value with an integration time of 1 s will only be updated every 100 ms even if a shorter cycle time is set.

For example, you can derive the average of the past 10 ms on the module e

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemModules


# Modules and Extensions

VT System » Modules and Extensions

## VT System – Modules

| Photo | Short Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | VT1004/VT1104 – Load and Measurement Module The VT1004/VT1104 Load and Measurement Module has four identical channels, each of which can be connected to an ECU output. The module has an internal electronic load for each channel. The VT1004/VT1104 measures and pre-processes the ECU's output signal. |
|  | VT2004 – Stimulation Module The VT2004 Stimulation Module has four identical channels, each of which can be connected to an ECU input. To simulate a sensor, the module can apply voltage at the ECU input and generate a resistance via an internal decade resistor. For potentiometer stimulation, a voltage is generated that is proportional to a specified factor and a set reference voltage. |
|  | VT2516 – Digital Module The VT2516 Digital Module has 16 identical channels, each of which can be connected to an ECU input or output. The VT2516 measures and preprocesses the voltage level of each channel's signal line. Also the digital data stream of each channel's signal line is captured. The VT2516 can output a digital voltage signal on each channel. The ECU interprets this as a sensor signal. High and Low levels can be set separately for each channel. |
|  | VT2710 – Serial Interface Module The VT2710 serial interface module contains four slots. Each one can be equipped with a piggyback (see below) for the serial automotive interfaces PSI5 and SENT. 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemSafetyFunctions


# Safety Functions

VT System » Safety Functions

The safety functions are used to prevent faulty setting of the VT System and thus to protect the VT System and its hardware from damage.

You can prevent the following, for example, using the safety functions:

- Inputs of your control units from being stimulated with excessive voltage

- A short-circuit being created by a relay while excessive currents are flowing

To implement such requirements, you must define so-called constraints.

## Constraints

Constraints can be defined for most settings of the VT System.

Proceed as follows:

- Open the VT System Configuration dialog.

- Select the channel concerned there.

- Select the Constraints page. Constraints can be displayed, edited and created here. The constraints cannot be changed during a measurement. It's best to specify the constraints for a configuration before the first measurement is started.

For setting values with which relays are switched, a condition under which the relay can be closed or opened can be specified with the constraints. In most cases, the condition checks the value of any desired long or float system variable. Thus a short-circuit can be prevented from switching if the measured voltage on a channel exceeds a certain value, for example. The opening or closing of a relay can also be forbidden in general.

|  | Caution! The constraint is only checked before the relay is switched. Continuous checking is not carried out. |

Conditions can be specified 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemSetup


# Setup

VT System » Setup

|  | Step by Step Procedure: Setup VT System To use the VT System with CANoe, please proceed as follows: Connect the VT System to the computer running CANoe via an Ethernet interface(see Notes for use with distributed mode). Now activateVT System in the VT System configuration dialog (Home ribbon tab|Hardware|VT System|Configuration).Choose the network interface (Intel® 82574L Gigabit Network Connection) on the VT6000 that is connected to the VT System backplane. Configure the VT System's modules and channels(Hardware ribbon tab|VT System Configuration).When you add a new VT System configuration, it is created automatically based on the VT System that is currently connected. You can work immediately with this configuration, though you may need to activate the measurement values to be used. |

The VT System configuration is saved to the same folder as the CANoe configuration with the same name but using VTCFG as the file extension. If this file exists, it is automatically used for the CANoe configuration. However, you must make sure that if you move or copy the CANoe configuration, you move or copy this file along with it, otherwise CANoe will not be able to find the file. You can also copy this file to other CANoe configurations or import it via the VT System configuration dialog.

Activation of the VT System and setting of the network adapter are settings that are specific to a particular CANoe installation, because they are dependent on the circ

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemStandaloneMode


# Standalone Mode

VT System » Standalone Mode

Effective with CANoe 8.2 SP2 you can use VT System also in CANoe's standalone mode. For the most part the needed files are automatically taken for the standalone configuration, as long as the VT System integration is already activated at that time when you create the configuration.

An additional step is only necessary for files that are loaded on the VT System modules when outputting Arbitrary Curves and Bitstreams. You have to register these files as so-called user files in CANoe's Options dialog (Extensions|User Files) to have them being found during execution in standalone mode. Thereby these files are also included in the configuration of the standalone mode. No further modifications are necessary; e.g. it is not necessary to adjust the path in CAPL test programs when loading arbitrary curves.

|  | Note on Using Systems with Lower Performance If you use a VT System in standalone mode the system requirements are higher than in normal mode. On systems with lower performance problems may occur that normally not occur in normal mode. In contrast the performance of the real-time modules VT6051/VT6051A are sufficient. Generally for the use of a VT System in standalone mode a system with at least two processors is recommended. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTSystem] VTSystemSysVarInterfaces


# System Variable Interfaces

VT System » System Variable Interfaces

| Quick access |
| --- |
| General | Interface check |
| System variable interfaces as parameter types | Access to namespace |
| Namespace transfer | Error codes |
| Parameter transmission | Further functions |

## General

There are groups of system variables that belong together logically and occur more than once as a group. Examples can be found, for example, in the modules of the VT System. Each system variable namespace that represents a VT1004 channel contains the same system variables that can be used to change settings and query measured values. This grouping is represented using system variable interfaces. In addition to accessing system variables, system variable interfaces can also be used to call member functions, such as SetLoadMode for VT1004 channels.

System variable interfaces define a data type that can be used for defining auxiliary functions in CAPL, similar to structures. For namespaces to which a system variable interface is assigned, it is displayed as a data type in the Symbol Explorer and in the System Variable Configuration dialog. Such a data type makes it possible to use an auxiliary function for all similar channels, e. g. VT1004 channels. Otherwise, this is only possible with the VT System, for example, if the path of a channel is passed as a string. Compared to string transfer, system variable interfaces have the advantage that many errors can be detected earlier. This way, t

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemTimestamps


# Timestamps

VT System » Timestamps

For the bigger part of VT System measuring values, timestamps are made available, that originate directly from the VT System hardware. For this reason the delay of the transmission from the VT System hardware to CANoe has no negative impact on the accuracy of the timestamps.

You have to use the CAPL function sysGetVariableTimeNS to have access to the timestamp that has been provided by the VT System hardware. The function returns a timestamp that uses the same time base as the CANoe simulation time. It is necessary to use this CAPL function since the VT System timestamps are not identical to the current CANoe simulation time, which you can query e.g. with timeNowInt64. Basically these synchronized timestamps that origin from the VT hardware are used when logging VT System measuring values or for the Graphics Window display. The page VT System: Trigger shows an example of how to use this command.

In rare cases it can be reasonable to access the unsynchronized timestamps of the VT System hardware directly. This is the case if solely VT timestamps are compared among each other. In such a case you avoid minimal inaccuracies that result from synchronizing with the CANoe simulation time and hence with the connected hardware devices. For that you can use the CAPL function SysGetOrigTimeNS. A comparison with timestamps from other sources — e.g. CAN interfaces — is not possible.

In order that VT System timestamps use the same time base as the 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemTrigger


# Trigger

VT System » Trigger

Triggers make it possible to measure a signal with higher accuracy than is possible when using the system variables with measured values. In concrete you can get more accurate time stamps for edges and you can thus also calculate the distance between edges with higher accuracy. Triggers can be configured with manifold settings to cover a wide spectrum of applications. Every VT1004/VT1104 module provides 8 triggers.

## Basic Configuration of the Input Channels

Triggers are working on a digital input signal. Hence the threshold between low and high voltage levels must be set on every input channel that is to be used in combination with triggers. The value of the threshold must be selected according to the signal that is being analyzed of course. The threshold can be set e.g. using the CAPL command SetPWMThreshold.

## Configuration of Triggers

For CAPL nodes and test modules you can use the functions SetTriggerParams and SetTriggerParamsEx to configure triggers. The parameters of the function SetTriggerParams are sufficient to cover simple applications like getting a time stamp for the next rising edge. It sets the input channel of the module that is used and whether the trigger detects rising or falling edges. The additional parameters of SetTriggerParamsEx make it possible to detect and measure more complex sequence of edges or pulses by combining multiple triggers.

## Activating Triggers

A trigger must be activated explicitly before it c

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemTroubleshooting


# Troubleshooting

VT System » Troubleshooting

|  | VT System: No modules found! CANoe does not detect any VT System modules (Message "VT System: No modules found!" appears in Write Window), even though the modules are connected and the correct network card is configured. If CANoe does not detect any VT System modules, this may be because they are already in use by another CANoe instance or by the program. Only one program can access the VT System at a time. In order for the modules to be detected, the other programs need to be closed. In the case of CANoe, you can simply switch off VT System support in the other instances. It may also be the case that access to the network card is being blocked by firewalls and other security programs, or by a network configuration program. This may occur, for example, with Endpoint Protection from Symantec and Control Point from DELL. To use the VT System, terminate these programs and/or release the network card. Please note that doing so can compromise the security of your system. |

|  | TCP/UDP Ports TCP/UDP Ports used by CANoe. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTSystem] VTSystemUserProgrammableFPGA


# User-Programmable FPGA

VT System » User-Programmable FPGA

The user-programmable FPGA feature is used to extend the standard functionality of a VT module with additional functions, that are defined by the user. For this purpose an additional FPGA is provided on the VT module, that can access the inputs and outputs of the module and communicate with the CANoe configuration via system variables.

|  | Note To use this feature the relevant VT module has to be available in the User FPGA variant. |

Programming and managing the user-programmable FPGA is done with the VT System FPGA Manager, which you'll find under Tools in the CANoe folder.

If there is a user-programmable FPGA on the VT module, depending on the configuration additional system variables are available for this module. They are managed by the VT System FPGA Manager.

- Is it about measurement values you have to activate them like other variables in CANoe's VT System Configuration dialog, before they can be used.

- If the user-programmable FPGA is used to stimulate values, the Stimulation Mode of the used module has to be set to Voltage and the Curve Type has to be set to User FPGA.

VT System User-Programmable FPGA Manual (PDF) | VT System Manual (PDF)

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTSystem] VTSystemUsingInNET


# Usage in .NET

VT System » Usage in .NET

In CANoe 7.5 SP2 and higher, the complete scope of the VT System can be used in .NET test cases. In so doing, the modules and channels of the system can be addressed using their names, which enables simple creation of abstract tests.

## Access to Module and Channels

The IVTSystem interface is used as the interface for accessing the VT System. This interface is defined in the Vector.CANoe.VTS.DLL assembly, which is added automatically as a reference to every new .NET test module.

At the start of a test module, an object that implements the IVTSystem interface can be created. Access to the VT System via VTSystem.Instance is also possible, but creating of the object requires a more transparent source code.

|  | Example // Get the VTSystem instance IVTSystem vts = VTSystem.Instance; |

The object enables access to the modules and their channels in two ways:

- Access via the module or channel name This approach uses the GetModule and GetChannel methods. The name assigned for the module or channel in CANoe is transferred as the parameter. Example // Get channel 1 of a VT1004 module IVT1004Channel vt1004_Channel1 = vts.GetChannel("VT1004_Ch1") as IVT1004Channel; // Get the VT7001 module called "Power" IVT7001 vt7001 = vts.GetModule("Power") as IVT7001; The interfaces thus obtained are derived from the general IVTModule or IVTChannel interfaces. In order to be able to access module- and channel-specific methods and properties these mu

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem] VTSystemUsingOfflineMode


# Using the VT System Offline Mode

VT System » Using the VT System Offline Mode

As of CANoe 11.0, one or more VT System modules can be switched to an offline mode. In this mode, it is no longer necessary for the actual module hardware to be present in your VT System.

The VT System Offline Mode is completely independent of whether you operate CANoe with a real or simulated bus. This allows you to use VT System modules in offline mode, even if the remaining hardware (including other VT System modules) is operated in real (online) mode.

## [Use Cases]

The VT System Offline Mode is especially helpful if you want to run a CANoe-configuration without the corresponding VT System hardware. To do this, simply switch the non-existing modules in your configuration to offline mode.

## [Offline Module Behavior]

Modules that are in offline mode do not provide measurement values. However, if you need measurement values for the correct execution of your CANoe configuration, you can generate them yourself. CANoe provides you with an offline stimulation variable for each measurement value. If you write to this variable, the corresponding measurement value is also updated. The offline stimulation variables can be found in the Offline namespace of the respective VT System channel.

All stimulation variables of the offline modules can be written to as usual. However, this has no effect on the CANoe measurement or the connected VT System hardware. Any constraints configured are taken into 

[...truncated for brevity...]

| General |

All channels have a two-wire layout, so the ECU outputs do not need to work against a fixed potential such as the ground. Line b typically carries the smaller potential, e.g. ground. |
| Notes on Module Series VT1004/VT1104 |

In addition to the expanded measuring range, the channels of the new VT1004A/VT1104 variants also support switchover to a mode with lower impedance. In this mode the accuracy is better for the measurement of

### [VTSystem/VT1004] VT1004BusBars


# Bus Bars

VT System » Modules » VT1004/VT1104 » Bus Bars

The VT1004/VT1104 has two bus bars on which the lines a and b of the four channels can be switched. The two lines and can be switched independently of one another, i.e., channel line a can be switched independently of channel line b to line a in respect to line b of one of the two bus bars.

The module also has swap relays between the bus bar connection and the internal bus bar lines on both bus bars. These relays can be switched individually, so that for instance bus bar input a can be switched onto both internal bus bar lines a and b.

Via the bus bar connections a signal switched to the bus bar can be transmitted to an external device, additionally several channels can be bundled via one bus bar.

Bus bar 1 typically carries Vbatt and Gnd (battery voltage and device ground), so that Vbatt and Gnd based short circuits of individual channel lines can be generated via the corresponding bus bar relays. However, this means that line a can initially only be connected to Vbatt and line b to Gnd.

By swapping the bus bar lines (i.e. both swap relays for bus 1 are on), you can also create ground based short circuits on channel line a and Vbatt based short circuits on channel line b. Bus bar line swapping is a module property, not a property of the individual channels. For this reason, you need to adjust the corresponding settings in the module namespace, not in the channel namespace.

Bus bar 2 remains freely available an

[...truncated for brevity...]
