### [VTSystem/VT7001] VT7001TimeSynchronization


# Time Synchronization

VT System » Modules » VT7001/VT7101 » Time synchronization with Vector network interfaces

All modules in a VT System are synchronized internally. The VT System is synchronized with the Vector network interface via CANoe. You can use hardware synchronization to further improve synchronization between the VT System and the network interfaces. The VT System can be integrated into the hardware time synchronization recognized by the Vector network interfaces via the VT7001/VT7101.

To do this, you use a sync line to connect all of the Vector network interfaces with the VT7001/VT7101. If several VT7001/VT7101 modules are used in a VT System, you simply connect the sync line to the first VT7001/VT7101 module.

After this you need to turn on hardware time synchronization in the network interface drivers.

If the VT6104/VT6204 Network Module is used in the VT System, time synchronization between network interfaces and the VT System is done by this module.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

| Usage | Features |
| The configuration for each application board is saved on the board when it is created. If a VT7900 extension module with a corresponding application board is used in a VT System, CANoe recognizes the module automatically, as it does all other VT modules. The name of the module is also stored in the application board configuration, so you can combine a variety of VT7900 modules and specialized application boards. ### [VTSystem/VTSystemControl] VTSControl


# VT System Control

VT System » VT System Control

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|VT System Control

In this dialog you can manually manipulate a previously configured VT System during an active measurement. Outside of the measurement, the channels of the VT System can also be configured and their states stored as start states. In addition, information about the hardware connected to the VT System can be entered in this dialog. This can be used to generate various types of documentation (such as a system overview).

## The main window of the VT System control

### The toolbar of the main window

The toolbar of the main window provides the following functions:

| Symbol | Description |

|  | Zoom in on the current view |
|  | Open the dialog for generating documentation |
|  | Go to front view in the connection display |
|  | Go to rear view in the connection display |
|  | Go to schematic view in the connection display |
|  | Open the area for generating source code |
|  | Open the dialog for specifying module positions |
|  | Open the Settings dialog for the VT System control |
|  | Display online help for the currently selected VT System module |

### The navigation area

The currently configured VT System is shown in a tree view on the left side. Here, it is possible to select the object to be displayed in the display area on the right side. You can also right-click to open a context menu that provides access to the various function

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem/VTSystemControl] VTSControlConfiguration


# Configuration

VT System » Graphic Control » Configuration

You can open the settings dialog with at the top right of the toolbar of the VT System control. This dialog is divided into the following categories:

## General

On this page you configure general settings of the VT System control.

### Schematics

- Update cycle time This value specifies the time interval at which the states of the relays in the circuit diagrams are updated.

### Sidebar

- Update cycle time This value specifies the time interval at which the measurement values in the sidebars are updated.

- Autoexpand groups in the sidebar If this option is selected, all groups in the sidebars (e.g., measurement value arrays and wave form parameters) are expanded automatically.

### Labels

- Clear all labels This can be used to erase all labels entered in the circuit diagrams and connection views.

## Code Generator

On this page you configure the code generator of the VT System control.

### General

- Add comments to generated code A short comment is generated for each line of code.

- Insert blank line after each code block A blank line is inserted after each generated block of code to make the code easier to read.

- Capture while the code window is closed If this option is selected, operations are also recorded when the code generator window is not open.

### CAPL Settings

- Use function/method syntax Depending on which option is selected, CAPL commands are generated in the function syntax (e.g., vtsSet

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem/VTSystemControl] VTSControlConfigurationTrigger


# Trigger Configuration

VT System » Graphic control » Configuration » Trigger

You can open the Trigger Configuration dialog via the channel functions (splitter on the right) of a selected VT System module:

This dialog allows configuring the triggers of the currently selected VT System module.

## Trigger Overview

The list on the left hand side shows all available triggers. The check box next to the trigger indicates whether the trigger is currently active. To edit a trigger's configuration simply select it in the list box. The panel on the right hand side then provides several controls to alter the trigger's configuration.

## Basic Trigger Settings

Here you can specify which channel of the current module this trigger is attached to. Also the kind of edge the trigger reacts to can be set here. Via Start Trigger you can start the trigger. If the trigger is already running it gets restarted.

## Advanced Trigger Settings

The advanced trigger settings allow configuring the trigger for special purposes. Please see the trigger documentation for details on these advanced settings.

## Linked Triggers

The check boxes in this section allow you to specify what triggers are used as a precondition for the current trigger to be startable. You can also define which triggers actually start the current trigger. The indices below the check boxes directly correspond to the indices of the triggers in the selection list.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [VTSystem/VTSystemControl] VTSControlDocumentation


# Generation of VT System Documentation

VT System » Graphic control » Generation of VT System Documentation

In addition to manual manipulation of the VT System, this dialog also provides the option for generating various types of documentation. This includes:

- Text lists of all modules and channels of the VT System including their connections.

- Tabular overviews in CSV format.

- Graphic representations of the configured VT System in various views (front view, rear view, and a schematic representation).

- Graphic representations as PDF vector graphics, which are particularly well-suited for printouts.

The dialog for creating this documentation can be opened via (Create documentation) in the toolbar.

The various documentation types can be selected, configured, and then created within the dialog. To obtain useful results, it is important here to assign labels and specify the layout of the modules beforehand.

## Labels

All connections of the VT System modules have labels both in the interactive circuit diagram representation and in the connection representation.

Each of these labels represents the connection of an external hardware that is connected to the VT System at this point. These labels make the connections easier to view, because they are kept consistent in all views, thereby facilitating the recognition of important connections. In addition, they are incorporated into the generated documentation.

To edit the text of a label, you simply click it. An editor 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [VTSystem/VTSystemControl] VTSControlModuleLayout


# Layout of the modules

VT System » Graphic control » Layout of modules

By default, the modules of the VT System appear in all graphic representations in the order in which they were created in the configuration dialog. However, free slots in the racks are not taken into consideration in this process. In order to adapt the virtual representation to a connected real VT System, it is therefore possible to specify the positions of the individual modules in more detail.

For this purpose, you must first change to the connection view. Now you can select the VT System or an individual rack in the navigation area. Then you can use the appropriate button in the toolbar to open the dialog for the layout of the modules.

In this dialog, you can use a drag-and-drop operation to move the modules to the correct positions. In doing so, you cannot change the order of the modules because this is specified by the configuration of the VT System. You can also add and remove racks by right-clicking in the graphic.

If modules that are not automatically displayed are used, you can add these manually. This pertains to VT6000 real time modules and VTC8920 voltage supplies, for example. To add these modules to the configuration, simply right-click in the graphic. In the context menu, you can then open a dialog for creating user-defined modules. In contrast to the other modules, you can move these modules to any position in the racks.

Related Topics

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows] CANoeCANalyzerAnalysisWindowsSignals


# Analysis Windows: Valid/Invalid Signals

CANoe » Windows » Analysis Windows: Valid/Invalid Signals

Invalid signals can occur in the following windows:

- Graphics Window

- Data Window

- Signal generators and signal replay

- Visual Sequencer

- State Tracker

- Start Value Window

The behavior does not apply to signals for which filtering is possible in the Trace Explorer. Because the Trace Window is message based, signals cannot be examined independently of their message. The same applies to signals in the Interactive Generator, their corresponding Signal Generators, and the signals in the user-defined filter of the trigger.

## Background

In CANoe, an attempt is made to sustain the linking of a signal that is configured in an analysis window to the definition in a database as long as possible. That is, even if changes are made in the database that directly or indirectly affect the signal, an attempt is made to consider these changes and to sustain the link. For example, it is possible to shift the signal from one message to another if the signal is unique within CANoe.

If the configured signal is not assigned uniquely to a signal of a database, the signal is displayed as an invalid signal in the window (icon, tooltip, grayed type). In the case of an invalid signal, settings can no longer be changed. However, it can be deleted from the window. For an invalid signal, the differentiation features used for the explicit qualification do not change until the signal become

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] CANoeCANalyzerDataGraphicSignals


# Missing Measurement Values for Signals in Data and Graphics Window

CANoe » Windows » Missing Measurement Values for Signals in Data and Graphics Window

## Unique channel assignment of received signals

Are you experiencing the problem that received signals are not displayed in the Data Window or Graphics Window? This can be due to an incorrect channel or database assignment:

## Retaining names of signals

The identification rules for signals have been revised. The names of the signals are retained even after changes in the database or relocation of the database. This saves a lot of work because it is easier to identify signals. The signals can be assigned to a new configuration more quickly.

## When signals are received the database is assigned to the correct channel

The originally assigned channel of a signal is retained even when the configuration changes. Thus, if you use the signal in a different scenario with different channels, you must adapt the database assignment or reconfigure the signals (delete and reinsert them). Otherwise, the Data Window or Graphics Window may not display any measured values although the signal appears to be present and active in the window.

|  | Example Assume you are working with a network to which the CAN 1 channel and a database are assigned. Signal1 is defined in the database. Consequently, Signal1 is assigned to the CAN 1 channel. You have also inserted Signal1 in the Graphics Window. This configuration functions correctly after 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] CANoeCANalyzerWindowsDragAndDrop


# drag and drop of Symbols between Windows

CANoe » Data Window / Trace Window / Graphics Window » drag and drop

You can select the symbols shown in the Graphics Window and Data Window either from the Symbol Selection dialog / MOST Symbol Explorer or by drag and drop from

- a Trace Window

- the legend of another Graphics Window

- another Data Window

- the Symbol Explorer / MOST Symbol Explorer

Apart from signals and variables you also can drag parameters of an unfolded diagnostic primitive from a Trace Window to a Graphics or Data Window.

When you drag symbols from one Graphics or Data Window to another Graphics or Data Window they retain their preconfigured properties. Therefore you may wish to select a new color.

|  | Note drag and drop without pressing <Shift> copies a symbol from one window to the other window.drag and drop with pressing <Shift> moves a symbol to the other window. |

You can drag symbols and messages from the Trace Window in the fixed position mode, in Pause mode or after the end of measurement. When you drag a message to the Graphics or Data Window all of the message’s signals are pasted there too. In the Graphics Window the already passed values are displayed as signal curve.

|  | Note You can use the Clipboard instead of drag and drop. You can also use either method to move symbols between two opened CANoe applications. |

|  | Note on drag and drop in the Graphics Window Environment variables of type Data and String are automatically disable

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] CANoeCANalyzerWindowsDragAndDropFileExplorer


# drag and drop (File Explorer)

CANoe » Simulation Setup / Main Window » drag and drop

## Main Window

You can open configurations using drag and drop. To do this, simply pull the configuration file from the file manager to the CANoe main window.

|  | Note Standard type windows (e.g., panels) and non-modal dialogs (e.g. macro configuration dialog) are exceptions to this rule. You cannot use them to open configuration files with drag and drop. |

## Simulation Setup

CAPL program files and Test Modules (CAPL or XML) can be dragged from the File Manager into the Simulation Setup or into a collection of nodes on a bus into the System view.

For each file a block is created on given bus and configured with the program file and the database name, if available.

drag and drop (Windows)

The produced ASCII files can be read in older versions of the program and, however, messages with long symbolic names are ignored. If you like to read ASCII files without exceptions in older versions, please, log numerically, while you deactivate the icon sym. |

## Variable Format

Choose whether the variable name should be supplementally qualified and saved with the name of its database.

## Delimiter

Specifies the separator used between the individual parts of a qualified signal or variable.You can enter up to 3 characters.Blank characters are considered.

- Write raw values Specify here whether the signal raw value should be output when exporting. If this option is disabled, the physical values are output.This setting has no effect when exporting from the Graphics Window since in such case the Graphics Window setting is used.

- Write symbolic values

- Save J1939 signals with invalid values Here you determine whether invalid values for J1939 signals shall be exported.If deactivated, invalid values will be ignored and not exported. This option is only relevant for the export.The source file must be loaded again to consider the changed option correctly.

In the Graphics Window or Data Window you can record (log) the configured symbols during the measurement.In the Signal Logging Configuration dialog the following settings are available to configure the logging file.

- Days (d): 0-99

- Hours (h): 0-23

- Minutes (m): 0-59

- Single Trigger: SingleTrigger

- Toggle Trigger: ToggleTrigger

- Entire Measurement: NoTrigger

### [Windows] CANoeCANalyzerWindowsSynchronization


# Synchronize Windows

CANoe » Windows » Synchronize Windows

Context Menu of the Graphics Window diagram: Synchronize WindowsContext Menu of the Trace Window: Synchronize WindowsContext Menu of the Test Trace WindowContext Menu of the Map Window: Synchronize WindowsContext Menu of the Test Execution dialog: Synchronize Windows

## You would like to synchronously analyze data in several analysis windows when a measurement is stopped?

The function Synchronize Windows is only available for windows that can display a time characteristic:

- Trace Window

- Test Trace Window

- Graphics Window

- Map Window

- State Tracker

- Test Execution dialog (test modules)

- Test Execution dialog (test units)

- Video Window

Except for the Video Window you can activate or deactivate the Synchronize windows function via the context menu of a window.

Alternatively, you can find the icon in the Home ribbon tab, which activates or deactivates the function globally for all windows.

## Example of Use

The Synchronize Windows function can be used very effectively in connection with the Trace Window, especially when you activate Continuous Mode there: . If you then click on a line in the Trace Window, blue measurement cursors are displayed at the synchronous time in the other synchronized analysis windows. You can then move the cursor in the Trace Window using the arrow keys on your keyboard, and the measurement cursor in the other windows will be moved with too.

Conversely, when the Synchr

[...truncated for brevity...]

If you want to display signals in the Graphics or Data Windows that are not yet defined in a database, you can use this dialog to create these user-defined signals. The message signal is defined using the message ID, start bit, bit length and other properties. The signal name is used to show the signal in the windows.

Custom signals are not saved to the database.

### [Windows] GlobalMarkers


# Markers

Graphics Window / Scope / Trace Window / Test Trace Window / State Tracker » Markers

Marker Context Menu

## General

Markers make it easy to tag and locate different time points in a measurement.

## Adding Markers

- Via New Marker... in the context menu of measurement area or marker bar

- Via a double-click on the marker bar

- State Tracker only: Via a simple trigger configuration in the Marker column of the State Tracker signal view.

- Clicking the [Set] button in the Marker column adds a marker to all measurement time points that correspond to the specified conditions.

|  | Note for Marker Groups Markers generated via a specific condition (e.g. trigger) belong to a marker group. All markers in a group have the same color and the same name, and are differentiated by means of a numerical postfix, e.g. Marker[1], Marker[2],… You cannot move markers of a group because they are firmly linked to a condition. The condition is shown as a tooltip for the marker. |

## Removing Markers

- Clicking Delete in the context menu of the marker bar deletes the selected marker or marker group.

- Clicking Delete All in the context menu of the marker bar deletes all markers configured in the window.

|  | Note for Marker Groups You cannot delete individual markers from a marker group. Only the entire group can be deleted. Scope:In the Scope Window marker groups can be deleted with Delete All. In the Scope Window for every measurement a group will be added. If you delete a 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] J1587ParameterDataGraphicWindow


# J1587 Parameters in Data and Graphics Window

J1587 » Graphics Window / Data Window » J1587 Parameters in Data and Graphics Window

The Data and Graphics Window shows the signals of J1587 messages. The signals can be selected for a database in the configuration dialog.

The configuration dialog of the Graphics Window contains a button [Edit…] and the context menu of the Data Window contains a menu command Edit, which can be used to configure a signal. The MID from where the message is sent, can be modified in the dialog. In the default setting the MID is not considered. In this case the signal value is overwritten, if the same parameter is received from different senders.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows] J1587ParameterMonitor


# J1587 Parameter Monitor

CANoe » Analysis Windows » J1587 Parameter Monitor

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|J1587 Parameter Monitor|View / New

The J1587 Parameter Monitor shows all parameters which are transmitted from the nodes of a J1587 network. The parameters are ordered by the sender (alt. sending) nodes. A symbolic representation is available if the assigned database contains the necessary information.

Via the context menu selected nodes and parameters can be added to page Watch with the command Add to watch. This page gives a more clear view if many nodes are contained in the network.

The J1587 parameter monitor can be inserted into the Measurement Setup as every other function block.

Option .J1587

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows] LINNetworkManagementWindow


# LIN Network Management Window

LIN » Functional Blocks / Analysis Windows » LIN Network Management Window

Ribbon: Simulation Tab|Simulation Setup|LIN Network Management

The Network Management Window monitors the current states of all defined LIN buses. Use the Sleep and Wake-up commands to interact on LIN buses.

The Network column shows the name of the corresponding network name in the Simulation Setup.

The Channel column displays the LIN channel number of the network.

In the Start State column you can define if the LIN bus shall be awake or asleep after start of the measurement. You have to set the property before measurement start.

The Current State column displays if the corresponding LIN bus is currently awake or asleep.

The Action column allows to set a LIN bus into the sleep or wake-up mode.

|  | Note The LIN Network Management Window is independent of the LIN hardware mode (i.e. Master and Slave mode). |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows] MOSTAudioRoutingWindow


# MOST Audio Routing Window

CANoe » Analysis Windows » MOST Audio Routing Window

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Audio Routing

The MOST Audio Routing Window shows the distribution of connection labels in the resource allocation table of the connected node.

## Analog – Line IN/OUT

- LineOut/Headphone lets you listen to audio channelsWhen you select a connection label, the routing engine of the connected node is programmed so that the relevant channels are routed as audio signals to the LineOut/Headphone output of the hardware interface. You can control the volume using the slider and the mute button. With MOST150 the bypass of the interface must be inactive to listen to a certain audio channel.

- Line IN lets you feed audio data onto the ringWhen you select a connection label from the allocation table, the routing engine of the connected node is programmed so that the signals at the LineIn input of the hardware interface are routed to the associated channels on the ring. You can control the amplification of the input signal using the slider and the mute button.

## Digital – S/PDIF IN/OUT

- S/PDIF Out lets you transmit digital audio signals to the S/PDIF output.Once you select a connection label, the routing engine of the connected node is programmed so that the associated channels are routed to the S/PDIF Out output of the hardware interface. You can mute the signal using the mute button. (MOST150 only)

- S/PDIF In enables you to feed in digital audio data from the S/PDIF In inp

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] MOSTFBlockMonitor


# MOST FBlock Monitor

CANoe » Analysis Windows » MOST FBlock Monitor

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|FBlock Monitor

The MOST FBlock Monitor displays the MOST device assignment, the reported functions and the most recently observed property values for all function blocks whose communication on the MOST ring has been observed by the spy.

The user interface is divided into four sections:

## System view

The system view shows a list of all devices and their function blocks. This list comprises the devices and function blocks identified on the basis of different messages observed in the MOST ring Obtaining Information. Function blocks are displayed in black if the respective function block has actually received status messages and the existence of the function block can therefore be considered as proven or if the NetBlock or MOST NetworkMaster has reported the existence of the function block. Otherwise the function blocks are displayed in gray, especially after the start of a new measurement if the previous list is not cleared from the display.

### Toolbar:

|  | Close the function block tree. |
| --- | --- |
|  | Open the complete function block tree. |
|  | Update the list of function blocks. For this purpose the NetworkMaster.CentralRegistry.Get message is sent and the corresponding status message is evaluated. This button can only be used when a measurement is in progress and the bus interface bypass is deactivated. |
|  | Clear all function blocks from the tree that are currently 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] MOSTRegistryWindow


# MOST Central Registry Window

CANoe » Analysis Windows » MOST Central Registry Window

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Central Registry

The MOST Central Registry Window displays the bus registry of the MOST ring in a clear manner. It also offers additional analytical possibilities.

It is divided into three areas:

Registry area

The Registry Area contains a list field that displays the registry. Each entry contains a node address, an FBlock ID, an instance ID and a symbol that proves whether the displayed information is secure (see section Correctness / Completeness of the Displayed Information). If a catalog is assigned to the configuration, then the names of those FBlocks are displayed as well.

Below this is a row of tabs that make it possible to switch between all of the configured channels.

The [Advanced] button at the lower edge of the area serves to open the area containing the analytical functions.

### There are several additional buttons next to it:

- With the button, the currently displayed registry can be copied to the clipboard.

- With the button, a registry can be imported from the clipboard.

Using these two buttons, a registry can be imported and then saved as reference. After importing a second registry, these two registries can be compared using the difference view. Note, that this button can only be clicked if no measurement is running, the window is set to registry view, no history entry is selected and the window is not paused.

- With the button, the registr

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] MOSTStateWindow


# MOST State Window

CANoe » Analysis Windows » MOST State Window

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|State

The MOST State Window gives the user a perspective on important system and node states on a MOST ring. In the MOST State Window one status line is shown for each MOST channel in CANoe.

Red icons are used for error states (e.g. no Light) so that the user can recognize at a quick glance whether something is wrong with the state of the ring. Green icons are shown for states that do not represent any errors g (e.g. Lock).

All states are unknown before the first measurement start. When the measurement is stopped the displays are no longer updated, and they show the last momentary states.

| State | Icon | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Light |  | This icon indicates that no Light & Lock Event has been provided by the HW Interface yet. |
|  | A Light is arriving at the input of the HW Interface. |
|  | No light is arriving at the input of the HW-Interface. |
| Lock |  | This icon indicates that no Light & Lock Event has been provided by the HW Interface yet. |
|  | (Non stable) Lock at the input of HW Interface. |
|  | Stable Lock at the input of the HW Interface. |
|  | There is no Lock at the input of the HW Interface. |
| Config State |  | This icon indicates that no NetState Event was provided by the HW Interface yet. |
|  | The MOST NetworkMaster has propagated ConfigOK through the ring. |
|  | The MOST NetworkMaster has propagated ConfigNotOK through the ring. |
| HW Mode (Ma

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] MOSTSystemViewer


# MOST System Viewer

CANoe » Analysis Windows » MOST System Viewer

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|System Viewer

The MOST System Viewer provides an overview of all connected MOST devices. To obtain this information, the MOST System Viewers the ring scan of the MOST NetworkMaster and other events on the MOST ring.

In addition, the MOST System Viewer offers the option to retrieve the desired information actively from individual control units or all control units. To exercise this option, select the Scan command in the menu bar for the entire ring or in the context menu for an individual node.

## Ring structure (number of devices in the ring, address information, etc.)

|  | Symbols / Numbers | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 4 | Node position |
| 559 | Node address |
| 300 | Grpup address |
| / | Master/Bypass closed |
| / | Slave/Bypass closed |
|  | Spy |

Additional information on the symbols can be found in the State Window.

For better recognition of individual control units, you can assign a name to the node. In the case of changing ring configurations, the control unit at the respective position can be identified automatically based on the logged-on function blocks.

## Position and configuration of the MOST interface(s)

In addition to the information listed above, a Vector interface is displayed with a red border.

## Statistics

The Statistics View displays the utilization of the MOST ring.

Detailed information on the individual control units is displayed underneath. Y

### [Windows] MOSTWindowsSelectionList


# MOST Windows: Selection List

MOST » MOST Windows » Selection Lists

Behavior of selection lists in MOST IG, MOST Application Filter and MOST custom event highlighting.

## Setup:

- The selection list consists of an entry box and the actual selection list.

- Each entry in the selection list contains a symbolic value and an ID (in brackets)

## Basic use:

- The selection of entries from the list can be carried out in the usual way using the mouse (open, scroll, select).

- It is possible to exit the edit mode at any time by means of the ESC key, without any change to the value in the field.

## Activating edit mode in a selection list:

- If the selected field in the message list has a selection list associated with it, the list can also be opened by means of the key combination <Alt> + <Down>. The focus is then on the selection list.

- If the field selected in the message list has a selection list associated with it, a selection can be made by simply typing a letter or number. The focus is then on the entry field.

## Working with the entry field:

- Everything the user enters in the entry field limits the selection in the selection list. The entry in the field is interpreted as a partial string that may occur anywhere in the entries in the selection list.

- <Ctrl> + <Del> clears all the characters to the right of the cursor position

- To move from the entry field to the selection list, press the <Down Arrow> key or the key combination <Ctrl> + <Down Arrow>

- If the

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows] SimulinkAnalysisModels


# Simulink Analysis Models

Simulink Model Viewer » Simulink Analysis Models

Ribbon: Environment Tab|Analysis Models

In this dialog, you can configure Simulink® models in such a way that you can perform evaluations during the measurement.

|  | Note You can only access this dialog via the Environment Ribbon Tab if a Simulink analysis model already exists. You can configure or assign Simulink analysis models using the Simulink Analysis Models context menu command of the root element in the Measurement Setup. |

In contrast to the models that are assigned for the node in the Simulation Setup, the analysis models have no effect on the simulation or measurement. For this reason, blocks that change signals or environmental variables cannot be used in these models. Analysis models are available in both online and offline mode and are started automatically when the measurement begins.

## Configured Models

With the [Add] and [Remove] buttons you can select or remove analysis models.

## Model Configuration

On the right side of the dialog with Simulink® Model you can select the path to the Simulink® model.

The [Generate Info] button allows you to generate information for the Simulink Model Viewer.

If a model is compiled with the Simulink Coder for CANoe, you must also enter the resulting DLL at Simulink Coder Model.

When the measurement begins, the Simulink® analysis models are started automatically from CANoe. If you have not specified a DLL, CANoe opens the specified model

### [Windows] SimulinkModelViewerValueDisplay


# Simulink Model Viewer: Displaying Values

Simulink Model Viewer » Display Values

The Simulink Model Viewer lets you directly observe the values of parameters, as well as signals and environment and system variables, in special value display fields. You can activate the display either for all subsystem values via the context menu of the Viewer, or only for a specific value via the context menu of the corresponding block. Alternatively, you can switch on this display via the options settings for that value in the Explorer.

|  | Important Note! To display parameter values, you need to activate the Parameters in C API and Enable Parameterization from CANoe options when generating the model in Simulink. |

You can move the value display field freely with your mouse. The anchor point of the arrow will remain fixed unless you select it directly when dragging the field. By double-clicking the display field, you can also display a dialog that lets you configure the display. You can specify:

- The color of the field and the text (or transparency)

- The color and width of the border (or no border)

- The text type/font

You can use a range of templates for the colors and font, or specify custom settings. The current value is shown in the text as %s.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

# Symbol Panel

CANoe » User Interfaces » Symbol Panel

| General |
| --- |
|  | You can use the Symbol Panel to display and edit symbol values (bus signals, system variables and environment variables) during the simulation. You can also add symbols to the Symbol Panel while a measurement is running. The Symbol Panel supports the following symbol values: strings (string), integers (int) and floating-point numbers (float). You can open the Symbol Panel for a selected symbol via the context menu of the Symbol Explorer, the Data Window or the Graphics Window, or via the Simulink Model Viewer. Alternately, you can drag and drop a symbol from one of the windows mentioned above into the Symbol Panel. If the Symbol Panel is already open and you select a new symbol, the symbol assignment in the Symbol Panel is updated. |
| Information Shown | Attributes Shown |
| The Symbol Panel shows the following symbol information: Symbol type Signal: Environment variable: System variable: If Invalid Symbol Selection is shown, this means that the type of the selected symbol is not supported. Please select a signal, an environment variable or a system variable to change its symbol value. Symbol name Current value The symbol value is shown as a physical value. | The lower part of the Symbol Panel shows the following attributes: Min The minimum signal value defined in the database. Max The maximum signal value defined in the database. Initial value The default value for the signal. Cycle time The s

# Analysis Windows

In CANoe the following windows are available for the analysis:

| CAN specific windows | J1939/ISO1183 specific windows |
| CANopen specific windows | LIN specific windows |

## General Analysis Windows

| Screenshot | Short Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Measurement Setup The data flow is displayed graphically in the Measurement Setup.All options for parameterizing the Measurement Setup are selected in this window. |
|  | Data Window The Data Window is used to display signal values.The signal value is displayed as a physical value, as a raw value and with a progress bar by default. |
|  | Frame Histogram The mean message frequency or mean message spacing of transmissions is displayed graphically. |
|  | Graphics Window In the Graphics Window symbols (signals, variables, and diagnostic parameters) are displayed graphically in an XY diagram. You can configure the time or any symbol as the X-axis. |
|  | Map Window With the Map Window you can display and evaluate logged position information from configured GNSS receivers on a map. In addition, you can use CAPL or .NET to draw your own objects on the map. With the .J1939 option or the .ISO11783 option you can also display and evaluate posit

|  | Virtual Terminal With only a few steps you can activate a Virtual Terminal and work with it. Additional options are available to analyze an object pool, so all objects of the object pool can be displayed and analyzed in an additional view. In passive mode, you can observe the bus traffic of a real Virtual Terminal. If problems occur, you can analyze the object pool in CANoe Virtual Terminal in more detail. |
|  | ISO11783 Interactive Task Controller The Interactive Task Controller can be used to access the process variable dictionary of an implement. The process variables can be set or read out. The process variables are selected symbolically after the device description has been transferred by the implement. When a task is started, the process variables of the default treatment zone are transmitted to the implement. |

### [Windows/ApplicationLayerStimulation] ApplicationPanel


# Application Panel

CANoe » Simulation/Stimulation Windows » Application Panel

Ribbon: Simulation Tab|Application Panel

| Select Application Layer Objects |
| --- |
|  | You can open the Select Application Layer Objects dialog via Application Panel|New of the Simulation ribbon tab. All supported application layer objects contained in the configuration are listed here. Select the desired application layer object and confirm the selection with [OK] (multiple selection is possible). A separate application panel is opened for each selected application layer object. Prerequisites for Selection The Application Panel can be used to stimulate the following objects from the application layer if the Simulated (CANoe) state is set for them in the System Explorer (Properties): Participants(COs) The participant contains an endpoint as consumer that can call a method at a provider. The participant contains an endpoint as a consumer that can call Get and Set methods for a field at a provider.Note that Get and Set methods are optional for a field. If they are not defined, then you cannot stimulate the field. The participant contains an endpoint as a provider that can trigger an event. Distributed objects (DOs) A method can be used by the distributed object (consumed). Get and Set methods can be used for a field by the distributed object (consumed). An event can be triggered by the distributed object (provided). Without a condition mentioned above, a participant or distributed object will

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/BusStatistic] BusStatistic


## Bus Statistics

Bus statistics are acquired cyclically from the CAN controller, whereby the sampling period can be configured in the CAN Controller Configuration dialog. The CAN controller monitors the bus traffic continuously for this purpose.

Since updating is cyclic, the total number of frames in the Bus Statistics Window may lag one period behind the actual number of frames registered. The actual number can be obtained, for example, from the Statistics Report.

Different values may result for bus load depending on option settings. In particular, with small sampling periods (t << 1 s) fluctuations may occur due to the measurement procedure, since the time resolution of the program increasingly affects accuracy here.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/BusStatistic] BusStatisticWindow


# Bus Statistic Window

CANoe » Analysis Windows » Bus Statistic Window

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Statistics|View / New

|  | Note Effective with version 7.5 we recommend the new Statistics Windows for CAN/LIN/FlexRay: CAN Statistics Window LIN Statistics Window FlexRay Statistics Window |

| General |
| --- |
|  | Serves as a numeric display of frequencies of data, remote, error and overload frames and bus loading, and also displays the state of the CAN controllers. In the Options dialog you can activate/deactivate the calculation of all bus statistics values. |
| Description of the Bus Statistics Window |
| In the Bus Statistics Window Bus Statistics are displayed for each controller. The window shows the overall sums and occurrence rates [Frames/Second] of the various frames, separated into Data, Remote, Error and Overload frames. Standard and Extended identifiers are also differentiated. Pressing the right mouse button allows the user to configure the Bus Statistics Window. Here you can configure the font type and size. Furthermore statistics can be reset during a measurement. The reset affects all statistics in the Bus Statistics Window as well as all bus statistics signals. If the display of Remote frames should be suppressed, the Remote switch should be reconfigured from 1 to 0 in the [Busstatistic] section of the CAN.INI file. |
| Bus/Protocol specific Settings |
| Information for CAN Information for LIN Information for FlexRay Information for MOST Information for Etherne

### [Windows/BusStatistic] BusStatisticWindowCAN


# Bus Statistics Window: CAN

Bus Statistics Window » CAN

The Bus Statistics Window (also) displays CAN-specific data and values.For CAN the following information are available in the Bus Statistics Window:

- Busload [%]

- Peakload [%]

- Std. Data [fr/s]

- Std. Data [total]

- Ext. Data [fr/s]

- Ext. Data [total]

- Std. Remote [fr/s]

- Std. Remote [total]

- Ext. Remote [fr/s]

- Ext. Remote [total]

- Error Frame [fr/s]

- Error Frames [total]

- Chip State

The momentary and maximum bus loads since the measurement start are displayed in percent. The CAN controller averages these parameters according to the configured Sampling period, which can yield different values depending on the setting.

## Error State (Chip State)

Also the Bus Statistics Window displayed are error status information on the individual CAN controllers and the momentary readings of their respective error counters. The following states may occur:

- SIMULATED CANoe is in simulated mode. Bus access (transmission and receipt of messages) is simulated by CANoe. The hardware is not accessed, which means that the controller status cannot be displayed.

- ACTIVE Normal state.The microcontroller reacts to bus errors by sending out an Error Frame consisting of 6 dominant bits.The CAN controller state is Error Active.

- WARNING The Warning state in the Bus Statistics Window indicates a special CAN controller state. Active means that the CAN controller is reacting to bus errors and that it is in the Erro

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/BusStatistic] BusStatisticWindowEthernet


# Bus Statistics Window: Ethernet

Bus Statistics Window » Ethernet

The Bus Statistics Window shows Ethernet specific data and values too.

|  | Note With a network-based access, the Bus Statistics Window shows a separate Port column for each port. The screenshot shows the Bus Statistics Window for Ethernet with network-based access. Each port has its own statistics values column, denoted as <Port Name> (Eth <application channel>) e.g. Port (Eth1). With a channel based network access, an application channel can be assigned to a segment with several hardware channels. In this case, the Bus Statistics Window shows a separate column for each hardware channel to properly represent the statistics data. The screenshot shows the Bus Statistics Window for an Ethernet application channel, which is assigned to a segment with several hardware channels. The respective application channel and hardware channel is shown in a separate column Eth <application channel>.<hardware channel> (e.g. Eth 1.1). |

The following information is available:

| Statistic | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Link speed [Mbit/s] | Transfer rate of the Ethernet connection (link), in working mode simulated bus 100 MBit/s |
| Rx Bus load [%] | Load of the Ethernet connection in receive direction relating to value Link Speed |
| Tx Bus load [%] | Load of the Ethernet connection in send direction relating to value Link Speed |
| Rx Peak load [%] | Maximum measured load of the Ethernet connection in receive direction re

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/BusStatistic] BusStatisticWindowFlexRay


# Bus Statistics Window: FlexRay

Bus Statistics Window » FlexRay

The Bus Statistics Window displays FlexRay specific data and values.

For FlexRay the following information are available in the Bus Statistics Window:

- Frames [fr/s]

- Frames [total]

- Null Frames [fr/s]

- Null Frames [total]

- Frame Errors [[fr/s]

- Frame Errors [total]

- Syntax Errors [[fr/s]…will be reported by the Communication Controller in accordance to the FlexRay specification at transmission during wrong bus state or on occurrence of decoding error.

- Syntax Errors [total]

- Content Errors [[fr/s]…will be reported by the Communication Controller in accordance to the FlexRay specification on reception of wrong frame header length or inconsistent frame header data.

- Content Errors [total]

- Boundary Violations [[fr/s]…will be reported by the Communication Controller in accordance to the FlexRay specification if the channel is not idle at the boundary of a slot.

- Boundary Violations [total]

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/BusStatistic] BusStatisticWindowMOST


# Bus Statistics Window: MOST

Bus Statistics Window » MOST

The Bus Statistics Window shows some statistical data and the last valid bus state like frame/s, frames(total), number of changes of Light & Lock and so on. These values are calculated by the Vector MOST driver.

For a channel operated in node mode, only the frames addressed to this node are included in the calculation. However the values are calculated before any filter or other CAPL node can influence the number of the received frames. If the spy is activated for this channel, only the Spy messages will be counted.

|  | Edit the sampling rate The sampling rate with which the statistic analysis is processed may be configured in the CAN.INI file: [MOST]// Sampling rate of bus statistic (value in ms)// -1: Off, 100-.. valid values (default=1000)Statistic = 1000 |

The Bus Statistics Window (also) displays MOST specific data and values.

For MOST the following information are available in the Bus Statistics Window:

- Frames [fr/s] Messages/frames - per second.More precisely: frames transmitted on the control channel.

- Frames [total] Received messages/frames - total.More precisely: frames transmitted on the control channel.

- Lgt&Lck [L&L/s] Light & Lock errors - per second.The MOST Interface reports L&L errors whenever it recognizes that there is no light (input) or that the synchronous timing of the ring broke down. An error is recognized and reported (only) once, e.g. when the light is switched off. As a resul

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] CommunicationSetup


# System And Communication Setup

CANoe » Window Overview » System And Communication Setup

Ribbon: Environment Tab|System And Communication Setup

| General Information |
| --- |
|  | The System and Communication Setup is the central starting point for communication configuration. You configure: the application layer (application models) the communication layer (communication, bindings) the transmission medium (hardware, bindings) |
| Configuration | Hardware Configuration |
| Publish Changed System Data You must publish changes to system data explicitly so that they are accepted in CANoe and become visible, for example, in the Symbol Explorer. An automatic publication is performed as soon as it is absolutely necessary for further action, e.g: open the Model Editor Re-import of data sources start a measurement save the configuration close CANoe The button is only displayed if you change something in the System and Communication Setup. As soon as you accept a change, the button is hidden again. You do not have to publish every change immediately. Despite the message, you can first enter all changes and then publish them all at once. System Definition On the System Definition page you select the data sources for the CANoe configuration the application models (files) that contain functions to be executed during the CANoe measurement the used Binding to determine which protocol and which network must be used for transmission between the endpoints a VTT model that was generated

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupCOEditor


# Model Editor

CANoe » System and Communication Setup

Ribbon: Environment Tab|System and Communication Setup|Model Editor

In the Model Editor dialog, you can define your own application layer objects (CO, DO) and edit the existing ones.

## Concept

Under Type Definitions, you create templates that you need to create new objects.

- Create a PDU type as template for new PDUs. In the PDU type you can describe signals.Create a new PDU under Communication Objects|PDUs.

- Create a service interface as template for new services. In the service interface you can describe events, methods, PDUs and fields.Create a new service under Communication Objects|Services.

- Function types, data types and encodings are required accordingly when creating events, methods, fields and signals.

In the left sub-window you select what you want to edit. Depending on your selection, the corresponding contents will be displayed on the right-hand side. Depending on your selection here, you can use additional splitters to display and edit data type properties.

Validation messages and references for the data are displayed in the splitter at the bottom of the editor.

## Pages

| Type Definitions | PDU Types | PDU Timings | Function Types | Service Interfaces | Data Types | Encodings |  |

| Communication Objects | All | PDUs | Services | Signals |  |

| Participants |  |

| Distributed Objects | Interfaces | Objects | Object References | Virtual Network |  |

| Ethernet Peer Setup |  |

| Safety & 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupCOEditorAll


# Model Editor

Model Editor » Communication Objects » All

Ribbon: Environment Tab|System and Communication Setup|Model Editor|Communication Objects|All

In the Model Editor dialog, you can define your own application layer objects (CO, DO) and edit the existing ones.

## Concept

Under Type Definitions, you create templates that you need to create new objects.

- Create a PDU type as template for new PDUs. In the PDU type you can describe signals.Create a new PDU under Communication Objects|PDUs.

- Create a PDU Timing that you can assign to a PDU.

- Create a service interface as template for new services. In the service interface you can describe events, methods, PDUs and fields.Create a new service under Communication Objects|Services.

- Function types, data types and encodings are required accordingly when creating events, methods, fields and signals.

To create distributed objects, you must create interfaces under Distributed Objects|Interfaces. In the interface you can describe data, events, fields, methods and basic interfaces.

To group distributed objects or to switch their communication on and off altogether, you can use virtual networks.

Under Ethernet Peer Setup you can create the network-relevant information for your Ethernet configuration. You can create IP stacks, network interfaces, VLANs, IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and ports and configure their properties.

In the left sub-window you select what you want to edit. Depending on your selection, the correspondi

[...truncated for brevity...]

To create distributed objects, you must create interfaces under Distributed Objects|Interfaces. In the interface you can describe data, events, fields, methods and basic interfaces.

To group distributed objects or to switch their communication on and off altogether, you can use virtual networks.

Under Ethernet Peer Setup you can create the network-relevant information for your Ethernet configuration. You can create IP stacks, network interfaces, VLANs, IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and ports and configure their properties.

In the left sub-window you select what you want to edit. Depending on your selection, the corresp

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupComDetails


# System Explorer

CANoe » System and Communication Setup

Ribbon: Environment Tab|System and Communication Setup|System Explorer

| System Definition | System Explorer |  |

On the System Explorer page all elements participants, endpoints and distributed objects are displayed after the configuration on the System Definition page.

It is the central view of all available elements and application models in CANoe. Here you can configure the communication of CANoe and see who is communicating with whom.

Since ARXML databases typically contain a lot of data, you can work with a predefined and user-defined system setup. Use this to insert shortcuts to elements you want to focus on (e.g. with drag-and-drop from the system definition).

The structure in the system setup gives you a better overview of communication inside (simulated) and outside (real) CANoe. In addition, the important elements are easier to find so that you can adjust their properties.

Scenarios help you to quickly change switching states and the binding configuration of elements. The settings refer to the elements in a folder of the system setup. So you don't have to make your settings for each individual element, but can make them bundled via the folder structure. For example, you can quickly switch from a partial simulation to a full simulation.

When importing data sources, you are supported in sorting the elements into the system setup.

The page is divided in the following areas:

| Navigation Bar Symbol Fu

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupDataSources


# Data Sources Import Wizard

CANoe » System and Communication Setup » Data Sources Import Wizard

Use the Data Sources Import Wizard to select data sources for the CANoe configuration. The files are imported so that the contained information about elements participants, endpoints and distributed objects can be provided for CANoe. After the import they are displayed on the System Explorer page of the System and Communication Setup.

## Import Data Sources

## File Selection

With [Select File...] you open the Windows Explorer, to select new files. Multiple selection is possible.

With [Select Folder...] you open the Windows Explorer, to select a folder that contains files for the import.

The selected files are displayed under File.

With or <Del> you can delete a file.

|  | Note When re-importing a file, you cannot select or delete files. |

Use [Next >] to switch for ARXML files to the Import Parameters page. For the other file types, you switch directly to the Import page.

With [Finish] you import the file without further settings.

With [Cancel] you cancel the import procedure, no changes are taken over.

## Import Parameters

The files selected in the first step are displayed under File.

[Import Parameters... ] opens the Import Parameters dialog for ARXML files to influence the import of the file by configuring appropriate parameters.

With [Next >] you start the import procedure and open the Import page.

With [Finish] you import the file without further settings.



[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupDataSourcesClusterSW


# Import Parameters

CANoe » System and Communication Setup » Data Sources » Import Parameters

Click [Import Parameters... ] in the Data Sources Import Wizard to open the Import Parameters dialog. Here, you can configure parameters for the import of the ARXML file. If you close the dialog by pressing [OK], the changes will be applied and the file will be automatically reimported.

You use settings to reduce the amount of data imported into the data model. You can edit this amount of data (e.g. by creating namespaces) and expand (e.g. using dummy endpoints).

## General

## OEM Type

For the import you can select a OEM here.

## Namespaces

The AUTOSAR file contains data objects and their data types. If you import several files into CANoe, you can enter clear namespaces to distinguish the imported data of different files.

|  | Note Namespaces for data types and data objects must be defined.The names must not include special characters begin with numbers |

## Namespace for participants

Enter here the namespace in which the participants are created. If the namespace has already been created by importing a different data source, the name of this namespace will automatically be provided with an index.

## Namespace for timings

Enter here the namespace in which the timings are created. If the namespace has already been created by importing a different data source, the name of this namespace will automatically be provided with an index.

## Namespace for data types

Enter here

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupDragAndDrop


# Drag And Drop

CANoe » System and Communication Setup » Drag And Drop

You can drag and drop the following files directly onto the System and Communication Setup:

- Data Sources When you drop the file, the Data Source Import Wizard is automatically started.If you drop several files at once, a group is automatically created and the Data Source Import Wizard is started.If you cancel the import process, the files are still displayed under Data Sources, but the data was not imported, which is indicated by an error symbol .

- Application Models Drop the file to add it directly to the list of application models.

- VTT Models Drop the file to import it.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupHowToUseScenarios


# How to Use Scenarios

CANoe » System and Communication Setup » System Explorer » How to Use Scenarios

On this page you will find examples of how you can implement certain use cases with the Scenario Editor.

You open the Scenario Editor on the top right of the page System Explorer of the System and Communication Setups:

|  | Note The examples refer to working with predefined scenarios and thus to the folders under Predefined System Setup. However, you can also use your own scenarios, for which the folder User-Defined System Setup and its subfolders can then also be considered. |

|  | Note In the scenario selection, Undefined is displayed until you select a scenario change a setting of a scenario outside the Scenario Editor |

|  | Example 1 Only a few endpoints of a participant are of interest to you. All other endpoints should be deactivated and not participate in the communication. Procedure Remove an existing shortcut of the participant to whom your endpoints are assigned from the Test Environment or System Under Test folder. In our example, the participant Drive_Master is removed from the Test Environment folder and the shortcut is deleted. Now select the endpoints that are to participate in the communication under System Definition|Endpoints. For example, use drag and drop to create a shortcut to the Test Environment or System Under Test folder. In our example, a shortcut is created in the Test Environment folder for the endpoint Navigation[Drive_Master]. Now selec

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupMeasurementPoint


# Measurement Point

CANoe » System and Communication Setup » System Explorer » Measurement Point

You can observe values of real endpoints in CANoe.

|  | Note To observe the real communication in CANoe, endpoints must be real connected. |

In the System and Communication Setup the following options are available:

## Observing the State

Use the following settings if you want to observe values that are set in a Provider/Server.

- Set the state for the desired participant/endpoint (Provider/Sender) to Remote.

- Activate the measurement point

- with a click on the circle of the graph in the System and Communication Setup

- via the participant properties, Measure contained endpoints option

- via the endpoint properties, Measure option

- Apply the changes with [ Apply].

## Observing the Communication via a Measurement Point

Use the following settings if you want to observe the communication between to endpoints via a measurement point.

- Click in the graph of the System and Communication Setup on the circle between two participants/endpoints.

- Set the state for the desired participants/endpoints to Romote.

- Apply the changes with [ Apply].

- Mirror_Driver participant: Services::Mirror[Mirror_Driver] endpoint as provider ()

- Console participant:: Services::Mirror[Console] endpoint as consumer ()

Basics of the Communication Concept| vCDL Language

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/CommunicationSetup] ComSetupSystemSetup


# System Definition

CANoe » System and Communication Setup

Ribbon: Environment Tab|System and Communication Setup|System Definition

| System Definition | System Explorer |  |

Do the following steps on System Definition page:

- Select on the right side new data sources for the CANoe configuration via Import Data Sources as Group and Import Data Source.The files are imported so that the contained information about elements participants, endpoints and distributed objects can be provided for CANoe. After the import they are displayed on the System Explorer page.Already imported files are displayed in the Data Sources area.

- If no data sources are available you can create, edit and delete elements in the Model Editor.

- Select under Application Models files that contain functions to be executed during the CANoe measurement.

- Binding is used to determine which protocol and which network must be used for transmission between the endpoints. Thus, binding creates transition between the virtual world of CANoe and the real word, for example in the form of a real ECU.

- You can also add a VTT model file (*.vttsut) that was generated from vVIRTUALtarget.

You can execute steps 3 and 4 in any order.

| Publish Changed System Data You must publish changes to system data explicitly so that they are accepted in CANoe and become visible, for example, in the Symbol Explorer. An automatic publication is performed as soon as it is absolutely necessary for further action, e.g: open the

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/FlexRayClusterMonitor] FlexRayClusterMonitor


# FlexRay Cluster Monitor

CANoe » Analysis Windows » FlexRay Cluster Monitor

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|FR Cluster Monitor|View / New

| General |
| --- |
|  | The FlexRay Cluster Monitor shows the statistics and bus activities of a FlexRay cluster during a measurement. The real data of the network traffic are compared with the data from the FIBEX model, and deviations or differences are registered and displayed. The window can be inserted in the Measurement Setup by using the context menu of a function block (bus statistics etc.). If there are several channels (clusters) the FlexRay Cluster Monitor can be assigned to a specific channel in the channel selection list. The channels list depends on the configuration and the channel usage settings. |
| View | Functions and Control |
| Views Icons | Functional Overview |

Option .FlexRay | FIBEX Support

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/FlexRayClusterMonitor] FlexRayClusterMonitorFunctions


# FlexRay Cluster Monitor: Function Overview

FlexRay » FlexRay Cluster Monitor » Function Overview

The FlexRay Cluster Monitor offers the following functions:

| Network monitoring |
| --- |
| The data traffic of the cluster is constantly being compared with the schedule from the FIBEX database. Deviations of the data traffic are highlighted in the node and frame view using different diagnostic icons. |
| Quick error diagnostics |
| The direct comparison of actual and desired values allows for quickly locating errors in the network model. The desired values are taken from the database, whereas the actual values are calculated in the on-going measurement. The direct view onto the network parameters and the running statistics support the analysis. For example, if a certain frame is not sent from the database contrary to the communication model. this deviation is highlighted in the frame view as well as at the corresponding node. |
| FIBEX data model |
| The communication model is represented node-based and subdivided by channels A and B. Frames that are not defined in the database, but are still being transmitted on the bus, are assigned to a special node ("undefined frames"). This also allows for quickly locating deviations in the communication model. Examples of typical deviations include: A frame is not being transmitted A frame is transmitted as Null Frame while a data frame is being expected |
| History Window |
| Use the context menu (right mouse button in the Node vie

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/FlexRayClusterMonitor] FlexRayClusterMonitorIcons


# FlexRay Cluster Monitor: Icons

FlexRay » FlexRay Cluster Monitor » Icons

| Icon | Meaning in Frame View | Meaning in Node View |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | The calculated statistics based on the data from the FIBEX database correspond to real measured values. | The calculated statistics based on the data from the FIBEX database correspond to the real measured values. |
|  | A deviation of the actual/desired data occurred. Contrary to the FIBEX schedule, the frame was not transmitted. | A deviation of the actual/desired data occurred. Contrary to the FIBEX schedule, at least one frame was not transmitted. |
|  | This icon identifies an unknown frame. Such frames are transmitted on the bus, but there is no communication model in the FIBEX database for these frames. | This icon identifies the unknown node. All unknown frames are assigned to this node. |
|  | A filter was enabled for this frame type, incoming data are not included in the statistical calculations of the frame and the corresponding node. The desired values were recalculated accordingly. | Node contains at least one frame whose type corresponds to an enabled frame type filter. The desired values were recalculated accordingly. |
|  | In the past, at least one deviation of the actual/desired data occurred. There are presently no deviations. Detailed information can be found in the history display. | In the past, at least one deviation (for one or several frames) of the actual/desired data occurred. There are presen

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/FlexRayClusterMonitor] FlexRayClusterMonitorViews


# FlexRay Cluster Monitor: Views

FlexRay » FlexRay Cluster Monitor » Views

The FlexRay Cluster Monitor offers the following views:

| View | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Statistics (general) | The global network statistics are displayed in frames/second (fr/s). However, with respect to nodes or frames, the display refers to frames per 64 communication cycles. This display allows for an exact comparison of the data with the communication model without rounding errors. |
| Network View | The page with the network data is divided into three areas: Parameter, counter, and statistics. The parameter area displays the statistical network parameters: Cycle length in ms Number of statistical slots Length of payload in the static area Length of payload in the dynamic area Number of nodes (configured in the application) Number of nodes (from the database) The statistics area displays the running statistics of the network: Frames (dynamic + static) in fr/s Dynamic frames in fr/s Static frames in fr/s The number of frames is included in the counter area: Number of cycles Frames (dynamic + static) Static frames Dynamic frames Null frame |
| Node View | The node view displays all the nodes of the FIBEX communication model. All frames that are either not assigned to any node in the FIBEX database or that are not defined at all, but that are received, will be assigned to the entry "Not assigned frames". The information of the statistics (all frames, static + null frames, dynamic frames) i

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/FrameHistogram] FrameHistogramWindow


# Frame Histogram

CANoe » Analysis Windows » Frame Histogram

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Statistics|View / New

|  | Note The Bus Statistics Window for CAN, LIN and FlexRay has been replaced by the new bus-specific Statistics Windows.To clarify and distinguish the features of the former Statistics Window from these Statistics Monitor Windows the Statistics Window has been renamed to Frame Histogram. |

| General |
| --- |
|  | The mean message frequency or mean message spacing of transmissions is displayed graphically. |
| Description of the Frame Histogram The Frame Histogram is used to output the mean message rate or the mean transmit spacing between messages with the same identifier. These parameters are displayed graphically above the identifiers axis. ID ranges of interest can be analyzed more precisely using the Zoom functions. The message rate/transmission interval can be represented precisely using tooltips. Switching between mean message rate or mean transmit interval is done in the dialog of the Frame Histogram in the Measurement Setup or in the Configuration dialog, which can be opened via context menu command Configure View…. The individual bars are split according to the controller source, CAN1 or CAN2, and by the message attributes Rx and Tx. |
| Configuration | Functions and Control |
| View configuration Configuration dialog Color Setup dialog | Context Menu Scaling Keyboard Operation Frame Histogram in Measurement Setup Frame Histogram Context Menu |
| Bus-Specifi

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/FrameHistogram] FrameHistogramWindowColors


# Frame Histogram: Color Setup dialog

Frame Histogram » Configuration » Color Settings

Context Menu Path: Configure View|View Configuration

In this dialog you can select Basic colors and compose Custom colors.

By clicking the [Define Custom Colors] button you can expand your dialog so that you can set the colors either based on RGB values or by direct selection within a color scale.

With the [Add to Custom Colors] button you can add this color to the palette of Custom colors.

Similarly, in this dialog you can also set the color component Hue, the color saturation Sat and the brightness Lum of the color.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/FrameHistogram] FrameHistogramWindowConfiguration


# Frame Histogram: Configuration Dialog

Frame Histogram » Configuration

Context Menu: Configure view

In this dialog you can make the following settings affecting the colors and display areas of the Statistics Window:

## Global Settings:

Here you can specify whether the message rate [msg/s] or the transmission time [s/msg] is to be displayed.

The settings made apply for the entire Frame Histogram.

## Range Settings:

Here you can specify the ID range to be displayed (horizontal axis segment) and the interval of the rates that are to be displayed (vertical axis segment) for the Statistics Window.

If you click the [Reset to default ranges] button, the standard values are restored.

## Color Settings:

In this area you can specify the colors for the signals (Tx, Rx), the background, and the text for the active window pane (View).

The Error color is used in the Special view.

Press the correspondingly colored button to open the Colors dialog, where you can select a new color.

If the Apply color settings to all active views option box is activated, the defined color settings will be used in all visible window panes in the Frame Histogram.

If you click the [Reset Colors] button, the standard values are restored.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

| Zoom out | <-> |

### [Windows/FrameHistogram] FrameHistogramWindowShortcutMenu


# Frame Histogram: Context Menu

Frame Histogram » Context Menu

Configuration... (Opens Frame Histogram configuration, which can also be opened from measurement setup.)

Configure View... (Opens the View Configuration dialog.)

Zoom Out (Enlarges the displayed area.)

Zoom Mode (When the left mouse button is pressed, the view zooms in, when a rectangle is drawn, the selected area is enlarged to occupy the entire view surface. To exit Zoom mode, select Zoom mode again.)

Fit (Displays only the area in which messages are present. When you select Fit, you automatically exit Zoom mode.)

Reset (Restores the bus-specific standard values for the settings of the active area. When you select Reset, you automatically exit Zoom mode.)

Fit All (Displays only the area in which messages are present for all visible views.)

Reset All (Restores the bus-specific standard values for the settings of the active area for all visible views.)

Tab View (Activates or deactivates the simultaneous display of all bus channels. If Tab view is activated, the following additional settings can be made:

Split The active window pane will be split.

Remove View The active window pane will be removed.

Show Special View Specifies whether Special view is to be displayed. This view displays special events such as Error Frames in the right pane. This menu command cannot be activated with FlexRay.)

Show Splitter (If the Tab View is not active, this item can be used to activate splitters between the individua

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfig


# Graphics Window: Configuration

Graphics Window » Configuration

Context Menu of the Diagram/Legend: Configuration...

In Graphic Configuration dialog the following settings are available:

## Signals/Axes

On this page you can make different settings for symbols, configured in Graphics Window, and the axis.

## Y-Axis Appearance

On this page you can configure the display of the Y-axis.

## Time

On this page you can configure the time.

## Graphic Mode

On this page you can configure the time or a symbol as the X-axis. In most cases, the display is configured to show symbols with respect to time. For frequency analyses or similar evaluations, the selection of an symbol as the X-axis is helpful.

## Diagram

On this page you configure the display of the diagram.

## Legend

On this page you can configure the display of the legend.

## Optimization

On this page you can optimize the measurement parameters of the Graphics Window.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfigDiagram


# Graphics Window: Configuration - Diagram

Graphics Window » Configuration » Diagram

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Diagram

| Symbols/Axes | Y-Axis Appearance | Time | Graphic Mode | Diagram | Legend | Optimization |  |

## Display Settings

- Show/hide grid

- Show/hide marker barIf you hide the marker bar, set markers will not be deleted.

- Show symbolic values Select this option if the labeling of the Y-axis should be taken from the symbol's defined value table.

- Show Screen Tip of measurement cursor If this option is activated, the measurement time, the symbol name and the absolute symbol value of this measurement point are displayed in a tooltip, if the measurement cursor is displayed and moved with the mouse.

- Configure the tooltip length.

- Show/hide measurement point (sample) drawing. The color of measurement points corresponds to the preset symbol color.

- Mark symbols bold

- Set display mode for symbols

- Show all graphs with color

- Gray unmarked graphs

- Show only marked graphs

## Color Settings

## Background Color

You can choose among the classical Black (scope) and the modern White.

- Grey/White sets the symbols legend’s background color to gray and the window’s background color to white.

- Grey/Black sets the symbols legend’s background color to gray and the window’s background color to black.

## Grid Line Color

You can chose separate grid colors for the two different background colors.

|  | Note The color settings affects all Graphi

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfigGraphicsMode


# Graphics Window: Configuration - Graphic Mode

Graphics Window » Configuration » Graphic Mode

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Graphic Mode

| Symbols/Axis | Y-Axis Appearance | Time | Graphic Mode | Diagram | Legend | Optimization |  |

## Graphic Mode

Here you can configure the time or a symbol as the X-axis.

- Time display In the Graphics Window the time is displayed as the X-axis for all symbols.

- XY display In the Graphics Window the symbol selected with X-axis selection is displayed as the X-axis.You can also set a symbol as x-axis via the context menu of the legend.

|  | Note The title of the Graphics Window/configuration dialog indicates whether the XY display is activated. |

## XY Display Options Regarding the Measurement

If you have selected the XY display, the following settings are available:

- Delete graphics every .. seconds Deletes all displayed curves after the specified time.

- Show all symbol values All measurement points are displayed. During longer measurements measurement points can be drawn multiple times on top of each other. As a result the drawing of curves can become slow.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

Graphics Window » Configuration » Legend

## General

Show unit in name column

Shows next to the name of the symbol its unit (if available in database).

Show details in name column

In a detailed legend, the minimum and maximum (symbol value range) is displayed in addition to the symbol name and unit (if available in the database).

## Display Behavior of Measurement Columns

The measurement columns can be shown permanently, so the legend's width does not change during activation of measurement cursor and difference cursor. Otherwise the measurement columns will be shown when

## Font

With the [Font...] button you open the Options dialog. Here you can set the font for the legend.
