### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfigOptimization


# Graphics Window: Configuration - Optimization

Graphics Window » Configuration »Optimization

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Optimization

| Symbols/Axes | Y-Axis Appearance | Time | Graphic Mode | Diagram | Legend | Optimization |  |

## Local Optimization

Here you can optimize the following measurement parameters of the Graphics Window.

## User-Defined Refresh

Here you specify how often the Graphics Window display should be updated.

If you do not activate the option box, the program automatically sets a favorable value.

You should only activate this option box if your measurement configuration places special demands on the Graphics Window (high bus load, simultaneous display of many symbols, etc.).

- Small values result in a nearly continuous display of the curve, but on the other hand they require a lot of computing power, and with slow computers this could lead to performance problems.

- High refresh values reduce the computational demand substantially, especially when many symbols are being displayed. However they result in a less smooth display of the curve.

- As long as no load problems occur during the measurement you should not change these options.

## Accuracy

The display accuracy setting is used to optimize the Graphics Window display. Using Pixel or Peak settings a delayed display can be avoided by a high amount of data.

## Display accuracy: Pixel

If multiple measurement points fall within the time range of one screen pixel, only those measurem

# Graphics Window: Configuration - Symbols/Axes

Graphics Window » Configuration » Symbols/Axes

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Symbols/Axes

| Symbols/Axes | Y-Axis Appearance | Time | Graphic Mode | Diagram | Legend | Optimization |  |

## Scaling

In the symbol list all in the Graphics Window configured symbols are displayed. The symbol name, the color of the curve and the activity state are identical to the display of the legend in the Graphics Window.

## Columns of the Symbol List

- In the first column you can select the symbols that should be displayed in the diagram.If the XY display is selected as the graphic mode, the symbol that was selected as the X-axis is marked here ().

- In the second column you can select the color of the symbol.

- In the Name input box you enter a short descriptive symbol name. This is then also displayed in the Graphics Window's legend. The display considers the global settings from the Options dialog

- You define the displayed symbol value range by entering a Minimum and Maximum.

- In the Y-Axis Caption column you enter a short descriptive name for the y-axis.

Via the context menu you can

- reset the scaling to the default

- make the scaling equal

## Common Axis/Group

You can assign several symbols to a common Y-axis/group in the Graphics Window.Matches and deviations between curves of the same type can be determined. The measurement functions such as Zoom and Fit work together for all symbol curves, so that the curves stay

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfigSignalsYAxisCommonAxis


# Graphics Window: Configuration - Common Axis/Group

Graphics Window » Configuration » Signals/Axes » Common Axis/Group

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Signals/AxesContext Menu Path of the Legend: Create Common Axis/Group

In the Graphics Window, you can group several symbols by assigning them to a common Y-axis or group in the.

Create a common axis for symbols using the same unit. Matches and deviations between curves of the same type can be determined. The measurement functions such as Zoom and Fit work together for all curves, so that the curves stay comparable in the view.

Create a group to enable or disable all symbols of the group at once.

## Create/remove a Common Axis/Group

Common axis/groups can be created, edited and deleted in the Graphic Configuration dialog or the legend.

To create a common axis/group, mark several symbols in the legend and select the Create Common Axis/Group context menu. In the New Common Axis/Group dialog you can select whether you want to create a axis or a group and give it a name.

The common axis/group is visualized by a colored bar with the axis/group name.Via drag and drop you can move symbols in and out of a common axis/group.You can also remove symbols via the context menu form the common axis/group.

In the Graphic Configuration dialog you can change a common axis to a group and vice versa via the context menu of the axis/group. Here, you can also remove a axis/group.

In the Graphic Configuration dialog, you can use the c

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfigSignalsYAxisCommonAxisProperties


# Graphics Window: Configuration - Group Properties

Graphics Window » Configuration » Signals/Axes » Common Axis/Group » Properties

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Signals/Axes

You can change the properties of a axis/group by marking the axis row.

You can change the following properties:

- Axis/Group Name The name of the group is displayed in the legend at the end of the name column within the vertical color bar.

- Axis Label The Y-axis label is displayed in the diagram on the common axis.

|  | Note Note that the axis label can be displayed in different ways or not at all, which you can set under Y-Axis Appearance. |

- Minimum / Maximum Here you can enter the minimum/maximum for the common Y-axis of the symbols.

- Axis/Group Color The color selection dialog can be used to configure the color of the common axis/group.

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfigSignalsYAxisCommonAxisSpecials


# Graphics Window: Special Features when Working with Grouped Symbols

Graphics Window » Configuration » Signals/Axes » Common Axis/Group » Special Features

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Signals/Axes

The following special features should be noted when working with grouped symbols:

## General

- Any symbols can be grouped, i.e. bit number and value type do not have to match.

- The common axis/group can be disabled in the legend. Individual symbols can be disabled, even if they belong to a common axis/group.

- The common axis/group can be collapsed/expanded to save space.

- A symbol can only belong to one common axis/group.

- Symbols of a group can only be sorted within the group with the keys.

- Copying/moving common axis/groups into other Graphics Windows is not supported.

## Y-Axis Views

- With the All Y-axes display option, symbols within a common axis only have one Y-axis in comparison to all other symbols.

- If the Separate views display option is selected, each grouped symbol is displayed as a non-grouped symbol in a separate diagram.

## Value Ranges/Scaling

- The Default all Y scaling function sets the standard values of the common axis/group. For common axis these are the lowest minimal value and highest maximum value of the grouped symbols from the database.

- The Store scalings function is used to save the value range for each common axis/group and associated symbols.

- The Get scalings function is used to restore the value range for a common ax

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsConfigYAxisAppearance


# Graphics Window: Configuration - Y-Axis Appearance

Graphics Window » Configuration » Y-Axis Views

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Y-Axis Appearance

| Symbols/Axes | Y-Axis Appearance | Time | Graphic Mode | Diagram | Legend | Optimization |  |

## Y-Axis Display

With the Show axis option you can switch the display of the axis on and off.

You have the option of displaying one or more Y-axes. You can choose between:

- Toggle Y-axes Only the selected symbol's Y-axis is shown.

- All Y-axes Each symbol is shown together with its associated Y-axis.

Sorting of axes from right to left corresponds to sorting of symbols in the legend from top to bottom. The axis of the symbol selected in the legend is located on the far right in the diagram. Any changes made to the symbol layout will result in changes to the sequence of Y-axes.

In this view selected axes can be shown on the right side of the diagram. To toggle the position of the axes just mark the symbols in the legend and chose Y-axis left/right from the context menu.

- Separate views All symbols are shown above one another with separate Y-axes.

To reset the height of the separate views to the initial height you have to select/mark all symbols in the legend and select from the toolbar.You can also reset the height of a single symbol and of selected symbols. In this case the heights of the non-marked symbols are adjusted so that their total height uses the same space as their individual initial height while the ratio

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagram


# Graphics Window: Diagram

Graphics Window » Legend

In the diagram the curve of a »symbol is displayed graphically.

## Functions of the Diagram

Via the context menu you can

- add symbols

- add measurement cursor/difference cursors

- search for values or minimum and maximum of symbols

- set specific time range

- set markers

- log a symbol curve

- import/export a symbol curve

- copy a screenshot of the Graphics Window to the clipboard

- delete measurement values from the Graphics Window

- synchronize data in analysis windows

- display measurement data statistically

- switch the Graphics Window on/off in the Measurement Setup

You can also add »symbols via drag and drop.

Via the toolbar you can

- add measurement cursor/difference cursors

- select the display of selected symbols

- set, if you want to work with one or all selected symbols

- select the axis mode

- zoom in and out in the diagram

- fit curves in the diagram

- save and load the time period and the display value range

- move the curves

- scroll in the diagram

In the configuration dialog of the Graphics Window you can make different settings for the axis (x-axis, y-axis, common axis).

Configuration of the Diagram | Context Menu of the Diagram | Function Overview of the Graphics Window | Function Overview of the Legend

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagramDeleteMeasurementValues


# Graphics Window: Delete Measurement Values

Graphics Window » Diagram » Delete Measurement Values

Context Menu of the Diagram: Delete Measurement Values...

Use this function to permanently remove old measurement data from the Graphics Window.

The Fit time Axis function resizes the Graphics Window to display all data. Use the Scroll to oldest measurement value to move the window to the oldest measured data. You can remove older data if you wish to focus on the latest data and the older data is no longer needed for your analysis.

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagramImportSignals


# Graphics Window: Symbol Assignment in Import

Graphics Window » Diagram » Import

Context Menu Path of the Diagram: Import/Export|Import...

In the Import dialog, you can import measurement series of symbols and assign them to a symbol.

For Source file, choose the file that contains your measurement series. The signal-based format MDF and different message-based formats (e.g. ASC, BLF, MF4, PCAP) are available for the source file.You can also move a file to the Graphics Window using drag and drop. The Import dialog is automatically opened.

|  | Note The import of large unsorted files can take a very long time.A dialog opens automatically when such files are imported where you can specify whether you want to sort the data before importing it.If you select [Yes], your data will be overwritten.If you select [No], the data will be sorted only temporarily for the import.You can activate and deactivate the display of the dialog in the Options dialog. The maximum number of symbols must not be exceeded during the import. See Hint in the dialog. |

After the extraction of the symbol information of the imported file, the dialog is initialized with the Default Mapping:

- If the imported file contains symbols already configured in the Graphics Window, the action under Signal Information is set automatically to Replace.

- If the imported file contains symbols that are not yet configured in the Graphics Window, the action under Signal Information is set automatically to Add.

|  | N

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagramMeasureDiffCursor


# Graphics Window: Measurement Cursor/Difference Cursor

Graphics Window » Diagram » Measurement Cursor/Difference Cursor

Context Menu Path of the Diagram: Cursors|No Cursor/Measurement Cursor/Difference Cursor

The functions are also available via the toolbar:

| Symbol | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Measurement cursor |
|  | Difference cursor |

Execute absolute and relative measured value analysis with the Measurement Cursor and/or the Difference Cursors.

With No Cursor already set measurement cursors will be deleted.

Symbol values of measurement points are displayed in the Graphics Window legend (y column) and in the tooltip of the measure cursor(when moving the mouse) by activating the measurement cursor/difference cursors. In addition, the value different (dy column) between the two measurement points is displayed for the difference cursors.

The measurement cursor and the difference cursors are displayed as vertical lines.

When the measurement cursor is switched on, the measurement time, symbol name and absolute symbol value of this measurement point is displayed in the legend and in the tooltip of the measure cursor when moving the mouse. When the difference cursors are switched on, the second measurement time and the difference time with regard to the second measurement point is also displayed. The value difference (dy) is displayed for all symbols.

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagramPause


# Graphics Window: Pause

Graphics Window » Diagram » Pause

Context Menu of the Diagram: Pause

You can use the Pause function during longer measurements to disable continuous scrolling of the Graphics Window. This may be done to observe a section of the measurement in any desired time resolution and analyze it with the help of measurement functions. If you choose Pause again normal display of the Graphics Window is resumed beginning at the current measurement time point.

In thecontext menu or in the toolbar you can see, whether pausing is active.

|  | Note Only those data are displayed that occurred up to the time point at which the Pause was activated. If the mouse is used to change the range of the X-axis that is shown during the measurement, and active measurement values no longer lie within the displayed section, Pause is automatically activated. All measurement functions only consider those data up to the time point at which the Pause was activated. Measurement functions such as Scrolling in X direction, Measurement cursor, Difference cursor, Search are activated The Minimum/Maximum column in the legend only considers data up to the time point at which the Pause was activated. Arriving data continue to be saved in the Graphics Window’s data buffer. As was the case previously, in the event of buffer overruns data may be lost and unavailable for viewing. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagramScrolling


# Graphics Window: Scrolling

Graphics Window » Diagram » Scrolling

The following functions are available via the toolbar:

| Symbol | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Scroll to oldest measurement value |
|  | Scroll left |
|  | Scroll right |
|  | Scroll up |
|  | Scroll down |
|  | Scroll to newest measurement value |

You can scroll the displayed section of the time axis according to the selected menu. For example, select Scroll left to shift the displayed curves to the right. This command always effects all symbols in the Graphics Window.

## Mouse

Deactivate the Drag Zoom mode to scroll with your mouse buttons.

| Cursor | Mouse Position | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | in the diagram | Moves all symbols in X and Y direction. |
|  | X axis | Moves all symbols in X direction. |

|  | Note Keep the button or key pressed to automatically repeat the function. These commands always act on all symbols in the Graphics Window. The scaling mode and single graph /all graphs mode is irrelevant for these commands. |

Move Curves

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagramSearch


# Graphics Window: Search

Graphics Window » Diagram » Searching for Values

Context Menu of the Diagram: Search...

You can search for values as well as the Minimum and Maximum of symbols in the Graphics Window.

## Symbol Name

Shown in the selection list are the names of all symbols that are displayed in the diagram.Disabled or hidden symbols do not appear in the selection list.

Default value: The symbol name that is selected in the legend

## Relation

The following relations are possible:

- =

- ca.

- hits

- Min, Max

- <

- >

- passes (up)

- passes (down)

Minimum and Maximum are global values referenced to the Graphics Window's data buffer. When selecting Min and Max the Value and Format fields cannot be edited. Hits searches for points where the curve passes the given value even if no measure point has exactly this given value. Passes up and Passes down are similar but they only find points, where the curve passes the value from below (Passes up) and from above (Passes down). Ca. searches for points where the measurement value approximately equals the searched value. The tolerance is 1% (one percent) of the current scaling of the curve in the diagram. Default value: hits

## Value

This field is used to enter the value to be searched. This field is not available for the relations Min and Max.Default value: 0

## Format

This field indicates the value format. This filed is available if symbolic values are provided for the selected symbol. The following values ar

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsDiagramSingleMultiSignalMode


# Graphics Window: Single-Graph Mode/All-Graphs Mode

Graphics Window » Diagram » Single-Graph Mode/All-Graphs Mode

The following functions are available via the toolbar:

| Symbol | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Single Graph |
|  | All Graphs |

Over a longer period of time, you can work with one selected symbol or with all symbols. To do this, use the single-graph mode/all-graphs mode. Depending on the selected mode, commands such as Zoom In, Zoom Out , Get Scalings effect the selected symbols or all the symbols in the Graphics Window.

Select All Graphs to work with all symbols. All symbols in the legend are automatically selected.

Select Single Graph to work with a particular symbol. Click on the symbol in the legend or diagram using the mouse to select the symbol.

In single-graph mode, you can work with several selected symbols. To do this, select the symbols in the legend or in the diagram. Selecting multiple symbols this way is helpful to quickly complete a few actions; cancel it by clicking anywhere in the Graphics Window.

|  | Note At any given time, either Single-graph mode or all-graphs mode will be active. Disabled symbols are not included in the measuring functions Zoom In, Zoom Out, etc. Operations which change time axis scaling are always executed for all symbols (independent of the option single-graph/all-graphs mode), since there is only one time axis for all symbols in each Graphics Window. |

X, Y, XY Mode

Use the commands to save and load the time period and the display value range. Furthermore, standard value ranges can be loaded into the diagram from the database.

The Graphics Window stores the current scaling setting (i.e. the displayed time period of all symbols and the value ranges of each symbol) by selecting Store scalings. Reload these saved settings by selecting Get scalings.

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsLegend


# Graphics Window: Legend

Graphics Window » Legend

You can open and close the legend via in the toolbar or directly with the splitter .

You can set the position of the legend within the window via the toolbar.

## Functions of the Legend

Via the context menu you can

- add symbols

- change symbol values

- select the line and sample type

- select the display mode of the symbols

- delete symbols

- copy symbols to the clipboard

- select the display of the Y-axis

- create a common axis/group for symbols

- set the selected symbol as x-axis

You can also add symbols via drag and drop.

Via the column view you can

- select and mark symbols

- change the symbol color

- change the symbol name for the diagram display

In the configuration dialog of the Graphics Window you can make different settings for the axis (x-axis, y-axis, common axis).

## Columns

In the list of the legend all symbols are displayed that are selected with drag and drop or via the context menu.

- In the first column you can select the symbols that should be displayed in the diagram.

- In the second column you can select the color of the symbol.

- In the Name column you can enter a short descriptive symbol name. This is then also displayed in the Graphics Window's Configuration dialog. The display considers the global settings from the Options dialog

With the context menu command Column Chooser of the table head you open the Customization dialog. You can add the following columns to the legend v

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsLegendLineTypes


# Graphics Window: Connection Type/Samples

Graphics Window » Legend » Display Options

Context Menu of the Legend: Connection Type/Samples

For the display of the symbols in the Graphics Window you can select the following line types:

| Line Style | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Line | The measurement points are connected by a line.This representation results in a continuous curve. |
| Steps | After a measurement point a horizontal line is drawn up to the time of the next measurement point, and from there a vertical line is drawn to the measurement point (staircase pattern).This representation type is well suited to binary states (e.g. On-Off symbol). |
| Dots | Only the measurement points themselves are marked.This line type automatically activates point marking. |
| Horizontal | After a measurement point, a horizontal line is output to the time of the next measurement point. |

|  | Note Rarely occurring symbols are extrapolated. |

The samples can be marked with different symbols (e.g. point, square, circle). To display the sample in the diagram, you can select the context menu commandShow Samples.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsLegendSelectMarkSignals


# Graphics Window: Selecting and Marking Symbols

Graphics Window » Legend » Selecting and Marking Symbols

For a better understanding the terms marking and selecting shall be defined in the following:

## Selection

One symbol at most is selected in the legend at any given time. The selected symbol is highlighted graphically in the legend. In the Alternating Y-axes view (Y-Axis Views) it is the axis of the selected symbol that is shown in the diagram.

In the figure CarSpeed is the selected symbol.

## Marking

Marking allows you to choose one or more symbols in order to enlarge, reduce or fit them. Multiple marking is intended for working with a small subset of the symbols for a brief period of time.

Mark the symbols in the legend or in the diagram by selecting the symbols successively while holding down the shift or control key.

In the figure CarSpeed and Gear are the marked symbols. Additionally Gear is the selected symbol.

|  | Note A marked symbol may also be selected. The measuring functions only affect the marked symbols. The measurement functions act exclusively on marked symbols. In single graph mode all commands (e.g. measure, zoom, scroll,...) refer to the marked symbols. In all graphs mode all commands refer to all symbols. You have the option of selecting/marking one symbol in the diagram by directly selecting the curve. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsLegendSignalValueDisplayMode


# Graphics Window: Symbol Value Display Mode

Graphics Window » Legend » Display Options

Context Menu of the Legend: Display

Symbol values can be displayed in two different display modes:

- In the Physical display mode the raw data are extracted from the CAN message, scaled with the (linear) conversion formula and then displayed in the Graphics Window as physical values. The necessary symbol information is taken from the database.

- In contrast, in the Raw value display mode the raw data are extracted from the CAN message and are displayed as a decimal value. There is no scaling with a conversion formula.

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowBufferAutoFit


# Graphics Window: Automatic Fitting to Buffer Size

Graphics Window » Automatic Fitting to Buffer Size

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|Time

The data you have imported might span a time period that is greater than the buffer you have configured.

- If you choose Yes the buffer size is automatically increased in size so that all data can be imported.

- If you choose No the buffer size is not changed.This means that only the last portion of measurement data that fit in the buffer is imported.

You can change the buffer size in the Graphic Configuration dialog.

Symbol Assignment in Import

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowBufferLimit


# Graphics Window: Buffer Limit

Options » Windows/Blocks » Graphics Window

Ribbon: File Tab|Options|Windows/Blocks|Graphics Window: Buffer Limit

## General

The buffer limit in KB defines the maximum memory allocation by all Graphics Windows. Above all, this makes sense if you have input a relatively large time span as the buffer size. Without a maximum limit, the operating system will continue to demand more and more memory over the course of the measurement. With page swapping this may lead to severe loads of the overall system.

The buffer limit does not restrict how much measurement data can be stored, but instead how much data can be displayed without having to compress or truncate it.

## Compression

After the measurement, the system always loads as much data as needed for display in the window from the entire program memory. If the size of this data exceeds the buffer limit, the graphs in the window are compressed. This means that not all measurement points are displayed. However, CANoe, attempts to maintain a clear symbol chart as it removed measurement points. Compressed graphs are identified by their lighter color and a notice found below graph area.

Compressed graphs do not show samples and cannot be used to determine values. If you would like to use the measurement cursor, use the Zoom In until No Compression function to reduce the size of the display.

Data is not loaded during the measurement. When the buffer limit is reached, old data is removed from the 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowDuplicateSignals


# Graphics Window: Adding Duplicate Symbols

Graphics Window » Adding Duplicate Symbols

Some of the symbols you have pasted in the Graphics Window by drag and drop or from the Clipboard may occur more than once.

- Answer the question with Yes if you wish to add each symbol in the Graphics Window just once.

- Answer the question with No if you wish to add duplicate copies of symbols in a particular case.

Duplicate pasting of a symbol might occur, for example, if you have selected multiple messages of the same type in the Trace Window and then dragged them to the Graphics Window. However, in most cases it is sufficient to show a symbol just once in the Graphics Window.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowEnableDisableSignals


# Graphics Window: Enable/Disable Symbols

Graphics Window » Enable/Disable Symbols

Via the check box in the first column of the legend or the Graphic Configuration dialog you can enable or disable symbols.

Disable allows you to disable symbols fully in the Graphics Window without removing them from the window configuration entirely. You can improve the performance of the Graphics Window in this way. Its measurement values are no longer written to the symbol buffer effective with the time point of disabling. Existing symbol buffers are preserved until the next measurement start.

|  | Note Disabling is not temporary; it is saved in the configuration. No measurement functions (Zoom, Fit, Scaling) are possible while symbols are disabled. No Y-axes are shown for disabled symbols. During the disabling phase past events are displayed as though no measurement values had been acquired. |

|  | Caution! Do not confuse disabling/enabling with hiding/displaying! |

Hide/Show/Highlight Symbols

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowExtrapolatedSignals


# Graphics Window: Extrapolated Symbols

Graphics Window » Extrapolated Symbols

Often the values of certain symbols are only sent in isolated transmissions on the bus, while other symbol values are transmitted frequently. During the measurement, the Graphics Window shows you the extrapolated last value of an infrequently occurring symbol by means of a dotted horizontal line. If a new symbol value is detected, the line is replaced by the new value and the selected line type.

The lines of all extrapolated symbol values are deleted automatically after the end of measurement.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowMOST


# Graphics Window: MOST

Graphics Window» MOST

The support of the function catalog enables message parameters to be displayed in the Graphics Window. Selecting the Add MOST signals... entry from the context menu of the given window opens the MOST Symbol Explorer. In this dialog, you can select MOST system signals such as Light, Lock or SBC and add them to the window.

You can also select message parameters. These are typically parameters of messages that have an OpType Status, which are then interpreted as signals and displayed in the Graphics or Data Window during measurement.

Alternatively the MOST Symbol Explorer can be opened via the Analysis ribbon tab, and the signals can be configured via drag and drop to the Graphics Window.

The values displayed are updated as messages arrive that transmit the parameter value and that match the selected settings (channel and InstanceID). The so-called Pos parameter, which specifies which part of the data is transmitted, e.g. in the case of arrays or records, is also evaluated.

MOST

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowOptimizationNotes


# Graphics Window: Optimization Notes

Graphics Window » Optimization Notes

Context Menu Path: Configuration...|OptimizationContext Menu Path: Configuration...|Time

The powerful symbol algorithms used in the Graphics Window place a relatively high demand on computing power, depending on the configuration and bus load. Since individual requirements vary significantly, special configuration options are provided for optimizing the display in the Graphics Window.

To optimize the Graphics Window, open the Graphic Configuration dialog from the context menu or the window's toolbar. Here you can find following Graphics Window parameters to optimize:

- Buffer limit

- User-defined refresh

- Scroll mode

Graphics Window: Troubleshooting

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowTimeNavigation


# Graphic Window: Time Navigator

Graphic Window » » Time Navigator

If you set the XY Display in the Graphic Window, a navigation bar will be displayed below the diagram. The first available time stamp is displayed on the left. If data already exist at time 0, no time stamp is shown.

With a right mouse click on the navigation bar you open the Time Navigator (orange). You can use it to move the analysis area ( the visible time slot), to jump to a dedicated time stamp, to jump to the oldest/newest time stamp, or to reduce/enlarge the time range.

Beside the navigation via the menu of the Time Navigator, you can shift the set time range with pressed left mouse button.

To shift the analysis area by 50%, you can also use a click with the left mouse button in the navigation bar on the left or right of the Navigator.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowTroubleShooting


# Graphics Window: Troubleshooting

Graphics Window » Troubleshooting

The Graphics Window is a particularly powerful but accordingly complex window in the Measurement Setup. Its performance capabilities are closely interrelated to your computer's graphics card and the graphics driver installed. Under certain conditions, effects may occur that disturb the graphics display when working with the window. Such unpleasant effects in the display can often be eliminated if you have windows redraw the window, for example by modifying the size of the window.

Make sure that the Graphics Window is not covered by other measurement windows, menus or dialogs during the measurement. Output performance may be severely impaired by such overlapping in certain situations.

Some driver-specific problems in displaying the measurement cursors and dotted lines, such as those used to draw extrapolated symbols and window grid lines, can be solved by special options in the project file CAN.INI. Refer to the documentation in section [Debug] of the project file regarding this.

## Error Messages

|  | FAQ: Some symbols were not added, because insufficient resources are available CANoe uses too many window system resources. Adding further symbols into the Graphics Window can cause an undesirable program behavior. |

Tips on Optimizing | Missing Measurement Values for Signals | Valid/Invalid Signals

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Graphics] GraphicsWindowUseCases


# Graphics Window: Use Cases

Graphics Window » Use Cases

|  | Configure fewer signals in the Graphics Window Task: Few signals should be displayed in the Graphics Window. The signals are defined in a database. From the Add Signals…context menu, insert two signals from the database into the Graphics Window. The signals are displayed in the legend. The associated Y-axis appears in the diagram, labeled with the signal name. The Y-axis value ranges are taken from the database. To change the Y value range of the signals, select the Configuration…context menu and select the Symbols/Axes page. Determine the Y value range using the Minimum and Maximum columns. To display all Y-axes, set the Y-axis view to All Y-axes using the toolbar. The Y-axis of a signal is displayed on the right side if you select the signal in the legend and open the Y-axis left/rightcontext menu. To save space, you can hide the legend via the toolbar. |

|  | Configure and evaluate signals after measurement Task: You would like to evaluate the signals of a message in the Graphics Window after a measurement. Select the message in the Trace Window and drag it into the Graphics Window. This displays all the message signals in the legend as well as their curve in the Graphics Window diagram. To display all curves completely in the diagram display area, select the Fit all graphs function in the toolbar.If you prefer to display all curves with their associated Y-axis one on top of the other, select the Y-axis view

[...truncated for brevity...]

| General |

|  | The J1587 DC Monitor examines transmitted diagnostic messages, displays their contents, and enables requests in order to retrieve diagnostic data. The J1587 DC Monitor can be inserted into the Measurement Setup as every other function block. The numerical values of the addresses (MIDs) of the send nodes, the parameter/subsystem and failure mode identifiers are shown together with their symbolic representations if the corresponding information is present in the database (dbc file). All values displayed in the time columns are reception time stamps of the diagnostic code message that is interpreted in the appropriate row. |
| Functions | Note |
| overview of ECUs sending diagnostic messages (marked red) request of all diagnostic codes display of active diagnostic codes of a specific ECU display of inactive diagnostic codes of a specific ECU history view: Illustrates status transitions of diagnostic codes enables requests of additional information ("Descriptive ASCII/proprietary message") of a specific code; deletion of Occurrence Counts; display of diagnostic responses | The DC Monitor supports Volvo proprietary diagnostic messages in terms of interpreting received content and sending requests. To enable this extension the network attribute ProprietaryFormat has to be set to Volvo in a CANdb++ database assigned to the CANoe configuration in

### [Windows/J1587DiagnosticMonitor] J1587DiagnosticMonitorHistoryView


# History View

J1587 » J1587 DC Monitor » History View

The view of the History page chronologically reveals all status changes of received diagnostic codes of a network node. In the column Status Change a transition of a specific diagnostic code to active or inactive is marked.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/J1587DiagnosticMonitor] J1587DiagnosticMonitorRequestDiagInfo


# Request of Diagnostic Information

J1587 » J1587 DC Monitor » Request of Diagnostic Information

With the J1587 DC Monitor it is possible to request diagnostic messages from ECUs on the bus. Information for specifying the request can either be provided by clicking a context menu command of a node or diagnostic code, or by using controls on the right side of a page.

Received diagnostic responses (PID 196) are displayed in the view of the Data Requestpage.

## Requests applying to a network node

Open the context menu of a node with the right mouse button and select one of the following entries to send a request:

| Menu Command | Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Request all DCs |  | Selection of this entry results in a specific request of all diagnostic codes from the selected node. |
| Clear all OCs |  | Reset of all Occurrence Counts present on the selected device |
| Insert Node |  | To enable requests directed to an ECU which is not shown (because it has not send any messages since start of the measurement), use this command to add it to the list. |

## Requests applying to a specific diagnostic code

For a specific diagnostic code, you have the following entries to send a request:

| Menu Command | Symbol | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Clear OC |  | Clears the Occurrence Count of the selected code |
| Req. Descr. Msg | ASCII |  | Request of an ASCII "Descriptive Message" for the selected diagnostic code |
| Req. Descr. Msg | proprietary |  | Request

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/J1587DiagnosticMonitor] J1587DiagnosticMonitorSpecifyDCDataRequest


# Specify DC Data Request

J1587 » » J1587 DC Monitor » Request of Diagnostic Information » Specify DC Data Request

This dialog allows to request information of specified diagnostic codes (DC) from network nodes. With [Send] the request is composed of the specified data and sent to the bus.

## Code

The values in this section specify the Failure Mode Identifier (FMI) of a system or parameter ID (SID/PID).

## Action

In this section the action requested by the receiver is defined.

- Clear OC): clears one or all (check box All) Occurrence Counts (OC) that are connected to the appropriate diagnostic code

- Req. Msg.: requests a text message (ASCII) or proprietary message (propr.) that is connected to the appropriate diagnostic code

## Receiver

MID and Channel specify the receive node of the request.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Map] MapWindowAPIdotNET


# Map Window API .NET

CANoe » Analysis Windows » Map Window » Map Window API .NET

With the Map Window API for .NET you can draw into a CANoe map window during measurement. On this help page a C# MeasurementScript is used as an example.

CANoe Configuration

To use the Map Window API, a map window must first be inserted into the measurement setup of the CANoe configuration.

A .NET MeasurementScript can then be added to either a network node or the communication setup.

It is recommended to use Visual Studio as .NET file editor. You can specify this in the Options dialog|tab External Programs|page Tools|area List of Tools.

For the .NET File Editor, select the appropriate script from the {CANoe_Exec64}/Scripts directory that matches your installed version of Visual Studio:

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2005.vbs

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2008.vbs

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2010.vbs

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2012.vbs

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2013.vbs

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2015.vbs

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2017.vbs

- Edit_NET_Source_with_VS_2019.vbs

You can then, for example, add a new C# file (.cs) to a node in the simulation setup via Configuration in the context menu of the node.

Classes

The following classes are available in the Map Window API:

| Class | Description |
| --- | --- |
| MapWindowAPI | Central class with methods to create, delete and draw map objects. |
| MapRectangle | Object for displaying rectangles. |
| MapEllipse | Object for displaying ellip

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Map] MapWindowAssignObjectToNodes


# Assign ITS objects to database nodes

Map Window » Assign ITS objects to database nodes

With the Car2x Network Explorer you can create send nodes for WLAN packets in the database, which are defined with a fixed MAC address. These send nodes can be used to display signals in analysis windows, such as the Data or Graphics Window.

During a certificate change the MAC address of a send node can change and the database node is no longer detected. For this case you can assign a received ITS object to a database node during the measurement. With this assignment the received data can always be displayed as signals, regardless if the MAC address of an object has changed or not.

To assign an object select the appropriate database node in the Node column. The displayed name is built by the schema Database name::Node name.

Option .Car2x|See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Map] MapWindowDisplayInfrastructureData


# Display of Infrastructure Data

Map Window » Map Window Functionality » Display of Infrastructure Data

The Map Window automatically displays received infrastructure data in the map:

## MAP (MAP Data Message)

Geometric data (typically static) of intersections are displayed. The tool tips of the individual elements contain additional data, e.g. the type and the width of a lane:

| Object/Element | Display | Comment |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Intersection ID |  | The IntersectionID is displayed at the reference position refPoint. The IntersectionID should be unique. All SPaT data are displayed according to the IntersectionID. |
| Vehicle Ingress Lane |  | Ingress lane for motor vehicles The stop line is located at the border of the lane element (node) which is closest to the intersection (first node). |
| Vehicle Egress Lane |  | Egress lane for motor vehicles |
| Various other lanes |  | e.g. crosswalk, bike, etc. |

## SPaT (Signal Phase and Timing Message)

Dynamic states of traffic light signals are displayed. As a precondition for the display a MAP with the associated IntersectionID has been received already.

For display of the signal state (MovementPhaseState) always the borders of the ingress and the egress lane which are located closest to the intersection (first node each) are connected with a line. The tool tip contains additional data, e.g. the signal group (SignalGroupID).

| Code | MovementPhaseState | Display |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | unavailable |  |
| 1 | d

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Map] MapWindowFunctionality


# Functionality

Map Window » Functionality

After measurement start the GNSS objects, ITS objects and ITS events are displayed in the map at their current positions. Depending on direction and speed the ITS objects are moved.

Dependent on the used protocol the following data are used:

| Protocol | Protocol Layer | Object ID | Position and Dynamic Data |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| GeoNetworking ETSI EN 302 636-4.1 | Extended Header Only packets are evaluated whose ETH Source address meets the GeoNetworking address of the sender ( eth.source.Address = geo_eh.lpvGNAddr). | Internally generated Event-ID | data of the sender in Source Long Position Vector |
| GeoNetworking ETSI TS 102 636-4.1 | Common Network Header | GeoNetworking address of the sender:geo_cnh.lpvNetAddr | data of the sender |
| BSM SAE J2735 2010-09 | Application Message Only packets with BasicSafetyMessage (BSM) are evaluated. | Internally generated Event-ID | data of the sender in blob1 |
| WAVE (WSA) IEEE 1609.3 (2010) | WAVE Service Advertisement (WSA) Only packets with one of the optional fields TwoDLocation or ThreeDLocationAndConfidence are evaluated. | Internally generated Event-ID | data of the sender in TwoDLocation or ThreeDLocationAndConfidence |
| DENM ETSI EN 302 637 3 V1.2.2 2014 11 | Application Message | Internally generated Event-ID | EventPosition from the DENM Management Container EventType from the DENM Situation Container |
| BSM SAE J2735 2016-03 | Application Message | Internally gen

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Map] MapWindowGNSSConfigJ1939Settings


# J1939 Settings

Map Window » GNSS Configuration » J1939 Settings

Analysis Tab|Map Window|GNSS Configuration|Data Source|J1939 Messages|Settings

In this dialog you can make extended settings for the data source J1939 messages in the Map Window. These extended J1939 settings are used both in online mode and offline mode.

## Channel

Here you define the channels whose messages are to be considered. Only the messages that come to the set channels are taken into consideration when determining the driven route.

Select the All option or Specific option (channel number 1-32).

## Node

Here you define the sender addresses whose messages are to be considered. Only the messages that come from the set address are taken into consideration when determining the driven route.

Select the All or Specific option (node address 0-253).

## Messages

Via the check boxes you define which messages are considered when determining the route.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Map] MapWindowGNSSConfiguration


# GNSS Configuration

Map Window » Configuration of GNSS Receiver

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|GNSS Configuration

This dialog allows you the configuration of GNSS receivers for usage in CANoe. The received data will be available as system variables in CANoe. You can visualize this data directly in the Map Window.

|  | Note You can configure up to 4 GNSS receivers. |

## Managing GNSS Receivers

With the button you can add a GNSS receiver to the configuration.

With the button you can delete a GNSS receiver from the configuration.

Via the Configuration menu you can import and export GNSS configurations as XML file. This functionality is useful to reuse an existing GPS configuration in different CANoe configurations.

## Configuring GNSS Receivers

On the left hand side all configured GNSS receivers are displayed.

On the right hand side the settings of the selected GNSS receiver are displayed and can be configured there.

## General Settings

### Receiver name

Use this name to identify a certain GNSS receiver more easily.

### System variables

For every GNSS receiver you have configured two system variable structures are created. These contain a basic set as well as an extended set of GNSS information. If the corresponding option is selected, enhanced GNSS information will be generated, too.

### Basic GNSS Information

| System Variable | Meaning | Range | Unit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Latitude | The current latitude. | -90…+90 | Degrees |
| Longitude | The current longit

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Map] MapWindowGNSSDisplaySettings


# GNSS Display Settings

Map Window » GNSS Display Settings

Context Menu: GNSS Display Settings

The context menu command GNSS Display Settings opens the dialog with the same name. In this Dialog you can make different settings regarding the visualization of the GNSS receiver and the covered track during the measurement. You can display the track as colored line.

|  | Note You can only see the distance traveled on the map if the options [Show GNSS Devices] and [Show Waypoints] are enabled in the toolbar. |

## Display of the receiver (device)

Color Here you set the color of the receiver.

## Display of the track

Show Track Here you set whether the track shall be displayed.

Width Enter the width of the line in pixel here.

Length Here you set the time limit of the track in seconds.

Color Here you set the color of the line. There are three options for coloring the track:

- Use device color: The color set for the top receiver is used for the track.

- Use static color: Here you can select any fixed color for the track.

- Use System Variable*: This option allows you to use color values defined by values of a system defined GNSS system variable, e.g. GPS::GPS1_Color for the first GNSS receiver. Each member (Track/Point/Fill) transmits a color value (RGB value) for the track traveled and track points.

|  | Note The color values can be set in a CAPL node that is inserted left of the vertical line in the Measurement Setup. The individual color values can be calculated using

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Map] MapWindowOpenStreetMapConfig


# Integrate Local OpenStreetMap® Map Data

Map Window » Integrate Local OpenStreetMap® Map Data

The Map Window provides the possibility to use OpenStreetMap® offline map data. The data is stored as tiles. You can either use sample tiles or create tiles tailored to your requirements.

Local OSM map data is especially useful if you do not have direct access to the internet during the usage.

## Use Sample Tiles

You can select local map data on the page Map Window (Options dialog). In the area OpenStreetMap® click on Local map data and select the path corresponding to the used protocol.

## Sample Tiles for EU protocols

You can find the local map data under OSM_Tiles parallel to the configuration files Car2xSystemDemo.cfg and Car2xSignalSendingDemo.cfg. The tiles show a map section of Stuttgart.

## Sample Tiles for US protocols

You can find the local map data under OSM_Tiles parallel to the configuration files V2xSystemDemo.cfg and V2xSignalSendingDemo.cfg. The tiles show a map section of Detroit.

## Create OSM-Tiles

You can generate tiles from free available data extracts packages from OpenStreetMap project. To generate the tiles, the free software Maperitive is needed. Afterward, the tiles can be used easily in the Map Window.

|  | Note The license terms for the use of OpenStreetMap maps are available here. |

## Additional software

Maperitive (http://maperitive.net/) is a FREE desktop application for drawing maps based on OpenStreetMap and GPS data.

|  | Note Maper

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] LoggingBranchParallelization


# Parallel Processing of Logging Branches

Measurement Setup » Parallel Processing of Logging Branches

Context Menu of the Measurement Setup: Show Parallelization Support

Some of the branches of the Measurement Setup are independent from each other. Processing these branches in parallel can use the processing power of multicore CPUs more efficiently.

Whether a logging branch can be processed in parallel to other branches depends on the functions in this branch. All blocks in a branch need to be independent and must support parallelization, so the branch can be executed in parallel.

There are several blocks and CAPL functions that cannot be used in parallelized branches, because they do not support parallelization yet.In addition, the following conceptual items prevent that a logging branch can be parallelized:

- write access to signals (read access can be activated in the Performance settings)

- write access to environment variables (in CAPL, you may use putValueAsync)

- write access to system variables (in CAPL, you may use sysSetVariableAsync)

- triggers that are configured to stop the simulation

## Configuration

You can configure when to parallelize a logging branch in the performance settings.

The Show parallelization support command in the context menu of the Measurement Setup activates the display which blocks can be executed in parallel.

In CAPL, you can enable a warning which is output whenever a function is called which prevents the parallelization.You c

[...truncated for brevity...]

CANoe » Analysis Windows » Measurement Setup

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Measurement Setup

| General |
| --- |
|  | The data flow is displayed graphically in the Measurement Setup.All options for parameterizing the Measurement Setup are selected in this window. |
| Description of the Measurement Setup The data flow plan is displayed in the measurement configuration window. It contains data sources, basic function blocks, hot spots, inserted function blocks and data sinks. Connecting lines and branches are drawn between the individual elements to facilitate understanding of the data flow. Either the bus connected via the hardware (Online) or a logging file (Offline) can serve as the data source. Square points (hot spots) exist between basic function blocks in the data flow diagram. Additional functional blocks can be added at these points, or data flow can be blocked at these points. If the data flow is not blocked, the hot spots permit all data to pass unhindered. Hot Spot Context Menu in Measurement Setup Insert Program Node Insert Channel Filter Insert Event Filter CAN/J1939/FlexRay/LIN/MOST Application Filter/MOST Filter/AFDX/Ethernet Insert Variables Filters Pass Filter Stop Filter Insert MOST Interactive Generator Insert Trigger Insert J1939 Node Filter Insert Break Note Bus- or protocol-specific context menus appear only if a corresponding channel is assigned in the configuration. |  | Note Bus- or protocol-specific context menus appear only if a corresp

### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] MeasurementSetupWindowConfigurationDuringMeasurement


# Measurement Setup: Configuration during Measurement

Measurement Setup » Configuration during Measurement

You have the possibility to activate/ deactivate specific analysis windows, function blocks or whole analysis branches during measurement. With it you can deactivate analysis branches which are not used any longer, to resolve performance problems or to configure isolated issues.

Analysis windows can be activated/ deactivated by activating a broken hot spot or inserting a break point in front of the window using the context menu.

Following analysis windows can be activated/ deactivated during measurement:

- Data Window

- Graphics Window

- Trace Window

- Statistics Window

- Bus Statistics Window

- Logging block

Functional blocks can be activated/ deactivated by selecting the block and choosing the context menu command Block active.

Functional blocks to be activated/ deactivated are:

- Stop and Pass Filter

- Stop and Pass Filter for variables

- Channel filter

- Trigger

- MOST Filter

- MOST Application Filter

## Particularities

- If a functional block cannot be activated/deactivated during measurement (e.g. CAPL block), the hot spots which are in the flow chart on the left side of the functional block in the Measurement Setup, can also not be activated/ deactivated.

- Logging files of Graphic, Data Window and the Logging block must have a unique name within the configuration. Otherwise blocks/ windows cannot be activated during measurement. If there is 

[...truncated for brevity...]

# Measurement Setup: Import

Measurement Setup » Import

With this function you can import Measurement Setups that you have created and saved in other configurations into your current configuration. This saves you time and effort that would otherwise be needed to repeatedly configure your measurement tasks.

Each configuration contains a separate Measurement Setup for online mode and offline mode. You can use the Import function in online mode and in offline mode.

You can import the entire Measurement Setup or parts of a Measurement Setup from other configurations.

## Import Source

select whether you would like to import individual branches from the measuring setup in online mode or offline mode for a pre-7.5 configuration with various different measuring setups

import individual branches from a measuring setup for a configuration in 7.5 or later

- select whether you would like to import the Measurement Setup from online mode or offline mode for a configuration < 7.5 with different Measurement Setups

- import a Measurement Setup for a configuration >= 7.5

The import target is the currently active mode.

- select whether you would like to impo

### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] MeasurementSetupWindowOnlineMode


# Online Mode

Measurement Setup » Online/Offline Mode » Online

## Select Online Mode

You can switch between online and offline mode in the following ways:

- By double-clicking the switch symbol

- Using the context menu of the switch symbol

- By pressing the <Space> with the switch symbol selected

- By using the Home ribbon tab

## Data Source in Online Mode

In online mode, the data of a current measurement are used.

Online represents a connection to the real hardware (working mode: Real Bus) or a simulated measurement (working mode: Simulated Bus). You can select the working mode in the Home ribbon tab.

| Symbol | Function |

|  | Start Starts, or resumes, the measurement. |
|  | Stop Stops the measurement. |

### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] MeasurementSetupWindowOnlineOfflineMode


# Measurement Setup: Online/Offline Mode

Measurement Setup » Online/Offline Mode

A differentiation is made between online mode and offline mode in the Measurement Setup.

In online mode, the data of the current measurement (hardware or simulation) is used.

In offline mode, a logging file (e.g. BLF, ASC, MF4) with recorded measurement values is used.

In the Measurement Setup, both data sources are displayed. The mode is displayed via the switch symbol and on the status bar.

In the following example, online mode is set:

You can switch between online and offline mode in the following ways:

- By double-clicking the switch symbol

- Using the context menu of the switch symbol

- By pressing the <Space> with the switch symbol selected

- By using the Home ribbon tab

## CANoe Versions

As of version 7.5, the Measurement Setups in online and offline modes remain identical.

You can no longer save different Measurement Setups for online mode and offline mode in one configuration. Instead, you must save the Measurement Setup in online mode and the Measurement Setup in offline mode in two separate configurations.

If you save a configuration in 7.5 or later in an older version (File ribbon tab|Save As), the Measurement Setup is saved twice in the configuration to ensure that it can be opened in older program versions.

See Also

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] OfflineModeConfigBreakpoint


# Breakpoints

Measurement Setup » Online/Offline Mode » Offline » Offline Mode Window » Configuration

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Offline Mode|Configuration

| Time Section | Channel Mapping | Breakpoints |  |

In offline mode you can configure the data source to stop the offline replay when a certain message or signal value occurs. This is a so called Global Breakpoint.

## Break at Time Stamp

With Active you activate the option.

If the Break at time stamp option is selected, the entire offline source is read and the offline mode is paused at the specified time. The time can be specified either in seconds from the start of a measurement or as a date and time using the Break at date, time option.

## Break on Condition

With Active you activate the option.

With the [Define Condition…] button you open the Set of User-defined Conditions dialog.

In this dialog you can input the set of conditions used to search or trigger.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] OfflineModeConfigChannelMapping


# Channel Mapping

Measurement Setup » Online/Offline Mode » Offline » Offline Mode Window » Configuration

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Offline Mode|Configuration

| Time Section | Channel Mapping | Breakpoints |  |

In the offline mode, you can adjust the channel assignment for the evaluation. It may be necessary if more channels than you require for your evaluations were recorded or if the channel sequence of the files does not match the configuration.

Channel mappings are assembled in the so-called channel mapping sets. These are defined independently of each other. A channel mapping set can be defined as standard; it is automatically assigned to all compatible offline sources if another set has not yet been assigned to them.

The individual channel mappings are defined by selecting the bus of both the source and the target channels.

|  | Note If no buses are available in the current configuration (see Channel Usage), no changes can be made to the channel mapping. |

## Toolbar

The toolbar provides the following icons for the configuration.

| Symbol | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Adds a channel mapping set. |
|  | Deletes the selected channel mapping set. |
|  | Duplicates the selected channel mapping set. |
|  | Defines the selected channel mapping set as default. |
|  | Removes selected channel mapping settings. A multiple selection is possible. |
|  | Removes all channel mapping settings. |
|  | Imports mapping settings from an XML file (

### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] OfflineModeConfigTimeSection


# Offline Mode Configuration

Measurement Setup » Online/Offline Mode » Offline » Offline Mode Window » Configuration

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Offline Mode|Configuration

| Time Section | Channel Mapping | Breakpoints |  |

## Processing Sequence

The processing of the offline sources can be configured for the offline mode via the following settings:

## According to time stamp without reference to date/time (standard setting)

The offline sources are processed according to their time stamp, date and time are irrelevant. This means that all files start with 0 and the events are arranged according to the time interval from the start. It is automatically considered whether the files have absolute or relative time stamps.

Example

In this example, three logging files are processed. A measurement was split to two files, thus the header contains the same time. The files are processed only according to their time stamp.

## According to date/time and time stamp

The offline sources are processed according to date/time and the time stamp. It is automatically considered whether the files have absolute or relative time stamps.

This option is only available if all offline sources have absolute time stamps (date and time).

Example

In this example, three logging files are processed. A measurement was split to two files, thus the header contains the same time. The files are processed according to date/time and their time stamp.

## According to source's sequence

The offline sources are 

[...truncated for brevity...]

Add Offline Source... Adds a new offline source to the list.Multiple files can be selected.New files are always added at the end of the list.

Replace All Offline Sources... Removes all offline sources and adds new offline sources to the list.

Remove Offline Source Deletes a selected offline source from the list.Multiple files can be selected.

Remove All Offline Sources Deletes all offline sources from the list.

Open Source Path Opens the target directory in Windows Explorer and the file is selected.

### [Windows/MeasurementSetup] OfflineModeWindowMetaData


# Extended Metadata

Offline Mode Window » Extended Metadata

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Offline Mode

In the Offline Mode Window, a view for extended metadata is available.

You can open this view via the splitter () below in the window.

The contents of the view depend on the contents of the selected log files. In the case of a multiple selection, the view only displays the information with the same content.

## Common

On Common page the following information is displayed:

- File name

- Creator (user name)

- Application, e.g. CANoe

- File comment

- File attachments (specific to MDF4: is only displayed if there are some)

- Measurement belonging to the file

- Index of the file if it is a part of a split measurement

## Configuration

On Configuration page the following information is displayed for every network that was configured during the creation of a log file:

- Network name

- Bus type

- Channel

- Transmission mode (Not specified, Send, Receive, Send and Receive)

- Data bases

- Additional information (data that are not further processed by CANoe)

## Miscellaneous

On Miscellaneous page the information that was neither displayed on the previous pages nor processed by CANoe is displayed.

The following information can be displayed:

### [Windows/MOSTStress] MOSTStressBusloadControlChannel


# MOST Stress Window: Bus Load on the Control Channel

MOST » MOST Stress Window » Bus Load on the Control Channel

## Control

The settings for bus load on the control channel are organized into these different areas:

## Bus Load

Here you set how high the bus load should be (messages per second) and how many messages to send (total number of messages).

- The bus load can be set to a value between one message per second and a maximum.This maximum, shown to the right of the input box for reference, will depend on the bus frequency (typically 44.1 or 48 kHz).

- If you specify a total number of messages, exactly this number of messages is generated at the preset rate, and afterwards the stress mode is ended.

- The total number of messages may lie between 1 and 2147483647.

- To the right of the input box is the Unlimited option.If this option is activated the bus load is generated continuously.

## Message

Here you configure the message to be used to generate the bus load.

- You can configure the message's destination address, message type (normal, remote read, remote write, allocate, deallocate, get source), priority and contents (Hexdump).

- The [Defaults] button resets the message contents (Hexdump) to the default value for the configured message type.This action does not reset the destination address, message type or priority.

- The Hexdump has the format specified for the configured MOST message type.

- In the Counter column you can add a passing counter to the g

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/MOSTStress] MOSTStressBusloadDataPacketChannel


# MOST Stress Window: Bus Load on the Data Packet Channel

MOST » MOST Stress Window » Bus Load on the Data Packet Channel

## Data Packet

The settings for bus load on the data packet channel are organized into these areas:

## Bus Load

Here you configure how high the bus load should be (packets per second) and the total number of packets to be sent.

- The bus load can be set to a value between one packet per second and a maximum.This maximum, shown to the right of the input box for reference, will depend on the packet size, bus frequency (typically 44.1 or 48 kHz) and value of the SBC register.

- If you specify a total number of packets, exactly this number of packets is generated at the configured rate, and afterwards the stress mode is ended.

- The total number of packets may lie between 1 and 2147483647.

- Just to the right of the input box is the Unlimited option.It enables sending of an unlimited number of packets.If this option is activated the bus load is generated continuously.

## Packet

Here you configure the packet to be used to generate the bus load.

- You can configure the packet's destination address, length, priority and contents (Hexdump).

- The packet length is automatically adjusted as you edit the Hexdump.However, you may manually change the length to a different value afterwards.

- The [Defaults] button resets the contents (Hexdump) and packet length to their default values.This action does not reset the destination address or priority.

- In t

[...truncated for brevity...]

- If you specify a total number of packets, exactly this number of packets is generated at the configured rate, and afterwards the stress mode is ended.

- The total number of packets may lie between 1 and 2147483647.

- Just to the right of the input box is the Unlimited option.It enables sending of an unlimited number of packets.If this option is activated the bus load is generated continuously.

- You can configure the packet's MAC source and destination address, length and contents (Hexdump).

| --- |

| Concept | Functions and Control |
| Concept Protocols Note With this version, the Protocol Monitor is only available for Ethernet networks. |  | Note With this version, the Protocol Monitor is only available for Ethernet networks. | Toolbar Window Networks Endpoints Details Connections |

### [Windows/ProtocolMonitor] ProtocolMonitorConcept


# Concept

Protocol Monitor » Concept

Most protocols, such as those in the TCP/IP reference model, follow a layered architecture (cf. ISO/OSI reference model). Each layer has a specific task, from pure bit transmission to the transmission of application data, and thus provides the infrastructure for the next higher layer. Most protocols can be assigned to a specific layer; some protocols also fulfill the tasks of several (adjacent) layers. In real communication, for example, there is rarely just one protocol involved, but usually an entire protocol stack.

What all protocols have in common is that they allow information to be transported from a source to a destination. The source and destination form the endpoints of the communication. To transport information, there must be a connection between the endpoints.

The layered architecture described above means that endpoints and connections themselves are also layered, following the protocol hierarchy. Hierarchically stacked endpoints and connections are the core element types of the Protocol Monitor.

## Example of the hierarchy in the representation of the endpoints

This example is intended to clarify the terms parent, child, build on, lowest, highest and root.

|  | Grouping element: Node |
| --- | --- |
| Ethernet endpoint 20:00:00:00:01:04 Endpoint of the lowest layer = root endpoint Parent endpoint of the IPv4 endpoint 192.168.1.4 |
| IPv4 endpoint 192.168.1.4 Child endpoint of Ethernet endpoint 20:00:00:00:01:04 IPv4 e

[...truncated for brevity...]

| Symbol | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Display of layers and their connections. |

|  | The connections of all protocol layers are displayed hierarchically, starting with the connections of the lowest layer. |
| Network selection. |
|  | Only the connections of the currently active network page are displayed. |
|  | The connections from all networks are displayed, regardless of the currently active network page. |

|  | Example If an Ethernet packet with the protocols IPv4 and UDP is observed, only the information of the UDP connection is listed in the view with the selection highest protocol layer (). The hierarchy view lists the information of the Ethernet connection, the IPv4 connection and the UDP connection. |

| Time | Last time at which the connection was observed. |

| Protocol | Protocol to which the connection b

### [Windows/ProtocolMonitor] ProtocolMonitorNetworkView


# Networks

Protocol Monitor » Networks

The central Network window provides a graphical summary of the network communication. The connections of the endpoints are symbolized by arrows. The endpoint hierarchy is represented by colored nested boxes. Only the connections of the selected layer are displayed.

You can configure the view of the endpoints and their connections using the toolbar.

You can expand and collapse the view of the layers within a box either by using the arrows in the box or by using the Endpoints window.

In addition to the colors, the layers are marked with an icon.

You can zoom in the view with <Ctrl>+mouse wheel. Further display functions are available in the toolbar.

A connection selected in the Connections window is highlighted in red and dashed.

Individual elements or the entire view can be moved by click and hold. To do this, click either on the desired element or on a free area in the window.

## Networks

The Networks page displays the endpoints and connections of all networks.

You can view the endpoints grouped by networks ( in the toolbar) or grouped by network nodes ( in the toolbar).

|  | Example In the toolbar are selected the highest protocol layer as connection and all endpoints. If an Ethernet packet with the protocols IPv4 and UDP is observed, the two UDP endpoints appear in the Network window. The UDP connection of the two UDP endpoints is shown as a line. The parent IPv4 and Ethernet connections are not shown according to the sele

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/ProtocolMonitor] ProtocolMonitorProtocols


# Protocols

Protocol Monitor » Protocols

The Protocol Monitor interprets protocols in the stack as far as they are known to it.

|  | Example If an Ethernet packet with IPv4 and UDP protocols is observed, in which data of a proprietary application protocol is transported, UDP is shown as the highest protocol layer. |

| Protocol | Note | OSI Layer |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Ethernet | — | Data Link (2) |
| ARP | — | Data Link (2) |
| NDP | Display of NDP connections only | Data Link (2) |
| ICMPv4 | Interpretation of Port unreachable messages | Network (3) |
| ICMPv6 | Display of ICMPv6 connections only | Network (3) |
| IGMP | Display of IGMP connections only | Network (3) |
| IPv4 | — | Network (3) |
| IPv6 | — | Network (3) |
| TCP | — | Transport (4) |
| UDP | — | Transport (4) |
| DHCP | Basic information (display of client and server) | Application (6) |
| DHCPv6 | Basic information (display of client and server) | Application (6) |
| HTTP | Basic information (display of client and server) | Application (6) |
| SOME/IP | — | Application (6) |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorAddSignalsVariables


# Signal Replay: Add Signals/Variables from Logging File

Signal Generators and Replay » Signal Replay » Add Signals and Variables from Logging File

The Add Signals and Variables from Logging File dialog you open via the Signal Replay page, if you add a logging file or via the context menu command of an existing logging file.

In this dialog, you can search a file for signals and variables.

Only one channel from each logging file is used. If the file contains signals for several channels (e.g. CAN1 and CAN2), only the signals from the channel that is read first are evaluated.

Setting a target channel determines which channel is used to output the imported signals. This makes it possible to allocate the channel imported from the logging file to a channel in the current configuration. The database in which the signals are defined must be available to the target channel.

Use the [Start] button to search the file for signals and variables.The signals and variables found are displayed in the list.

In the case of very large logging files, the search is paused after two or three seconds. The desired signals may already be listed in the results. If this is not the case, use the [Resume] button to continue the search. The progress bar indicates what percentage of the logging file has been searched.

Use the [Pause] button to stop the search when the desired signals are found.

Select the desired signals and variables from the list (you can make multiple selections) and confirm y

### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorCommonSettings


# Signal Generators: Configuration

Signal Generators and Replay » Signal Generators » Configuration

This dialog you open via Signal Generators page and the context menu command Common Settings.

Here you can configure several selected signal in common.

## General Settings

- Type TheType field defines the generator type for all selected signals.

- Log-File If you selected the generator type Logging file you must select the source file here.This field is not configurable for all other generator types.

- Active If the option is selected, the generators of all selected signals are active for measurement. The signal replay can be controlled using the Start, Stop and Pause buttons. The values are transmitted in consideration of the sending model.

- Repetitive Sending Depending on the chosen generator type this option can be selected or not, see the description of the corresponding generator type.

- Generators starts on measurement start If the option is selected the generator is started at measurement start and replayed in consideration of the sending model.This option is not available for the generator of type Logging file.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorOverview


# Signal Generators

CANoe » User Interfaces » Signal Generators and Replay » Introduction

Ribbon: Simulation Tab|Signal Generators and Signal Replay

Signal Generators are used to define a signal's sequence and measure an ECU's reaction.

Signal Generators can be assigned to signals, environment variables, system variables.

Signal Generators and how they are controlled during a measurement are defined centrally via the Simulation ribbon tab|Signal Generators.

The supported Signal Generator types are similar to those of the interactive Generator.

## About the Concept

In contrast to the generators of the interactive Generator, here it is the CANoe signal layer that transmits signal values on the bus (the CANoe signal layer is also used by the CAPL interface and by the panels).

The generators of the interactive Generator circumvent the CANoe signal layer completely. Messages sent from the interactive Generator are always assigned the signal values of the IG generator.

For this reason, we do not recommend using the interactive Generator and the CANoe signal layer in parallel for the same message.Should you decide to use both in parallel for a single message, actual bus behavior may not conform to expected behavior.

|  | Note In CAN, the transmit behavior of the signal layer is determined via an interaction layer. In LIN, the transmit behavior is determined exclusively by the scheduler. If an interaction layer is configured in FlexRay, it determines the transmit behavior

[...truncated for brevity...]
