### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorTabSignalReplay


# Signal Generators and Signal Replay: Signal Replay

Signal Generators and Signal Replay » Signal Replay

Ribbon: Simulation Tab|Signal Generators and Signal Replay

| Signal Generators | Signal Replay |  |

You can add one or more logging files on this page and use them to configure signals/variables as Signal Generators.

A logging file that is already allocated to a signal in the Signal Generators page cannot be assigned in this page.

## Toolbar

| Symbol | Funktion |
| --- | --- |
|  | Select here a new logging file. After the file selection the Add Signals and Variables from Logging File dialog is opened. Here you can search for available signals/variables. |
|  | Deletes the selected Signal Generator. |

## Context Menu

Add File... Select here a new logging file. After the file selection the Add Signals and Variables from Logging File dialog is opened. Here you can search for available signals/variables.

Add Symbol... Select a logging file in the list to configure symbols from this logging file as Signal Generators. The Add Signals and Variables from Logging File dialog is opened. Here you can search for available signals/variables.

Remove Deletes the selected symbols or logging files.

Replace File… Replaces a configured logging file with another logging file. The existing signal selection remains the same. The Verify Log File opens automatically, giving you the option of checking whether the new logging file contains the current signal selection.

Verify Symbol

### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorTypeLogFile


# Signal Generator Type: Logging File

Signal Generators and Replay » Signal Generators » Type: Logging File

The Logging File generator type is used to replay logged signal curves. The signals are transmitted on the bus using the corresponding sending model (Interaction Layer or schedule table).

In this dialog you can display and set general settings of the logging file generator.

## Generator Settings

- File Here you configure the logging file from which the signal curve is replayed.

- Verify File... Here you can verify, if the signal is in the logging file.

- Active If the check box is set the generator is started with measurement start. The signals are replayed using the corresponding sending model.

|  | Note If the Active check box is grayed an active generator already exists for the signal (tab Signal Generators or Signal Replay). |

Log Data | Signal-/Message-oriented Logging Formats

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorTypeRamps


# Signal Generator Type: Ramps and Pulses

Signal Generators and Replay » Signal Generators » Type: Ramps and Pulses

This generator type generates ramps and pulses.

Under Settings, you can determine whether the selected signal chart should be sent only once or repeatedly.

The graphic representation makes it easier for you to evaluate the defined signal chart.

## Signal Information

Here you can find information about signal values and cycle time.

- Signal Here you can find the name of the selected signal and further information to database name, node name, message name if available.

- CANdb min This field displays the minimum signal value as defined in the database

- CANdb max This field displays the maximum signal value as defined in the database

- Type min Minimum signal type value calculated from the signal length, the offset and the factor defined in the database.

- Type max Maximum signal type value calculated from the signal length, the offset and the factor defined in the database.

## Settings

The basic generator settings are displayed and defined here.

- Repetitive Sending Output Mode If this option is activated the generator continuously repeats the send operation (infinite loop).If the option is deactivated the signal is sent exactly one time.

- Start generator on measurement start The generator is started when the measurement is started.

## Generator Settings

You can make the following settings in this area:

- Pre Delay You enter the pre-delay time

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorTypeRandom


# Signal Generator Type: Random

Signal Generators and Replay » Signal Generators » Type: Random

This generator type sends random numbers between two values. Generating a random curve can be used, for example, to test system stress or responses to undefined events.

## Signal Information

Here you can find information about signal values and cycle time.

- Signal Here you can find the name of the selected signal and further information to database name, node name, message name if available.

- CANdb min This field displays the minimum signal value as defined in the database

- CANdb max This field displays the maximum signal value as defined in the database

- Type min Minimum signal type value calculated from the signal length, the offset and the factor defined in the database.

- Type max Maximum signal type value calculated from the signal length, the offset and the factor defined in the database.

## Settings

The basic generator settings are displayed and defined here.

- Start generator on measurement start The generator is started when the measurement is started.

## Generator Settings

You can make the following settings in this area:

- Min Here you enter the lower limit of the value range for the random signal.

- Max Here you enter the upper limit of the value range for the random signal.

## Graphical Output

Here the signal curve is displayed graphically.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

This type of generator toggles between two values and is, for example, suited to test bit signals.

Here you can find information about signal values and cycle time.

### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorTypeUserDefined


# Signal Generator type: User-defined

Signal Generators and Signal Replay » Signal Generators » User-defined

You can use user-defined Signal Generators

- to create signal sequences as needed,

- and to modify logged signal sequences in a targeted way.

Signal sequences created in this way are written to the selected symbol (bus signal, system or environment variable) during measurement.

|  | FAQ: Terms Sampling point A point in the signal sequence where the X value is a multiple of the sampling interval. Sampling node Fulfills the same conditions as a sampling point, but is either explicitly defined (via a table or visual editor) or is created on read-in to describe the signal sequence.The following applies: Number of sampling nodes <= number of sampling points |

|  | Note You can create a maximum of 512 sampling nodes for each user-defined generator (for memory/performance reasons).You can create a maximum of 20 user-defined generators in a CANoe configuration. The first sampling node always lies at X = 0; it represents the start value of the generator. If you zoom in very closely or make the grid spacing very small in the Editor view, the grid lines will be so close together that the area appears gray. You can return to the familiar view by zooming out or changing the grid spacing. Increasing the zoom factor in the Editor View limits the scrolling area.The Editor can only display a limited number of pages. If you want to scroll through a larger area, you need to zoom 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SignalGenerator] SignalGeneratorVerifySignalsVariables


# Signal Replay: Verify Signal Selection

Signal Generators and Signal Replay » Signal Replay » Verify Signal Selection

You open this dialog via Signal Replay page and the context menu command Verify Signal Selection.

This list shows all the signals and variables configured for the selected logging file. It indicates whether or not each signal was found in the file. Start the search with the [Start] button.

The search ends automatically once all signals are found. In the case of very large logging files, the search is paused after two or three seconds. Use the [Resume] button to resume the search and the [Pause] button to pause it.

When the search has ended, you can sort the symbols so that e.g. all symbols and variables that were not found are listed together. You can then select all of them together and deactivate them via the context menu. Click [OK] to apply these settings to the parent dialog (Signal Replay tab).

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupAddChannel


# Simulation Setup: Adding a Channel

Simulation Setup » Adding a Channel

In the System View of the Simulation Setup, you can see a tree representation of the current configuration.

If you go to Channels with the mouse pointer and click with the right mouse button, you can bring up the Add… command in the context menu. You can use this command to add a channel to the corresponding bus or add another channel to the bus.

- After you have brought up the command, the Add channel dialog appears.

- There you can see the name of the current bus as well as the bus type.In addition, all configured channels are displayed with the assigned buses if there are any.

- Use the left mouse button to select a channel.

- If you click on the [OK] button, your new assignment is accepted for the Simulation Setup.

|  | Note You can assign exactly one channel to each bus, i.e.: …if you have more channels than buses, not all channels will be assigned. …if you have defined more buses than there are channels available, there will be a configuration error.Since a (free) channel must be assigned to each bus, there is no point in defining more buses than there are (free) channels available. |

|  | Note: Closed Hardware Filters You get this warning message if you add blocks or channels to a bus in Simulation Setup and the hardware filter of this channel is closed, i.e. all messages are blocked. You may change the settings in the Network Hardware Configuration dialog. |

Configured Channels | Simul

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupAssignDatabase


# Simulation Setup: Assigning Databases

Simulation Setup » Assigning Database

|  | Note Depending on whether the network is a CAN, LIN or FlexRay network, you select the corresponding database in XML, ARXML, or DBC format. |

In the Simulation Setup, you can add one or more databases to the current network:

- Click an empty spot in the Simulation Setup to open the context menu, and then click System View.

- In the System View , open the context menu of Databases.

Here, you are offered several possibilities:

## Add

In the following dialog, select the desired file of the database. After confirmation with [OK]the database will be accepted for the corresponding network.

## Import Wizard

The Import Wizard helps you to assign the database to the corresponding node. Using the Remove nodes which are not defined in the database check box, it is possible to automatically remove nodes that do not match the database from the selection. The database is then not assigned to these nodes.

## Node Synchronization

You can also make use of the Import Wizard dialog to adapt the configuration of previously imported databases:

- Open the context menu of the database and choose Node Synchronization.

- The dialog of the Import Wizard appears. You can replace, remove, or add new nodes to which the database is assigned. See above.

- You can use the Remove context menu command to remove the database again.

## Additional Notes for Databases

|  | Note The database name shall be started w

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupAssistantDefineBusesDatabases


# Simulation Setup Assistant: Definition of Buses

Simulation Setup » Simulation Setup Assistant » Definition of Buses

| Definition of Buses | Definition of Nodes | Definition of Channels | Result |  |

You can create new configurations with the aid of the Simulation Setup Assistant. Therefore select on the File ribbon tab|New the command Create Configuration .

## Now you can make the first entries for the new configuration

- Enter the name you want the new bus to have in the input box.After that, click on the [Add] button and the new bus to be created will be displayed in the upper list box (Buses). No bus type has been defined for it yet.

- Now click on the [Database…] button.Next you have the option of loading either a template or an already existing DBC database. The name of the selected database now appears in the lower list field (Databases). In addition, a bus type that was defined by the selected database is now specified in both list boxes.

In the simplest case, you are now finished with the first input mask. However, you can also assign additional databases to the bus you have entered and/or create additional buses and then assign one or more databases to these buses. To do this, repeat the steps explained above with appropriate changes.

- When you have finished creating all buses and databases, click on the [Next >] button. This will bring you to the next input mask ("Definition of nodes").

|  | Note New databases are always assigned to the bus that is mark

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupAssistantDefineChannels


# Simulation Setup Assistant: Definition of Channels

Simulation Setup » Simulation Setup Assistant » Definition of Channels

| Definition of Buses | Definition of Nodes | Definition of Channels | Result |  |

After you have defined the nodes, assign an available channel to each bus in this input mask.

## Procedure

- In the upper list box (buses), mark the bus you would like to edit by clicking with the left mouse button.In the Available channels list box, you can see all currently available channels.

- Now use the left mouse button to select the channel you want to assign to the bus that is displayed.

- To assign the channel, click on the [>>] button.Now you can see the selected channel in the Assigned channel list box.

Repeat the steps outlined above if necessary for additional buses.

- When you have assigned a channel to each bus, click on the [Next >] button. This will bring you to the Concluding overview ("Result").

Clicking on the [< Back] button brings you back to the previous input mask ("Definition of nodes").

|  | Note You can assign exactly one channel to each bus, i.e.: …if you have more channels than buses, not all channels will be assigned. …if you have defined more buses than there are channels available, there will be a configuration error.Since a (free) channel must be assigned to each bus, there is no point in defining more buses than there are (free) channels available. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupAssistantDefineNodes


# Simulation Setup Assistant - Definition of Nodes

Simulation Setup » Simulation Setup Assistant » Define Nodes

| Definition of Buses | Definition of Nodes | Definition of Channels | Result |  |

After you have specified the buses and databases, you can assign the desired nodes to buses in this input mask.

## Procedure:

- In the upper list box (buses), mark the bus you would like to edit by clicking with the left mouse button.In the Available nodes list box, you can see all the nodes of all databases you have assigned to this bus in the previous input mask.

- Now use the left mouse button to select the nodes you want to have integrated into the new configuration.You can select multiple items by holding down the <Ctrl> key while you select all the desired nodes.

- To accept the nodes into your bus, click on the [>>] button.Now you can see the selected nodes in the Assigned nodes list box.

Repeat the steps outlined above if necessary for additional buses.

- After you have assigned all the nodes, click on the [Next >] button. This will bring you to the next input mask ("Definition of channels").

Clicking on the [< Back] button brings you back to the previous input mask ("Definition of buses").

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupAssistantResult


# Simulation Setup Assistant: Result

Simulation Setup » Simulation Setup Assistant » Result

| Definition of Buses | Definition of Nodes | Definition of Channels | Result |  |

After you have made all the necessary entries, you will see an overview of the configuration that has been created.

To accept the configuration into CANoe, click on the [Complete] button.

The assistant closes and the new configuration is displayed in CANoe.

Clicking on the [< Back] button brings you back to the previous input mask ("Definition of channels").

In the System View of the Simulation Setup you will see a tree representation of the active configuration.

If you move the mouse pointer to a block, either there or directly in the Simulation Setup, and then press the right mouse button, a context menu appears in which you can call the Rename… command. With this command you can give the active block a new name.

After you have called the command the Rename dialog is opened.

Here you enter the new/changed name of the block.

When you click the [OK] button the new block name for the Simulation Setup is accepted, and it is shown in the System View.

Insert CAN Generator - Only available for 32 bit variant Inserts a new Generator in the Simulation Setup.

Insert Interactive Generator Block Inserts a new Interactive Generator in the Simulation Setup.

Insert Replay Block CAN Inserts a new Replay Block in the Simulation Setup to play previously recorded/logged data.

Insert CANopen Interactive Generator Inserts a new CANopen IG in the Simulation Setup.

- When you have assigned all nodes, click on the [Next >] button. This will bring you to the Concluding overview (Result).

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupConcepts


# Simulation Setup: Concepts

Simulation Setup » Concepts

## Working in the Simulation Setup

Via the context menu (right mouse button) of the Simulation Setup you have access to standard operations like copy, cut, paste etc. You can also apply these functions on different bus systems.

By means of the tabbed pages at the bottom of the Simulation Setup, you can easily change between the different networks of your configuration.

Additionally you can easily shift objects. Just select the object with the left mouse button and drag and drop it by pressing the left mouse button (drag and drop.

For a simple indication and display of the real and the simulated bus the following color code applies:

- real Bus

…is displayed as a dark blue line.

- simulated Bus

… is displayed as a red line.

Additionally a Simulation Setup Assistant is provided. This offers support in setups for both simple single-bus systems and complex hierarchical network systems which can be created with relatively little effort.

If a node outputs a message to a network, a check is performed to determine whether the node actually has a link to this bus. The behavior of this function can be adjusted (see Configuration: Compatibility).

## Gateway

Several nodes can be assigned to one ECU, which makes modeling of gateways possible. A gateway is a special ECU, which is used as connection between two or several buses. The information exchange between the networks is done via the nodes of a gateway. This can co

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupDatabaseRename


# Simulation Setup: Renaming a Database

Simulation Setup » Renaming a Database

In the System View of the Simulation Setup you will see a tree representation of the active configuration.

If you move the mouse pointer to a database there, and then press the right mouse button, a context menu appears in which you can call the Rename… command. With this command you can give the selected database a new name.

- After you have called the command the Rename dialog is opened.

- Here you enter the new/changed name of the database.

- When you click the [OK] button the new database name for the Simulation Setup is accepted, and it is shown in the System View.

|  | Important Note Please observe proper notation for database names. Database names are case sensitive. The file name and its notation do not need to agree with the internal CANoe identifier (name) or its notation which you can change in this dialog. For example, if you have tied the call of a message to a specific database in CAPL programs ("message database01::mymessage m1" instead of "message mymessage m1") the program might not be compiled error-free any longer. In this case you must either change the program or undo the renaming. |

|  | Note on Names/Descriptors and on Zip/Unzip Some Zip programs have problems with certain characters, e.g., umlauts (mutated vowels; ä, ö, ü), "ß", space. These characters are altered during a zip/unzip operation without any feedback/confirmation. This results in configurations that do 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupFilter


# Simulation Setup Filter

Simulation Setup » Filter

Context Menu of Line between Bus and Node

Messages received or sent by blocks in the Simulation Setup can be filtered. Therefore you can use message filters and channel filters.

For the following function blocks you can use filters:

- ECU Input and output filters

- Replay Blocks Output filters

- Generators Output filters

Filters can be inserted in the Simulation Setup using a right mouse click on the connection line between the block and the bus.

|  | Note The filters are only available for CAN and MOST. The filters are not supported during standalone mode. |

Filter | Configuring a Filter | Configuration of the Environment Variable Filter | Channel Filter | Configuring Channel Filters

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupInsertNode


# Simulation Setup: Insert Network Node

Simulation Setup » Insert Nodes

Context Menu: Insert Network Node

This menu command is used to insert a network node in the Simulation Setup. The node is represented by a rectangular function block that is initially connected with the real bus, i.e. the black line. The function block contains two text lines. The upper line indicates the name of the node and is initially empty when a node has just been inserted. The status line beneath this provides information on the node status and type.

From the context menu called with the right mouse button you can open the Configuration dialog for the node. Here you can choose the node name to be displayed in the first text line in the function block of the simulation Setup for the network node from the database.

Simulated nodes are represented as red connection lines to the simulated bus and are identified as sim in the status line for the network node. During a measurement the node's functionality is simulated by the associated CAPL program, whereby the node's bus behavior does not differ from that of a real controller with the same functionality.

Real nodes are shown in the Simulation Setup with black connection lines to the real bus. The word real is entered in the status line. In this case the network node model has an influence on the measurement.

Simulation Setup: Context Menu of the CAN Bus |Simulation Setup: Context Menu of the LIN Bus | Simulation Setup: Context Menu of the MOST B

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupLayout


# Simulation Setup: Layout

Simulation Setup » Layout

You can insert any number of nodes in the simulation configuration by repeated selection of the menu command Insert network node in the context menu of the bus (bus lines). The status of all nodes can be switched over simultaneously with the two menu commands Switch all nodes to real mode and Switch all nodes to simulation.

To switch over the status of the node that is currently active in the simulation configuration, simply press <Space>. With each press of the <Space> key the node status changes from simulated to real to off and then to simulated again.

To remove a network node from the simulation configuration, select the command Remove in the node's contextual menu or select the node in the simulation configuration using the cursor keys and then press the <Del> key. The associated CAPL program is not deleted by this action.

Simulation Setup Filter

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupLINBusShortcutMenu


# Simulation Setup: Context Menu of the LIN Bus

Simulation Setup » Context Menu of the LIN Bus

Insert Network Node Inserts a new Network Node in the Simulation Setup.

Insert Test Module Inserts a new CAPLtest module in the Simulation Setup. In order to keep a clear separation between tests and simulations we strongly recommend to insert test modules in the Test Setup.

Insert Test Module XML Inserts a new XMLtest module in the Simulation Setup. In order to keep a clear separation between tests and simulations we strongly recommend to insert test modules in the Test Setup.

Insert Interactive Generator - Only available if minimum one J1708 channel is used. Inserts a new Interactive Generator in the Simulation Setup.

Insert LIN Interactive Master Inserts a new Interactive LIN Master in the Simulation Setup.

Insert LIN Replay Block Inserts a new Replay Block in the Simulation Setup to play previously recorded/logged data.

Insert… Opens the file selection dialog to insert a CANoe block file (CAN, XML, VXT).

Paste Inserts blocks that you previously cut out of a different network.

Paste Shortcut Inserts blocks that you previously copied from a different network. With Paste Shortcut you create a Gateway between these two networks.

Switch All Blocks to Simulation Thereby you switch the status of all nodes in the Simulation Setup over to simulation mode simultaneously. During a measurement the functionality of nodes is simulated by the assigned CAPL programs.

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupNetworkAdd


# Simulation Setup: Adding a Network

Simulation Setup » Adding a Network

In the System View of the Simulation Setup, you can see a tree representation of the current configuration.

If you go to Networks with the mouse pointer and click with the right mouse button, you can bring up the Add… command in the context menu. You can use this command to add a network to the current Simulation Setup.

- After you have brought up the command, the Add network dialog appears.

- You can enter the name of the new network here.

- You can define the bus system type of the new network.

- If you click on the [OK] button, the new network is accepted for the Simulation Setup and displayed in the System View.

|  | Note The bus system type of the new network is defined directly. There is no way to change the type later on. The new network will be automatically placed into the tree view of the System View. All further action for this network (add database, add node) are filtered so that only blocks or types for this bus system are possible. |

Simulation Setup: Concepts

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupNetworkRemove


# Simulation Setup: Deleting a Network

Simulation Setup » Deleting a Network

In the System View of the Simulation Setup, you can see a tree representation of the current configuration.

If you go there or go directly to a network in the Simulation Setup with the mouse pointer and click with the right mouse button, you will bring up a number of commands including Remove network in the context menu. You can use this command to permanently delete this network from the current configuration.

After you have brought up the command, the Remove network dialog appears and you can see a list of all databases, blocks and nodes that were affected by deleting this network.

If you click on the [OK] button, the nodes, blocks and/or databases that are displayed will be permanently deleted from the current configuration.The modified configuration is accepted for the Simulation Setup and is displayed in the System View.

Simulation Setup: Concepts

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupNetworkRename


# Simulation Setup: Renaming a Network

Simulation Setup » Renaming a Network

In the System View of the Simulation Setup, you can see a tree representation of the current configuration.

If you go there or go directly to a network in the Simulation Setup with the mouse pointer and click with the right mouse button, you can bring up a number of commands including Rename… in the context menu. You can use this command to give a new name to the current network.

- After you have brought up the command, the Rename dialog appears.

- You can enter the new or modified name of the network here.

- If you click on the [OK] button, the new network name is accepted for the Simulation Setup and displayed in the System View.

Simulation Setup Concepts

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupNodeComponentConfiguration


# Simulation Setup: Component Configuration

Simulation Setup » Network Node » Component Configuration

Context Menu of a Node in the Simulation Setup: Component Configuration…

In the Component Configuration dialog you can configure component specific parameters for the components (IL/NL). These are parameters for the component itself, for the network, for the nodes or for ECUs.

The Component Configuration dialog is active if a component has been assigned to a node in the Simulation Setup that allows configuration via parameters; e.g. ISO11783_IL that is also part of the sample configuration Virtual Terminal Demo.

Several filter possibilities are available in the dialog. With the Filter menu you can filter out the different parameter types. The two lists below can be used to restrict the view to dedicated components and nodes. The Value column can be used to set the parameters. Bold values differ from the default values specified by the component.

## Sending Behavior of Simulated Nodes

Available with included CANoeILNL_AUTOSAR_ETH.dll.

In the CANoeILNL_AUTOSAR_ETH group, you can set the sending behavior of the simulated nodes. The setting only acts on the sending behavior of the notification messages of events and fields. For SendBehavior, select one of the following Values:

- AccordingToStandard The notification messages will be sent according to the SOME/IP specification as follows:

- Event notifications: at a value update

- Field notifications: at a value change


[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupNodeConfiguration


# Simulation Setup: Network Node Configuration

Simulation Setup » Network Node Configuration

With this dialog you configure a Network node in the Simulation Setup.

## Common

## Settings

- You can give the node a title in the Title field. It is only used for displaying in the Simulation Setup. There is no other semantic meaning. The database assignment is done using the node database name.

- In the Network Node box choose the node name of a network node that was predefined in the database. This name is then shown in the first line of the function block in the Simulation Setup. If no name is selected the default name <<default>> is set to call the user's attention to a possible error.

- The behavior of a network node can be influenced by an appropriate selection in the State line.

- In the Execution field you can select how the assigned programs shall be executed.

- Normal:The program (CAPL, C# ,…) will be executed in the simulation environment.

- On hardware interface (CAPL-on-Board):CAPL programs can be executed directly on the Vector interfaces (VN1630/VN3000/VN7600). Please refer to the CAPL Code Execution Modes for Improved Real-time Characteristics.

- [Extended…] provides access to further special settings for executing a node application:

- With Start Delay and Drift/Jitter the time behavior of the simulated network node can be influenced.The Start Delay acts as an activation delay, while Drift and Jitter affect the individual timers of the node.

## Node Sp

# Simulation Setup:Deleting an Object

Simulation Setup » Deleting an Object

If you go to an object (node) in the Simulation Setup with the mouse pointer and click with the right mouse button, you will bring up a number of commands including Remove… in the context menu. You can use this command to permanently delete the selected object from the current configuration.

After you have brought up this command, the Remove Network dialog appears and you can see a list with the node that is about to be deleted.

If you click on the [OK] button, the node that is displayed will be permanently deleted from the current configuration.The modified configuration is accepted for the Simulation Setup and is displayed in the System View.

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupRemoveNodeType


# Simulation Setup: Remove all Nodes of a Certain Type

Simulation Setup » Remove all Nodes of a Certain Type

If you go to the collection of Nodes, Generators,... below a network object in the System View and click with the right mouse button, you can bring up the Delete all blocks... command in the context menu. You can use this command to remove all CAPL nodes and test modules from the given network.

After you have brought up the command, a message box appears.

If you click on the [OK] button, all blocks of given type are removed from given network.

### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupWindow


# Simulation Setup

CANoe » Simulation/Stimulation Windows » Simulation Setup

| General |
| --- |
|  | In the Simulation Setup, the overall system is displayed graphically with networks, devices and all network nodes.All options for parameterizing the Simulation Setup are selected in this window. |
| Description of the Simulation Setup In the Simulation Setup, the overall system is represented graphically with all defined networks, devices and all Network nodes. You can switch between the different networks (CAN1-CAN2, CAN-LIN, etc.) via the defined Gateways. The simulated bus from Phase 1 and Phase 2 is represented by a red horizontal line. The dark blue line above it symbolizes the physical bus from Phase 3. The two buses (real and simulated) are connected to one another through the PC card. Parameterization of the card (baud rate selection, acceptance filtering) is performed using the Hardware ribbon tab|Network Hardware or the context menu of the network card icon, which is opened by selecting the icon with the right mouse key or by selecting it with the cursor keys and then pressing the <F10> key. Simulated network nodes are represented by red connection lines to the simulated bus in the simulation configuration, and they are labeled in the node's status line with sim. During a measurement, the functionality is simulated by the assigned CAPL program. The bus behavior of the network node does not differ from that of a real controller with the same functionality. ### [Windows/SimulationSetup] SimulationSetupWindowShortcutMenu


# Simulation Setup: Context Menu of the Simulation Setup

Simulation Setup » Context Menu

Click on the white background in the Simulation Setup and press the right mouse key, so that the following context menu appears:

Object View In the Simulation Setup, an additional window is opened, which displays the properties of a selected object, e.g. IG or network node. Properties can be displayed in alphabetical order or sorted in categories.

System View In the Simulation Setup, an additional window is opened, which displays the whole simulation as tree view with networks, network nodes, functional blocks, corresponding databases and channel assignment.

Group Blocks If you would like to switch the simulation view into a remaining bus simulation part (network nodes only) and additional modules part (IG and Replay Block) use the function Group blocks. The blocks are sorted in these two different branches in the Simulation Setup. The grouping can only be undone manually by drag and drop the nodes to different places in the Simulation Setup.

Font... Here you can set the font for the blocks labeling in the Simulation Setup. The Object View and the System View are not considered here.

Zoom This function sets the zoom to x% of the standard view size.

### [Windows/StartValues] StartValuesColumns


# Start Values Window: Columns

Start Values Window » Columns

| Column | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Activation | If a group is activated, then the start values of the activated symbols in the group are set at the start of measurement. If a group is deactivated, all contained symbols are regarded as deactivated. The start values of all symbols in the group will not be set at the start of measurement. |
| Icon | The icon (variable/signal) is displayed here: System variables Environment variables Signals |
| Symbol | Contains the symbol name for which the start value is to be set.The following symbols are available: System variables Environment variables Signals |
| Physical Value | The physical value that is to be set as a start value.For system variables and environment variables, this value is equal to the raw value. For signals, the conversion factors from the databases are used. The raw value, the physical value, and the symbolic values are synchronized automatically. |
| Unit | The unit of the value. The values in this column cannot be changed. They are read from the databases and displayed as additional information here. |
| Raw Value | The raw value that is to be set as a start value.The raw value, the physical value, and the symbolic values are synchronized automatically. |
| Symbolic Value | If a value table is assigned to the symbol, the start value can be selected symbolically here.The raw value, the physical value, and the symbolic values are synchronized autom

### [Windows/StartValues] StartValuesGlobalActions


# Start Values Window: Functionality

Start Values Window » Functionality

In the Start Values Window, values that are set on measurement start can be preassigned for symbols (system variables, environment variables, and signals).

## Actions

- Action 1 The start values of the selected symbols are reset to the initial values from the data source (context menu).

- Action 2 The symbol value of the selected symbols is set to the configured start value. This is also the standard action at the start of the measurement (context menu).

- Action 3 The start values of the selected symbols are set to the current symbol value (context menu).This action is also available during a measurement.

- Action 4 The symbol value of the selected symbols is reset to the initial values from the data source (context menu).This action is also possible from the Options dialog.

## Behavior during Measurement

During measurement the following functions are not supported:

- Import of start values

- Append start values

- Export of start values

- Settings in Options dialog

- Set symbol to:

- Individual start values

- Data source values

During measurement the following functions are supported:

- Activating/deactivating of groups and start values.

- Editing group and symbol names in the start value list.

- Editing of the start values (physical value, raw value, symbolic value, comment) in Start Value Window.

- Creating and removing of groups and symbols.

- Copying and pasting of groups and 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/StartValues] StartValuesValueList


# Start Values Window: Configure Start Value List

Start Values Window » Configure Start Value List

## Groups

You can add groups to the Start Values Window via the toolbar or the context menu.

With a double click on the group cell you can directly change the group name.

By activating the Persist column, the start values of these symbols are updated outside and after a measurement and can be retained throughout the runtime of CANoe.

## Symbols (Variables/Signals)

## Enter Symbols

You can insert »symbols in the list as follows:

- Keyboard In the Symbol column, enter a portion of the symbol name.A list of all available symbols containing the typed text string is displayed. You can select the desired symbol using the up and down arrow keys. The symbol is inserted with the <Tab> or <Enter> key.

- This button is used in the Symbol column to open the following menu:

- Add Variables… Adds system variables and environment variables

- Add Signals… Add signals

The Symbol Selection dialog is opened. In so doing, the displayed »symbols are pre-filtered to contain just the relevant types (environment variables, system variables and signals).

- Context Menu With the menu commands Insert Variable... and Insert Signals... the Symbol Selection dialog is opened. In so doing, the displayed symbols are pre-filtered to contain just the relevant types (environment variables, system variables and signals).

|  | Note In contrast to opening the dialog via multiple symbols can be selecte

### [Windows/StateTracker] StateTracker


# State Tracker

CANoe » Analysis Windows » State Tracker

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|State Tracker|View / New

| General |
| --- |
|  | You can use the State Tracker to analyze states, state transitions, signals and CAN messages in order to visualize temporal dependencies. The data can stem from a range of sources (e.g. XCP, buses, I/Os on VTs) that are transmitted via system variables, environment variables, signals, diagnostic parameters or CAN messages. The State Tracker combines the functionality of a logic analyzer with the look & feel of a Vector analysis Window. |
| Use Cases and Requirements | Usage and Configuration |
| Typical Use Cases Troubleshooting and monitoring a system by analyzing the chronological sequence of states, signals and state transitions. Joint analysis of diverse data, such as: Internal ECU communication states (variables, ports, BSW and SWC states) Bus signals ECU I/Os Monitoring the states of AUTOSAR Runnables Reading out runnable states via the Vector hardware Simple analysis of binary signals and digital I/Os | Variables, objects (e.g. signals and messages), and networks are inserted via the context menu; they show up in the Legend once you've inserted them. The display of the Name column considers the global setting of the Options dialog. The window is divided into two areas: Navigation and Signal view. The navigation offers two views: Legend and Search. In Legend view, you can configure signals and simple triggers. In Search view, you can search 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/StateTracker] StateTrackerConfiguration


# Configuration

State Tracker » Configuration

State Tracker Toolbar: Show Configuration Dialog

## Measurement Objects

Here you define the most important settings for the individual measurement objects (environment variables, system variables, signals, messages, diagnostic parameters, ECUs, networks).

The page Presentation and Configuration of Measurement Objects gives a detailed description of the elements and settings.

## Time Axis

## Display

Here you set the Time unit (Microseconds/Milliseconds/Seconds/Minutes).

## Axis Mode

The signal view can either be Smart scrolling or Fixed.In smart scrolling mode you can set the range (percentage) the graph will be shifted.In fixed mode the State Tracker can Draw label values as offset.

## Optimizations

## Memory Optimization

Here you adjust the Maximum size of reserved buffer for each signal (1 MB/5 MB/10 MB).The buffer size defines the memory that will be reserved in RAM for each signal. Old data will be removed from the RAM buffer and cannot be displayed any more as soon as the buffer limit is reached.

If you Display value changes only the State Tracker logs and shows only changed values. This setting may increase the display range of a signal – depending on the update behavior of the signal/variable.

The State Tracker supports the writing of data to the system buffer (data history). For this purpose you have to activate Store signal values in history buffer.

## Performance Optimization

To improve the performance 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/StateTracker] StateTrackerPresentationConfiguration


# Presentation and Configuration of Measurement Objects

State Tracker » Presentation and Configuration of Measurement Objects

## Automatic Configuration for Variables and Signals

Signals that have a value table are assigned a color scheme during configuration. This makes it easy to identify certain values and states. The screenshot below shows a signal with a value table.

For signals you can configure a color scheme manually.You can enter a condition by specifying a value or a value range in the left column:

## Presentation of Value-Updates for Signals and Variables

Signal updates are shown as vertical lines, which are easiest to see at Sampling Points; see red highlight in the following image:

After a signal update, the horizontal lines are initially displayed as dotted lines (default setting). After a further update the two points are connected by a solid line and the state is dyed:

This settings can be changed in the configuration dialog on the Measurement Objects page; see screenshot:

When you deactivate Draw Missing Data, the range will be dyed:

## Presentation of CAN Frames

Similar to Signals and variables, CAN Frames are represented as rectangles. The length of the rectangle results from the frame duration. If the frame duration cannot be determined, only update points are displayed as vertical lines.

## Configuration of CAN Frames

There are 4 ways to configure CAN messages in the State Tracker:

- Network Section All frames that are transmitted in the ne

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/StateTracker] StateTrackerUseCases


# Use Cases

State Tracker » Use Cases

|  | Example 1: Measuring the Duration of a Value You can measure the duration of a value using the diff cursor (see screenshot below). Activate the diff cursor via the toolbar. Use your mouse to place the diff cursor in the desired positions. The values are then shown at each cursor. Note You can use the cursor either in pause mode or when a measurement is not running. |  | Note You can use the cursor either in pause mode or when a measurement is not running. |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | Note You can use the cursor either in pause mode or when a measurement is not running. |

|  | Example 2: Interrupting a Measurement Using a Defined Condition You can halt a measurement using a simple trigger (see screenshot below). Make the simple trigger visible via the toolbar. Define the condition and the value. Set the trigger action to Pause. As soon as the condition is met, State Tracker automatically switches the mode to pause. A trigger marker is shown at the time the condition was met. You can now exit pause mode by clicking the Pause button or by ending the measurement. |

|  | Example 3: Analysis of the bus workload for CAN using the Sample configuration CANSystemDemo Configure the frames according to the screenshot (below). Configure the statistic variables FramesPerBurst and Burst. Set the Stop trigger for Bursts to >50. Start the measurement. As soon as the number of bursts exceeds 50, the measurement will be stopped; see screenshot

### [Windows/StatisticsMonitor] CANStatisticsMonitor


# CAN Statistics Window

CANoe » Analysis Windows » CAN Statistics Window

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Statistics|View / New

| General |
| --- |
|  | The CAN Statistics Window displays statistics about bus activities during measurement. This Statistics Window can be inserted into the Measurement Setup via the context menus of the relevant function blocks (Trace etc.). To show the window use the Show Window context menu command. All CAN bus events that arrive at the input of the Measurement Setup are evaluated for this purpose. If a database is assigned to a CAN channel, statistics can also be evaluated individually for each available CAN node and partly for available CAN messages. CAN statistics can also be evaluated by using statistics system variables. Various statistic CAPL functions are available. In the Options dialog you can activate/deactivate the calculation of all bus statistics values. |
| Available Statistics | Description |
| Statistics Function Burst Time [ms] Duration of a message burst in milliseconds.The Current/Last value shows the longest measured time in the last sampling interval (1 second). Bursts [total] Total number of message bursts. Busload [%] Bus load in percent for a sampling interval of one second. Hints for busload calculation Calculation by the Hardware The total busload (row Busload) is calculated on the CAN hardware and considers all events on the CAN bus, including Error Frames. Note If there is no value available from the hardware (e.g. in offline

[...truncated for brevity...]

Ribbon|Analysis Tab|Statistics|View / New

| General |
| --- |
|  | The FlexRay Statistics Window displays statistics about bus activities during measurement. This Statistic Window can be inserted into the Measurement Setup via the context menu of the relevant function block (bus statistics etc.). To show the window use the Show Window context menu command.All FlexRay bus events that arrive at the input of the Measurement Setup are evaluated for this purpose. If a database is available on a FlexRay channel, statistics can also be evaluated individually for each available FlexRay node. FlexRay statistics can also be evaluated by using statistics system variables. Various statistic CAPL functions are available. In the Options dialog you can activate/deactivate the calculation of all bus statistics values. |
| Available Statistics | Description |
| Statistics Function Frames Number of valid data frames on the bus Null Frames Number of Null frames on the bus Frame Errors Number of frame errors on the bus (sum of (1), (2) and (3)) Syntax Errors (1) Number of syntax errors that occurred Content Errors (2) Number of content errors that occurred Boundary Violations (3) Number of boundary violations that occurred PDUs Number of valid PDUs on the bus Bus load of the static part Utilization of the static segment for valid data and Null frames Bus load of the dynamic part Utilization of the dynamic segmen

| General |
| --- |
|  | The LIN Statistics Window shows statistical information about the LIN bus activity during a measurement. This Statistic Window can be added to the Measurement Setup via the Function Block's context menu. To show the window use the Show Window context menu command. All LIN bus events received at the input of the Measurement Setup are evaluated. If an LDF database is assigned to a LIN channel, then some of the statistics can also be evaluated for each LIN node. The relationship between bus events and individual nodes is made using the event's Tx relationship. In the case that a Tx relationship to a node can not be uniquely identified, this event will only be considered at network level. LIN statistics can also be evaluated by using statistics system variables. Various statistic CAPL functions are available. In the Options dialog you can activate/deactivate the calculation of all bus statistics values. |
| Available Statistics | Description |
| Statistics Function Errors Bus [total] No. of total errors detected on the bus – including errors in the frame's response. Errors Resp [total] Total no. of errors in the frame's response. Errors Resp Detected [total] Total no. of errors in the frame's response detected with a response error bit. Diag No Resp [total] Total no. of unanswered Slave responses following a Master Request. Busload [%] Bu

### [Windows/TestConfigurations] TC


# Test Setup for Test Units

CANoe » Test » Test Window » Test Setup for Test Units

Ribbon: Test Tab|Test Setup for Test Units

| General Information |
| --- |
|  | All test configurations and Test Trace Windows within a CANoe configuration are managed here. |
| Description |
| A test configuration represents a set of test units that can be executed sequentially one after the other. The Test Setup for Test Units on Test Configurations page allows all actions to be performed, such as creating new test configurations, configuring a test configuration, or deleting a test configuration. To perform these actions, right-click in any free area of the window and select the appropriate action in the context menu. The purpose of the Test Trace Window is to observe and analyze single or multiple tests during the test run. All executed actions of the tests are directly displayed in the Test Trace Window during test execution. The Test Traces page allows all actions to be performed, such as creating, deleting, or renaming of Test Trace Windows. You can create and configure any number of Test Trace Windows. The window is part of the normal window management and can be opened or brought to the front at any time via the Test ribbon tab. |
| Configuration |
| A test configuration can be configured in the configuration dialog. The dialog can be opened from the context menu or the toolbar. |

The following is specified in the list for each test cases, test sequence, and test group: name result of the last test execution (verdict or"not executed") a check box for selecting or deselecting the element the time required for executing the element When the test configuration starts, all selected elements are executed sequentially in the order they appear in the list. Elements that are not executed receive the result "not executed", which is also noted in the report. ### [Windows/TestConfigurations] TCConfigTC


# Configuring a Test Configuration

Test Setup for Test Units » Test Configuration Dialog » Configuring a Test Configuration

Toolbar of the Test Configuration Window: Configuration...

You use this dialog to configure a test configuration.

## Start/Stop Event

Here you can define when the test configuration is to be started/stopped:

- Immediately after measurement start.

- When the specified environment variable changes to a value other than 0 during the measurement.

- When the specified system variable changes to a value other than 0 during the measurement.

- When the specified key is pressed during the measurement.

Alternatively, the test configuration can also be started/stopped manually in the test configuration dialog. A test configuration can be started as often as desired during a measurement. Therefore, multiple start/stop conditions can also be specified and used. While a test configuration is being executed, however, it cannot be started a second time.

|  | Note A test configuration can be started again only a short time (1 ms) after completion. Start events are ignored until processing of the test report has finished. |

## Execution Options

## Multiple Execution

Here you can specify whether a test configuration is to be executed multiple times in succession. It is possible to define the number or duration of the test runs. If you limit the duration to a particular time (days, hours, minutes), the current test run will still be completed once this time e

[...truncated for brevity...]

# Configuring Test Units

Test Setup for Test Units » Test Configuration Dialog » Configuring Test Units

Context Menu of the test unit: Configuration...

## Common

Here, you see the name under which the test unit is displayed in the CANoe configuration. The name does not have to match the test unit name from vTESTstudio. However, it must be unique within the CANoe configuration. You can change the name directly in the list of test units.

## Test Report

## Test Report

If an overall test report for all test units is not to be created (see setting in the configuration dialog of the test configuration), you can specify via the Enable test report generation check box whether or not a test report is to be created for this test unit.

## Output Options

### Test Report Path

Specify the file name of the test report file in the Test report path input box:

- use your keyboard to type in a file path in the field

- use the Field Codes button to create the test report file path

- use the […] button to open a file selection dialog

An existing file will be overwritten when the test configuration is executed.

### Test Report in XML/HTML Format

If you selected the XML/HTML test report format in the global CANoe options, you can activate an additional test report in HTML format by selecting Create HTML test report using the following XSLT stylesheet. For that purpose the XML test report file is transformed into an HTML test report file after the test has been performed. This HTML

### [Windows/TestEnvironments] TestEnvironment


# Test Environment

Test Setup for Test Modules » Test Environment

A test environment in CANoe consists of a directory structure in which the individual test blocks are inserted.

In each directory, the test environment can be edited using the context menu. The individual nodes are parameterized and checked via their respective context menu.

The nodes and directories can be moved or copied into the structure at any time using drag and drop. drag and drop with a depressed mouse button also enables you to change the sequence of the individual elements as you wish.

Each test environment is saved in an individual file and can be incorporated into as many CANoe configurations as desired. The following information is a component of the test environment file:

- Any directory structure with the individual directory names

- Comments for each individual directory

- State of the directories (activated/deactivated, expanded/not expanded)

- As many nodes as desired (test block, generator, replay) in the individual directories

- Comments for each node

- State of each node (activated/not activated) as well as its additional settings such as the generator's send list or the link to the replay file.

The test environment file has the file extension *.TSE ( Test Setup Environment).

|  | Note Even if the test environment can be used in different configurations, only one file exists in which the information is stored. A change of the test environment in a configuration thus affects al

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/TestEnvironments] TestEnvironmentExport


# Export

Test Setup for Test Modules » Test Environment » Export

Context Menu of a Test Environment: Export…

Using the export function, an individual block, a directory or a complete directory structure can be exported into a new test environment file.

Use the right mouse button to select the object in the test environment that you want to export. Using the Export entry on the context menu you can reach the file selection dialog on which you specify the storage location and the name for the new test environment.

|  | Note Please note that only the structure and the links to the individual blocks in the test environment are saved. The underlying CAPL source files and replay files are not stored too. Thus after export you have several references to the same files. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TestEnvironments] TestEnvironmentImport


# Import

Test Setup for Test Modules » Test Environment » Import

Context Menu of a Test Environment: Import…

With the import function, an existing test environment can be imported to any point in an existing test environment.

Use the right mouse button to select the point in the test environment at which you want to import the test environment. Using the context menu command Import you can reach the file selection dialog on which you can select the test environment.

|  | Note Please note that only the structure and the links to the individual blocks in the test environment are loaded into the test environment. The underlying CAPL source files and replay files are not copied too. After saving the entire test environment, you have several references to the same files. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/TestEnvironments] TestEnvironmentSave


# Save / Save As

To save a Test Environment select it with the right mouse button in the Test Setup for Test Modules and select the context menu command Save.

The test environment is now saved with all settings (activated/non-activated blocks, expansion status of the directories).

All test environments can be saved at the same time using the context menu in the Test Setup for Test Modules.

If you save or close the CANoe configuration, you will be asked for the file name for all test environments that were not yet saved in a file.

| Event | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Peer-To-Peer (NSC, TMC) | A message with a LCC = {NSC|TMC} was registered. For Data Loading Messages (ARINC 826), a different color is used. |

| Periodic Health Message (TMC) | Message with LCC = {TMC}; Function ID = 125; SMT-Bit = 1 |
| ARINC 826 SDL (loader) | Software Data Loading: Message of the Loader; Client FID = 127 |
| ARINC 826 SDL (target) | Software Data Loading: Message of the Target; Server FID = 127 |
| Direct Message Channel (DMC) | Message with LCC = {DMC} |
| Protocol Error | The following errors are recognized: wrong DLC (with LCC = {NSC|TMC}) Fragmentation error (Software Data Loading) CRC error (High Integrity Message) |
| Zero Sequence Number (High Integrity) | The sequence number of a High-Integrity-Message has the value 0. |
| Message Level Functional Status | The bit FSB and the DLC are

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowBuffer


# Trace Window: Buffer Concept

Trace Window » Buffer Concept

Each Trace Window has a main buffer and an analysis area. The main buffer contains a large portion of the measurement or the entire measurement. This is dependent on the configured size of the main buffer and the duration of the measurement. The analysis area is an excerpt of the main buffer that corresponds to the current filter settings (predefined and analysis filter). Through a suitable filter selection, you see a corresponding time range of the measurement. Older events can be subsequently reloaded from the main buffer using the Buffer Navigator.

Events that occur only rarely are saved separately if they conform to the selected filter settings. If these filter settings are changed following the measurement, it is possible that other events falling within the same time range are no longer contained in the main buffer. A system comment will be displayed to inform you of this.

## Usage/Navigation

## Chronological mode

If the number of recorded events won't fit into the analysis range, this is indicated at the left edge of the Trace Output Window. The first event of the measurement is displayed at the top.The bar represents the size of the analysis range.

With a right mouse click on the bar you open the Buffer Navigator (orange). You can use it to move the analysis area upward/downwards, to jump to a dedicated time stamp or to jump to the oldest/newest time stamp.

The loading of events to the analysis area

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowCommentEvent


# Trace Window: Set Markers

Trace Window » Set Markers

Context Menu: New Marker

In the Trace Window, you can set markers for quick location and commenting of events. The marker is assigned an event, and therefore its time stamp as well.

|  | Note Markers which were set in the Trace Window can be displayed, but not moved, in the Test Trace Window, Graphics Window and State Tracker. These markers are indicated by a small lock . If a marker is set to a time stamp which does not fit any time stamp in the Test Trace Window, Graphics Window or State Tracker, the marker is then displayed on the next fitting event in the Trace Window. Thus there can be multiple markers for a single event. A tooltip displays the markers. Only those markers which are in the analysis area are displayed. If you navigate to another marker outside this area, the trace content is reloaded. |

## Set Markers, Navigate to Markers

You can set markers via the context menu of the Trace Window or via a double-click on the marker column.

Navigation to the individual markers is possible either using the context menu of the marker column, shortcuts or the toolbar .

|  | Note Navigating between markers and also adding, editing or removing markers is only possible after the measurement or during the pause. The comments column is only then visible. In the fixed position mode not all marker actions are available. |

## Visualization

For visualization of markers, a marker column is available to you on the left s

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowEthernet


# Trace Window: Ethernet

Option Ethernet Trace Window » Ethernet

The Trace Window provides an extended functionality for analyzing Ethernet packets.

## Additional Columns for Ethernet and IP based Protocols

You can activate a set of default columns with the button [Use defined standard columns]. With [Add column(s)] you can activate more columns.

- Ethernet Contains columns for the Ethernet level, e.g. MAC addresses.

- Ethernet IP/UDP/TCP Contains columns for Internet and transport layer, e.g. IP addresses and port numbers.

- Ethernet IP/RTP/RTCP Contains columns for AVB and RTP, e.g. Payload Type.

- Ethernet IP/Services Contains columns for service oriented communication, e.g. SOME/IP.

You can configure the columns arbitrarily, not all Ethernet columns are contained in the predefined column configurations.

## Filter

## Column Filter

Since Ethernet usually transfers a lot of data, the Trace Window quickly becomes confusing. The column filters help you, for example, to display only certain protocols or IP addresses.

You can activate several column filters at the same time.

## Predefined Filter

Another way to improve the clarity of Ethernet is to use the predefined filters of the Trace Window. These filters can be used to filter certain event categories. If, for example, you want to concentrate on AUTOSAR PDU or SOME/IP, it can be helpful to hide the Ethernet packets.

|  | Step by Step Procedure: Hide Ethernet Packets Open the Trace Window. Open the predefined 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowEthernetEventHighlighting


# Trace Window: Highlight Messages

Option Ethernet Trace Window » Ethernet » Highlight Messages

Context Menu: Highlight Messages

Similar Ethernet messages can be highlighted in color in the Trace Window using the Highlight Messages context menu command.

The following commands are available in the submenu:

## Removal of highlighting

- Disable Ethernet Highlighting If events have been highlighted in color, you can use this menu command to remove the highlighting.

## For all IP-based events

- Same Source IP All events whose source IP address matches that of the selected event are highlighted.

- Same Source/Destination IP All events whose source or destination IP address matches the source or destination IP address of the selected event are highlighted.

## For IP fragments

- Same IP Fragment All events that belong to the same IP fragment are highlighted.

## For events with TCP or UDP protocol, the following submenu entry is offered in addition to the above-named entries.

- Same TCP/UDP Conversation All events that belong to the same TCP or UDP conversation are highlighted.These are events whose source IP address and source port as well as destination IP address and destination port match those of the selected event, or vice versa.

## For SOME/IP, FSA, or VCC IP CP events, the following highlighting options are also provided.

- Same Service ID All events whose Service ID matches that of the selected event are highlighted.

- Same Function ID All events whose Functi

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowEthernetInterpretationSOMEIP


# Trace Window: SOME/IP Interpretation

Option Ethernet Trace Window » Ethernet » Some/IP Interpretation

SOME/IP Scalable Service-Oriented MiddlewarE over IP defines a serialization format for exchanges between ECUs practicing Remote Procedure Call in automotive environment. SOME/IP is transported over TCP or UDP. Several of so-called SOME/IP messages can be carried inside the TCP or UDP payload.

The identification of TCP or UDP frames carrying SOME/IP messages is made with the port. CANoe looks for the list of TCP and UDP ports which are reserved for SOME/IP in the FIBEX 4.1 database, attached to the Ethernet bus,.

During measurement each TCP and UDP frame is parsed and CANoe checks if either the source or destination port of the frame corresponds to a port reserved for SOME/IP. If it is the case the frame is marked as a SOME/IP frame (in column Protocol), more exactly as a "SOME/IP over TCP" or "SOME/IP over UDP" frame (in column Protocol Info).

By pausing the Trace Window it is possible to click onto the expand icon of the frame to see the SOME/IP message's de-serialized content. Each SOME/IP message appears one after the other, with the SOME/IP header information and directly following the decoded SOME/IP payload. As SOME/IP allows the transportation of complex structures the content of the frame payload is displayed as a tree architecture. For each node of the tree a dual interpretation [raw value] [physical value] is available, so far as the SOME/IP decoding went s

[...truncated for brevity...]

Creating user-defined column layouts for the Trace Window To use the same column layout whenever you generate a new Trace Window, you can proceed as follows: Open the Trace Window configuration dialog for an existing Trace Window and go to the Column Configuration page. Select a column layout with the list field Layout. Create the column configuration you want to use by adding, removing and sorting the relevant column entries. Switch to the Column Layouts page. Select under available layouts the currently configured view. Select [Save as Standard Layout]. Newly created Trace Windows will automatically be displayed with this layout. You select a column layout for an open Trace Window using the toolbar ().
