### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFeatures


# Trace Window: Function Overview

Trace Window » Function Overview

The Trace Window offers the following functions:

| Window Configuration |
| --- |
| You have numerous options for configuring the Trace Window. In addition to the basic settings you can also configure the columns. With the splitter function of the Trace Output Window, you can analyze two different periods of time in parallel. Signal values can be displayed symbolically. With the Trace Highlighting function, you can highlight important events and messages in the Trace Window. With the fading out of unmodified data, you can set that rare events or unchanged bytes will be fade out slowly.In the global configuration dialog you can activate/deactivate this fading for all new Trace Windows. Configure the event highlighting for MOST by setting the highlight colors for events and messages to make them easier to find. In addition you can set the Event Filter for MOST messages and events. Messages with different multiplexors can be displayed in fixed mode position as separate rows. This function enhances the clarity when working with multiplexor signals.In the global configuration dialog you can set this functionality for CAN based protocols and FlexRay. You can drag and drop signals and messages from the Trace Window into a Graphics Window or another Trace Window. This requires that the contents of the Trace Window remain unchanged. You can use this function, for example, when the measurement is completed or in Pau

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFilter


# Trace Window: Filters

Trace Window » Filters

There are three basic types of filters available in the Trace Window.

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. Pinned events are not considered by Trace filters. They are always displayed at the top in the Trace Window. Configured column filters can be taken over in user-defined column layouts. |

## Predefined Filters

In the area of predefined filters, you can activate filters from a fixed set of events. Events can be deleted from the data stream.

## Analysis Filters

In the area of analysis filters you can configure Stop and Pass Filters.

The analysis filters only affect the view of events displayed in the Trace Window. No events are deleted from the data stream.

## Column Filters

You can configure ad-hoc filters directly in the columns of the Trace Window (cf. Excel AutoFilter).

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFilterAnalysis


# Analysis Filters

Trace Window » Filters » Analysis Filters

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. Pinned events are not considered by Trace filters. They are always displayed at the top in the Trace Window. Configured column filters can be taken over in user-defined column layouts. |

You can open the analysis filters in the Trace Explorer via the icon in the toolbar of the Trace Window.

The analysis filters only affect the view of events displayed in the Trace Window. No events are deleted from the data stream.

## Filter Groups

The analysis filters can be divided into different filter groups. Accordingly, you can easily switch back and forth between different trace outputs.You can add, rename and delete filter groups via the context menu of the Trace Explorer.

|  | Note The individual filter groups exist in parallel. This means they can cancel each other out, depending on their definition. |

## Filter Action Principle

- A filter group that is not activated is not taken into account. All events are displayed in the Trace Window.

- A Stop or Pass Filter is generally not taken into account when it is deactivated.This means an event passes a deactivated filter and is displayed in the Trace Window.

- An activated filter group in which no Stop or Pass Filter is activated allows all events to pass. All events are displayed in 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFilterColumn


# Column Filters

Trace Window » Filters » Column Filters

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. Pinned events are not considered by Trace filters. They are always displayed at the top in the Trace Window. Configured column filters can be taken over in user-defined column layouts. |

If you move the cursor over a table heading, a filter symbol appears in the upper right corner, e.g. .

With a left mouse click a list with all column items from the analysis area is shown, e.g. all IDs, message names, ....

You can now directly select one or more of these entries, or you can configure a complex filter condition (Custom...).

A small orange filter symbol symbolizes a set filter condition.

|  | Note The item list considers already set predefined filters, analysis filters and further column filters. The column filter is not supported for the following columns: Time (only for rel. time), Diff Time, Diff Time (Ref. Event), Diff Time (last. Occ). |

If the number of recorded events won't fit into the analysis range, further items can be loaded from the data history via Display all items. Now these items can also be used as filter.

If not all possible items can be displayed, an corresponding icon and a tooltip will be shown.

You can delete column filters with the context menu command Reset All Column Filters.

## Custom Column Filter

Col

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFilterDiagnostics


# Trace Window: Analysis Filter | Diagnostic Service

Trace Window » Filter » Analysis Filter » Diagnostics

Context Menu Path of Analysis Filter: Add Condition|Add Diagnostic Service

The analysis filter () for Diagnostic Services allows easy configuration of Stop Filter or Pass Filter in the Trace Window.

Use one of the following ways to create such an analysis filter:

- context menu of Stop Filter or Pass Filter: Add Condition|Add Diagnostic Service. In the Selection of Diagnostic Objects dialog select a service or class and add it with OK.

- Open the Symbol Explorer (Environment ribbon tab). Select filtered by Diagnostics. drag and drop a service or class to Stop Filter or Pass Filter.

- Create a filter by drag and drop of a diagnostic event from the Trace Window to the filter.

Analysis filters for diagnostics you created this way can be defined more detailed via the Filter Condition dialog. This dialog you can open on two ways:

- with the context menu command Edit Condition

- with a double click on the filter

Filter Condition dialog provides three options for Filtered Primitives:

- Only requests

- Only responses

- Requests and responses

If a filter is created using drag and drop, the specific values of the event are applied as filter conditions.

You can remove a Stop Filter or Pass Filter via the context menu (Remove Condition). Alternative you select the filter and press the <Del> key.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFilterEthernetEvents


# Trace Window: Analysis Filter | Ethernet Events

Trace Window » Filter » Analysis Filter » Ethernet Events

Context Menu Path of Analysis Filter: Add Condition|Ethernet Filter.../AVB Filter.../IP Filter

The analysis filters for Ethernet events allow easy configuration of Stop or Pass Filters in the Trace Window. In order to create filters that are as effective as possible, a distinction is made between Ethernet filter, AVB filter, and IP filter, each of which enables configuration of protocol-specific filter conditions.

You can create these filters not only using the context menu but also with drag and drop from the Trace Output Window.

The Filter Condition dialog is opened

- with context menu command Edit Condition

- when a filter is created using the context menu

- with a double click on the filter

If a filter is created using drag and drop, the specific values of the event are applied as filter conditions.If a filter is created using the context menu, the configuration dialog opens immediately where you can set the filter conditions.

## Filter Conditions

## Attributes Ethernet Filter, AVB Filter, IP Filter

General attributes of the events can be configured for all three filters:

- With Rx and Tx you can specify the direction into which the filter is to act. At least one direction must be selected.

- In case of channel-based access: With the Channel attribute you can limit the effect of the filter to a particular channel. If the filter is to act on events of 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFilterMOSTEvents


# Trace Window: Analysis Filter | MOST Events

Option MOST Trace Window » Filter » Analysis Filter » MOST Events

Context Menu Path of Analysis Filter: Add Condition|Add MOST Event...

With the MOST Filter Condition dialog, you can either use an existing MOST event in the Trace Window as a template for the filter or create a new filter.

To edit an existing event from the Trace Window, move this event from the Trace Output Window using drag and drop so that it becomes defined as an analysis filter. The filter dialog can then be opened using the Edit Condition... context menu command.

|  | Note When you drag and drop a message the analysis filter is set per default to filter all protocol types (CMS, AMS, MHP) as well as node and spy messages. This concentration on application-relevant content facilitates a quick creation of filters. If too many messages have been filtered you can restrict(/open) the filter in the filter dialog. |

For a new filter, open the dialog directly using the menu path Add Condition|Add MOST Events....

## Filter Conditions

For addressing purposes, the Channel and the Source or Destination address can be specified

The function blocks can be specified individually. If a database exists, the available values from the database are listed for the various elements. Automatic consideration is given to interrelationships of the database during selection.You can also enter the values for the elements numerically. If the database contains this value, it is u

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowFilterPredefined


# Trace Window: Predefined Filters

Trace Window » Filters » Predefined Filters

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. Pinned events are not considered by Trace filters. They are always displayed at the top in the Trace Window. Configured column filters can be taken over in user-defined column layouts. |

You can open the predefined filters in the Trace Explorer via the icon in the toolbar of the Trace Window.

## Filter Concept

A multi-level filter concept is used for the predefined filters. With a left-click on an icon you open the selection for the filter settings.

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
|  | Do not Save Events Events are not saved in the buffer of the Trace Window and thus cannot be displayed later. You can use this setting selectively to remove unimportant events from the data flow in order to free up memory space for data of interest. |
|  | Hide Events Events are saved in the buffer of the Trace Window but they are hidden in the Trace Window. The saved events can be displayed later. |
|  | Display Events Events are saved in the buffer of the Trace Window and displayed in the Trace Window. The saved events can be hidden later. |

Left-click an entry to open a field with further selection options where you can set the filters.

Fields in which a further selection exists are displayed as a link (underlined in blue

[...truncated for brevity...]

# Trace Window: Analysis Filter | SOME/IP Events

Option Ethernet Trace Window » Filter » Analysis Filter » SOME/IP Events

The analysis filters for SOME/IP events allow easy configuration of Stop or Pass Filters in the Trace Window.To allow you to specify filters more exactly, a distinction is made between a SOME/IP Filter and a SOME/IP – Service Discovery Filter, each of which enables configuration of protocol-specific filter conditions.

You can create these filters drag and drop from the Trace Output Window.

- with context menu command Edit Condition

- with a double click on the filter

## Filter Conditions

## Attributes

- With Rx and Tx you can specify the direction into which the filter is to act. At least one direction must be selected.

- In case of channel-based access: With the Channel attribute you can limit the effect of the filter to a particular channel. If the filter is to act on events of all channels, you can activate this by selecting Eth all.

- the same source address,

- the same destination address,

- the same function block,

- the same instance,

- the same function,

- the same OpType,

- the same TelID (only for CtrlFrames and MHP frames!),

- the same TelLen,

- the same channel,

- the same direction (Rx, Tx),

- the same Type (node, spy, CMS, AMS, MHP)

are highlighted.

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowJ1587ErrorCodes


# Trace Window: J1587 Error Codes

J1587 Trace Window » J1587 Error Codes

| Code | Description |
| --- | --- |
| IDLE TIME VIOLATION | The minimum time between J1708 frames has not been adhered to. Additionally, any inter-character bit time violations will be flagged using this code. |
| FRAMING ERROR | The Universal Synchronous/Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter (USART) reported an error in the data stream (e.g., erroneous start or stop bit). |
| CHECKSUM ERROR | The J1708 frame’s checksum does not match the transmitted checksum. |
| DLC ERROR | The data length of the transmitted message doesn’t match the transmitted protocol information. |
| COLLISION (@Byte …) | A collision has been detected (e.g., more than one device attempting to transmit on the J1708 bus at the same time). |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowJ1939Checks


# Trace Window: Checks of J1939 Parameter Groups

Option J1939 Trace Window » J1939 » Checks of J1939 Parameter Group

Several checks of J1939 parameter groups are processed to detect violations against the J1939 specification. If a violation is detected, the appropriate row is highlighted in color and marked with an error icon. The column J1939 Violation displays an error message.

|  | Note During measurement start error messages about the transport protocol are possible. This is caused by measurement start during a transport protocol transmission so CANoe cannot evaluate the start of the transport protocol. |

|  | Note If an import to the Trace Window was executed not all checks are processed. No checks are processed if a sequence of parameter groups has to be evaluated, e.g. the transport protocol. |

The following J1939 checks are processed:

| J1939 specification | Message |
| --- | --- |
| General checks |
| J1939-21 | [J1939] Source address must not be Null (254) |
| J1939-21 | [J1939] Source address must not be Global (255) |
| J1939-21 | [J1939] DLC must be 8 |
| Request |
| J1939-21 | [RQST] Response later than 1250 ms after request |
| J1939-21 | [RQST] Request is sent more than 3 times per second |
| J1939-21 | [RQST] Negative Acknowledge not allowed after global request |
| J1939-21 | [RQST] Previous request not responded |
| J1939-21 | [RQST] Timeout after request at <time> |
| J1939-21 | [RQST] DLC for Request must be 3 |
| J1939-21 | [RQST] Invalid destinat

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowJ1939Filter


# Trace Window: Analysis Filter | J1939

Option J1939 Trace Window » J1939 » Filter » Analysis Filter » J1939

Analysis filters are available for specialized evaluation. These filters can be configured as Pass or Stop Filters.

With option J1939 you can filter for parameter groups instead for CAN identifier. You can create analysis filters with single parameter groups or use topic-based filter conditions for specific subject areas of the J1939 protocol. A mixed usage is possible too.

## Analysis Filter with Single Parameter Groups

Single parameter groups can be added to the analysis filter as follows:

- with drag and drop from the Trace Window output

- with drag and drop from the Symbol Explorer

- via the context menu from the database

If you add a parameter group from the Trace Window output the source and target address (if applicable) are initially considered by the analysis filter. So it is possible to add parameter groups that are not defined in the database.

If you add a parameter group from the Symbol Explorer or the database the source and target address are considered as configured in the database. If an address is configured as fixed value this value is used in the analysis filter. If an address is configured as wildcard this address is initially ignored.

Initially the priority is ignored regardless how the parameter group was added.

After adding the parameter group you can configure it. For this open the configuration dialog via the context menu command E

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowJ1939Highlighting


# Trace Window: Highlighting of J1939 Parameter Groups

Option J1939 Trace Window » J1939 » Trace Window: Highlighting of J1939 Parameter Groups

J1939 parameter groups can be highlighted depending on different categories. The color can be set in the context menu of the Trace Window with the entry Configuration|View|Standard Highlighting. Alternatively the settings can be done via the Options dialog (Windows/Blocks|Trace Window).

The following categories are available for J1939:

| Parameter groups |
| --- |
| Parameter group | J1939 parameter group, which does not belong to one of the other categories |
| Request | J1939 request parameter group (EA00h) |
| Positive Acknowledgement | Positive Acknowledge parameter group (E800h) |
| Negative Acknowledgement | Negative Acknowledge parameter group (E800h) |
| Protocol Violation | J1939 parameter group that violates the J1939 specification |
| Transport Protocol |
| Connection Management | Connection Management parameter group (EC00h) |
| Connection Management - Abort | Connection Management parameter group (EC00h) with Abort command |
| Data PG | Transport protocol data parameter group (EB00h) |
| Assembled PG | assembled parameter group with a DLC more the 8, which was transmitted via transport protocol |
| Network Management |
| Address Claim | Address Claim parameter group (EE00h) |
| Request for Address Claim | Request (EA00h), with which the Address Claim parameter group (EE00h) is requested |
| Commanded Address | Comman

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowJ1939TPObserver


# Trace Window: TP Observer | J1939

Option J1939 Trace Window » J1939 » Trace Window: TP Observer | J1939

When using option J1939 the transport protocols BAM, CMDT, and Fast Packet are evaluated and the transmitted parameter groups are defragmented. Then they are handled and displayed as discrete events in the Trace Window and are available in CAPL too.

Furthermore events that indicate e.g. protocol violations referring to time-outs are displayed in the Trace Window too.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

Trace Window » Keyboard Operation

Copy, cut, paste signals by means of the standard Windows key combinations

- <Ctrl>+<c> for copy,

- <Ctrl>+<x> for cut,

- <Ctrl>+<v> for paste

Key combinations marked with a (*) are not contained in the context menu.

| Scroll one line down | <Down Arrow> |
| Scroll one line up | <Up Arrow> |
| Scroll one page down / Shift analysis range | <Page Down> |
| Scroll one page up / Shift analysis range | <Page Up> |

| Search forwards (*) | <F3> |
| Search backwards (*) | <Shift> + <F3> |
| Increa

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowPerformance


# Trace Window: Performance

Trace Window » Performance

If the Trace Window is not really needed in online mode it is advisable to interrupt the Trace branch in the data flow plan. This will free up a lot of processing capacity which can be advantageous e.g. for the performance limits of the logging function.

Since the Trace Window display functions require a certain processor capacity, there can be a delay in the display of data when the bus is under increased load. Every message is marked with a correct time stamp, however, which is issued by the PC card. If possible you should switch to the fixed position mode, to increase the performance of your system.

Disable the fading feature if processing power is low.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowPinEvent


# Trace Window: Pin Event

Trace Window » Pin Event

Context Menu: Pin Event

Pinned events make it easier for you to monitor selected messages, especially when numerous messages and environment variables are displayed in the Trace Window.

Instead of defining an additional Trace Window with an event filter, you can simply use the Pin event function.

## How it Works

- Pinned events are displayed at the top of the Trace Window ().

- You can change the sequence of pinned events by dragging & dropping the event entries.

- Pinned events remain in place, even if you clear the rest of the Trace Window contents.

- Pinned events are not considered by Trace filters. They are always displayed at the top in the Trace Window.

- The selected settings are stored in the configuration.

## Availability

- This function is only available in the fixed position mode.

- This function can only be used when a measurement is not in progress or when the Trace Window is paused.

- This function is not available for MOST messages.

- To pin system variables persistent they have to be stored in the configuration, too.Implicitly defined system variables (e.g. by I/O hardware, the VT System or XCP-defined system variables), are not persistently supported by this function.

Reference Event | Searching in the Trace Window | Filter

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowQuickFind


# Trace Window: Context Search

Trace Window » Search » Context Search

Context Menu: Context Search

The context search feature makes it easy to navigate between the messages of a channel in the Trace Window (sequential mode). The Trace Window must be paused during measurement. The following functions are available, depending on the selected message:

## J1939

| Search Criteria | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Next J1939 PG from the same source | The search looks for the next parameter group with the same source address as the current selected parameter group. |
| Previous J1939 PG from the same source | The search looks for the previous parameter group with the same source address as the current selected parameter group. |
| J1939 request from selected parameter group | If a request parameter group is selected, the search looks for the response parameter group. If another parameter group is selected, the search looks for a a request parameter group. |
| Next J1939 transport protocol from the same source | The search looks for a J1939 transport protocol transmission of the current selected source address. |
| Previous J1939 transport protocol from the same source | The search looks for a J1939 transport protocol transmission of the current selected source address in reverse order. |
| Start of J1939 transport protocol | The search looks for the start of a J1939 transport protocol transmission of the current selected source address. |
| End of J1939 transport protocol | The 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowReferenceEvent


# Trace Window: Reference Event

Trace Window » Reference Event

Context Menu: Set as Reference Event / Find Reference Event

A reference event can be used like a bookmark in the Trace Window.After you set a reference event the Difference view will always show a comparison of the last selected event with the reference event.

## Set as Reference Event

Using this function you can define the selected event as your reference event.Only one event can be defined as the reference event at any given time.

## Find Reference Event

This function can be used to find the previously defined reference event.This causes the visible area of the Trace Window to shift so that the reference event is visible.

Searching in the Trace Window | Pin event | Filter

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowSearch


# Trace Window: Search

Trace Window » Search

The Trace Window contains various search criteria in the Search page of the Trace Explorer.

The search is always started from the current cursor position in the display area. If the display area search has been completed, you can extend the search to the data history after the measurement. You can cancel the search at any time.

|  | Note General information about the search functions All displayed columns are searched. Hidden columns are not taken into account in the search. The detail view is not searched. If the search is started during the measurement, the Trace Window goes into pause mode when the first event is found. In fixed position mode, only the current data (current display) is searched and not the entire buffer. |

## Search Options

Depending on the search criterion, the following options are available.

- Match case

- Find whole words only

- Search in subordinate rows Here you can specify whether the text in the subordinate rows (generally display of signals of a message) should also be searched. The lines do not have to be expanded for the search. If searching for a result in the first row only, deactivate this option to accelerate the search.

- Consider marker text Here you can specify whether the text of the set markers (marker name and comment) will also be searched during a text search.This search option is only available in continuous mode of the Trace Window.

- Keep selection All search results remain 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowSplitter


# Trace Window: Splitter

Trace Window » Splitter

After the measurement, the Trace Output Window can be split into two views (horizontal or vertical adjustment) via the toolbar .Because the analysis area of the buffers can be moved in the two views independently of one another, you can analyze different time segments in parallel in the Trace Window. Selected lines as well as expanded information are displayed independently of each other.

If you specify a reference event, pin an event, or enter a marker for an event, this setting will be applied to both views.

All configuration settings for the Trace Window, e.g., filters or columns apply to both views.

The second view is closed again at the start of a measurement.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Trace] TraceWindowToolbar


# Trace Window: Toolbar

Trace Window » Toolbar

The toolbar provides the following symbols for controlling the Trace Window:

## The functions are as follows:

| Symbol | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Detail view in Trace Explorer. |
|  | Statistics view in Trace Explorer. |
|  | Difference view in Trace Explorer. |
|  | Predefined filter in Trace Explorer. |
|  | Analysis filter in Trace Explorer. |
|  | Search view in Trace Explorer. |
|  | Clear Trace Window contents. |
|  | Faded events/data can be deleted from the Trace Output Window. |
|  | Suspends/Resumes Trace Window update. |
|  | Toggles the Trace Window time mode (absolute/relative). |
|  | Toggles the Trace Window display mode (fixed/chronological): Chronological mode (activated symbol)The window content is updated with each newly arriving event. Fixed position (deactivated symbol)Events are only cyclically updated. |
|  | Activates/deactivates analysis filters. |
|  | Activates/deactivates column filters. If you have not defined a column filter, the function is inactive. |
|  | Field to insert a string to be searched for |
|  | Search backwards in the Trace Window |
|  | Search forwards in the Trace Window |
|  | Navigates to previous navigation time point in the navigation history of the analysis area. |
|  | Navigates to next navigation time point in the navigation history of the analysis area. |
|  | Go to marker (only available in chronological mode) |
|  | Opens the Trace Window configuration dialog. |
| 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace/Columns] TraceColumnsARINC825


# Columns for ARINC 825

ARINC-825 Trace Window (ARINC-825) » Column Configuration » ARINC 825

## Standard Trace Window Layouts

There are various predefined layouts for the Trace Window.

|  | View | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1. | ARINC 825 Basic | This layout shows the all parts of the CAN identifier for peer-to-peer and one-to-many communication. |
| 2. | ARINC 825 Services | This layout is preferred for presenting node services. The columns Services and Interpretation are shown. |
| 3. | ARINC 825 DMC | This layout displays the addressing parts of DMC (direct message communication) messages. |
| 4. | ARINC 826 SDL | This layout corresponds to "ARINC 825 Services" without displaying the columns LCC and SMT. |

## Columns for ARINC 825

|  | Field | Column Title | Description | Default |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1. | 2. | 3. | 4. |
| 1. | Time | Time | Rx time stamp in seconds since measurement start | • | • | • | • |
| 2. | Date and Time | Date and Time | Receive time stamp with date and time since measurement start. Logging files generated with CANoe always contain date and time of measurement start in the Header. If a logging file does not contain this information, then date and time of the offline measurement start are displayed in this column. | – | – | – | – |
| 3. | Diff Time | Diff Time | Time relative to the previous event. In chronological mode, the column displays the time difference to the visible, previous row.This means that the value depe

[...truncated for brevity...]

## Columns for CAN

| Field | Title | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |

| DLC | DLC | Length of the data field in decimal representation. |
| Data | Data | The data in decimal or hexadecimal representation.For remote frames the Data column indicates Remote Frame. At the interpretation of the OSEK TP protocol square brackets are displayed to distinguish between TP protocol bytes, unused bytes and user data. TP protocol bytes and not used bytes are displayed in brackets. |
| Attribute | Attr | Additional attributes:WU = Wake-up,TE = Transceiver Error,XX = Transceiver Error and Wake-up |
| Protocol time | Frame Duration | Duration of the complete event in milliseconds and bit times. It also contains the Inter-Fra

### [Windows/Trace/Columns] TraceColumnsCANopen


# Trace Window: Columns for CANopen

Option .CANopen Trace Window » CANopen » Configuration » Column Configuration » CANopen

Context Menu Path: Configuration|Column Configuration|CANopen

To select CANopen specific columns select CANopen in the list box Columns layout. The columns defined in the standard column set of CANopen are displayed in the Trace Window. The additional columns have the following meaning:

## Transfer Data

The data transmitted by the current service is displayed in the set numeric basis (hex or dec). In contrast to the standard column Data, these are only the pure reference data here. With an expedited SDO, for example, only 1-4 bytes are displayed instead of the 8-byte raw data.

Generally this data is output as raw values, with the following exceptions:

| Service | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| Abort SDO | Error code from abort message as 32 bit value |
| Guard request | Output of the identifier RTR |
| Guard response or heartbeat | The symbols T0 and T1 display the current value of the toggle bit. The device state is specified in plain text. |
| Expedited SDO | For the transmission of mapping entries in a SDO, the mapped object is displayed. This makes the evaluation of mapping changes easier. Example:08 01 00 62 [6200.01] [1600.01] Initiate... |

## Error

This column contains notes about errors. Error types are here:

| Abbreviation | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| A | SDO Abort |
| E | Emergency |
| G | Guard-Error: unknown status in guard response or h

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace/Columns] TraceColumnsCANopenSafety


# Trace Window: CANopen Safety

Option .CANopen Trace Window » CANopen » Configuration » Column Configuration » CANopen

Context Menu Path: Configuration|Column Configuration|CANopen Safety

To select CANopen Safety specific columns select CANopen Safety in the list box Columns layout. It provides additional columns besides the CANopen specific columns.

## Error

In addition to the CANopen specific error types a V (for Violation) can be displayed here. The exact cause can be found in the column Interpretation. If an error cannot be ruled out a question mark can be displayed.

## Interpretation

The columns displays the frame that belongs to the SRDO, including the ID of the corresponding frame in brackets. Protocol violations are logged too.

| Output | Description |
| --- | --- |
| SCT expired | Exceeding the Safeguard Cycle Time |
| SRVT expired | Exceeding the Safety Relevant Validation Time |
| Frame order error | Violations of the order rule: it is monitored for each SRDO whether the first and second transmission as well as two succeeding transmission take place in the correct order. If this error occurs no more errors are output. |
| Inconsistent DLC | The data length code is inconsistent. Data might be incorrect too but due to the higher priority of the DLC error only the DLC error is output. |
| Inconsistent Data | An error was detected during bit-wise inversion. |

## Refresh Time

This column displays the time span between the both first frames of succeeding SRDOs

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace/Columns] TraceColumnsFlexRay2


# Trace Window: FlexRay Type Column

Option .FlexRay Trace Window » Configuration » Column Configuration » FlexRay » Type Column

Context Menu Path: Configuration|Column Configuration|FlexRay

The following events are displayed in the Type column:

| Event | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Data Frame | Indicates a FlexRay frame with valid data. |
| Null Frame | Indicates a FlexRay Null Frame with invalid data. The null indicator bit in the frame header is 0. |
| Frame Error | Indicates erroneous frame receipt. The received frame is damaged or is outside its transmission time window. For more detailed information on the error, see the Frame State (Ab.) and Frame State columns. |
| Raw Frame | Indicates that a frame was received, not a PDU!These events are only displayed with certain FIBEX databases with appropriate PDU information. |
| PDU | This is an interpretation of a PDU, not a frame.These events are only displayed with certain FIBEX databases with appropriate PDU information. |
| StartCycleEvent | The time stamp of this event indicates the beginning of a cycle. |
| Status | This indicates a notification of a POC (Protocol Operation Control) state change of the FlexRay Communication Controller. |
| Symbol | A symbol was received. This can be one of the following symbols: wake-up, CAS or MTS. |
| Error Event | Indicates a FlexRay bus error event. The table below contains possible error events and their meanings: Error Event Type Meaning Driver receive queue overflow The dri

Option .Ethernet Trace Window » Ethernet » Configuration » Column Configuration » Ethernet Ethernet IP/RTP/RTCP

Context Menu Path: Configuration|Column Configuration|Ethernet IP/RTP/RTCP

## Columns for Ethernet IP/RTP/RTCP

| Field | Column | Function | Default-Set |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Time | Time | Receive time stamp in seconds since start of measurement | • |
| Channel | Chn | Channel of the Ethernet packet | • |
| Dir | Dir | Direction Rx: reception Tx: transmission | • |
| DLC | Payload Length | Number of payload bytes of the protocol display in column Protocol | — |
| Data | Payload Data | Payload data of the protocol display in column Protocol | — |
| Protocol time | Frame Duration | Duration of the complete event in microseconds (refers to the physical length of the packet). If you use Vector hardware for sending and receiving, the frame duration of the Rx packet is measured by the hardware. of the Tx packet is calculated based on its length and the underlying speed of the connection. Minimal discrepancies are possible in this case for identical frames. These depend on the transmission speed and have the following orders of magnitude: 0.008 microseconds at 1000 Mbits 0.040 microseconds at 100 Mbits 0.400 microseconds at 10 Mbits Note The transmission time is provided by the Ethernet interface. Therefore, the Frame Duration is only available in online mode with real bus working mode.In offline mode, the Frame

### [Windows/Trace/Columns] TraceColumnsSOA


# Trace Window: Columns for Communication Objects

Trace Window » Configuration » Column Configuration » Communication Objects

Context Menu Path: Configuration|Column Configuration|Communication Objects

## Columns for Communication Objects

| Field | Title | Function |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Time | Time | Absolute time from the measurement start, or relative to the previous event. |
| Name | Name | Name of the communication object, e.g. of a field, a PDU, a function |
| Dir | Dir | Communication direction in relation to the sender and receiver. Tx = Start of communication from sender to receiver Rx = Communication reception at receiver. * = Communication at measurement point |
| Source | Src | Sender (provider) side of the communication. The content is displayed in brackets [] when no communication between sender and receiver is sent over the bus, e.g. if a receiver has connected to the sender. |
| Destination | Dest | Receiver (consumer) side of the communication. The content is displayed in brackets [] when no communication between sender and receiver is sent over the bus. |
| Side | Side | Specifies to which side the value update of the communication object belongs. |
| Type | Type | Data type |
| Comment | Comment | If you add a comment to a data source it will appear here. |
| Namespace | Namespace | Namespace of the communication objects. |
| Diff Time (Ref. Event) | Diff Time (Ref. Event) | Time difference between the current event and the selected reference event.Th

[...truncated for brevity...]

| Date and Time | Date and Time | Displays the absolute time stamp (Date and Time) for the value update of the corresponding variable. In offline mode this corresponds to the time the update was logged. You can specify the display format (Date and Time or Time) in the Options dialog. Logging files generated with CANoe always contain date and time of measurement start in the Header.If a logging file does not contain this information, then date and time of the offline measurement start are displayed in this column. |

### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfig


# Trace Window: Configuration

Trace Window » Configuration

Context Menu: Configuration

The configuration dialog of the Trace Window consists of the following pages:

## General

Here you configure the view of the Trace Window.

## Column Configuration

Here you configure the number of columns and their sorting as well as labeling of the individual columns for the Trace Window.

## Column Layouts

Here you can create, delete, rename column layouts, and set them as default layout.

## Buffer

Here you determine a prioritized data storage for the buffer of the current Trace Window. The prioritization has an effect on all Trace and Test Trace Windows.Additionally you get the current storage information.

## View

Here you configure the font, decimal places and the standard event highlighting.For all settings you are forwarded to the global Options dialog.

To display signal values as symbolic description you have to activate the icon in the ribbon.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfigARINC825


# Configuration

ARINC-825 Trace Window (ARINC-825) » Configuration

## Columns

### Additional columns

You can activate a set of specific columns with [Use defined standard columns]. With [Add column(s)] you can activate additional columns.

- Trace Window Columns For ARINC 825

## View

### Event highlighting

The Trace Window allows you to highlight events depending on different categories.

You can open the dialog to set the colors via the context menu of the Trace Window with the entry Configuration…|View|Standard Highlighting|[Change…]. Alternatively you can open the Options dialog (Windows/Blocks|Trace Window|Event Highlighting).

|  | Cross Reference You can find more configuration options on Trace Window: Configuration. |

Different event types are visualized by icons in the Trace Window.

In addition to bus events the Trace Window displays service events, too. A service event contains summarizing information related to a node service (ARINC-825, see /1/) or an SDL service (ARINC-826, see /3/).

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfigBuffer


# Trace Window Configuration: Buffer

Trace Window » Configuration » Buffer

Context Menu Path: Configuration|Buffer

| General | Column Configuration | Column Layouts | Buffer | View |  |

On this page you can make the following settings:

## Prioritized Data Storage

The storage capacity of the comprehensive buffer is limited. When the storage capacity is exceeded, older events are overwritten by the recording of newer events. If there are multiple Trace and Test Trace Windows in the configuration, the process of deleting older events can be delayed as long as possible for a specific Trace or Test Trace Window by prioritizing that window.If this option Prioritized data storage is activated, the data in this Trace Windows will be saved as long as possible.

## Buffer Settings

Here you can find information about the location and the size of the pagefile.To adjust the buffer settings you are forwarded to the Options dialog.

Tip: The default program settings should be suitable for almost all cases, so you don't normally need to make any changes here.

Trace Window: Buffer Concept

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfigGeneral


# Trace Window Configuration: General

Trace Window » Configuration » General

Context Menu Path: Configuration|General

| General | Column Configuration | Column Layouts | Buffer | View |  |

On this page you can configure basic settings for the Trace Window.

## Display Mode

The display mode setting determines how the rows in the Trace Window are to be written.

- In chronological mode each new row is inserted below the previous row, and when the window is full it scrolls upward.This display mode shows the chronological sequence of messages very well. With high bus loads, however, this display mode may cause performance problems since it requires a lot of processing.

- In fixed Position mode each message (type) is assigned to a specific line the first time it occurs, and all further messages of the same type are written to the same line.

## Time

- If you select absolute as the time setting, the time display will indicate the specific point in time at which the message was received (beginning from the measurement start).

- The relative time setting, on the other hand, indicates the time in relation to the preceding message.In chronological mode this is the message received directly before the current message, whereas in fixed position mode the relative time is displayed in relation to the previous message of the same type.

## Signal Sorting

Here you can set, if the signals should be displayed

- Sorted corresponding to the order in the database.

- Sorted by their st

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfigMOSTAckNackInformation


# Trace Window: Ack/NAck Information for MOST Messages

Option MOST Trace Window » Configuration » Columns » MOST » Ack/NAck Information

Transmit status information for control and AMS messages is displayed in the Ack column (in the Trace Window ) and can also be called up using the ACK CAPL selector of mostMessage, mostAMSMessage or mostRawMessage variables.

| Bit | 7 | 6 | 5 | 4 | 3 | 2 | 1 | 0 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | EarlyTermination | n.a. | NAck | Ack | RxBufFull | CRC Error | ValidReceipt | NoResponse |
| MOST25 | — | — | Rx, Tx, Spy | Rx, Tx, Spy | Tx*, Spy | Tx*, Spy | Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy |
| MOST50 | Spy | — | Rx, Tx, Spy | Rx, Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy |
| MOST150 | Spy | — | Rx, Tx, Spy | Rx, Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy | Tx, Spy |

* only available with the VN2600/2610

| Bit | Description |
| --- | --- |
| EarlyTermination | 1: Message transmission was aborted prematurely because there is no recipient, a CRC error was detected, or the receive buffer is full. 0: Transmission was not aborted prematurely. |
| NAck | 1: The recipient did not receive the message and acknowledged it negatively. In the case of group/broadcast addressing, at least one recipient did not receive the message and acknowledged it negatively. Negative acknowledgment means that either the receive buffer is full or a CRC error was detected (see bits 3 (RxBufFull) and 2 (CRC Error)). 0: Message receipt was not negatively 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfigMOSTEventFilter


# Trace Window: Predefined Filter | MOST Events

Option MOST Trace Window » Filter » Predefined Filter » MOST Events

## Messages

These settings determine which messages are to be filtered.

An activated check box means that this message type passes the filter and is displayed in the Trace Window.

The check box AMS atoms (CMS) should be activated, if analyzing mainly the communication on bus level.

If working on the application level, it is advisable to activate the AMS check box and to deactivate AMS atoms (CMS).

With the Packet check box the display of packets of the asynchronous channel can be disabled.

|  | Note When using the VN2600/VN2610 or VN2640 of Vector as hardware interface the asynchronous channel can be deactivated in the hardware configuration dialog. Thus the CPU as well as the USB bandwidth are discharged, whereas the filter described here solely filters out the packets. |

## MOST Events

These settings determine the filtering of event types.

An activated check box means that this event type passes the filter and is displayed in the Trace Window.

## Default Settings

Here you can save your currently selected settings as the default MOST filter settings, and also load or delete the default settings.

If a Trace Window is inserted in the Measurement Setup with standard settings, then the stored filter settings are used. If no standard settings were stored, the factory settings are used instead.

If a Trace Window is inserted in the Measurement Setup wi

# Trace Window Configuration: Event Highlighting - MOST

Option MOST Trace Window » Configuration » Event Highlighting MOST (user-defined)

Context Menu Path: Configuration|View|MOST Highlighting

In the MOST Highlighting dialog you can set colors for the event highlighting MOST.

The user-defined event highlighting allows the coloring of specific MOST messages in the Trace Window. This can be done for each Trace Window individually.

### Advantages

- MOST messages can clearly be highlighted in the Trace Window

- MOST messages can easily be found in the Trace Window

|  | Note The event highlighting has the following priorities: CAPL (highest priority) user-defined event highlighting default event highlighting (lowest priority) That means, that an event, which is highlighted in CAPL and configured for the user defines as well as for the default event highlighting, will be highlighted in the colors defined in CAPL. It is also possible to highlight one or more MOST message directly via the context menu of the Trace Window. |

With [Trace Highlighting] you open the Options dialog. Here you can configure the default colors for MOST events.

## Configuration

- Message list

- Working without a database

- Buttons

- Keyboard operations

- Choice lists

### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfigMOSTEventHighlStandard


# Trace Window Configuration: Default Event Highlighting - MOST

Option MOST Trace Window » Configuration » Event Highlighting MOST » Default Event Highlighting - MOST

Context Menu Path: Configuration|View|MOST Highlighting|[MOST Highlighting]

Essentially this involves important network states (e.g. Light & Lock), unusual messages (e.g. system messages) and error states (e.g. OpType Error, NACK bit set).

The settings configured on this page are specific to the Windows user configuration.That is, they apply to all Trace Windows in all MOST configurations set up by a specific Windows user on a specific computer.

The On/Off switch can be used to activate/deactivate Event Highlighting for MOST completely.The previously configured settings remain in place, however.

In the Network states area, colors can be configured for the various states of Light & Lock and ConfigStatus events.You can even activate/deactivate each color option individually.

In the Messages and Addressing areas, you can configure the colors for various message types and attributes.Again, you can activate/deactivate each color option individually.

|  | Note Please note that a given message may exhibit several of these attributes simultaneously.For such cases a color highlighting sequence is defined.The first attribute listed in this sequence determines the color in which the message is displayed.The attributes are checked according to the following sequence: RemRead / RemWrite Alloc / Dealloc OpType Error

### [Windows/Trace/Configuration] TraceConfigMOSTEventHighlUserDefButtons


# Trace Window Configuration: MOST Highlighting - Buttons

Option MOST Trace Window » Configuration » Event Highlighting MOST » User-defined » Buttons

Context Menu Path: Configuration|View|MOST Highlighting

The following buttons are provided to assist you in compiling the message list:

- [New] Inserts a new message in the message list.A left mouse click on the button opens the MOST Symbol Explorer, which enables messages to be selected directly from an integrated XML function catalog.

- [Clone] Duplicates the marked message. The duplicate is added at the end of the message log.This function is very useful if a message of the same type is to be filtered with different data bytes.

- [Delete] Deletes the marked message from the message list.

- [Up] Moves the message marked in the message list one row up.

- [Down] Moves the message marked in the message list one row down.

- [Layout] Arranges the column widths in the message list automatically to ensure the best possible visibility for the entries.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

## Creating a New Message

- To create an empty message, click on the bottom-most row in the message list (which is marked with a dark grey background).

- You will need to enter any message parameter into the message list manually.

You must enter all values into the fields as numerical values. If you enter an invalid value into a field, a corresponding message appears in the status bar. Because there is no corresponding data to be found in a function catalog, the FBlock, Function and OpType fields are marked with a colored background in the message list.

### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowBuffer


# Test Trace Window: Buffer Concept

Test Trace Window » Buffer Concept

Each Test Trace Window has a main buffer and an analysis area. The main buffer contains a large portion of the tests or the entire tests. This is dependent on the configured size of the main buffer and the duration of the tests. The analysis area is an excerpt of the main buffer that corresponds to the current filter settings (predefined and analysis filter). Through a suitable filter selection, you see a corresponding time range of the tests. Older events can be subsequently reloaded from the main buffer using the Buffer Navigator.

## Usage/Navigation

If the number of recorded events won't fit into the analysis range, this is indicated at the left edge of the Test Trace Output Window. The first event of the measurement is displayed at the top.The bar represents the size of the analysis range.

With a right mouse click on the bar you open the Buffer Navigator (orange). You can use it to move the analysis area upward/downwards, to jump to a dedicated time stamp or to jump to the oldest/newest time stamp.

The loading of events to the analysis area may take some time. You can cancel this operation in the Buffer Navigator before the analysis area is completely filled. You can use Reload analysis buffer content to manually load the analysis area.

Beside the navigation via the menu of the Buffer Navigator, following possibilities are available:

## Navigation to a specific time stamp

Shift the bar upward

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowCommentEvent


# Test Trace Window: Set Markers

Test Trace Window » Set Markers

Context Menu: New Marker

In the Test Trace Window, you can set markers for quick location and commenting of events. The marker is assigned an event, and therefore its time stamp as well.

|  | Note Markers which were set in the Test Trace Window can be displayed, but not moved, in the Trace Window, Graphics Window and State Tracker. These markers are indicated by a small lock . If a marker is set to a time stamp which does not fit any time stamp in the Trace Window, Graphics Window or State Tracker, the marker is then displayed on the next fitting event in the Test Trace Window. Thus there can be multiple markers for a single event. A tooltip displays the markers. Only those markers which are in the analysis area are displayed. If you navigate to another marker outside this area, the Test Trace content is reloaded. |

## Set Markers, Navigate to Markers

You can set markers via the context menu of the Test Trace Window or via a double-click on the marker column.

Navigation to the individual markers is possible either using the context menu of the marker column, shortcuts or the toolbar .

|  | Note Navigating between markers and also adding, editing or removing markers is only possible after the measurement or during the pause. The comments column is only then visible. |

## Visualization

For visualization of markers, a marker column is available to you on the left side of the Test Trace Output Window:

| 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowCopy


# Test Trace Window: Copy to Clipboard

Test Trace Window » Copying Contents to the Clipboard

The selected rows of the Test Trace Window are copied to the clipboard.You can then paste the copied rows as text in another applications.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowExamples


# Test Trace Window: Sample Configurations and Applications

Test Trace Window » Sample Configurations and Applications

|  | Example Creating user-defined column layouts for the Test Trace Window You can define for each Test Trace Window an own column layout. This individual layout is stored for every window and will be used at every opening of a configuration. Open the Test Trace Window configuration dialog for an existing Test Trace Window and go to the Columns page. Select in the list view Available Fields a column and move it with to the list view Displayed Fields. You can change the sequence of columns with drag and drop or with and . Custom Test Trace Windows with filters You can create special Test Trace Windows for different evaluations. You can create as any number of Test Trace Windows. Add a Test Trace Window to the Test Setup for Test Units, page Test Traces. You can use the predefined filters in the Test Trace Window to filter out special test configurations completely from the data flow (no event storage in the buffer) that are not of interest in the respective window. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowFeatures


# Test Trace Window: Function Overview

Test Trace Window » Function Overview

The Test Trace Window offers the following functions:

| Window Configuration |
| --- |
| You have numerous options for configuring the Test Trace Window. In addition to the basic settings you can also configure the columns. With the splitter function of the Test Trace Output Window you can analyze two different time segments in parallel. The number of decimal places to be displayed for time stamps in the Test Trace Window can be specified in the global configuration dialog. The Test Trace Window offers a range of additional views. |
| Recording and Evaluation |
| With the available analysis filters you can show/hide specific events. Markers make it easy to tag and locate events. If you mark an Event in the Test Trace Window, the position of the event is highlighted. On this way for a detailed analysis you can set several reference points with corresponding comment. With this you can set several reference points with corresponding comment for a detailed analysis.This indication is only available in pause mode or after measurement stop. You can use the search function to search through the complete buffer of the Test Trace Window for events. You can use the context menu, the keyboard or the toolbar to control the Test Trace Window functions. Selected rows in the Test Trace Window can be copied to the clipboard, to use them for analysis in other applications. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH 

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowFilter


# Test Trace Window: Filters

Test Trace Window » Filters

There are three basic types of filters available in the Test Trace Window.

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. |

## Test Configuration Filter

In the area of test configuration filter, you can show and hide the events of existing test configurations. Events can be deleted from the data stream.

## Analysis Filters

With the available analysis filters you can show/hide specific events.

The analysis filters only affect the view of events displayed in the Test Trace Window. No events are deleted from the data stream.

## Column Filters

You can configure ad-hoc filters directly in the columns of the Test Trace Window (cf. Excel AutoFilter).

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowFilterAnalysis


# Test Trace Window: Analysis Filters

Test Trace Window » Filters » Analysis Filters

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. |

Several predefined analysis filters are available.

You can activate/deactivate the analysis filters via the toolbar of the Test Trace Window.

The analysis filters only affect the view of events displayed in the Test Trace Window. No events are deleted from the data stream.

The following analysis filters are available:

## Show Test Steps

- Show All Test Steps All events of test steps will be displayed.

- Show Only Test Steps Not Passed Only test steps with verdicts inconclusive, fail and error in test system will be displayed.

- Hide All Test Steps All events of test steps will be hidden.

## Show Commands

All events that represent a command or the begin of a command will be displayed.

## Show Wait Instructions

All events that represent a wait condition will be displayed.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowFilterColumn


# Test Trace Window: Column Filters

Test Trace Window » Filters » Column Filters

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. |

If you move the cursor over a table heading, a filter symbol appears in the upper right corner, e.g. .

With a left mouse click a list with all column items from the analysis area is shown, e.g. all actions, verdicts, ....

Now you can filter on one of these items directly, or you can configure a complex filter condition (Custom...) – wildcards are also permitted.

A small orange filter symbol symbolizes a set filter condition.

|  | Note The item list considers already set predefined filters, analysis filters and further column filters. |

If the number of recorded events won't fit into the analysis range, further items can be loaded from the data history via Display all items . Now these items can also be used as filter.

If not all possible items can be displayed, an corresponding icon and a tooltip will be shown.

You can delete column filters with the context menu command Reset All Column Filters.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowFilterPredefined


# Test Trace Window: Test Configuration Filter

Test Trace Window » Filters » Predefined Filters

|  | Note The selected filter settings are stored in the configuration. If filter settings are changed, the corresponding events are loaded into the analysis area of the Trace Window. |

You can open the test configuration filter in the Trace Explorer via the icon in the toolbar of the Test Trace Window.

## Filter Concept

A multi-level filter concept is used for the test configuration filters. With a left-click on an icon you open the selection for the filter settings.

| Symbol | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
|  | Do not Save Events Events are not saved in the buffer of the Test Trace Window and thus cannot be displayed later. You can use this setting selectively to remove unimportant events from the data flow in order to free up memory space for data of interest. |
|  | Hide Events Events are saved in the buffer of the Test Trace Window but they are hidden in the Test Trace Window. The saved events can be displayed later. |
|  | Display Events Events are saved in the buffer of the Test Trace Window and displayed in the Test Trace Window. The saved events can be hidden later. |

|  | Example |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

# Test Trace Window: Keyboard Operation

- <Ctrl>+<v> for paste

Key combinations marked with a (*) are not contained in the context menu.

| Navigate to oldest/newest event | <Ctrl> + <Shift> + <Pos 1> /<Ctrl> + <Shift> + <End> |
| Go to next marker | <Ctrl> + <Shift> + <Down Arrow> |
| Go to previous marker | <Ctrl> + <Shift> + <Up Arrow> |
| Scroll one line down | <Down Arrow> |
| Scroll one line up | <Up Arrow> |
| Scroll one page down / Shift analysis range | <Page Down> |
| Scroll one page up / Shift analysis range | <Page Up> |
| Select next line | <Ctrl> + <Down Arrow> /<Ctrl> + <Up Arrow> |
| Select next page | <Ctrl> + <Page Down> /<Ctrl> + <Page Up> |

| Description | Keys |

| Activate the search field in the Trace toolbar (*) | <Ctrl> + <f> |
| Search forwards (*) | <F3> |
| Search backwards (*) | <Shift> + <F3> |
| Increase font size | <Ctrl> + <+> |
| Decrease font size | <Ctrl> + <-> |
| Default font size | <Ctrl> + <0> |

### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowSearch


# Test Trace Window: Search

Test Trace Window » Search

The Test Trace Window contains various search criteria in the Search page of the Test Trace Explorer.

The search is always started from the current cursor position in the display area. If the display area search has been completed, you can extend the search to the data history after the measurement. You can cancel the search at any time.

|  | Note All displayed columns are searched. Hidden columns are not taken into account in the search. If the search is started during the measurement, the Test Trace Window goes into pause mode when the first event is found. |

## Text Search

With the text search, you have the ability to quickly and simply search for text within the Test Trace Window.

Enter the text or text part you would like to search for in the Search for this textfield.

|  | Note The wildcard (*) is automatically used for the search, e.g. *text*, text* and *text. Wildcards are not automatically used if the Find whole words only option is selected. |

You can also start the text search in the toolbar.

| Toolbar Icon | Description | Keyboard Operation (in active Test Trace Window) |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Enter here the text or text part you would like to search for. | <Ctrl>+<f>After the measurement |
|  | Search event upward | <Shift>+<F3>After the measurement |
|  | Search event downward | <F3>After the measurement |

## Search Options

Depending on the search criterion, the following options are availabl

### [Windows/TraceTest] TestTraceWindowSplitter


# Test Trace Window: Splitter

Test Trace Window » Splitter

After the measurement, the Test Trace Output Window can be split into two views (horizontal or vertical adjustment) via the toolbar .Because the analysis area of the buffers can be moved in the two views independently of one another, you can analyze different time segments in parallel in the Test Trace Window. Selected lines are displayed independently of each other.

If enter a marker for an event, this setting will be applied to both views.

All configuration settings for the Test Trace Window, e.g., filters or columns apply to both views.

The second view is closed again at the start of a measurement.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/TraceTest/Configuration] TestTraceConfig


# Test Trace Window: Configuration

Test Trace Window » Configuration

Context Menu: Configuration

The configuration dialog of the Test Trace Window consists of the following pages:

## General

Here you configure the view of the Test Trace Window.

## Columns

Here you configure the number of columns and their sorting as well as labeling of the individual columns for the Test Trace Window.

## Buffer

Here you determine a prioritized data storage for the buffer of the current Test Trace Window. The prioritization has an effect on all Trace and Test Trace Windows.Additionally you get the current storage information.

## View

Here you configure the font and the decimal places.For all settings you are forwarded to the global Options dialog.

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TraceTest/Configuration] TestTraceConfigBuffer


# Test Trace Window Configuration: Buffer

Test Trace Window » Configuration » Buffer

Shortcut Menu Path: Configuration|Buffer

| General | Columns | Buffer | View |  |

On this page you can make the following settings:

## Prioritized Data Storage

The storage capacity of the comprehensive buffer is limited. When the storage capacity is exceeded, older events are overwritten by the recording of newer events. If there are multiple Trace and Test Trace Windows in the configuration, the process of deleting older events can be delayed as long as possible for a specific Trace or Test Trace Window by prioritizing that window.If this option Prioritized data storage is activated, the data in this Test Trace Windows will be saved as long as possible.

## Buffer Settings

Here you can find information about the location and the size of the pagefile.To adjust the buffer settings you are forwarded to the Options dialog.

Tip: The default program settings should be suitable for almost all cases, so you don't normally need to make any changes here.

Test Trace Window: Buffer Concept

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |


### [Windows/TraceTest/Configuration] TestTraceConfigColumns


# Test Trace Window Configuration: Columns

Test Trace Window » Configuration » Columns

Shortcut Menu Path: Configuration|Columns

| General | Columns | Buffer | View |  |

On this page you can select the columns to be included in the Test Trace Window and also specify the column titles and sequence.

## Fields

## Available Fields

Here the available fields for the column layout are displayed.

## Displayed Fields

Here the selected column layout for the Test Trace Window is displayed. Only the visible columns are listed, indicating for each column the title and field. You can edit the column titles and also change the sequence of columns.

|  | How to add/delete a column to the Test Trace Window: Select in the available fields the desired column with the mouse. Click on the button. The new column is then inserted in the Displayed fields list (after the currently selected entry). If no entry was selected, the new column will be added at the end of the list.You can also select multiple columns and add them all in one step. You can delete an already added field with <Del> or . How to change sequence of columns You can change the sequence of columns with drag and drop or with and . |

|  | Note Test Trace Window columns can directly be inserted/removed using the table head context menu commandField chooser… (also during measurement). |

Test Trace Window: Sample Configurations and Applications

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

### [Windows/Video] VideoWindowConfiguration


# Configuration

Video Window » Configuration

Ribbon: Hardware Tab|Video Configuration

In the Video Configuration dialog, you can manage and configure Video Windows.

|  | Note You can configure up to four Video Windows. |

The most commonly used video formats are supported in the Video Window for the Online Source depending on the current system configuration and the DirectShow components already installed on the system. Thus, for example, uncompressed AVI, compressed AVI (with corresponding installed Codec, e.g., DivX), MPG, MPEG, WMV, and MP4.The AVI format is available for the Recorder File.

If the recording of a video file is paused and restarted during an active measurement, visible artifacts may occur in the image at the intersection points of the video file. You can prevent occurrence of these artifacts by setting the pre-trigger time (see Recorder below) to a value greater than 0.

## Buttons

You can add a new Video Window to the configuration with the button.

You can remove a selected Video Window from the configuration with the button.

With the DirectX Diagnostics…, you can open a Windows-internal dialog that checks your system for installed DirectX components. You should run the diagnostics if you encounter problems with the Video Window.

|  | Note DirectX Version 8.1 or higher should be installed on your computer. |

## Configuring a Video Window

All configured Video Windows are listed on the left side.

The settings of the selected Video Window are disp

# Video Window: Navigation

Video Window » Navigation

### [Windows/Video] VideoWindowSynchronization


# Video Window: Synchronization

Video Window » Synchronization

Video files displayed in the Video Window can be synchronized with data in other Analysis Windows when the measurement is stopped.

The maximum synchronization precision can be achieved with video files that were recorded during an online measurement using the video window. In this case, in addition to the video file an identically named synchronization file with file extension *.VideoSync is recorded. With this supplement to the video file, precise synchronization between the CANoe measurement time and the camera hardware is ensured by storing the associated measurement time instant of the measurement for each video frame.

|  | Note For a typical frame rate of 30 frames/second, synchronization data for a maximum of 12 hours of video are stored per window. That is, a precise synchronization is possible for the last 12 hours of the measurement. If the video file is longer, the video position in areas for which no more data exist is derived from the measurement time through a direct conversion. This enables a synchronization same as before, but it is less precise. |

## Offline Source

If you select a video file in the Offline Mode Window with associated synchronization file as the offline source, the synchronization file will be played again automatically within the scope of offline mode so that synchronization data also exist for the offline analysis.

|  | Note If no synchronization file is present, the video

[...truncated for brevity...]

### [Windows/Write] WriteWindowStateIndicator


# Write Window: State Indicator

Write Window » State Indicator

The button at the left in the status bar of CANoe enables you to quickly access the Write Window. The button shown changes when new, unread warnings or error messages are displayed in the Write Window. Click the button to open the Write Window. In this case, messages are regarded as read and the state indicator is reset.

## States of the state indicator

| Button | Priority | When |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | 1 | Visualizes a new error message in the Write Window. |
|  | 2 | Visualizes a new error warning in the Write Window. |
|  | 3 | Visualizes messages that do not correspond to an error message or warning in the Write Window.This icon is also displayed before the start of a measurement. |

## Priority of the state indicator

The state indicator always visualizes the message with the highest priority.

For example, if a new error message occurs in the Write Window, is displayed until the state indicator is reset. If warnings also occur after the arrival of an error message, continues to be displayed with Priority 1.

## Reset of the state indicator

A reset of the state indicator means that the visualization of the state starts over with .

A reset is triggered by the following events:

- When the configuration is closed

- When a measurement is started

- When the icon of the state indicator used to open or activate the Write Window is clicked.

Animated State Indicators

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH

Unfortunately, this also applies to CANoe. To better localize and correct system errors that occur infrequently, CANoe has diagnostic mechanisms for reporting these errors. Please be sure to write down the exact text of the error message. With this information our telephone Customer Service can then help you to solve the problem more quickly. |

| 02-0001 | Trace Window '<Trace Name>': Fading out of events with unchanged values, as well as fading out of unmodified data are not supported for additional events. To implement the hiding of events without data change and the hiding of unchanged data, information must be stored for each event. When there are many different events, this cannot be absolutely guaranteed due to memory use. |
| 02-0002 | Trace Window '<Trace Name>': The maximum number of <number> events in fixed mode has been reached. Additional events cannot be displayed anymore. To reduce the number of different events, please place event or channel filters in the Measurement Setup before the Trace Window.. The maximum number of events/lines in the display buffer of the fixed mode is limited. In the case of many different events, this may cause that not all events can be displayed in the Trace Window. The filters in the Trace Window only act as display filters. The hidden events nevertheless occupy a space in the display buffer and can be made visible again by deactivating the display filters. If many different events are sent and most of them are hidden by the trace filters, this message may appear although only a few events are visible in the Trace Window. ### [Windows/Write/WriteWindowNotifications] 07CAPLNETCompilation


# CAPL/.NET Compilation

Write Window » Notifications » CAPL/.NET Compilation

| [07] CAPL/.NET Compilation |
| --- |
| ID | Notification / Description |
| 07-0001 | CAPL compilation is turned off in this configuration The CAPL compilation was turned off in this configuration, to relay the configuration without CAPL source files. If you have CAPL source files, activate the compilation: Open the Options dialog. Under Programing on CAPL page in the CAPL Compiler area deactivate the Suppress compilation to work without CAPL sources (.can) check box. A CAPL compilation may be necessary when starting measurement, if the databases or the system variable configuration have changed since last compilation. |
| 07-0020 | Assembly <path> is no longer binary compatible, please rebuild it Due to changes in CANoe 14 some old assemblies still built for .NET 2.0 are no longer binary compatible to the CANoe API and will not work properly. CANoe detects such assemblies and warns you with this warning. The solution is to rebuild the assembly against the current CANoe .NET API (you can still use .NET 2.0 as target framework). The API is still source-compatible, so you do not need to modify the assembly source code. If you cannot rebuild the assembly, for example because you no longer have the source code, please contact Vector support for a solution. |

| Version 15© Vector Informatik GmbH |  |

| 08-0020 | Breakpoint during measurement start / stop ignored For technical reasons, the execution of the CAPL or .NET program cannot be stopped during the measurement start and stop. Breakpoints that are reached, for example, from the on start CAPL event procedure are therefore ignored. |

### [Windows/Write/WriteWindowNotifications] 12TimeSynchronization


# Time Synchronization

Write Window » Notifications » Time Synchronization

| [12] Time Synchronization |
| --- |
| ID | Notification / Description |
| 12-0001 | Time-Sync: Software time synchronization is required but not activated (or drivers need to be restarted). When multiple hardware interfaces are used in one configuration, their clocks need to be synchronized because otherwise their time stamps will drift apart. Some hardware interfaces only support software time synchronization. If you use such an interface in a configuration, you need to activate software time synchronization. You can do this in the Control Panel's Vector Hardware dialog under General Information|Settings. Once you've activated software time synchronization, you need to restart the hardware interface drivers. You can make sure this happens by re-booting the computer. However, you can also do this by removing the hardware interfaces from the computer and then reconnecting them. |
| 12-0002 | Time-Sync: Software or hardware time synchronization is required but neither is activated (or drivers need to be restarted). When multiple hardware interfaces are used in the same configuration, their clocks need to be synchronized because otherwise their time stamps will drift apart.Hardware time synchronization generates more precise time stamps than software time synchronization does. You can activate global hardware time synchronization (Hardware ribbon tab|Network Hardware|Hardware Sync).Activation of soft

[...truncated for brevity...]
